# How to contact Valimail Support | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9318679#main-content)

Table of contents

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

How to contact Valimail Support

How to contact Valimail Support
===============================

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[Accessing the messenger window](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9318679#h_3b98486477)

[Valimail AI Chat](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9318679#h_67bea53ad7)

[Logging a ticket](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9318679#h_166ee594de)

The following changes are in effect as of May 7th, 2024

This article does not apply to Valigov.

Accessing the messenger window
------------------------------

The messenger will be available directly in the Valimail app:

1.  Navigate to [app.valimail.com](https://app.valimail.com/)
    
2.  Login to your account
    
3.  Click on the messenger icon in the lower right corner
    

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380872/681954f67b2e0deb517744e2/6CQOq-xPK8W4Z0m2W07-ePfXF0DpBqa2cA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=467653b1ddb8e518bb90fc9b976914c6cc2187c75068909a9b75524a22d73d35&req=dSAjH8p2nYlYW%2FMW1HO4zcBdVpxerQnWHCisdDBQt58W17dPZ97CR%2FAU3kym%0A9q3LP8gZhJJ4x0pjtqk%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380872/681954f67b2e0deb517744e2/6CQOq-xPK8W4Z0m2W07-ePfXF0DpBqa2cA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=467653b1ddb8e518bb90fc9b976914c6cc2187c75068909a9b75524a22d73d35&req=dSAjH8p2nYlYW%2FMW1HO4zcBdVpxerQnWHCisdDBQt58W17dPZ97CR%2FAU3kym%0A9q3LP8gZhJJ4x0pjtqk%3D%0A)

Valimail AI Chat
----------------

The Valimail AI is here to help with all inquiries directly from the messenger:

1.  Open the messenger window
    
2.  Click on "Ask a question"
    

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380883/d253360e3e0e750f36fdb9ed/P16nBbWuJnsmFEmNegzsSy71dK_OZuNcnw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=90be3b35331595286f91da4dcbba949d7511c59d3eb1e543d7d2d456fa8bceb5&req=dSAjH8p2nYlXWvMW1HO4zcThXIYjFGFNPLNasBhBAljwkKdGFIECki0l4Fmd%0ABDDeJQSrrh6zMVM9b%2Fs%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380883/d253360e3e0e750f36fdb9ed/P16nBbWuJnsmFEmNegzsSy71dK_OZuNcnw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=90be3b35331595286f91da4dcbba949d7511c59d3eb1e543d7d2d456fa8bceb5&req=dSAjH8p2nYlXWvMW1HO4zcThXIYjFGFNPLNasBhBAljwkKdGFIECki0l4Fmd%0ABDDeJQSrrh6zMVM9b%2Fs%3D%0A)

Logging a ticket
----------------

1.  From the messenger window click on "Ask a question"
    
2.  After the AI reply, you can click on "Get More Help" to open a support ticket
    
3.  Click on "Create Ticket"
    
4.  Fill out the ticket creation form
    

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380897/5c61b722431fef6f75bdb9ac/q4zaY6kCxPvDZLx0BnNv5K9z_ERiuROcWA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5d37241ee887cd40922f22c1df0fc0d022d6154f1af9b7cf47a2ae39402693a6&req=dSAjH8p2nYlWXvMW1HO4zUvSRxgmPBViYgUsq5vgcwrfx7mWeIS2n4oWsjA7%0Al3WYYaPNKn7YgJO%2BXLw%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380897/5c61b722431fef6f75bdb9ac/q4zaY6kCxPvDZLx0BnNv5K9z_ERiuROcWA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5d37241ee887cd40922f22c1df0fc0d022d6154f1af9b7cf47a2ae39402693a6&req=dSAjH8p2nYlWXvMW1HO4zUvSRxgmPBViYgUsq5vgcwrfx7mWeIS2n4oWsjA7%0Al3WYYaPNKn7YgJO%2BXLw%3D%0A)

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380911/413728faeb3dcfea46d3970e/hWdtEX8qRK3lgR0yDzlak-PdsVRDxaxzLg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=686a0d09f2a2f6f4c6ea9f6d5754f2edb3da89c33199cd61ed4d6e9ea73ef974&req=dSAjH8p2nYheWPMW1HO4zXciyagGQA34cYEJcuqjWOSUlSvTK%2Bg5leQqIvfp%0Aks5AC%2Fjl7U5cVspKVzc%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380911/413728faeb3dcfea46d3970e/hWdtEX8qRK3lgR0yDzlak-PdsVRDxaxzLg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=686a0d09f2a2f6f4c6ea9f6d5754f2edb3da89c33199cd61ed4d6e9ea73ef974&req=dSAjH8p2nYheWPMW1HO4zXciyagGQA34cYEJcuqjWOSUlSvTK%2Bg5leQqIvfp%0Aks5AC%2Fjl7U5cVspKVzc%3D%0A)

How to contact Valimail Support

How to contact Valimail Support
===============================

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9318679-how-to-contact-valimail-support#h_3b98486477)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9318679-how-to-contact-valimail-support#h_67bea53ad7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9318679-how-to-contact-valimail-support#h_166ee594de)

The following changes are in effect as of May 7th, 2024

This article does not apply to Valigov.

Accessing the messenger window
------------------------------

The messenger will be available directly in the Valimail app:

1.  Navigate to [app.valimail.com](https://app.valimail.com/)
    
2.  Login to your account
    
3.  Click on the messenger icon in the lower right corner
    

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380872/681954f67b2e0deb517744e2/6CQOq-xPK8W4Z0m2W07-ePfXF0DpBqa2cA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=467653b1ddb8e518bb90fc9b976914c6cc2187c75068909a9b75524a22d73d35&req=dSAjH8p2nYlYW%2FMW1HO4zcBdVpxerQnWHCisdDBQt58W17dPZ97CR%2FAU3kym%0A9q3LP8gZhJJ4x0pjtqk%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380872/681954f67b2e0deb517744e2/6CQOq-xPK8W4Z0m2W07-ePfXF0DpBqa2cA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=467653b1ddb8e518bb90fc9b976914c6cc2187c75068909a9b75524a22d73d35&req=dSAjH8p2nYlYW%2FMW1HO4zcBdVpxerQnWHCisdDBQt58W17dPZ97CR%2FAU3kym%0A9q3LP8gZhJJ4x0pjtqk%3D%0A)

Valimail AI Chat
----------------

The Valimail AI is here to help with all inquiries directly from the messenger:

1.  Open the messenger window
    
2.  Click on "Ask a question"
    

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380883/d253360e3e0e750f36fdb9ed/P16nBbWuJnsmFEmNegzsSy71dK_OZuNcnw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=90be3b35331595286f91da4dcbba949d7511c59d3eb1e543d7d2d456fa8bceb5&req=dSAjH8p2nYlXWvMW1HO4zcThXIYjFGFNPLNasBhBAljwkKdGFIECki0l4Fmd%0ABDDeJQSrrh6zMVM9b%2Fs%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380883/d253360e3e0e750f36fdb9ed/P16nBbWuJnsmFEmNegzsSy71dK_OZuNcnw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=90be3b35331595286f91da4dcbba949d7511c59d3eb1e543d7d2d456fa8bceb5&req=dSAjH8p2nYlXWvMW1HO4zcThXIYjFGFNPLNasBhBAljwkKdGFIECki0l4Fmd%0ABDDeJQSrrh6zMVM9b%2Fs%3D%0A)

Logging a ticket
----------------

1.  From the messenger window click on "Ask a question"
    
2.  After the AI reply, you can click on "Get More Help" to open a support ticket
    
3.  Click on "Create Ticket"
    
4.  Fill out the ticket creation form
    

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380897/5c61b722431fef6f75bdb9ac/q4zaY6kCxPvDZLx0BnNv5K9z_ERiuROcWA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5d37241ee887cd40922f22c1df0fc0d022d6154f1af9b7cf47a2ae39402693a6&req=dSAjH8p2nYlWXvMW1HO4zUvSRxgmPBViYgUsq5vgcwrfx7mWeIS2n4oWsjA7%0Al3WYYaPNKn7YgJO%2BXLw%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380897/5c61b722431fef6f75bdb9ac/q4zaY6kCxPvDZLx0BnNv5K9z_ERiuROcWA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5d37241ee887cd40922f22c1df0fc0d022d6154f1af9b7cf47a2ae39402693a6&req=dSAjH8p2nYlWXvMW1HO4zUvSRxgmPBViYgUsq5vgcwrfx7mWeIS2n4oWsjA7%0Al3WYYaPNKn7YgJO%2BXLw%3D%0A)

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380911/413728faeb3dcfea46d3970e/hWdtEX8qRK3lgR0yDzlak-PdsVRDxaxzLg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=686a0d09f2a2f6f4c6ea9f6d5754f2edb3da89c33199cd61ed4d6e9ea73ef974&req=dSAjH8p2nYheWPMW1HO4zXciyagGQA34cYEJcuqjWOSUlSvTK%2Bg5leQqIvfp%0Aks5AC%2Fjl7U5cVspKVzc%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1049380911/413728faeb3dcfea46d3970e/hWdtEX8qRK3lgR0yDzlak-PdsVRDxaxzLg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=686a0d09f2a2f6f4c6ea9f6d5754f2edb3da89c33199cd61ed4d6e9ea73ef974&req=dSAjH8p2nYheWPMW1HO4zXciyagGQA34cYEJcuqjWOSUlSvTK%2Bg5leQqIvfp%0Aks5AC%2Fjl7U5cVspKVzc%3D%0A)

# How do I delete my free Valimail Monitor account? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10871129-how-do-i-delete-my-free-valimail-monitor-account#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

How do I delete my free Valimail Monitor account?

How do I delete my free Valimail Monitor account?
=================================================

Updated over 5 months ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10871129-how-do-i-delete-my-free-valimail-monitor-account#h_bc4d541db7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10871129-how-do-i-delete-my-free-valimail-monitor-account#h_0c1bca9f5c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10871129-how-do-i-delete-my-free-valimail-monitor-account#h_d6e74124c6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10871129-how-do-i-delete-my-free-valimail-monitor-account#h_78e8c74b62)

How to Delete Your Valimail Monitor Account
===========================================

Valimail Monitor is a free service that helps you identify the email sending activity from your domains so you can see where you're passing or failing DMARC. If you no longer wish to use this service, you can request to have your account deleted. Please follow the steps below to request the deletion of your Valimail Monitor account.

Step 1: Send an Email to Support
--------------------------------

To request the deletion of your free Valimail Monitor account, please email [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#04777174746b7670447265686d69656d682a676b69)
. This email must be sent from an email address that is associated with your Monitor account. Our support team will not honor the request for deletion if the email is received from an address that is not associated with the Monitor account.

Step 2: Wait for Confirmation
-----------------------------

Once we receive your request, our support team will review it and process the deletion of your account. You will receive a confirmation email once your account has been successfully deleted.

Additional Information
----------------------

If you have any questions or concerns about the deletion of your Valimail Monitor account, please do not hesitate to contact our support team at [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#2d5e585d5d425f596d5b4c4144404c4441034e4240)
. We are happy to assist you with any issues you may have.

Thank you for using Valimail Monitor. We hope to see you again in the future!

* * *

# How to view and export my old tickets | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9318696-how-to-view-and-export-my-old-tickets#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

How to view and export my old tickets

How to view and export my old tickets
=====================================

Updated over a year ago

Any open tickets prior to May 7th have been migrated to our new support system.

As of May 13th, Valimail support has migrated to a new support system. Not to worry, your old tickets are still viewable for the time being and can be exported. Here's how

1.  Navigate to [https://valimailhelp.freshdesk.com/support/tickets](https://valimailhelp.freshdesk.com/support/tickets)
    
2.  Log in with your old support credentials
    
3.  Click Export Tickets on the right hand side of the page
    
    [![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/1049394451/1ca65f460fd6c95a977de37a/image.png?expires=1755792000&signature=86b8ccf52cbc9bdbf88b24ad53b66d68dde5c9256587ddb08f900e596a6b79d6&req=dSAjH8p3mYVaWPMW1HO4zVHQNA39RzQ4OQHELK0vNXQzNboxxh0mZVpN2j%2FG%0AKRuy%0A)](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/1049394451/1ca65f460fd6c95a977de37a/image.png?expires=1755792000&signature=86b8ccf52cbc9bdbf88b24ad53b66d68dde5c9256587ddb08f900e596a6b79d6&req=dSAjH8p3mYVaWPMW1HO4zVHQNA39RzQ4OQHELK0vNXQzNboxxh0mZVpN2j%2FG%0AKRuy%0A)
    
4.  Under "Filter tickets created in", select "Set Date"
    
5.  Choose a large date range, preferably from the time you started opening tickets with Valimail.
    
6.  Select any options for export that you'd like.
    
7.  Click "Export"
    
    [![Export tickets](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/1049392188/bd11ca0fd120b3116099ed85/image.png?expires=1755792000&signature=06168f26286cd58bedb752851732a0d4c00ce10b7f24f40f811db23a5b1ad674&req=dSAjH8p3n4BXUfMW1HO4zTGtEj2urFWuLqsYCaq8plcFjvuugvCKLXq6pvpJ%0AINHl%0A)](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/1049392188/bd11ca0fd120b3116099ed85/image.png?expires=1755792000&signature=06168f26286cd58bedb752851732a0d4c00ce10b7f24f40f811db23a5b1ad674&req=dSAjH8p3n4BXUfMW1HO4zTGtEj2urFWuLqsYCaq8plcFjvuugvCKLXq6pvpJ%0AINHl%0A)

# Pointing an SPF Record to Valimail | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466368-pointing-an-spf-record-to-valimail#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

Pointing an SPF Record to Valimail

Pointing an SPF Record to Valimail
==================================

This article describes how to point the SPF record to Valimail.

Updated over a year ago

This article applies to customers using the Valimail Align and Enforce products.

**Note**: _Before you point SPF to Valimail, it is advisable and also best practice to make sure you also create a back up of your current SPF content._

Valimail uses a part of the SPF standard called Macros. These are supported by all email receivers. Macros, along with Valimail’s patented technology, allow Valimail to provide SPF services and directly address the 10 domain lookup limit in the SPF standard. ([https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7208#section-4.6.4](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7208#section-4.6.4)
)

To manage SPF for this domain from the Valimail Enforce platform, backup and remove your existing SPF record, then add the following TXT record for the domain to your DNS:

Record Name: Domain.com.

Record Type: TXT

Record Value: "v=spf1 include:%{i}.\_ip.%{h}.\_ehlo.%{d}.\_spf.vali.email ~all"

\*Replace ‘Domain.com’ with your domain name.

We recommend a TTL of 300 seconds, although using a longer TTL (up to 3600 seconds) should be fine if you'd like to reduce the load on your DNS server. Please note that because of existing DNS TTLs it may take some time for Valimail to detect that you've configured the DNS record correctly.

* * *

Related Articles

[Setup your DNS records to use Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142775-setup-your-dns-records-to-use-valimail)

[DMARC](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959039-dmarc)

How to change policy on a specific subdomain

How to change policy on a specific subdomain
============================================

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242930-how-to-change-policy-on-a-specific-subdomain#h_67d9a13a91)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242930-how-to-change-policy-on-a-specific-subdomain#h_8568c74ee9)

**Do subdomains have a default policy?**
----------------------------------------

Subdomains have the default policy "None". If a subdomain's main domain has a Valimail enforced policy, the default policy of all subdomains is the policy of the main domain. This can be changed from the "Advanced Options" menu in the Policy pop-up:

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031534475/7c3c19c7439d759fff8510f2/NM3G7iLucjrBAOaOvVAm2YlmJnQnFy2aOQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d5de6350ca35f6f50fc932089f6a8dcb44f5cca8cbd1df066a77c03dd4f59013&req=dSAkF8x9mYVYXPMW1HO4zZvtb9SGqRkaYSEd4Cq8QJukYXcAgRSw%2BvBP5RzE%0AsXF3qNZhK7wcp1vi3Gk%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031534475/7c3c19c7439d759fff8510f2/NM3G7iLucjrBAOaOvVAm2YlmJnQnFy2aOQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d5de6350ca35f6f50fc932089f6a8dcb44f5cca8cbd1df066a77c03dd4f59013&req=dSAkF8x9mYVYXPMW1HO4zZvtb9SGqRkaYSEd4Cq8QJukYXcAgRSw%2BvBP5RzE%0AsXF3qNZhK7wcp1vi3Gk%3D%0A)

There you will see the Subdomain policy setting:

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031534482/c271baa6b6629fff462f0642/FngpHKDF-EFBPz2fi1_zIw2brt20-7kBcA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d9db935f957c60c20acbceb5f31e9649558c663368936cbd8f8c09d75f946832&req=dSAkF8x9mYVXW%2FMW1HO4zbpCuEDaqKXPVZO4GnZ6Ew0SVJWkiUM9wnm6jTwb%0AhEKSUWuNHufAVNajDwk%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031534482/c271baa6b6629fff462f0642/FngpHKDF-EFBPz2fi1_zIw2brt20-7kBcA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d9db935f957c60c20acbceb5f31e9649558c663368936cbd8f8c09d75f946832&req=dSAkF8x9mYVXW%2FMW1HO4zbpCuEDaqKXPVZO4GnZ6Ew0SVJWkiUM9wnm6jTwb%0AhEKSUWuNHufAVNajDwk%3D%0A)

As a side note the policy percentage should not be switched from 100% in order for the domain to be considered at Enforcement.

**Changing the policy on individual subdomains**
------------------------------------------------

If you wish to change the policy on only specific subdomains, you will need to scroll to that subdomain in the configuration page and click on it. The change policy option will appear on the newly opened subdomain configuration page. This will only work if the specific subdomain points DMARC to us.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031534485/281d224176aac16c29458bde/Zt129BYYSZmDQj_a3ZEHb6MJZht66l42zw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=80d053b20b1732f53ef2524477a101f19c60200ee052aea1193e7686a4c2fd3f&req=dSAkF8x9mYVXXPMW1HO4zUZ%2Bu5FLFF21%2BUJC9T1jAOX91naRayLDZuREmFG4%0AAbCkPY8ofe%2FdH5WQe0Q%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031534485/281d224176aac16c29458bde/Zt129BYYSZmDQj_a3ZEHb6MJZht66l42zw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=80d053b20b1732f53ef2524477a101f19c60200ee052aea1193e7686a4c2fd3f&req=dSAkF8x9mYVXXPMW1HO4zUZ%2Bu5FLFF21%2BUJC9T1jAOX91naRayLDZuREmFG4%0AAbCkPY8ofe%2FdH5WQe0Q%3D%0A)

  
​**NOTE**: This option is only available for customers with the Enforce Premium package or higher.

* * *

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_3ff0aa7be3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_60371e2b41)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_6230ea76c3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_f9342fb9d2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_9804cd571e)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[DMARC](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959039-dmarc)

Pointing a DMARC Record to Valimail

Pointing a DMARC Record to Valimail
===================================

Updated over 11 months ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_3ff0aa7be3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_60371e2b41)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_6230ea76c3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_f9342fb9d2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466367-pointing-a-dmarc-record-to-valimail#h_9804cd571e)

_This article describes how to point DMARC to Valimail via NS, CNAME and TXT record._

This article mainly applies to Valimail Enforce and Align customers. Customers that use the free Valimail Monitor tool can click below to begin the process of pointing DMARC to Valimail.

[Getting Started with Monitor](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466256-getting-started-with-valimail-monitor)

**Pointing DMARC to Valimail for Enforce/Align customers**
----------------------------------------------------------

You will need to add a DMARC NameServer (NS) record to your DNS zone to manage a domain’s DMARC policy within the Valimail system.

To enable DMARC for a domain, please add the following NS record for the “\_dmarc” domain to your DNS:

Record Name: **\_dmarc.yourdomain.com.**

Record Type: **NS**

Record Value: **ns.vali.email.**

We recommend a TTL of 300 seconds, although using a longer TTL (up to 3600 seconds) should be fine if you'd like to reduce the load on your DNS server. Please note that because of existing DNS TTLs it may take some time for Valimail to detect that you've updated your DNS with the correct settings.

**DNS Host does not support custom NS records**
-----------------------------------------------

In this case, you will need to point your DMARC record to Valimail, by adding the following CNAME record for the “\_dmarc” subdomain to your DNS:

Record Name: \_dmarc.yourdomain.com.

Record Type: CNAME

Record Value: yourdomain.com.\_dmarca.vali.email.

When using this method, only the DMARC record and not the reporting domains are managed by Valimail. For more information about setting up external reporting domains when using a CNAME \_dmarc record, [click here](https://dmarc.org/2015/08/receiving-dmarc-reports-outside-your-domain/)
.

**Best Practice**

Delete the “\_dmarc” TXT record from your DNS zones after you’ve added the NS record (or CNAME record). We have noticed possible conflicts between the two records when the DNS Provider is Azure DNS or Cloudflare. As a best practice, make sure the only DMARC record from DNS is the NS record pointing to Valimail.

**Pointing DMARC to Valimail for Monitor customers**
----------------------------------------------------

**Update your \_dmarc TXT record**

This allows Valimail to receive your DMARC aggregate reports.

**If you already have a \_dmarc TXT record:** add **mailto:[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#71151c1003122e1016163107101d185f141c10181d)
** to the "rua" parameter. Your TXT record should look as follows:

"v=DMARC1; p=none; rua=mailto:[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)
;"

**If you don't have a \_dmarc TXT record:** create the following TXT record in DNS:

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Type** | TXT |
| **Host/Name** | \_dmarc.<your domain name> |
| **Value** | "v=DMARC1; p=none; rua=mailto:[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection)<br>;" |

#### Example: If your domain name is vmcs1.com, the host/name value to create for the TXT record would be \_dmarc.vmcs1.com

⚠️ Note:
--------

*   If a \_dmarc TXT record already exists, be sure to only add **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#acc8c1cddecff3cdcbcbecdacdc0c582c9c1cdc5c0)
    ** to the "rua" parameter; do not modify any other parameters in the record unless you're sure of what you're doing, you may impact email deliverability for your organization.
    
*   No change to your email flow will occur. Valimail will see no PII.
    

⚠️ Note:
--------

*   _We do not recommend pointing your DMARC record via NS to Valimail. For our Monitor users pointing via TXT will suffice._
    
    _Monitor users cannot manage the DMARC record in the Monitor platform, therefore we advise all Monitor customers to only point DMARC via TXT._
    

* * *

[Getting Started with Valimail DMARC Monitor for FireEye](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466261-getting-started-with-valimail-dmarc-monitor-for-fireeye)

# What are Custom Directives? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466313-what-are-custom-directives#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466313-what-are-custom-directives#h_1911b9fb92)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

What are Custom Directives?

What are Custom Directives?
===========================

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466313-what-are-custom-directives#h_1911b9fb92)

Introduction to Valimail's Instant SPF Solution

What are Custom Directives?
---------------------------

The Sender Policy Framework (SPF) is an email authentication protocol used to detect and prevent email spoofing by allowing domain owners to specify which mail servers are authorized to send emails on their behalf. An SPF record consists of directives, mechanisms, and qualifiers that assist email receivers in verifying if incoming mail from a domain comes from a legitimate host authorized by the domain's administrator.

In Valimail Enforce we manage this email authentication protocol using our Instant SPF technology, which is built on a part of the SPF standard called macros. Valimail's patented instant SPF response returns only the specific and targeted response needed for a given email-sending service that has been enabled by the customer to send on their behalf. Our catalog contains thousands of sending services, with more being added every day.

* * *

Related Articles

[Enforce Self-Onboarding Guide](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466339-enforce-self-onboarding-guide)

# How does the Valimail SPF Macro actually work? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9141676-how-does-the-valimail-spf-macro-actually-work#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

How does the Valimail SPF Macro actually work?

How does the Valimail SPF Macro actually work?
==============================================

How does Valimail’s Instant SPF® solution work?

Updated over a year ago

Pointing SPF to Valimail will basically allows you to leverage our patented instant SPF technology. That is why performing this step is a very important part or the onboarding process.

Through our patented instant SPF technology, we leveraging Macros from the SPF specs using the brackets below, so that when a query is done by the receiver's gateway, the SPF Macro will fill in the blanks of what's needed for any receiver that's out there so that whatever you say you use, will automatically be aligned for SPF once we add it to this list. That is we need to make sure that the Enabled Senders list only represents what you officially use.

The Macros that Valimail uses are as follows:

${i} - _The IP address of the server that sent the email_

${h} - _The EHLO/PTR name of the server that sent the email_

${d} - _The Mail From domain_

Therefore, when an email receiver will see this, it will recognize it's an SPF Macro and it will see the correct values for the respective queried item. In doing so, our response will be just for that sending service/specific IP.

So if an email comes from M365 for example, we do not respond with your whole SPF record containing all your services, like a regular DNS host does for example - we will only respond with the specific SPF include for M365.

This is the instant and targeted response that the SPF Macro brings to the table and what sets it miles apart from the way DNS hosts are responding to these queries. This action described above, allows the SPF Macro to act as an umbrella, underneath you can have as many services and IP as you want, without ever having to worry about the 10 SPF lookup limit anymore.

* * *

# Why Valimail Uses an SPF Soft Fail and Not a Hard Fail | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9146530-why-valimail-uses-an-spf-soft-fail-and-not-a-hard-fail#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

Why Valimail Uses an SPF Soft Fail and Not a Hard Fail

Why Valimail Uses an SPF Soft Fail and Not a Hard Fail
======================================================

SPF softfails and hardfails and why they matter.

Updated over a year ago

[Sender Policy Framework](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7208)
, or SPF, is the oldest of the common email authentication protocols, a group that also includes DKIM and DMARC. A domain’s SPF record as published in the DNS lists the servers and networks authorized to send mail messages using that domain as the Return-Path, or “bounce” domain. However, SPF is not a binary decision where every message generates either a pass or fail verdict; there are nuances to the failure mode in SPF, and how a domain constructs its SPF record can have quite an impact on how failures are handled.

**The “all” Mechanism**

Every SPF record includes the “all” mechanism, which serves as a default match for anything not listed in the SPF record. The “all” mechanism is expressed with one of the following qualifiers, each of which causes the evaluation result shown:

*   "+" pass
    
*   "-" fail
    
*   "~" softfail
    
*   "?" neutral
    

For example, a domain that includes “+all” in its SPF record will always receive an SPF result of pass for every message, regardless of whether or not it was actually sent by or on behalf of the domain.

**The Difference Between “fail” and “softfail”**

According to [RFC 7208](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7208)
, the SPF specification, a “fail” result (often called a “hard fail”) means that the domain is explicitly saying that the host is not authorized to send mail using this domain, while a “softfail” is a weaker statement saying only that the host is probably not authorized. The two can be treated differently by receiving domains, especially older mail systems; a “hardfail” result sometimes means immediate rejection of the message, while a “softfail” rarely causes a bounce by itself (although it can contribute to a decision to deliver the message to a spam folder or reject it outright).

**SPF and DMARC**

DMARC is an authentication technology that relies on both SPF and DKIM, requiring only that one of the two evaluations generate a passing result (along with domain alignment) in order for the DMARC validation check to pass. DMARC makes no distinction between an SPF softfail and an SPF hardfail; both are SPF failures when used in a DMARC evaluation. However, in order for DMARC to be properly evaluated, both SPF and DKIM must be checked for the message at hand, something that is impossible to do if the message is rejected by the receiver due to an SPF hardfail.

M3AAWG, an email anti-abuse organization of which Valimail is a proud member, recommends in its [Email Authentication Best Practices](https://www.m3aawg.org/sites/default/files/m3aawg-email-authentication-recommended-best-practices-09-2020.pdf)
 document that when a receiving domain is doing DMARC validation, a DMARC pass should override any SPF “fail” except in the specific case where a domain’s SPF record indicates that it sends no mail (“v=spf1 -all”). The recommendation means that no message should be rejected due solely to SPF hardfail, but unfortunately, not all domains follow this best practice. This means that use of the “-all” mechanism puts a domain’s legitimate mail at higher risk of rejection than does “~all”, and so Valimail recommends that all of its customers use “~all” in their SPF records in order to ensure that each valid message is given the full opportunity to receive a DMARC pass verdict.

* * *

# If I point SPF to Valimail, will that cause mail delivery problems? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242835-if-i-point-spf-to-valimail-will-that-cause-mail-delivery-problems#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

If I point SPF to Valimail, will that cause mail delivery problems?

If I point SPF to Valimail, will that cause mail delivery problems?
===================================================================

SPF FAQs

Updated over a year ago

Pointing SPF to Valimail is perfectly safe and it will not cause any deliverability issues if your domain is at DMARC policy p=None.

Pointing SPF to Valimail will not change the contents of the SPF record on the domain you perform the pointing for, this action just means 2 simple things:

*   You will be transferring management of the SPF from your DNS to the Valimail platform.
    
*   It will allow you to use Valimail's patented SPF Macro technology with the help of which you can have as many approved and configured services as you want for your DNS, without having to ever worry about the 10 SPK lookup limit anymore.
    

* * *

# I pointed SPF to Valimail, but it's not reflecting that in the platform. Why is that? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242847-i-pointed-spf-to-valimail-but-it-s-not-reflecting-that-in-the-platform-why-is-that#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

I pointed SPF to Valimail, but it's not reflecting that in the platform. Why is that?

I pointed SPF to Valimail, but it's not reflecting that in the platform. Why is that?
=====================================================================================

SPF FAQs

Updated over a year ago

Sometimes after you point SPF to Valimail you might see in the Configuration page that this is not reflected there.

This can happen due to multiple things:

**1.** If you set the TTL higher than what is recommended in the instructions (300 seconds), this DNS change will take a bit longer until it finishes propagating through the internet. This change does reflect when you dig your domain for the SPF record, as shown here: [https://toolbox.googleapps.com/apps/dig/](https://toolbox.googleapps.com/apps/dig/)

**2.** It looks like the SPF pointer record was added before the previous v=spf1 record in your DNS was removed. In order to properly point SPF to Valimail, you will first need remove from your DNS any TXT record containing v=spf1.

After that is done, then create a TXT record named for your domain and add this value: **v=spf1 include:%{i}.\_ip.%{h}.\_ehlo.%{d}.\_spf.vali.email ~all**

**3.** It looks like the SPF pointer record was improperly published/added. Please modify the SPF TXT record in your DNS and make it contains only this value: **v=spf1 include:%{i}.\_ip.%{h}.\_ehlo.%{d}.\_spf.vali.email ~all**

**4.** It looks like you have another 1 or more includes added before the Macro include formula in your SPF pointer record.

Please modify the SPF TXT record in your DNS and make it contains only this value: **v=spf1 include:%{i}.\_ip.%{h}.\_ehlo.%{d}.\_spf.vali.email ~all**

  
​

* * *

# I added a service/IP to the Enabled Senders/Netblocks list and the emails are still failing. Why is that? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242856-i-added-a-service-ip-to-the-enabled-senders-netblocks-list-and-the-emails-are-still-failing-why-is-that#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

I added a service/IP to the Enabled Senders/Netblocks list and the emails are still failing. Why is that?

I added a service/IP to the Enabled Senders/Netblocks list and the emails are still failing. Why is that?
=========================================================================================================

SPF FAQs

Updated over a year ago

Adding a sending service or IP to your SPF in the platform, will not guarantee that email will pass DMARC with SPF for your domain.

That only takes care of half the problem, which is you whitelisting that service/IP for your domain. However, if those emails are not sent in SPF alignment for your domain, they will not pass DMARC.

Once you have added the respective service/IP on your domain's configuration in the platform, you will need to contact your service administrator (person/team in your organization that has set up that service to send emails on behalf of this domain) and let them know they will need to turn on SPF alignment in their admin console for your domain. That will enable those emails to be sent in SPF alignment for your domain and thus, pass DMARC with SPF.

If you added the sending service in your Enabled Senders list and the emails are still failing DMARC even though the emails are sent in SPF alignment for your domain, please contact the Valimail Support Team, so we can check and make sure the response for that sending service is up to date.

* * *

# I noticed that some services we use are missing from your Enabled Senders list. Is it safe to point SPF to Valimail now? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242861-i-noticed-that-some-services-we-use-are-missing-from-your-enabled-senders-list-is-it-safe-to-point-spf-to-valimail-now#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

I noticed that some services we use are missing from your Enabled Senders list. Is it safe to point SPF to Valimail now?

I noticed that some services we use are missing from your Enabled Senders list. Is it safe to point SPF to Valimail now?
========================================================================================================================

SPF FAQs

Updated over a year ago

We typically do not do a direct mirror of the SPF record, except for the cases in which the domain that is about to point SPF to Valimail is already at DMARC Enforcement.

Our SPF approach is a service based approach, which translates into a targeted and instant SPF response. The reasons for that are multiple:

**1.** There are many smaller services that might be using bigger ones under the hood for SPF configuration.

**2.** You could have deprecated services and IPs in your current SPF record and the Enabled Senders list must only represent the services/IPs you are currently using.

**3.** Sometimes vendors can give out incorrect info and you do not want to mirror something that is not directly approved by you.

With that said, you can confidently point SPF to Valimail, as any configuration work like adding new services and/or Netblocks can be performed after that as well. This will not cause any email deliverability problems for you, because you are not enforcing DMARC at present time - your DMARC record being p=None.

* * *

# In what instance would we add a Netblock to the list? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242889-in-what-instance-would-we-add-a-netblock-to-the-list#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

In what instance would we add a Netblock to the list?

In what instance would we add a Netblock to the list?
=====================================================

SPF FAQs

Updated over a year ago

Normally, there are 2 scenarios when you need to add an IP in the Netblocks section on your domain:

**1\.** If you have an internal IP that is sending emails on behalf of your domain, you will need to add it in your Netblocks and make sure the emails sent by that IP, are being sent in SPF alignment for your domain.

**2.** If you are using just 2 IPs from a certain service for example, it is advisable that you only add those 2 specific IPs in the Netblocks section, instead of adding the entire service in your Enabled Senders list - the reason being that you do not want to add extra IPs from that service that should not be authorized.

* * *

# I have enabled that service in the SPF on my domain. Why do I see emails from it that are passing with DMARC Override? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242869-i-have-enabled-that-service-in-the-spf-on-my-domain-why-do-i-see-emails-from-it-that-are-passing-with-dmarc-override#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

I have enabled that service in the SPF on my domain. Why do I see emails from it that are passing with DMARC Override?

I have enabled that service in the SPF on my domain. Why do I see emails from it that are passing with DMARC Override?
======================================================================================================================

SPF FAQs

Updated over a year ago

When an email has the Override policy applied to it, that email is not considering as passing DMARC, unless it passes with either SPF or DKIM. The Override policy is applied by the receiver, which chooses to disregard your DMARC policy when it comes to that email and just allow the email to be delivered, even though it might not be authenticated properly.

When utilizing DMARC reporting, policy overrides might be a situation your company encounters, especially if your emails are being delivered to a large and wide variety of customers. Essentially, a DMARC policy override occurs when an email recipient decides to override the policy that you have specified in your DMARC record.

Typically, when a receiver's email gateway choses to receive an email and apply a DMARC Override policy regardless of what the sender's DMARC policy is, it generally does so because it trusts the source of that email.

For example, a policy override could happen when you have a DMARC policy of reject (p=reject) and your outbound email goes through a mailing list, which breaks both SPF and DKIM. In this instance, DMARC will fail; however, the receiver may decide to override your policy and accept the email because they know and trust the source.

In conclusion, a DMARC Override policy does not necessarily mean that email is DMARC authenticated - it just means that the receiver chooses to ignore that. You still need to check in the email header and make sure the email is passing SPF and/or DKIM.

* * *

Related Articles

[DMARC Enforcement advanced options](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466456-dmarc-enforcement-advanced-options)
[What is DMARC Override?](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466458-what-is-dmarc-override)
[Why am I seeing emails with the disposition P=None when my domain is enforcing DMARC?](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9143136-why-am-i-seeing-emails-with-the-disposition-p-none-when-my-domain-is-enforcing-dmarc)
[Why Valimail Uses an SPF Soft Fail and Not a Hard Fail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9146530-why-valimail-uses-an-spf-soft-fail-and-not-a-hard-fail)

# How can I tell if an email is passing SPF? | Valimail Help Center

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

How can I tell if an email is passing SPF?

How can I tell if an email is passing SPF?
==========================================

SPF FAQs

Updated over a year ago

The best and simplest way to see if an email is passing SPF for your domain is to check a certain detail in the full email header.

You will need to look in the full email header to where the Authentication Results section is and to check if the visible **header.from** is aligned with the **smtp.mailfrom** (**return path/envelope from**). In your case, they both need to be your domain (the domain the email in sent on behalf of).

If those 2 are aligned, it means that email is SPF aligned and it will pass DMARC with SPF.

Example:

**1\.** If an email is sent with a From address of sales@**example.com** and the Return Path is mail.**example.com**. In this case, the email is considered to be SPF aligned.

**2\.** If an email is sent with a From address of sales@**example.com** and the Return Path is mail.**acme.com**. In this case, the email is not SPF aligned.

* * *

What should I do if I get an Email Bounceback Message or NDR that contains an SPF error?

What should I do if I get an Email Bounceback Message or NDR that contains an SPF error?
========================================================================================

Troubleshooting NDR bounceback emails

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242900-what-should-i-do-if-i-get-an-email-bounceback-message-or-ndr-that-contains-an-spf-error#h_10b1a38dd3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242900-what-should-i-do-if-i-get-an-email-bounceback-message-or-ndr-that-contains-an-spf-error#h_79914cdbe8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242900-what-should-i-do-if-i-get-an-email-bounceback-message-or-ndr-that-contains-an-spf-error#h_03dd8c1982)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242900-what-should-i-do-if-i-get-an-email-bounceback-message-or-ndr-that-contains-an-spf-error#h_dcdd902c3b)

Valimail Note
-------------

On rare occasions, we get a ticket from a customer that has issues sending emails to a specific receiver.

We have noticed a pattern for all these tickets and were able to discover the root cause for them.

If you have this issue, please see the most likely scenario below and a temporary fix:

Preconditions
-------------

*   Domain was recently moved to reject
    
*   The reported issue is coming from a sender with a good passing rate and with no visible issues in the GUI Reports
    
*   DKIM is also turned on for the affected sender
    
*   You get an email bounceback from a receiver or an NDR generated by your gateway that shows details about an SPF error and a DMARC fail similar to this:
    

[![a screenshot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031528730/2499be8557ebdbfd1f7e3c61/S9FdG8ZErmHhfnkpRR-FzaiCw_rJnLCFqA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=17a208469f3d6e266b2254f2f8ba03f0e9f55c5ab5aceb0f50cf286135e0ee9c&req=dSAkF8x8lYZcWfMW1HO4zTESmf6rpZso1uF4NFZwZTLhR7%2F4gQ4R6acJ92SA%0AcstLPHp1YNTzOELp%2BBg%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031528730/2499be8557ebdbfd1f7e3c61/S9FdG8ZErmHhfnkpRR-FzaiCw_rJnLCFqA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=17a208469f3d6e266b2254f2f8ba03f0e9f55c5ab5aceb0f50cf286135e0ee9c&req=dSAkF8x8lYZcWfMW1HO4zTESmf6rpZso1uF4NFZwZTLhR7%2F4gQ4R6acJ92SA%0AcstLPHp1YNTzOELp%2BBg%3D%0A)

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031528754/1dea25f89b07747d810e1ff7/1jsF7hV8uPMxx5QNPf8E8xzTyxVY-CpWDg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1c7e8f15812ee204a615fad48880e1f3170e24d9824d20431f383c0c7a5fa34c&req=dSAkF8x8lYZaXfMW1HO4zXDZgcipvc0SK2RtIx34FN0xrbP2dbO2xfIe0b54%0AfLkr2eYP%2FCAgGT4Ih2I%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1031528754/1dea25f89b07747d810e1ff7/1jsF7hV8uPMxx5QNPf8E8xzTyxVY-CpWDg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1c7e8f15812ee204a615fad48880e1f3170e24d9824d20431f383c0c7a5fa34c&req=dSAkF8x8lYZaXfMW1HO4zXDZgcipvc0SK2RtIx34FN0xrbP2dbO2xfIe0b54%0AfLkr2eYP%2FCAgGT4Ih2I%3D%0A)

The example above is an NDR (Non-Delivery Report) generated by Microsoft. The NDR will also contain the name of the server or gateway that rejected the message.

Root Cause
----------

*   The receiving gateway is not able to correctly parse SPF Macros. They either modify the ehlo name of the sending server prior to checking SPF or they straight reject the email when they see the SPF record is not readable by their gateway and it does not contain the expected include statement.
    
*   The receiver sends an SPF validation error or temperror back to the sender and they do not go further and check DKIM signing after the SFP error
    

  
​

Solutions
---------

*   Ideally, the receiver should upgrade/update their mail gateway to correctly translate SPF macros as these are part of the SPF standard and have been for a long time now. However, this might be a lengthy process and a temporary fix needs to be applied;
    

*   Workaround 1: The receiver whitelists the sending IPs/sending domain in order to accept your emails even if they believe they are failing DMARC authentication. This might be dangerous as it will also open up the receiver to spoofing attempts coming from your domain if not setup correctly;
    
*   Workaround 2: The sender (you) add the SPF include statement of the Sender to your SPF record after the Valimail Macro so that the receiving gateway is able to accept the emails based on that. Please note that this should only be temporary and that we do not recommend adding other include statements after the Macro. The Valimail SPF Macro alone should take care of all SPF authentication checks, however, this is an exception and should be treated accordingly.
    

If your issue does not match the above, please contact Valimail Support and we will provide another solution to your problem.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242923-what-happens-if-we-stop-pointing-spf-to-valimail#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242923-what-happens-if-we-stop-pointing-spf-to-valimail#h_2e93206975)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242923-what-happens-if-we-stop-pointing-spf-to-valimail#h_5bc6a924c8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242923-what-happens-if-we-stop-pointing-spf-to-valimail#h_b8d418f17b)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959037-spf)

What happens if we stop pointing SPF to Valimail?

What happens if we stop pointing SPF to Valimail?
=================================================

SPF FAQs

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242923-what-happens-if-we-stop-pointing-spf-to-valimail#h_2e93206975)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242923-what-happens-if-we-stop-pointing-spf-to-valimail#h_5bc6a924c8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9242923-what-happens-if-we-stop-pointing-spf-to-valimail#h_b8d418f17b)

**What happens if the Valimail SPF macro is removed from my SPF record?**
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

In case the proprietary Valimail Macro syntax is removed from the SPF record, receiving MTAs will no longer query into the Valimail system in order to validate if a sending IP address is authorized in your SPF configuration.

include:%{i}.\_ip.%{h}.\_ehlo.%{d}.\_spf.vali.email

**What problems can arise if the SPF macro is removed from the SPF record?**
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

The removal of the Valimail SPF Macro can potentially cause the following issues:

*   Recipient MTAs will no longer query into the Valimail system. Meaning that all of the sending services and netblocks configured in Valimail will no longer help authenticate emails.
    
*   Without Valimail's instant SPF solution, your SPF record could potentially break the 10 DNS lookup limit.
    
*   In case your SPF record does not contain any other directives or mechanisms besides the Valimail SPF macro, SPF authentication will fail. DMARC authentication will pass only with DKIM authentication.
    

**What needs to be done in this case?**
---------------------------------------

In order to reinstate the Valimail instant SPF solution you will need to replace your SPF record with the Valimail SPF record below.

v=spf1 include:%{i}.\_ip.%{h}.\_ehlo.%{d}.\_spf.vali.email ~all

  
​

* * *

# Pointing DKIM to Valimail | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466369-pointing-dkim-to-valimail#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959036-faq)

[DKIM](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959038-dkim)

Pointing DKIM to Valimail

Pointing DKIM to Valimail
=========================

This article describes how to point DKIM to Valimail.

Updated over 11 months ago

This article applies to customers using the Valimail Align and Enforce products.

**Very Important**: _Before you point DKIM to Valimail, you will need to make sure that all the current DKIM keys from your DNS host, are added in the Valimail platform. Pointing DKIM to Valimail without mirroring the keys from your DNS could create issues with their functionality._

You will need to add a DKIM NameServer (NS) record to your DNS zone to manage a domain’s DKIM keys within the Valimail system.

To enable DKIM management for a domain, please add the following NS record for the “\_domainkey” subdomain to your DNS:

Record Name: \_domainkey.yourdomain.com.

Record Type: NS

Record Value: ns.vali.email.

We recommend a TTL of 300 seconds, although using a longer TTL (up to 3600 seconds) should be fine if you'd like to reduce the load on your DNS server. Please note that because of existing DNS TTLs it may take some time for Valimail to detect that you've updated your DNS with the correct settings.

DNS Host does not support custom NS records

In this case, DKIM is unable to be managed by Valimail and will need to continue to be hosted in your DNS

Best Practice

Verify that all current DKIM keys are added to Valimail before delegation. See [Adding a DKIM Key in Enforce](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Related articles:

[Video Guide: Adding a Sender](https://support.valimail.com/en/support/solutions/articles/48001166621)

[Why is it safe to point SPF/DKIM/DMARC to Valimail?](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466437-why-it-s-safe-to-point-spf-dkim-dmarc-to-valimail)

# How to set up TLS reporting and MTA-STS | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10707728-how-to-set-up-tls-reporting-and-mta-sts#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10707728-how-to-set-up-tls-reporting-and-mta-sts#h_dbbb94445f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10707728-how-to-set-up-tls-reporting-and-mta-sts#h_14ea23011c)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Authentication Fundamentals](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959040-email-authentication-fundamentals)

How to set up TLS reporting and MTA-STS

How to set up TLS reporting and MTA-STS
=======================================

This article will explain how to set up TLS reporting in Valimail, as well as the MTA-STS policy set up in the DNS

Updated over 3 months ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10707728-how-to-set-up-tls-reporting-and-mta-sts#h_dbbb94445f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/10707728-how-to-set-up-tls-reporting-and-mta-sts#h_14ea23011c)

TLS Reporting
=============

TLS (Transport Layer Security) is a system that allows domain owners to obtain automated feedback on email delivery problems linked to TLS encryption. TLS reporting plays a crucial role in protecting emails during transmission by encrypting the connection between mail servers.

In order to have TLS reports ingested in Valimail, the following record must be published:

Publish a TXT record

**HOST(aka Name or Address)**

`_smtp._tls.yourdomain.com`

VALUE(aka Data, Content, or Text)

`v=TLSRPTv1; rua=mailto:[[email protected]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) `

We recommend a TTL of 300 seconds.

That being said, several other steps are required to take full advantage of TLS.

MTA-STS
=======

TLS reporting is often used together with Mail Transfer Agent Strict Transport Security (MTA-STS). A well-recognized internet standard that enhances the security of connections between SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) servers is the SMTP Mail Transfer Agent-Strict Transport Security (MTA-STS). MTA-STS addresses current challenges in SMTP email security by mandating the use of TLS encryption during transmission.

In a nutshell, MTA-STS lets sending servers know if they should reject or accept a message when a TLS connection cannot be established as expected.

In order to set up a policy for MTA-STS, a text file will need to be fetched from your website, and another DNS record must be published.

The mentioned DNS record must be published as such:

**HOST(aka Name or Address)**

`_mta-sts.yourdomain.com`

VALUE(aka Data, Content, or Text)

`v=STSv1; id=(a random ID of 32 alphanumerical charaters);`

The policy itself will be set in the file previously mentioned. This file should be fetched from your website and should have the following address:

[https://mta-sts.yourdomain.com/.well-known/mta-sts.txt](https://yourdomain.com/.well-known/mta-sts.txt)

As the link suggests, the file should be found under a domain directory called ".well-known" with the file name: mta-sts.txt

The file format should contain:

"version: STSv1

mode: testing

(This is where the policy is. For now, we recommend the "testing" policy be used until you are comfortable switching to "enforce")

mx:(you should add all your mx records in this format)

mx:(one at a time as seen here)

max\_age:604800 "

(This value should be between 86400 and 31557600, which represents 1 day to 1 year) The value shown here is the recommended value of 7 days

Please note that anytime you wish to change the MTA-STS policy, the ID in the previously mentioned DNS record needs to be updated to a **new, unique value**

In order to publish the file on your website, you will have to have a webserver and purchase an SSL certificate.

We are also working on a feature that will allow you to instead host the MTA-STS policy in Valimail in the future. Feel free to check our "NEW AND UPCOMING FEATURES" tab inside the Valimail platform.

* * *

# Apache Log4j (CVE-2021-44228) | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142816-apache-log4j-cve-2021-44228#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[General](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/9031225-general)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/9031228-faq)

Apache Log4j (CVE-2021-44228)

Apache Log4j (CVE-2021-44228)
=============================

Updated over a year ago

Valimail is aware of the recently discovered zero-day vulnerability in Apache Log4j (CVE-2021-45046 and CVE-2021-44228). Valimail is continuing to monitor and analyze the vulnerabilities related to the Apache Log4j disclosure. **Valimail applications do not use Log4j.** We are checking our other tools and apps to verify possible usage and working to patch and secure anything we find.

To keep your Valimail application and services secure, our security professionals continue to monitor the overall impact of the Log4j remote-code execution vulnerabilities. We will provide new information as it becomes available.  
​

Valimail Blog post on Log4j - [Log4Shell Vulnerability: What You Need to Know!](https://www.valimail.com/blog/log4shell/)

Enforce FAQs

Enforce FAQs
============

Enforce Frequently Asked Questions

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_213004972b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_bb255f8276)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_6d23412e7d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_32b1f66966)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_721e2e00a5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_28f9f8f746)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_943abd5cbb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_bf00ca505c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_2e6c67e173)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_d4fb72abd6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_48cae0ce81)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_b7de88c0e2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_5ce24337c1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142822-enforce-faqs#h_14f0abff77)

How do I add a subdomain on the refreshed configuration page?
-------------------------------------------------------------

On the Configuration page, please navigate to the Email Subdomains area, by scrolling toward to bottom of the page.

Once there, click on **Add Email Subdomain**.  
​  
You can read more about the new configuration page and detailed step-by-step instructions by accessing the following article: [Redesigned Configuration Page](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466366-redesigned-configuration-page)

Does Enforce come with any new features? If so, where can I find a list of them?
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Enforce will bring additional features like the Alert Notifications Center and the Suspicious Sending Alert.

To access the New Features page, you will need to log in as an admin to your Valimail Enforce account.

After you have logged in, you will find the New Features page on the lower side of the left menu panel (just below Help).

You can read more about the New Features page by accessing the following article: [New Features Page](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466364-new-features-page)

  
What is an external reporting domain?
----------------------------------------

An external reporting domain allows you to receive DMARC reports outside your own domain. You can read more about external reporting domains by accessing the following article: [External Domain Verification](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142845-external-domain-verification)
  
​  
​

How do I configure senders in Enforce?
--------------------------------------

Each individual sender will need to be configured for either SPF in an aligned manner or DKIM. As a best practice, we recommend turning on both when available. You can read more about configuring senders in Enforce and detailed step-by-step instructions by accessing the following article: [Redesigned Configuration Page](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466366-redesigned-configuration-page)

  
Should I mirror my SPF record in Valimail?
---------------------------------------------

As a best practice, we do not recommend mirroring (copying) the existing SPF record, most of the time the SPF record can be legacy and over time IPs and SPF includes are added but never removed. We recommend reviewing the existing SPF record and adding only the IPs and Senders that are authorized to send on behalf of your domain.

Will pointing SPF and DKIM affect our email flow?  
Email deliverability will not be affected.
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before pointing SPF and DKIM, it is recommended to add the Authorized senders from your SPF record to Valimail as well as import existing DKIM keys from DNS to Valimail for a seamless transition.

  
How do I add an additional reporting address in Enforce?
-----------------------------------------------------------

At the top of the configuration page, click on **DMARC record**, on the new window click **Aggregate Reports** then **Add Email**.  
​  
You can read more about adding additional RUA addresses and detailed step-by-step instructions by accessing the following article: [Adding an additional reporting address](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466371-adding-an-additional-reporting-address)

Can I add SPF and DKIM information that is not for a specific service?
----------------------------------------------------------------------

Yes, you can add SPF information as netblocks for internal senders and dedicated IPs as well as add DKIM keys that cannot be associated with a specific vendor.  
​  
You can read more about adding SPF and DKIM information and detailed step-by-step instructions by accessing the following article: [Redesigned Configuration Page](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466366-redesigned-configuration-page)
.

  
  
What should I do if a specific service is not on the list for Enabled Senders?
------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you cannot find the service in the drop-down list, please [open a support ticket](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9318679-how-to-contact-valimail-support)
 and a Valimail engineer will be able to assist you.

How do I submit a bug?
----------------------

To report a bug, please open a support ticket on support.valimail.com

  
How do I submit feedback about new features?
-----------------------------------------------

We appreciate any feedback you can provide regarding our new features, you can submit feedback by opening a ticket on support.valimail.com

  
Why should I connect Google Workspace to Valimail?
-----------------------------------------------------

Connecting Google Workspace to Valimail will allow us to generate Service Owner Insights that provide additional context on possible owners for your sending services. This will help you identify stakeholders during the onboarding process.

You can read more about connecting your Microsoft Office 365 mailbox to Valimail by accessing the following article: [Connecting to your Google Workspace Mailbox](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466379-setting-up-the-valimail-mailbox-connector-with-google-workspace)

  
  
Why should I connect Microsoft Office 365 to Valimail?
------------------------------------------------------------

Microsoft365 mailboxes do not send DMARC reporting data in the normal manner. This longstanding issue can prevent some domains from getting to DMARC enforcement. By connecting Microsoft Office 365 to Valimail, you get DMARC reporting data from your own mailboxes as well as Valimail Service Owner Insights. You can read more about connecting your Microsoft Office 365 mailbox to Valimail by accessing the following article: [Connecting to your Microsoft 365 Mailbox](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466380-setting-up-the-valimail-mailbox-connector-with-microsoft-365)

I like the legacy UI, can I keep using it?
------------------------------------------

Yes, you will need to navigate to the configuration page and at the very top, you will have the option to switch the view to the legacy UI for the time being. You can read more about switching to the legacy UI by accessing the following article: [Redesigned Configuration Page](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466366-redesigned-configuration-page)
  
​

* * *

# Why it's safe to point SPF/DKIM/DMARC to Valimail | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466437-why-it-s-safe-to-point-spf-dkim-dmarc-to-valimail#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466437-why-it-s-safe-to-point-spf-dkim-dmarc-to-valimail#h_cdb6e1f996)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Authentication Fundamentals](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959040-email-authentication-fundamentals)

[DNS: The Single Source of Truth](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449880-dns-the-single-source-of-truth)

Why it's safe to point SPF/DKIM/DMARC to Valimail

Why it's safe to point SPF/DKIM/DMARC to Valimail
=================================================

Updated over a month ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466437-why-it-s-safe-to-point-spf-dkim-dmarc-to-valimail#h_cdb6e1f996)

Hosting these records to help customers is the core of our business.
====================================================================

1.  Valimail does not host your entire DNS. We only host SPF, DKIM and DMARC records after you give us permission with a one time change to your DNS records.  
    ​  
    ​
    
2.  You retain ultimate control of your DNS records. You can revoke the delegation of records at any time, by editing your own DNS records again so they don't point to Valimail at all.  
    ​  
    ​
    
3.  While Valimail is hosting these records, you have direct control over the configuration of SPF/DKIM/DMARC through our purpose-built interface.  
    ​  
    ​
    
4.  Due to the use of underscores in the DNS records themselves, the DNS protocol also prevents Valimail from hosting other services like Web/Mail etc.  
    ​  
    ​
    
5.  Valimail's infrastructure is globally resilient with POPs in Ireland, US (West and East) as well as South Korea.
    

For further technical reading, see [DMARC/DKIM NS Pointer Records](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466430-dmarc-dkim-ns-pointer-records)
.

* * *

# Valimail DNS Downtime | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466435-valimail-dns-downtime#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466435-valimail-dns-downtime#h_a714d941ea)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466435-valimail-dns-downtime#h_915d53b52f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466435-valimail-dns-downtime#h_a640a5b399)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Authentication Fundamentals](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959040-email-authentication-fundamentals)

[DNS: The Single Source of Truth](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449880-dns-the-single-source-of-truth)

Valimail DNS Downtime

Valimail DNS Downtime
=====================

Updated over 6 months ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466435-valimail-dns-downtime#h_a714d941ea)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466435-valimail-dns-downtime#h_915d53b52f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466435-valimail-dns-downtime#h_a640a5b399)

What happens if Valimail’s DNS service is unavailable
=====================================================

If there is a problem with the Valimail platform it "fails open". This means that in the unlikely event that our service goes down, your email still flows, but it would be unauthenticated. For the few minutes it would take for the service to be restored, it would be potentially possible for your emailing domain to be spoofed.

Valimail provides our customers the ability to check the availability of our platform as well as the option to subscribe to get notified in the event of an incident [here](https://status.valimail.com/)
.

You can find Valimail status updates at [status.valimail.com](https://status.valimail.com/)

What do we mean by failing open?
================================

Valimail manages your \_dmarc and \_domainkey records. In the unlikely event of our DNS being unavailable, a receiver querying for your DMARC record would not get an answer. The receiver would treat the domain as if there is no DMARC record, meaning that there is no policy in place. The same goes for your DKIM keys as Valimail will not respond to queries against \_domainkey.

SPF "fail open" works differently than DMARC/DKIM since we are using a TXT record and not a delegation. The SPF record that Valimail provides its customers ends with a softfail (~all). If Valimail's service were to go down, our include statements would not work, and the querying entity would get an SPF softfail result causing SPF to fail open. With no DMARC policy in place during this brief period the softfail will have no effect on the message reaching the intended recipient.

DNS Downtime and Mailbox Provider Deliverability
================================================

In the very unlikely event that Valimail DNS servers are offline, or if a client’s DNS servers are offline, how mailbox providers will handle these failures is “left to the discretion of the Mail Receiver” per RFC 7489.

Mailbox providers cache DNS records, including email authentication and email deliverability-related entries, like public DKIM keys and DMARC records. If the DNS failure exists only for a very brief period of time, there may be no impact at all, with locally cached DNS allowing the mailbox provider to continue to read DMARC and SPF records and decode DKIM signatures.

In the event that the DNS failure persists, some mailbox providers will “fail open,” resulting in email messages continuing to be delivered.

Other mailbox providers, for example, Gmail, will temporarily defer mail delivery when they are unable to properly process email authentication records for a domain due to a DNS failure. Gmail will return an error with the code “451-4.7.26” that states that “Unauthenticated email from example.com is not accepted due to the domain's DMARC policy, but temporary DNS failures prevent authentication. Please contact the administrator of example.com. domain if this was a legitimate mail. To learn about the DMARC initiative, go to [https://support.google.com/mail/?p=DmarcRejection](https://support.google.com/mail/?p=DmarcRejection)
.”

In this case, when DNS is restored, any queued mail will be successfully accepted and processed at the next retry. Errors related to DNS downtime cause no negative lasting impact to email deliverability and will not damage a domain’s sending reputation or a mail server’s IP reputation.

* * *

Related Articles

[DNS Configuration DNS Made Easy](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466438-dns-configuration-dns-made-easy)
[Valimail SPF Records](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466461-valimail-spf-records)
[Setup your DNS records to use Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142775-setup-your-dns-records-to-use-valimail)

# Understanding the DNS resolution path | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466434-understanding-the-dns-resolution-path#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Authentication Fundamentals](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959040-email-authentication-fundamentals)

[DNS: The Single Source of Truth](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449880-dns-the-single-source-of-truth)

Understanding the DNS resolution path

Understanding the DNS resolution path
=====================================

Updated over a year ago

When troubleshooting an issue with DNS, it is often helpful to understand what DNS servers are involved in resolving a query. Dig allows you to see this using the +trace flag. This will cause Dig to show you all of the servers that were queried, and the answers they gave, while resolving the name. One added benefit to the +trace flag is that it does not use DNS caching, making it ideal for checking minute by minute DNS changes

If you want to know the full resolution path for a DNS name, you can use the below command. Due to the length of the output, the path will be documented in blue below:

**dig google.\_domainkey.valimail.com TXT +trace**

Start with the Internet Root Servers

; <<>> DiG 9.10.6 <<>> google.\_domainkey.valimail.com TXT +trace

;; global options: +cmd

. 518400 IN NS [a.root-servers.net](http://a.root-servers.net/)
.

. 518400 IN NS [b.root-servers.net](http://b.root-servers.net/)
.

<snip>

;; Received 239 bytes from 208.67.222.222#53(208.67.222.222) in 24 ms

Root server responds with names of .com servers

com. 172800 IN NS [f.gtld-servers.net](http://f.gtld-servers.net/)
.

com. 172800 IN NS [h.gtld-servers.net](http://h.gtld-servers.net/)
.

) in 271 ms

MOVEit vulnerability

MOVEit vulnerability
====================

Is Valimail using MOVEit?

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

_MOVEit vulnerability and Valimail. Should you be concerned?_

It was discovered that this file transfer solution has a major vulnerability
----------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Is Valimail using this solution?
    

Valimail is currently not using MOVEit and thus it is not subject to its vulnerability. There are no concerns for MOVEit and its vulnerability when using any Valimail Product

# Why do some DNS providers not allow Valimail’s NS delegation for DMARC and DKIM? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466436-why-do-some-dns-providers-not-allow-valimail-s-ns-delegation-for-dmarc-and-dkim#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Authentication Fundamentals](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/3959040-email-authentication-fundamentals)

[DNS: The Single Source of Truth](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449880-dns-the-single-source-of-truth)

Why do some DNS providers not allow Valimail’s NS delegation for DMARC and DKIM?

Why do some DNS providers not allow Valimail’s NS delegation for DMARC and DKIM?
================================================================================

Updated over a year ago

The Valimail DMARC and DKIM configuration are standard DNS delegations and are supported by any DNS server. Some DNS providers, typically those focussed on small business and non technical customers choose to limit what customers can configure in DNS. They do this to make things easier for their customers and to reduce their support burden. Unfortunately, one of the things that sometimes get excluded from the providers' portals are the ability to delegate subdomains.

If the customer is using a DNS provider that has this restriction, the customer has two choices:

1.  Manage DMARC and DKIM TXT records manually in their DNS
    
2.  Move to a different DNS provider that allows delegation. Godaddy and Amazon Route 53 are examples of DNS providers that support NS delegation
    

* * *

Related Articles

[Pointing DKIM to Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466369-pointing-dkim-to-valimail)

# Does Enforce DNS support Anycast? | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9142806-does-enforce-dns-support-anycast#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[General](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/9031225-general)

[FAQ](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/9031228-faq)

Does Enforce DNS support Anycast?

Does Enforce DNS support Anycast?
=================================

Anycast and compatibility information

Updated over a year ago

No, this is not currently supported. Since Enforce DNS only gets DNS requests from email receivers and not general Internet devices, there is no need for Anycast to provide routing to closest servers.

* * *
