ARMS

ARMS
====

How to set up DMARC for ARMS in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471009-arms#h_c06d324639)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471009-arms#h_c585848212)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471009-arms#h_bf98022b82)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **ARMS** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **ARMS** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your ARMS emails
====================================================

ARMS can generate a unique Domain Key which you can add to your DNS records. This will allow ARMS to digitally sign emails on behalf of your organization.

In order to setup DKIM in ARMS for your domain, please reach out to ARMS Support at **help@armssoftware**, or **(855) 215-2767**.

They will provide you with 3 CNAME records - one you will add in your DNS and the 2 CNAME DKIM records you will add on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

_Example records_:

(in DNS)

Record Name: em5865.yourdomain.com

Record Type: CNAME

Record Value: u308154.wl152.sendgrid.net.

(In Valimail)

DKIM selector: **arm**

CNAME Value: **arm.domainkey.u308154.wl152.sendgrid.net.**

DKIM selector: **arm2**

CNAME Value: **arm2.domainkey.u308154.wl152.sendgrid.net.**

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for ARMS, **[here](http://help.armssoftware.com/en/articles/3832122-whitelisting-emails-from-arms)
**.

Add an ARMS DKIM key in Valimail
================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your ARMS emails
===================================================

Once you establish that ARMS is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

APSIS

APSIS
=====

How to set up DMARC for APSIS in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471007-apsis#h_9da36cfd9d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471007-apsis#h_2412f7d2a7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471007-apsis#h_a8c4c1eff0)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **APSIS** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your APSIS emails
=====================================================

APSIS' Delivery department provides you with everything you need in order to get DKIM ready.

In order to set up DKIM in APSIS for your domain, you will need to **reach out to your APSIS Account Manager**, who will be able to redirect you to their Delivery professionals.

APSIS issues and supports 2048bit TXT DKIM keys.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce

You can also find more on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Apsis, **[here](https://help.apsis.one/en/articles/6104691-email-authentication#dkim)
**.

Add an APSIS DKIM key in Valimail
=================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your APSIS emails
====================================================

Once you establish that APSIS is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

AuthSMTP

AuthSMTP
========

How to set up DMARC for AuthSMTP in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471011-authsmtp#h_79ab856c50)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471011-authsmtp#h_8129eb9ebf)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471011-authsmtp#h_1c9bfa4b0b)

SPF(supported)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **AuthSMTP** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your AuthSMTP emails
========================================================

To enable DKIM on your domain names please login to the [control panel](https://www.authsmtp.com/login/)
 and go to **Advanced Config** -> **DKIM Signing**.

AuthSMT issues a unique TXT DKIM key for each domain name so your DKIM reputation cannot be affected by other senders.

The DKIM key uses a AuthSMTP specific 'selector' and the signing will only apply to messages sent via your AuthSMTP account.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for AuthSMTP, **[here](https://www.authsmtp.com/faqs/faq-81.html)
**.

Add an AuthSMTP DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your AuthSMTP emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that AuthSMTP is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Arlo | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471008-arlo#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471008-arlo#h_8022d49fde)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471008-arlo#h_1d269ddce6)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Arlo

Arlo
====

How to set up DMARC for Arlo in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471008-arlo#h_4349be641b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471008-arlo#h_8022d49fde)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471008-arlo#h_1d269ddce6)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Arlo** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Arlo** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Arlo emails
====================================================

Setting up DKIM for your platform is a three step process that requires steps by both the Arlo Support team and your technical staff (as it requires the addition of some CNAME records to your DNS).

**1.** **Arlo generates the CNAME records**

If you would like to set up DKIM for your emails, the first step is to contact [Arlo Support](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#21525451514e53556140534d4e0f424e)
 or your Arlo service owner. You should provide the email address your emails are sent from, the name and email address of the technical staff member who will be adding the records to your DNS and, if known, your DNS host.

**Note**: The Arlo platforms send emails using SendGrid. If you already have CNAME records for SendGrid for another application, please let us know. We are able to add custom identifiers to the records to differentiate them in your DNS.

The Arlo Support agent will generate the CNAME records for your platform and send the details through to your technical staff member for addition to your DNS.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add the 3rd CNAME record in your DNS.

**2.** **Add the CNAME records to your DNS**

Once received, you will need to add the CNAME records on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and in your DNS.

Once added, please let Arlo Support (or your Arlo service owner) know so we can complete the setup.

**3.** **Arlo verifies the records**

Once the records have been added to your DNS, Arlo can verify the records have been added correctly and finalize the DKIM setup.

The records may take up to 24 hours to be propagated through your DNS so it may take a day or two to verify the records.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Arlo, **[here](https://support.arlo.co/hc/en-gb/articles/211902723-Allow-Arlo-to-send-emails)
**.

Add an Arlo DKIM key in Valimail
================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Arlo emails
===================================================

Once you establish that Arlo is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471015-beamery#h_051fd9e962)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471015-beamery#h_c5e49615a9)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Beamery

Beamery
=======

How to set up DMARC for Beamery in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471015-beamery#h_a91fc17b11)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471015-beamery#h_051fd9e962)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471015-beamery#h_c5e49615a9)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Beamery** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Beamery** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Beamery emails
=======================================================

Authenticating the sending domain is done with three easy DNS records. These three records add a Sender Policy Framework (SPF) record to establish your Beamery sending IP as legitimate for your domain, and a pair of rotating DKIM keys, they will also add an MX record for Beamery to establish a return path for message analytics. Since DKIM is a paired-key system, the keys need to be changed regularly for maximum security. Having two DNS records for DKIM allows this change to happen with no email downtime.

TYPE HOST VALUE

CNAME careers.yourdomain.com u10101010.wl000.sendgrid.net

CNAME bmy.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com bmy.domainkey.u10101010.wl000.sendgrid.net

CNAME bmy2.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com bmy2.domainkey.u10101010.wl000.sendgrid.net

Beamery needs to use a subdomain (often careers.yourdomain.com) in order to create a way for our click and open tracking statistics to be routed back to Beamery

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Beamery, **[here](https://support.beamery.com/hc/en-us/articles/4408166459537-Sender-Authentication-Fact-Sheet)
**.

Add a Beamery DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Beamery emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Beamery is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

AON Assessment Solutions

AON Assessment Solutions
========================

How to set up DMARC for AON Assessment Solutions in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471004-aon-assessment-solutions#h_8f53ad192f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471004-aon-assessment-solutions#h_4651c012a7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471004-aon-assessment-solutions#h_47a82ec5cd)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **AON Assessment Solutions** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your AON Assessment Solutions emails
========================================================================

Since DKIM requires an active key management that affects the mail server configuration as well as

the DNS, the public keys are not published in the client DNS directly. Instead, AON Assessment Solutions asks you to set

up CNAME records that point to your key sets:

Type Record Location Value

CNAME aas.1.\_domainkey.<sender domain> dkim.<sender domain>.1.aas.services.

CNAME aas.2.\_domainkey.<sender domain> dkim.<sender domain>.2.aas.services.

CNAME aas.3.\_domainkey.<sender domain> dkim.<sender domain>.3.aas.services.

All installed keys are domain-specific; please do not point CNAME records for different sender

domains to the same keys, since this will not be reflected by Aon's server configuration.

Please note that the DKIM selector, being part of an automated solution, cannot be customized.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add those 3 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce, as outlined in the Add an AON Assessment Solutions DKIM key in Enforce section below.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for AON Assessment Solutions, **[here](https://www.yellowcouch.cz/wp-content/uploads/2020/06/Automated-E-mail-set-up.pdf)
**.

Add an AON Assessment Solutions DKIM key in Valimail
====================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your AON Assessment Solutions emails
=======================================================================

Once you establish that AON Assessment Solutions is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Atlassian Cloud

Atlassian Cloud
===============

How to set up DMARC for Atlassian Cloud in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471010-atlassian-cloud#h_0155435319)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471010-atlassian-cloud#h_bee7a5d08f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471010-atlassian-cloud#h_4b698262c2)

SPF(supported)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Atlassian Cloud** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Atlassian Cloud emails
===============================================================

In order to enable DKIM signing for your domain, you will need to add the following two CNAME records in your DNS.

s1.\_domainkey IN CNAME s1.\_domainkey.atlassian.net.

s2.\_domainkey IN CNAME s2.\_domainkey.atlassian.net.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Atlassian Cloud, **[here](https://confluence.atlassian.com/adminjiracloud/configuring-jira-cloud-to-send-emails-on-behalf-of-your-domain-900996548.html)
**.

Add an Atlassian Cloud DKIM key in Valimail
===========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Atlassian Cloud emails
==============================================================

Once you establish that Atlassian Cloud is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

AfterShip

AfterShip
=========

How to set up DMARC for Aftership in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470999-aftership#h_d0ba6c6606)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470999-aftership#h_175c8d9fe5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470999-aftership#h_154e8f2a28)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **AfterShip** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **AfterShip** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your AfterShip emails
=========================================================

**1.** Access your [Organization account](https://organization.automizely.com/welcome)
 -> **Sender Emails** -> Click on **Verify Domain** to get 3 CNAME entries.

**2.** Add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail and add the 3rd CNAME record in your DNS.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152273/b554173f73cc229776c3145a/evBK1pbqFN2mWebc7Ajkm_xHeXD4u3_bHw.png-1683879494?expires=1755792000&signature=eb5c3d6b24837f5f9b27efa0f2c5f45aa893445b59a4d2ec979ea85a66603bb3&req=fCUlF8x8n4ZcFb4f3HP0gIM33HzVafn2tc2dQ5Bc2JHlxg5ptRHdBbBlVGjX%0A419Fnt58z4%2F622qnTg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152273/b554173f73cc229776c3145a/evBK1pbqFN2mWebc7Ajkm_xHeXD4u3_bHw.png-1683879494?expires=1755792000&signature=eb5c3d6b24837f5f9b27efa0f2c5f45aa893445b59a4d2ec979ea85a66603bb3&req=fCUlF8x8n4ZcFb4f3HP0gIM33HzVafn2tc2dQ5Bc2JHlxg5ptRHdBbBlVGjX%0A419Fnt58z4%2F622qnTg%3D%3D%0A)

**Note**: the s1 and s2 are the 2 DKIM keys.

**3.** After all records were added in their proper places, come back to the [Sender Emails](https://organization.automizely.com/sender-emails)
 page and -> Click on **Verify** to finish the process.

**Important Note**: If CNAME records pre-exist in SendGrid, instead of creating entries starting with values s1 and s2, you need to use **a01** and **a012** respectively.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152277/0059958144dd5f954a191978/573nLPUPkdLcKzqug8tBmdM9-EeuMZSRdQ.png-1683879654?expires=1755792000&signature=0e3b1261bd5a457f1a78d84f265684b4d9e1817817642797395d146ccef5b48d&req=fCUlF8x8n4ZYFb4f3HP0gBoaNi%2BgoSWF0HbjqaN39yqYPdB5Wqfx%2B6vAuB8L%0AfqCD2G9kV59ffFV4HQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152277/0059958144dd5f954a191978/573nLPUPkdLcKzqug8tBmdM9-EeuMZSRdQ.png-1683879654?expires=1755792000&signature=0e3b1261bd5a457f1a78d84f265684b4d9e1817817642797395d146ccef5b48d&req=fCUlF8x8n4ZYFb4f3HP0gBoaNi%2BgoSWF0HbjqaN39yqYPdB5Wqfx%2B6vAuB8L%0AfqCD2G9kV59ffFV4HQ%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for AfterShip, **[here](https://support.aftership.com/en/article/verify-sending-domain-for-delivery-notifications-1728bak/)
**.

Add an AfterShip DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your AfterShip emails
========================================================

Once you establish that AfterShip is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

# Amazon SES | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471003-amazon-ses#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471003-amazon-ses#h_145d412dc9)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471003-amazon-ses#h_7ca69139be)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471003-amazon-ses#h_3cd7952b96)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471003-amazon-ses#h_d15530918f)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Amazon SES

Amazon SES
==========

How to set up DMARC for Amazon SES in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471003-amazon-ses#h_145d412dc9)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471003-amazon-ses#h_7ca69139be)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471003-amazon-ses#h_3cd7952b96)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471003-amazon-ses#h_d15530918f)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Amazon SES** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Below you will see how to set up **Easy DKIM** for your domain and how to configure Amazon SES in Valimail.

Once you setup **Easy DKIM** for your domain, then you can start sending authenticated Amazon SES emails on behalf of that domain.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Amazon SES email with Easy DKIM
========================================================================

**Note**: If you need to create or verify a domain, see [Creating a domain identity](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ses/latest/dg/creating-identities.html#verify-domain-procedure)
.

**1.** Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon SES console **[here](https://console.aws.amazon.com/ses/)
**.

**2.** In the navigation pane, under **Configuration**, choose **Verified identities**.

**3.** In the list of identities, choose an identity where the **Identity type** is _Domain_.

**4.** Under the **Authentication** tab, in the **DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM)** container, choose **Edit**.

**5.** In the **Advanced DKIM settings** container, choose the **Easy DKIM** button in the **Identity type** field.

**6.** In the **DKIM signing key length** field, choose **RSA\_2048\_BIT**.

**7.** In the **DKIM signatures** field, check the **Enabled** box.

**8.** Choose **Save** changes.

**9.** Add the 3 DKIM keys in Valimail Enforce.

**10.** Once the DKIM keys are published in Enforce, the DKIM Verification Status in the AWS console, will change to: **verified**.

_Amazon SES usually detects the publication of the DKIM records between 24-48 hours._

You can find the instructions on how to setup Easy DKIM in AWS **[here](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ses/latest/dg/send-email-authentication-dkim-easy.html)
**.

Add the Amazon SES DKIM keys in Valimail
========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Amazon SES emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Amazon SES is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in Amazon SES for your domain
==================================================

Amazon SES only supports SPF alignment using a dedicated subdomain configuration.

The process of setting up a dedicated subdomain requires you to add records to the DNS configuration for that subdomain. You have to publish an MX record so that your domain can receive the bounce and complaint notifications that email providers send you. You also have to publish an SPF (type TXT) record in order to prove that Amazon SES is authorized to send email from your domain.

Below are the steps to configure SPF using a dedicated subdomain for Amazon SES.

**Setting up a MAIL FROM domain for a verified domain**

1\. Open the Amazon SES console **[here](https://console.aws.amazon.com/ses/)
**.

2\. In the left navigation pane, under **Configuration**, choose **Verified identities**.

3\. In the list of identities, choose the identity you want to configure where the **Identity type** is **Domain** and **Status** is _Verified_.

4\. If the **Status** is _Unverified_, complete the procedures at [Verifying a DKIM domain identity with your DNS provider](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ses/latest/dg/creating-identities.html#just-verify-domain-proc)
 to verify the email address's domain.

5\. At the bottom of the screen in the in the **Custom MAIL FROM domain** pane, choose **Edit**.

6\. In the **General details** pane, do the following:

a. Select the **Use a custom MAIL FROM domain** checkbox.

b. For **MAIL FROM domain**, enter the subdomain that you want to use as the MAIL FROM domain.

c. For **Behavior on MX failure**, choose one of the following options:

*   **Use default MAIL FROM domain** – If the custom MAIL FROM domain's MX record is not set up correctly, Amazon SES uses a subdomain of _amazonses.com_. The subdomain varies based on the AWS Region that you use Amazon SES in.
    
*   **Reject** **message** – If the custom MAIL FROM domain's MX record is not set up correctly, Amazon SES returns a _MailFromDomainNotVerified_ error. Emails that you attempt to send from this domain are automatically rejected.
    

d. Choose **Save** **changes** - you'll be returned to the previous screen.

Publish the MX and SPF (type TXT) records to the DNS server of the custom MAIL FROM domain:

In the **Custom MAIL FROM domain** pane, the **Publish DNS records** table now displays the MX and SPF (type TXT) records in that you have to publish (add) to your domain's DNS configuration. These records use the formats shown in the following table.

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Name | Type | Value |
| `subdomain`.`domain`.`com` | MX  | 10 feedback-smtp.`region`.amazonses.com |
| `subdomain`.`domain`.`com` | TXT | "v=spf1 include:amazonses.com ~all" |

In the preceding records,

*   `subdomain`.`domain`.`com` will be populated with your MAIL FROM subdomain
    
*   region will be populated with the name of the AWS Region where you want to verify the MAIL FROM domain (such as _us-west-2_, _us-east-1_, or _eu-west-1_, etc.)
    
*   The number 10 listed along with the MX value is the preference order for the mail server and will need to be entered into a separate value field as specified by your DNS provider's GUI
    
*   The SPF's TXT record value has to include the quotation marks
    

From the **Publish DNS records** table, copy the MX and SPF (type TXT) records by choosing the copy icon next to each value and paste them into the corresponding fields in your DNS provider's GUI.

**IMPORTANT**: To successfully set up a custom MAIL FROM domain with Amazon SES, you must publish exactly one MX record to the DNS server of your MAIL FROM domain. If the MAIL FROM domain has multiple MX records, the custom MAIL FROM setup with Amazon SES will fail.

You can find the instructions to set up SPF in Amazon SES using a dedicated subdomain **[here](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ses/latest/dg/mail-from.html)
**.

* * *

[eSputnik](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8835048-esputnik)

Alchemer (ex SurveyGizmo)

Alchemer (ex SurveyGizmo)
=========================

How to set up DMARC for Alchemer (ex SurveyGizmo) in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471002-alchemer-ex-surveygizmo#h_eb851a51d5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471002-alchemer-ex-surveygizmo#h_eb23d202ca)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471002-alchemer-ex-surveygizmo#h_4a992367d8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471002-alchemer-ex-surveygizmo#h_a87d09d656)

SPF(supported)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Alchemer** (ex SurveyGizmo) and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Alchemer emails
========================================================

**Note**: In order to configure your DKIM and SPF records, you will need to be an Alchemer Account Administrator.

To begin your DKIM configuration within Alchemer, follow these steps:

**1.** Navigate to your account integrations page via **Integrations** -> **Data connectors** on the left hand navigation menu.

**2.** Scroll to the bottom-half of the **Integrations** page and click the **Configure** button next to the **Custom Email Settings** option.

**3.** Provide an **Integration Name**. This is the internal title that will be used to identify this integration in Email Actions and Email Campaigns. It is important to make this name something meaningful like _DKIM & SPF for alchemer.com_.

**4.** (Optional) Determine whether you want this integration to serve as the default for any Email Actions and Email Campaigns that are created going forward.

The option to **Use this integration as the default for all new email invitations and email actions** will be automatically selected. Un-check the option if needed.

**5.** Select **Send emails using Alchemer's servers (Setup SPF and DKIM)**. This will expose additional fields for configuring this integration.

**6.** Within the **From Address** section, provide your **Domain Name** and **Default From Address**.**​**

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152347/3c6c6901fc2d5f6efcd6be5c/hlGpNLyqCuXTX7yinMbDWawQpYmbl8RLLQ.png-1668828217?expires=1755792000&signature=fd0fe54c7cbb89c75827b560e0934238629ae3be8483ab24df65ab23c0348012&req=fCUlF8x8noVYFb4f3HP0gARKio%2BIrm%2Bfx%2BFySGEhHu%2Fao616TsLav9Q4LPSw%0AV5TsycQcoXTIdqOx8A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152347/3c6c6901fc2d5f6efcd6be5c/hlGpNLyqCuXTX7yinMbDWawQpYmbl8RLLQ.png-1668828217?expires=1755792000&signature=fd0fe54c7cbb89c75827b560e0934238629ae3be8483ab24df65ab23c0348012&req=fCUlF8x8noVYFb4f3HP0gARKio%2BIrm%2Bfx%2BFySGEhHu%2Fao616TsLav9Q4LPSw%0AV5TsycQcoXTIdqOx8A%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Under **DKIM Settings** you will need to generate your public key. This public key will then need to be added to **Valimail Enforce**.

**8.** Click **Generate DKIM** to generate your public key.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152355/8fc55e0e2e3f9e1bbb7d7c20/gnryqniL217-QGFFfDa0oV5yLdVn_Mw90A.png-1668828327?expires=1755792000&signature=6f104d7eac7af3b455769153f39f716e2ac25dae6621a68cf33a3691b96b7db1&req=fCUlF8x8noRaFb4f3HP0gO1%2B3vkwCMZm04R%2BpcDHyEGlwCmRB02HoTgfkeDH%0AeQ8TfN8SHUDPrV2bnA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152355/8fc55e0e2e3f9e1bbb7d7c20/gnryqniL217-QGFFfDa0oV5yLdVn_Mw90A.png-1668828327?expires=1755792000&signature=6f104d7eac7af3b455769153f39f716e2ac25dae6621a68cf33a3691b96b7db1&req=fCUlF8x8noRaFb4f3HP0gO1%2B3vkwCMZm04R%2BpcDHyEGlwCmRB02HoTgfkeDH%0AeQ8TfN8SHUDPrV2bnA%3D%3D%0A)

**9.** A DKIM selector (the DNS domain name for the public key) as well as the DNS Public Key will be generated:.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152367/8cf16f8d30139a734fced0a7/5958877iDKjcWiwxsCJnB79REvmdFWE6lw.png-1668828404?expires=1755792000&signature=eacbcc5e1979a10f7b08ae72a148a2e0a2c9e7e13e84096dc3765a698a8bd6d1&req=fCUlF8x8nodYFb4f3HP0gL%2B6%2BAEECGlJqWbhvs2iG5gZzZ%2BLRfBNVihEWrgU%0AYCqFlzgzqceXwm0e%2Fw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152367/8cf16f8d30139a734fced0a7/5958877iDKjcWiwxsCJnB79REvmdFWE6lw.png-1668828404?expires=1755792000&signature=eacbcc5e1979a10f7b08ae72a148a2e0a2c9e7e13e84096dc3765a698a8bd6d1&req=fCUlF8x8nodYFb4f3HP0gL%2B6%2BAEECGlJqWbhvs2iG5gZzZ%2BLRfBNVihEWrgU%0AYCqFlzgzqceXwm0e%2Fw%3D%3D%0A)

**10.** Add the newly created DKIM key in **Valimail**.

**11.** After the key has been added in Valimail, go back in Alchemer, click the **Save** button to finish setup.

**Note**: You will notice that both your SPF and DKIM Settings are not validated during setup. We will attempt to validate your settings when you **Save** your integration. We perform this validation by sending a test email (to one of our servers) and reviewing the email headers of that test email.

Your DKIM & SPF Records will need to be verified before you can start using them in email sends. On the main **Integrations** page, look for the green check-mark under the **Verified** column.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152371/404efeadd59b6fc45e791c10/yFcftR9El2FeEvQB26XvJVIC6W0j-PQVJw.png-1668828777?expires=1755792000&signature=e86bc3b697549c1e4dde09c1d995cc70304c02c1823795e230f881e4a90f25ab&req=fCUlF8x8noZeFb4f3HP0gGGl86wQo6ypO4qvIbO1rIkjs3Ox98wnwmoiOpqr%0Aje5MzyfXcW7qkvLRVg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152371/404efeadd59b6fc45e791c10/yFcftR9El2FeEvQB26XvJVIC6W0j-PQVJw.png-1668828777?expires=1755792000&signature=e86bc3b697549c1e4dde09c1d995cc70304c02c1823795e230f881e4a90f25ab&req=fCUlF8x8noZeFb4f3HP0gGGl86wQo6ypO4qvIbO1rIkjs3Ox98wnwmoiOpqr%0Aje5MzyfXcW7qkvLRVg%3D%3D%0A)

You can find the instructions on how to set up DKIM for Alchemer **[here](https://help.alchemer.com/help/setup-dkim-spf-records)
**.

Add an Alchemer DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Alchemer emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Alchemer is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in Alchemer for your domain
================================================

In theory, Alchemer should send SPF aligned emails once it has been configured to do so.

However, you may notice that Alchemer emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added Alchemer to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by Alchemer not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by making sure SPF alignment if turned on in Alchemer for your domain.

You can find more information about SPF setup **[here](https://help.alchemer.com/help/setup-dkim-spf-records)
**.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

AFAS Software

AFAS Software
=============

How to set up DMARC for AFAS Software in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470998-afas-software#h_9b904a0658)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470998-afas-software#h_189f8d12f2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470998-afas-software#h_20804c3c1e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470998-afas-software#h_5055229a5b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470998-afas-software#h_578de2dd21)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **AFAS Software** and how they are managed in Valimail While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your AFAS Software emails
=============================================================

**Step 1: Request via the customer portal**
-------------------------------------------

**1.** Log on to the [AFAS customer portal](http://klant.afas.nl/)
 as the Administrator.

**2.** On the customer portal, to **Mijn gegevens** -> **Abonnementen** -> **Mijn abonnement**.

**3.** Click the subscription of your Profit environment.

If, for example, the Profit environment is called **O98765AA**, the subscription is **98765**.

**4.** Click **Create DKIM request**.

**5.** Enter the domain that you want to use in the DKIM domain field.

It is important that the correct domain name is entered in the **DKIM domain** field. Enter here the domain for which you want to use DKIM. The domain is part of the e-mail address.

_Example_:

Your email address is [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#2742514667514848554542424b4343484a464e4909494b)
.

We only need the section that comes after @, therefore **exampledomain.nl**.

**6.** First add two CNAME records in the DNS or your domain. This refers to the two records below where exampledomain.nl must be replaced with your domain.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail, you will need to add these 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

DKIM Selector: **afasonline1**

CNAME Target: **afasonline1.domainkey.afas.online.**

DKIM Selector: **afasonline2**

CNAME Target: **afasonline2.domainkey.afas.online.**

It is allowed to already make the request with AFAS before the CNAME records are created. In this case, the request will be rejected with the option to have the request reassessed. It is also mandatory that you already have the AFAS Online SPF record configured before making this request.

In Valimail, that is accomplished by adding AFAS Software in your Enabled Senders list.

**Step 2: Settings in the Profit environment**
----------------------------------------------

**1.** You will receive a username and password through the request. Configure these as follows in Profit:

*   Open the environment.
    
*   Go to: General / Environment / Management / Properties.
    
*   Go to the tab E-mail.
    
*   Select Apply DKIM in relation to outgoing emails and enter the email address and password.
    

**2\.** The figure below includes example data. Enter your own data.

[![a screenshot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152231/d0d5fd61f83d06bec5132a2b/wKiLVNmI38NQkIxTIxAws6bc2WHHOXgHgg.png-1683276357?expires=1755792000&signature=b833fdf02737f21c8f85c06546c36d033a5f5d0a63dc8cb357f28466b7ab02fe&req=fCUlF8x8n4JeFb4f3HP0gKnqnqbEaXBc%2FdZruTWHv4CMQOszcSbOMQeJqWXL%0AMhYE%2F7VsVHjQR2rzKw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152231/d0d5fd61f83d06bec5132a2b/wKiLVNmI38NQkIxTIxAws6bc2WHHOXgHgg.png-1683276357?expires=1755792000&signature=b833fdf02737f21c8f85c06546c36d033a5f5d0a63dc8cb357f28466b7ab02fe&req=fCUlF8x8n4JeFb4f3HP0gKnqnqbEaXBc%2FdZruTWHv4CMQOszcSbOMQeJqWXL%0AMhYE%2F7VsVHjQR2rzKw%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** After the configuration, your emails will be sent after applying DKIM encryption.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for AFAS Software, **[here](https://help.afas.nl/help/EN/SE/Bas_Output_SetEmail_ServerAndFrom.htm#o91547)
**.

Add an AFAS Software DKIM key in Valimail
=========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your AFAS Software emails
============================================================

Once you establish that AFAS Software is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471006-apptivo#h_e894b66fbe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471006-apptivo#h_007b9ae9f9)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Apptivo

Apptivo
=======

How to set up DMARC for Apptivo in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471006-apptivo#h_e5c1b41341)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471006-apptivo#h_e894b66fbe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471006-apptivo#h_007b9ae9f9)

SPF(supported)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the process of authenticating emails sent from the **Apptivo** service on behalf of your organization's domain(s) and how the authentication records are managed in Valimail.

Appitvo does not send SPF aligned emails by default, but as long as DKIM is configured for Apptivo on your domain, your emails sent with Apptivo will be properly authenticated.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Apptivo emails
=======================================================

**1.** Log in and access your Apptivo account.

**2.** Click on your name from the top right corner of the screen and then click on **Business settings** from the drop-down menu.

**3.** From the left navigation panel, click on **Email deliverabilty.**

**4.** From the top right side of the screen, click **Create.**

**5.** Select **Display your email address as sender Address from SPF/ DKIM information**, enter your domain, and then click the **Generate SPF and DKIM record** button.

**6.** You will be provided with an SPF and DKIM record that need to be added in Valimail Enforce.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152468/a98b731320021d6e3dfedeb0/DE3GQXCy9dbIIiCkIXFg1_MrrYdPGt_MhA.png-1666044052?expires=1755792000&signature=530dfa1c710c44b1ef96c7cf717596025a55745c3e09f72679c34d5c996b9f52&req=fCUlF8x8mYdXFb4f3HP0gNPeiYyhmTe101Jv0ZpiWh%2BJFZjfZgkz9weam85d%0A0z7iB9v%2Ft19mq8zoEA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152468/a98b731320021d6e3dfedeb0/DE3GQXCy9dbIIiCkIXFg1_MrrYdPGt_MhA.png-1666044052?expires=1755792000&signature=530dfa1c710c44b1ef96c7cf717596025a55745c3e09f72679c34d5c996b9f52&req=fCUlF8x8mYdXFb4f3HP0gNPeiYyhmTe101Jv0ZpiWh%2BJFZjfZgkz9weam85d%0A0z7iB9v%2Ft19mq8zoEA%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Publish the DKIM key in Enforce with the information provided in the Apptivo console.

**8.** After you add the DKIM key, the Apptivo console should detect the changes automatically and start sending authenticated emails on behalf of your domain. In case this does not happen after adding the necessary records, please contact the vendor's support team.

Add an Apptivo DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Apptivo emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Apptivo is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

**Important Note**: In order to validate the configuration you may need to append Apptivo's include mechanism to your SPF record, after the macro directive. Your SPF record should look as follows: **v=spf1 include:%{i}.\_ip.%{h}.\_ehlo.%{d}.\_spf.vali.email include:spf.apptivo.com ~all**

* * *

Related Articles

[Marketo](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705889-marketo)

[Postmark](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757814-postmark)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Action Network​

Action Network​
===============

How to set up DMARC for Action Network in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470996-action-network#h_c58ddd6151)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470996-action-network#h_acd225bcfe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470996-action-network#h_20b7d29d86)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Action Network** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Action Network** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Action Network emails
==============================================================

In order for you to setup DKIM in Action Network for your domain, you will email them at **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#c0b3b5b0b0afb2b480a1a3b4a9afaeaea5b4b7afb2abeeafb2a7)
** and tell them what you use for your reply-to, meaning your domain.

They will send your domain to SendGrid (which delivers their emails). SendGrid will give them your public key which they will then share with you.

You will need to add the public key (CNAME records) to your DNS records.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for this domain in Valimail, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail and the 3rd CNAME record in your DNS.

Email them back when you’ve added their records to your DNS and Valimail. They will verify that you did this correctly.

Use that domain in all of your reply-tos.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Action Network, **[here](https://help.actionnetwork.org/hc/en-us/articles/360014433271-Installing-DKIM-and-SPF-records)
**.

Add an Action Network DKIM key in Valimail
==========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Action Network emails
=============================================================

Once you establish that Action Network is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Flodesk

Flodesk
=======

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466386-flodesk#h_66bcc591b3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466386-flodesk#h_17c0503be3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466386-flodesk#h_06d7c6d5b9)

SPF (supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Flodesk** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages **_Sendgrid_**'s email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Flodesk** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the **Sendgrid** infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Flodesk emails
=======================================================

**1.** In the Flodesk tab, go to your **Account** -> **Domain setup**.

[![Domain setup](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/851211665/235f5d6b433be869d1bbb01e/_RymbC0UP3PiqbyGDlx9jUP26MAGGD5-rg.png-1690837215?expires=1755792000&signature=c0d61671d021b2eaec7f7ef92694aa368b60b0ad1cc717368059b21535d5c2ae&req=fCUmFMh%2Fm4daFb4f3HP0gN7DP7Fy55JBugEMZIDdBqPINvGtXnipCoJUMQt0%0AAZuEN2Fis7THPbj64w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/851211665/235f5d6b433be869d1bbb01e/_RymbC0UP3PiqbyGDlx9jUP26MAGGD5-rg.png-1690837215?expires=1755792000&signature=c0d61671d021b2eaec7f7ef92694aa368b60b0ad1cc717368059b21535d5c2ae&req=fCUmFMh%2Fm4daFb4f3HP0gN7DP7Fy55JBugEMZIDdBqPINvGtXnipCoJUMQt0%0AAZuEN2Fis7THPbj64w%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click on the **Let's Get Started** button to start verifying your domain. It will bring you to a summary screen of the next steps.

[![Let's get started button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/851211674/ec9feaac3ce10efd1801562a/zR0UsFTU00v6RH85RayPea7OJcH06VIAkQ.png-1690837325?expires=1755792000&signature=0b4e35d5db4c2938996355d4b082d9423cd8587ac6772e1bd5f288f9ba3462b1&req=fCUmFMh%2Fm4ZbFb4f3HP0gKmzZ13nYt82Nz1QdcvjUsuS%2BuHrqOIoDwZhQe9Q%0AuDP2Xf8M4ZpuWe68QA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/851211674/ec9feaac3ce10efd1801562a/zR0UsFTU00v6RH85RayPea7OJcH06VIAkQ.png-1690837325?expires=1755792000&signature=0b4e35d5db4c2938996355d4b082d9423cd8587ac6772e1bd5f288f9ba3462b1&req=fCUmFMh%2Fm4ZbFb4f3HP0gKmzZ13nYt82Nz1QdcvjUsuS%2BuHrqOIoDwZhQe9Q%0AuDP2Xf8M4ZpuWe68QA%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Select your domain provider from the drop-down menu. Then click on **Continue**.

[![your domain name box](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/851211683/68fa2ced8aecdb92ebaf951a/-rGOa7_5w4Hgub36Kd2UTeplWa9RimsN4A.png-1690837391?expires=1755792000&signature=95fb5f4f95c303836c26222c6fd5b9f02cd13e0c1eb35098bd56cd80c8bdb7ec&req=fCUmFMh%2Fm4lcFb4f3HP0gM5rUcupNfSwoxrXHK4C89iUvon9HIi%2F0dcmAotW%0A5RrqaDie0m3M%2BEmceA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/851211683/68fa2ced8aecdb92ebaf951a/-rGOa7_5w4Hgub36Kd2UTeplWa9RimsN4A.png-1690837391?expires=1755792000&signature=95fb5f4f95c303836c26222c6fd5b9f02cd13e0c1eb35098bd56cd80c8bdb7ec&req=fCUmFMh%2Fm4lcFb4f3HP0gM5rUcupNfSwoxrXHK4C89iUvon9HIi%2F0dcmAotW%0A5RrqaDie0m3M%2BEmceA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** On the next screen, you will see the 3 sets of CNAME records that you need to publish. If the CNAMEs are DKIM keys (have \_domainkey. in the **HOST** name) those keys should be published from Valimail Enforce as seen in the " Add a Flodesk DKIM in Enforce" section in this article below.

[![Flodesk dkim records](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/851211689/6156dfdbc79364dd4fdd6f52/zqJk9Kss9GBlAcI0WLgKNPdfM_t6uCrJmg.png-1690837594?expires=1755792000&signature=482d91e16e46de230b9389194de43e2869b2b1a2557a27ea4d16332d69734e0e&req=fCUmFMh%2Fm4lWFb4f3HP0gJkkyT1fUSCAmO2SCtX%2B1V9KCLp7Cei1ir%2BrifpL%0AuNvCYgS2wLQ6TcEGlA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/851211689/6156dfdbc79364dd4fdd6f52/zqJk9Kss9GBlAcI0WLgKNPdfM_t6uCrJmg.png-1690837594?expires=1755792000&signature=482d91e16e46de230b9389194de43e2869b2b1a2557a27ea4d16332d69734e0e&req=fCUmFMh%2Fm4lWFb4f3HP0gJkkyT1fUSCAmO2SCtX%2B1V9KCLp7Cei1ir%2BrifpL%0AuNvCYgS2wLQ6TcEGlA%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** After the records are published you should Verify the Domain. You should now see the yellow Pending status label at the top of your domain setup card. Wait 48-72 hours and come back to check if your domain has been successfully authenticated. The top of your domain setup card should now display the **Verified** green label.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM **[here](https://help.flodesk.com/en/articles/3200817-authenticate-your-domain-via-dkim)
**.

Add a Flodesk DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Flodesk emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Flodesk is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470995-act-on#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470995-act-on#h_bbcda74186)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470995-act-on#h_4a99a40e2e)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Act-On

Act-On
======

How to set up DMARC for Act-On in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470995-act-on#h_0470b7a916)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470995-act-on#h_bbcda74186)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470995-act-on#h_4a99a40e2e)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Act-on** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Act-On uses a CNAME DKIM key and a dedicated subdomain configuration setup.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Act-On emails
======================================================

**1.** Gather the list of domains you are using for your From addresses (eg., [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#107d71627b7564797e7750737f7d60717e693e737f7d)
, [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#e78d868982948a8e938fa788929584888a9786899ec984888a)
, [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#f49d9a929bb4979b9984959a8dda9a9180)
) and make a list of the domains.

**2.** From the Start page in Act-On, locate your **Account ID**

*   Your Account ID number is listed next to your **Account Name**
    
*   You can also find your Account ID in the top-left menu under **Account Details**
    

**3.** **For each domain**: Work with your IT to create a new CNAME entry in your DNS:

*   Name or Host: {AOAccountID}**aoauth.\_domainkey**
    
*   (Replace {AOAccountID} with your **Account ID** from Step 2)
    
*   Value or Points To: **dkim.act-on.com**
    
*   See [Editing Your DNS to Implement DKIM](https://connect.act-on.com/hc/en-us/articles/360023939693)
     for detailed steps to create this entry
    
*   **For multiple accounts**: Please use your parent account's ID for the CNAME entry on all child accounts.
    

**4.** Continue the process below.

After setting up SPF and DKIM for your Email From domain, [contact Act-On support team](https://connect.act-on.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
 to complete the process.

In the support ticket include the subdomain you have chosen. Support will reply to confirm that setup is complete.

**Important Note**: **_You will also need to identify a subdomain that can be set aside for Act-On email. Act-On may require other subdomains for other purposes but this subdomain must be separate from those._**

**_Act-On will send you the proper DNS changes required for DKIM. The DKIM key will be placed on the top level domain (not the subdomain) and can therefore be added through the Enforce UI._**

**_For SPF, add the SPF record on the subdomain and also ad the MX record on the subdomain, as specified [here](https://connect.act-on.com/hc/en-us/articles/360025707434)
. Do not delegate the subdomain SPF to Enforce since Act-On will not be able to recognize this._**

**Note**: You can add the subdomain selected above as a Single Sender subdomain in Enforce with Act-On as the selected sender. While Enforce will not be managing SPF for the subdomain, this acts as a record of the subdomain and also guides Helios when classifying email sent with that MailFrom.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM for Act-On **[here](https://connect.act-on.com/hc/en-us/articles/360025707374-Email-From-Setup)
**.

Add an Act-On DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Act-On emails
=====================================================

Once you establish that Act-On is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Accredible

Accredible
==========

How to set up DMARC for Accredible in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470991-accredible#h_c74a8d137e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470991-accredible#h_5ce8a6cf8b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470991-accredible#h_f9b020dbb3)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Accredible** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Accredible** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Accredible emails
==========================================================

Accredible uses SendGrid for email delivery. SendGrid sets the return path on any email that's delivered to your recipients to match our domain.

In order to set up DKIM in Accredible for your domain, you will need to contact Accredible's Support team at **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#bac9cfcacad5c8cefadbd9d9c8dfded3d8d6df94d9d5d7)
**

They will generally provide you with 3 CNAME records to be added in your DNS

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and the 3rd CNAME record, in your DNS.

You can also find the info on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Accredible, **[here](https://help.accredible.com/email-spf-dkim)
**.

Add an Accredible DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Accredible emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Accredible is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471000-agile-crm#h_aff07b7963)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Agile CRM

Agile CRM
=========

How to set up DMARC for Agile CRM in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471000-agile-crm#h_d2f63f8caa)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471000-agile-crm#h_c6d97d08eb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471000-agile-crm#h_aff07b7963)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Agile CRM** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Agile CRM** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Agile CRM emails
=========================================================

In order to authorize Agile CRM to send emails on behalf of your domain, you must configure DKIM and SPF in Agiel CRM for your domain.

The SPF and DKIM records will be provided to you inside of Agile CRM once you add your domain and click on **Get Settings**.

**1.** Log in to your Agile CRM account.

**2.** Go to to **Admin Settings** -> **Analytics**.

**3.** Scroll down to **Email(SPF and DKIM) settings**.

**4.** Enter the name of the domain for which you want to create a TXT record for DKIM.

[![Your domain and Get Settings button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152312/f0450260e76cca99c5f553d1/CY1upStehqmZPQTgQ_krZdJu9pOOo6pxLA.png-1686157154?expires=1755792000&signature=6fcb876434198e9d69dc5e506d989411db75c6475a6964dd3d538ce9ca3f9d5c&req=fCUlF8x8noBdFb4f3HP0gKjoKlh27Dbqp0PD5BLoeI2AWXxIzuwxLA9zFVzo%0AMdrs8yZ8yTwb0j747A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152312/f0450260e76cca99c5f553d1/CY1upStehqmZPQTgQ_krZdJu9pOOo6pxLA.png-1686157154?expires=1755792000&signature=6fcb876434198e9d69dc5e506d989411db75c6475a6964dd3d538ce9ca3f9d5c&req=fCUlF8x8noBdFb4f3HP0gKjoKlh27Dbqp0PD5BLoeI2AWXxIzuwxLA9zFVzo%0AMdrs8yZ8yTwb0j747A%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** The TXT records are provided by default as shown below, however you have an option to request CNAME keys setup by clicking on the **Get CNAME RECORDS** button and CNAME records will be provided to you instead.

[![DKIM TXT records](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152325/fe69cfabe4223f98564d0b13/frEkLc2TqLnrbrJZNOSLOUhZxJb4nMqRhw.png-1686157384?expires=1755792000&signature=fcc237feec04ac93feb2f15741e6d8b2a32c8777cae0dbef04ce41c26eac1068&req=fCUlF8x8noNaFb4f3HP0gJnAFJ969Buk95TRLUkaBd6dKyxUabTpX0NXHCzo%0Acc5tNxuHOQDePVoyUw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152325/fe69cfabe4223f98564d0b13/frEkLc2TqLnrbrJZNOSLOUhZxJb4nMqRhw.png-1686157384?expires=1755792000&signature=fcc237feec04ac93feb2f15741e6d8b2a32c8777cae0dbef04ce41c26eac1068&req=fCUlF8x8noNaFb4f3HP0gJnAFJ969Buk95TRLUkaBd6dKyxUabTpX0NXHCzo%0Acc5tNxuHOQDePVoyUw%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Add the records in your DNS for your domain.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail, then add **Agile CRM** as an Enabled Senders for your domain in Enforce and add the remaining mx record in your DNS.

**7.** Once you have created the SPF and DKIM records in your DNS, you will have to click on the **Verify** button above.

If you need any other assistance from Agile CRM in setting this up, you can contact their support here:

Phone number: **1-800-980-0729**

Agile CRM Support: **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#d5b6b4a7b095b4b2bcb9b0b6a7b8fbb6bab8)
**

More information about Agile CRM's newsletters and marketing automation, **[here](https://www.agilecrm.com/marketing-automation/)** and **[here](https://www.agilecrm.com/marketing-automation/newsletter-campaigns)
**.

Add an Agile CRM DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Agile CRM emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Agile CRM is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Salesforce Marketing Cloud | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700364-salesforce-marketing-cloud#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700364-salesforce-marketing-cloud#h_103ea6f72e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700364-salesforce-marketing-cloud#h_93931ad5ca)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Salesforce Marketing Cloud

Salesforce Marketing Cloud
==========================

How to set up DMARC for Salesforce Marketing Cloud in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700364-salesforce-marketing-cloud#h_103ea6f72e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700364-salesforce-marketing-cloud#h_93931ad5ca)

SPF(supported) (dedicated IP only)  
DKIM(recommended)

Website: [https://www.salesforce.com/products/marketing-cloud/overview/](https://www.salesforce.com/products/marketing-cloud/overview/)

*   Salesforce Marketing Cloud almost always uses a dedicated subdomain. There are a few very old implementations that do not but any new implementation will use a dedicated subdomain
    
*   Salesforce Marketing Cloud has multiple ways to set up their service in DNS. The preferred option is to delegate the subdomain to Salesforce Marketing Cloud which allows Salesforce Marketing Cloud to host all DNS records on their DNS servers. This means that Salesforce Marketing Cloud will host SPF and DKIM
    
*   The subdomain must be added to the Enforce configuration as a Single Sender Subdomain
    
*   In order to send authenticated emails, the customer will need to purchase a Sender Authentication Package (SAP) from Salesforce: [https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.mc\_es\_sender\_authentication\_package.htm&type=5](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.mc_es_sender_authentication_package.htm&type=5)
    
*   Salesforce Marketing Cloud uses a single TXT DKIM key with a selector of **200608**. This same key is used for all Salesforce Marketing Cloud customers and was created in Aug 2006. Since this is a TXT record, DKIM key rotation is problematic.
    
*   Salesforce Marketing Cloud can apparently create customized DKIM keys/ selectors based on some data we have seen in the wild but how this works in practice is unknown
    

Add the Salesforce Marketing Cloud DKIM key in Valimail
=======================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Salesforce Marketing Cloud emails
=========================================================================

Once you establish that Salesforce Marketing Cloud is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Table of contents
