[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705849-google-workspace-g-suite#h_87cc45031c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705849-google-workspace-g-suite#h_ebde9af2dd)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Google Workspace (G Suite)

Google Workspace (G Suite)
==========================

How to set up DMARC for Google Workspace in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705849-google-workspace-g-suite#h_e3563f6893)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705849-google-workspace-g-suite#h_d13f9ec1e1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705849-google-workspace-g-suite#h_87cc45031c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705849-google-workspace-g-suite#h_ebde9af2dd)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Google Workspace** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Google Workspace emails
================================================================

**Note**: You need to signed into the Google Workspace console as a **super administrator**. More details **[here](https://support.google.com/a/answer/2405986#super_admin)
**.

**1.** Sign in to your **Google Admin console** (at admin.google.com) with **super administrator** privileges.

**2.** Go to **Apps -> Google Workspace -> Gmail.**

**3.** Click **Authenticate email**.

**4.** In the **Selected domain** menu, select the domain where you want to set up DKIM.

**5.** Click the **Generate New Record** button.

**6.** In the **Generate New Record** box, select your DKIM key settings. We recommend you choose the 2048 key value.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **Setting** | **Options** |
| **DKIM key bit length** | **2048—**If your domain provider supports 2048-bit keys, select this option. Longer keys are more secure than shorter keys. If you previously used a 1024-bit key, you can switch to a 2048-bit key if your domain provider supports them. Read more about [domain keys and TXT record limits](https://support.google.com/a/answer/11613097)<br>.<br><br>**1024**—If your domain host doesn't support 2048-bit keys, select this option. |
| **Prefix selector** | The default selector prefix is **google**. We recommend you use the default.<br><br>If your domain already uses a DKIM key with the prefix **google**, enter a different prefix in this field. Read more about [DKIM selectors](https://support.google.com/a/answer/11611356)<br>. |

**7.** At the bottom of the Generate new record box, click **Generate**. On the setting page, the text string beneath TXT record value changes to a new value and this message is displayed: _DKIM authentication settings updated_.

**8.** Copy the DKIM values shown in the Authenticate email window and publish the Google generated DKIM key in the **Valimail Enforce** platform.

**9.** After adding the DKIM TXT key record in Valimail Enforce, click on **Start Authentication** in your Google Workspace admin panel.

You can find the instructions on how to turn on DKIM in Google Workspace **[here](https://support.google.com/a/answer/180504)
**.

Add a Google Workspace DKIM key in Valimail
===========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Google Workspace emails
===============================================================

Once you establish that Google Workspace is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment Google Workspace for your domain
=====================================================

You may notice that Google Workspace emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added Google Workspace to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by Google Workspace not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by the service owner turn on SPF alignment for your domain in Google Workspace.

You can find more info about setting up SPF and turning on SPF alignment **[here](https://support.google.com/a/answer/10683907?hl=en&ref_topic=10685331)
**.

* * *

Related Articles

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure)
[Poppulo](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757812-poppulo)
[SPEKTRIX](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8759534-spektrix)
[Email Blaster](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8792112-email-blaster)
[Mailfence](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8825073-mailfence)

# Sendgrid | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700033-sendgrid#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700033-sendgrid#h_f30d80199f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700033-sendgrid#h_008b7fab23)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700033-sendgrid#h_d24abb3395)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700033-sendgrid#h_986f41c8d7)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Sendgrid

Sendgrid
========

How to set up DMARC for Sendgrid in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700033-sendgrid#h_f30d80199f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700033-sendgrid#h_008b7fab23)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700033-sendgrid#h_d24abb3395)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8700033-sendgrid#h_986f41c8d7)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(Recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Sendgrid** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Sendgrid typically uses a dedicated subdomain for DKIM and SPF configuration.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Sendgrid emails
========================================================

**1.** Login to your SendGrid admin account.

**2.** In the SendGrid UI, select **Settings** -> **Sender Authentication**.

**3.** In the domain authentication section, click **Get Started**.

**4.** Next, add in information about your DNS host, and indicate whether you also want to set up link branding. Click **Next**.

**5.** Fill in the domain that you want to send from and add advanced settings as needed. Make sure that you only enter the name of your root domain. Do not include **www** or **[http://www](http://www/)
** in this field. Your domain needs to match the domain of your FROM address on the emails you are sending out. For example, if I am sending an email from **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#82e7fae3eff2eee7c2f1e7ece6e5f0ebe6ace1edef)
**, I would set my domain authentication domain to be **sendgrid.com**. Click **Next**.

**6.** Next, you need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM records to **Valimail Enforce** and the 3rd CNAME record to your **DNS host**.

**7.** Once you publish the 2 DKIM keys in **Valimail Enforce** and the 3rd CNAME record in your **DNS host**, return to the **Sender authentication** page in the Sendgrid console and click **Verify**.

[![a close-up of a logo](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/907720724/0f4f9afcc0a9e36c7cd1d26e/mzQMD5Xdq1KwdmNbNkTyWJhiSb8FPcX1mQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=fbb0ce3e3b2e880fe1913c83ca44dd25c8d0b6ba69f178f1a4fe1051c4817468&req=fSAgEct%2BmoNbFb4f3HP0gK1HOcKvQlZsM4jyJKs2aTbHEyvAvglUeV4jXCI6%0A5jmcjMGgotAdX0cUAw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/907720724/0f4f9afcc0a9e36c7cd1d26e/mzQMD5Xdq1KwdmNbNkTyWJhiSb8FPcX1mQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=fbb0ce3e3b2e880fe1913c83ca44dd25c8d0b6ba69f178f1a4fe1051c4817468&req=fSAgEct%2BmoNbFb4f3HP0gK1HOcKvQlZsM4jyJKs2aTbHEyvAvglUeV4jXCI6%0A5jmcjMGgotAdX0cUAw%3D%3D%0A)

**Note**: It can take up to 24-48 hours for the records to verify after you upload them into **Valimail Enforce** and your **DNS host**, so you will likely have to come back later to verify.

You can find these instructions on how to setup DKIM for your domain in Sendgrid **[here](https://docs.sendgrid.com/ui/account-and-settings/how-to-set-up-domain-authentication#setting-up-domain-authentication)
**.

Below is an example of the CNAME values under the HOST column as they are displayed and how you will need to enter them into your DNS management with one of these providers:

**a. Record Name**: em722.yourdomain.com

Record Type: CNAME

Record Value: u20722350.wl101.sendgrid.net

**b. Record Name**: s1

Record Type: CNAME

Record Value: s1.domainkey.u20722350.wl101.sendgrid.net

**c. Record Name**: s2

Record Type: CNAME

Record Value: s2.domainkey.u20722350.wl101.sendgrid.net

**IMPORTANT**: The entries made in the VALUE or POINTS TO field from the 2 DKIM keys are the ones that need to added in Valimail Enforce.

The records that look like the **b** and **c** ones, need to be added in Valimail Enforce - the one that looks like the **a** one, needs to be added in your DNS host.

Add a Sendgrid DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Setup a custom DKIM key for your domain in Sendgrid
===================================================

It is important to remember that the **s1** and **s2** DKIM key selectors are the default ones that Sendgrid issues.

Therefore, if your organization uses multiple Sendgrid instances or other services that use Sendgrid under the hood, you will need to **set up a custom DKIM key** that has a custom DKIM selector for those instances/services, if you already have one Sendgrid instance configured with **s1** and **s2** selectors on the DKIM keys.

You can find the instructions on how to set up a custom DKIM key in Sendgrid **[here](https://docs.sendgrid.com/ui/account-and-settings/how-to-set-up-domain-authentication#using-a-custom-dkim-selector)
**.

**Note**: The Sendgrid ID and WL ID are optional but if they are given to you by the service, we recommend you add them as well. Add only the numerical characters in the **SendGrid ID** and **WL ID** fields.

Configuring SPF authentication for your Sendgrid emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Sendgrid is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Related Articles

Acoustic

Acoustic
========

How to set up DMARC for Acoustic in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470993-acoustic#h_6a0799630e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470993-acoustic#h_e175312fe6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470993-acoustic#h_cfb145b640)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Acoustic** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Acoustic emails
========================================================

In order to set up DKIM in Acoustic for your domain/s, you will need to contact your Acoustic service owner.

The Acoustic Provisioning Team will generate all necessary SPF and DKIM records that you can then provide to your IT team for implementation.

Acoustic uses a 1024-bit encryption for its default DKIM key and the key they provide is a TXT key.

If you manage the DKIM record for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the Acoustic TXT DKIM key in your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Acoustic, **[here](https://help.goacoustic.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043024713-Sender-Policy-Framework-SPF-and-Domain-Keys-Identified-Mail-DKIM-)
**.

Add an Acoustic DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Acoustic emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Acoustic is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

# Mailgun by Sinch | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705824-mailgun-by-sinch#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705824-mailgun-by-sinch#h_f4eea75724)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705824-mailgun-by-sinch#h_71c22cfcb6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705824-mailgun-by-sinch#h_0acda8d0b8)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Mailgun by Sinch

Mailgun by Sinch
================

How to set up DMARC for Mailgun by Sinch in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705824-mailgun-by-sinch#h_f4eea75724)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705824-mailgun-by-sinch#h_71c22cfcb6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705824-mailgun-by-sinch#h_0acda8d0b8)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Mailgun by Sinch** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Mailgun emails
=======================================================

**1.** Login to your Mailgun account.

**2.** Go to the **Domains** tab

**3.** Add your domain in the **Domain name** box.

**4.** **Choose your DKIM key length**. Longer keys equal more protection from spammers, so we recommend you to chose the 2048 option.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908820261/33d98e3762ebcf44c77a5777/3zkYHqvdRAG3ada8ZyXOk4ex4eryvfWw2Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e10fab43b814fcdc7e346036797e3ec82778aa67dce139883bba9330e727c033&req=fSAvHst%2Bn4deFb4f3HP0gF03TMmFhzE1EBygbsvb1rwXj0kp7uFHU8awJmvH%0ArFYCEjIvzRIy9vRLRQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908820261/33d98e3762ebcf44c77a5777/3zkYHqvdRAG3ada8ZyXOk4ex4eryvfWw2Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e10fab43b814fcdc7e346036797e3ec82778aa67dce139883bba9330e727c033&req=fSAvHst%2Bn4deFb4f3HP0gF03TMmFhzE1EBygbsvb1rwXj0kp7uFHU8awJmvH%0ArFYCEjIvzRIy9vRLRQ%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** On the domain settings page, you will find the DKIM records generated for your domain in the **Domain Verification & DNS** section.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908820272/90d73409232a703167c220fa/N6WXTBXALM-mivskBZHviltb7f6BC4GmjA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6cd4fd6d8e4eca0f4b0f79d73e147b1d7ab53b0af6d0ae0938a1cb274e326427&req=fSAvHst%2Bn4ZdFb4f3HP0gKmmttZd04xMUde9CwKItmgIcqUNOehCGtg4qviC%0AdjSLjPZnvPHJYqEuBw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908820272/90d73409232a703167c220fa/N6WXTBXALM-mivskBZHviltb7f6BC4GmjA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6cd4fd6d8e4eca0f4b0f79d73e147b1d7ab53b0af6d0ae0938a1cb274e326427&req=fSAvHst%2Bn4ZdFb4f3HP0gKmmttZd04xMUde9CwKItmgIcqUNOehCGtg4qviC%0AdjSLjPZnvPHJYqEuBw%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Copy the DKIM record key value provided - then you need to go and add this DKIM keys in **Valimail Enforce**.

**7.** After you publish the Mailgun DKIM key in Valimail Enforce, log back into your Mailgun account, and check for a verified status (a green verification badge) against your domain name to confirm that your domain has been verified and set up with DKIM email authentication.

You can find the instructions on how to set up DKIM in Mailgun **[here](https://www.mailgun.com/blog/deliverability/understanding-dkim-how-it-works/#chapter-4)
**.

Add a Mailgun DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Mailgun emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Mailgun is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705860-microsoft-office-365#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705860-microsoft-office-365#h_cb6c7f73ba)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Microsoft Office 365

Microsoft Office 365
====================

How to set up DMARC for Microsoft Office 365 in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705860-microsoft-office-365#h_4130ec6120)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705860-microsoft-office-365#h_119e5342e0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705860-microsoft-office-365#h_cb6c7f73ba)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705860-microsoft-office-365#h_b1888ae345)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Microsoft Office 365** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Microsoft Office 365 emails
====================================================================

All the accepted domains of your tenant will be shown in the Microsoft 365 Defender portal under the DKIM page. If you do not see it, add your accepted domain from [domains page](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/admin/setup/add-domain#add-a-domain)
. Once your domain is added, follow the steps as shown below to configure DKIM.

**Step 1**: On the [DKIM page](https://security.microsoft.com/dkimv2)
, select the domain you wish to configure.

[![Selected domain to configure](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908830982/306fe88721702d1875d49c4f/-bpJGFxuy6Fi4-jd6RY6vDi_CY_96IiSow.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5d663ff0db9d1bb09fe3c7419f6b41091c5ee2cc86564dad2573a7db1ed8fd7d&req=fSAvHsp%2BlIldFb4f3HP0gHHwm2st6CboyEZjLiOhAqumYl7AVTrmNfN1F4wd%0AHIaiTffZ8CtuZfI%2BqQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908830982/306fe88721702d1875d49c4f/-bpJGFxuy6Fi4-jd6RY6vDi_CY_96IiSow.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5d663ff0db9d1bb09fe3c7419f6b41091c5ee2cc86564dad2573a7db1ed8fd7d&req=fSAvHsp%2BlIldFb4f3HP0gHHwm2st6CboyEZjLiOhAqumYl7AVTrmNfN1F4wd%0AHIaiTffZ8CtuZfI%2BqQ%3D%3D%0A)

**Step 2**: Slide the toggle to **Enable**. You will see a pop-up window stating that you need to add CNAME records.

[![Create DKIM Keys button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908830988/d5a6fe877af4dd801ea6b9bf/zRxQn_kgZljao4Vf43QYX2a6F-kIQ4bM9g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=491be34252ec4476b5116b80bb050724f22279f8a881b87381f4f78b2c203afc&req=fSAvHsp%2BlIlXFb4f3HP0gHVkkldogkVvCdLVRZaUY0qdsyArUq1XqdtupCJT%0AnaZ6u35uwbKXvj5peA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908830988/d5a6fe877af4dd801ea6b9bf/zRxQn_kgZljao4Vf43QYX2a6F-kIQ4bM9g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=491be34252ec4476b5116b80bb050724f22279f8a881b87381f4f78b2c203afc&req=fSAvHsp%2BlIlXFb4f3HP0gHVkkldogkVvCdLVRZaUY0qdsyArUq1XqdtupCJT%0AnaZ6u35uwbKXvj5peA%3D%3D%0A)

**Step 3**: Copy the CNAMES shown in the pop up window.

[![Copy the DKIM records](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908830997/c03389f5a35ed5b7d3f8593b/1spd8FpVBWQ0okQe3EkCErCBhRufMa38nQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9ccedba24aa35e0262416c9ec872f11fc27a3a555be9314c37bb710d14260fb6&req=fSAvHsp%2BlIhYFb4f3HP0gD4pLH4iv6V03th3Z2yCmpNluI4iGOLQrJ%2Fvoj%2F9%0A8sMSRcexSDZgdavCxw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908830997/c03389f5a35ed5b7d3f8593b/1spd8FpVBWQ0okQe3EkCErCBhRufMa38nQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9ccedba24aa35e0262416c9ec872f11fc27a3a555be9314c37bb710d14260fb6&req=fSAvHsp%2BlIhYFb4f3HP0gD4pLH4iv6V03th3Z2yCmpNluI4iGOLQrJ%2Fvoj%2F9%0A8sMSRcexSDZgdavCxw%3D%3D%0A)

**Step 4**: Publish the copied CNAME records to your DNS service provider.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail, you will need to add the resulting 2 CNAME keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

**Step 5**: Return to DKIM page to enable DKIM.

[![Click to enable DKIM](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908831008/2235c7e3686a8ca1c38b5002/ohj1mUXAn3nomduWc4C-P7am3zdiyzsmhw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6c8ef3cdd895b515949feb1902be10be59ed33153f5d646a82b5b1d7f7a1134d&req=fSAvHsp%2FnYFXFb4f3HP0gAkJjPptc9cBRQpy%2BSqbbAt%2F5h8ydEjnfv58om0T%0Al8syHYMqoEG69aFRiw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908831008/2235c7e3686a8ca1c38b5002/ohj1mUXAn3nomduWc4C-P7am3zdiyzsmhw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6c8ef3cdd895b515949feb1902be10be59ed33153f5d646a82b5b1d7f7a1134d&req=fSAvHsp%2FnYFXFb4f3HP0gAkJjPptc9cBRQpy%2BSqbbAt%2F5h8ydEjnfv58om0T%0Al8syHYMqoEG69aFRiw%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to turn on DKIM in M365 for you domain, **[here](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/security/office-365-security/email-authentication-dkim-configure?view=o365-worldwide)
**.

Adding the Microsoft Office 365 DKIM keys in Valimail
=====================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Microsoft Office 365 emails
===================================================================

Once you establish that Microsoft Office 365 is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

**Note**: You may notice that M365 emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added M365 to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by M365 not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by making sure SPF alignment for your domain has been turn on in M365.

Enable SPF alignment in Microsoft Office 365 for your domain
============================================================

You can find out more details on how to make sure SPF alignment is enabled for your domain in Microsoft Office 365, **[here](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/security/office-365-security/set-up-spf-in-office-365-to-help-prevent-spoofing?view=o365-worldwide)
**.

* * *

# Acquia Cloud | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470994-acquia-cloud#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470994-acquia-cloud#h_6c23eaec41)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470994-acquia-cloud#h_abc2f586f5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470994-acquia-cloud#h_85cc20c957)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Acquia Cloud

Acquia Cloud
============

How to set up DMARC for Acquia Cloud in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470994-acquia-cloud#h_6c23eaec41)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470994-acquia-cloud#h_abc2f586f5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470994-acquia-cloud#h_85cc20c957)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM configuration process for **Acquia Cloud** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Amazon SES's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform.

Adding Acquia Cloud as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Acquia Cloud infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Acquia Cloud emails
============================================================

To configure and verify an email sending domain:

**1.** [Sign in to the Cloud Platform user interface](https://cloud.acquia.com/)
 and select the application for which you want to configure the domain.

**2.** In the navigation pane, click **Email**.

**3.** On the Platform Email page, on the Domain Verification tab, click **ADD DOMAIN**.

**4.** In the Add domain dialog box, in the **Domain to add** field, enter the domain name that you want to configure, and click **ADD DOMAIN**.

The Platform Email page displays the following message:

_The domain has been registered._

**5.** In the Email domain verification list, verify that the new domain shows the **Verification pending** status.

**6.** Click the expand arrow for the domain to view the type, status, and value of the DNS records.

The list of DNS records includes:

*   **DKIM**: Three entries of type CNAME
    
*   **TXT**: Two entries of type TXT
    
*   **SPF**: One entry of type TXT and one entry of type MX
    

At this step, the status of the entries is **Verification pending**.

**NOTE**: _The only action needed in Valimail will be to add the 3 CNAME DKIM keys and also to add the Acquia Cloud service in your Enabled Senders list._

**7\.** From the **VALUE** column of the list, copy all strings and add the CNAME DKIM keys to Valimail Enforce and the rest of the records in your DNS provider.

**8.** Wait until all the DNS records are verified.

Verification of the DKIM keys in Enforce is instant - the DNS records may take up to 72 hours.

**9.** On the Domain Verification tab, verify the status of the domain and do one of the following:

If the status is **OK**, associate the domain to the application.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Acquia Cloud **[here](https://docs.acquia.com/cloud-platform/manage/platform-email/platform-email-configuration/)
**.

Add an Acquia Cloud DKIM key in Valimail
========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Acquia Cloud emails
===========================================================

Once you establish that Acquia Cloud is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Mailchimp | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752153-mailchimp#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752153-mailchimp#h_ff0c8685a0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752153-mailchimp#h_3f68350688)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752153-mailchimp#h_6a967430e7)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Mailchimp

Mailchimp
=========

How to set up DMARC for Mailchimp in Valimail

Updated over 7 months ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752153-mailchimp#h_ff0c8685a0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752153-mailchimp#h_3f68350688)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752153-mailchimp#h_6a967430e7)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Mailchimp** and how they are managed in Valimail.

**Note**: Be advised that multiple users can use the same domain for sending DMARC authenticated messages via Mailchimp. Each User needs to access the account independently and enable the Authentication and Domain Verification.

Before you can setup DKIM for your domain in Mailchimp, you will need to verify your domain within the Mailchimp platform. Please see this article on [How to verify your domain in Mailchimp](https://mailchimp.com/help/verify-a-domain/)
.

After your email domain is verified, you will then need to enable DKIM for your domain in Mailchimp.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Mailchimp emails
=========================================================

**1.** Click the **Website** icon.

[![a hand pointing at a website](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483158/85d708d8933ebcfc397c3804/aLzUUGYV_RPOsGGMu2woCKSKLoeYzD8_zw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=30a526e845b38cea7d989faaf13683d173bcb87178170e8e435c3d90aacfd432&req=fSEuEsF9nIRXFb4f3HP0gGGxDECxkiOADqW8sWc7%2FqUki4mf0RJ%2FBCRhN2%2B6%0ATQVWjJbnJz1qjGDrIw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483158/85d708d8933ebcfc397c3804/aLzUUGYV_RPOsGGMu2woCKSKLoeYzD8_zw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=30a526e845b38cea7d989faaf13683d173bcb87178170e8e435c3d90aacfd432&req=fSEuEsF9nIRXFb4f3HP0gGGxDECxkiOADqW8sWc7%2FqUki4mf0RJ%2FBCRhN2%2B6%0ATQVWjJbnJz1qjGDrIw%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click **Domains**.

[![a hand cursor pointing to a screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483162/820bae97740d744ac67be0eb/UtDQpDSAv-0u5AeZAe6f-NkHCaPIf5Bx-A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=23770a273a97ad11b4cd51d30f432af7026935b8132c7d25188ae1c64e62622a&req=fSEuEsF9nIddFb4f3HP0gFoFQ0JDb3iEURYixl1ljsPwzbfUDlkuLWqTHv%2Fa%0AVccHHQhrcXELJto6gw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483162/820bae97740d744ac67be0eb/UtDQpDSAv-0u5AeZAe6f-NkHCaPIf5Bx-A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=23770a273a97ad11b4cd51d30f432af7026935b8132c7d25188ae1c64e62622a&req=fSEuEsF9nIddFb4f3HP0gFoFQ0JDb3iEURYixl1ljsPwzbfUDlkuLWqTHv%2Fa%0AVccHHQhrcXELJto6gw%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Click **Start Authentication** next to the verified email domain you want to work with.

[![a white rectangular object with black text](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483166/4b4b89af0f917f115787f358/Xmd4cR98BWb3h-YGX34qN54NT36FoxUBag.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d40a73fe67531a35b0a47b8acd9287647e5c094dbc35c803bf19eda0b333cb41&req=fSEuEsF9nIdZFb4f3HP0gBnv1IGzuMYfzfQq1j70GHxjKyyNbhLPyrC05Gur%0ALxV35ei3LEbAjofgyA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483166/4b4b89af0f917f115787f358/Xmd4cR98BWb3h-YGX34qN54NT36FoxUBag.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d40a73fe67531a35b0a47b8acd9287647e5c094dbc35c803bf19eda0b333cb41&req=fSEuEsF9nIdZFb4f3HP0gBnv1IGzuMYfzfQq1j70GHxjKyyNbhLPyrC05Gur%0ALxV35ei3LEbAjofgyA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Choose your domain provider from the dropdown and click **Next**. If your service or software is not listed, choose **Other**.

[![a screenshot of a email address box](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483168/24d593d2d064f20360993ae4/tqllIJR2vI2eR7WU3tSJqrDdlg7ihA1OMw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=56837b63b6e90d6f3600fcb6a492f1de1a96d701abfcc97c17196a81184eb819&req=fSEuEsF9nIdXFb4f3HP0gMZtkJb%2BnVLPsKXDD6xKeBi%2FjIafg8ieIKWF0SEH%0AMP%2FcNLSCwRIagf7mBg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483168/24d593d2d064f20360993ae4/tqllIJR2vI2eR7WU3tSJqrDdlg7ihA1OMw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=56837b63b6e90d6f3600fcb6a492f1de1a96d701abfcc97c17196a81184eb819&req=fSEuEsF9nIdXFb4f3HP0gMZtkJb%2BnVLPsKXDD6xKeBi%2FjIafg8ieIKWF0SEH%0AMP%2FcNLSCwRIagf7mBg%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** In a separate browser window or tab, navigate to your **DNS host** and find your domain's records.

**6.** After you find your domain's records, navigate back to Mailchimp and click **Next**.

[![a computer screen shot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483171/921c1c783046b26464bb1dfe/hGgfaHB4YkFQO6pWsU6XCb_kzelk8FZMxg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5bc8b85aaa383471a8184bae1d4e223ff2e9008c817e13f7cacc25de9ef18180&req=fSEuEsF9nIZeFb4f3HP0gP8FpelxNk93hGbuf9YEKKbbBcQzSUbOQ9rffPH4%0AwVuPK39oR%2FmY46eIKw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483171/921c1c783046b26464bb1dfe/hGgfaHB4YkFQO6pWsU6XCb_kzelk8FZMxg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5bc8b85aaa383471a8184bae1d4e223ff2e9008c817e13f7cacc25de9ef18180&req=fSEuEsF9nIZeFb4f3HP0gP8FpelxNk93hGbuf9YEKKbbBcQzSUbOQ9rffPH4%0AwVuPK39oR%2FmY46eIKw%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Follow the instructions for creating new CNAME records. These values will be customized for your domain and the service you are using. **Copy** the values for your new records and paste them into the appropriate fields for your domain.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483175/5591899d457784563f5bdcf9/39YxSq9qYLHTK0ZnAgvQmsSRCjlav4K8xQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=59fccf91f1c418a967fde74c70ce1d1dbb370cc931a2c9bc34020c605031df52&req=fSEuEsF9nIZaFb4f3HP0gD1BsOq9tiEPCRYSMonMZM2vucbpstYbDSv2D1Mv%0AfgyY87SmEziKR3yVUA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483175/5591899d457784563f5bdcf9/39YxSq9qYLHTK0ZnAgvQmsSRCjlav4K8xQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=59fccf91f1c418a967fde74c70ce1d1dbb370cc931a2c9bc34020c605031df52&req=fSEuEsF9nIZaFb4f3HP0gD1BsOq9tiEPCRYSMonMZM2vucbpstYbDSv2D1Mv%0AfgyY87SmEziKR3yVUA%3D%3D%0A)

**8.** Copy the values for your new records and **add the DKIM keys** in **Valimail Enforce.**

**9.** When you're done, click **Next**.

[![a computer screen shot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483178/ff89aacba2f37db16c37f36d/txWZtHpe_O936RvLwmKv0SBMiZuO6Q_L7Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2b8f915827fcd3bdd0bbc2359ac7518cabd2394973b03e68468feb67d0c10210&req=fSEuEsF9nIZXFb4f3HP0gGcZUH2a1zy1VDapl9e64RixlUTg8JdDougWFQYY%0AQmMqvQjUwPBZXJ%2Felg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483178/ff89aacba2f37db16c37f36d/txWZtHpe_O936RvLwmKv0SBMiZuO6Q_L7Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2b8f915827fcd3bdd0bbc2359ac7518cabd2394973b03e68468feb67d0c10210&req=fSEuEsF9nIZXFb4f3HP0gGcZUH2a1zy1VDapl9e64RixlUTg8JdDougWFQYY%0AQmMqvQjUwPBZXJ%2Felg%3D%3D%0A)

You can find these instructions on how to set up DKIM in Mailchimp for your domain **[here](https://mailchimp.com/help/set-up-email-domain-authentication/)
**.

Add the Mailchimp DKIM keys in Valimail
=======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

**Note**: If you add Mailchimp in your **Enabled Senders** list in Valimail Enforce, the k2 and k3 DKIM keys will be added in the DKIM managing section of your domain's **Configuration** page.

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Confirm Authentication
======================

Your email domain will be authenticated as soon as Mailchimp can confirm your records are updated and correct.

When authentication is successful, you'll see the **Authenticated** label next to the domain on the **Domains** page in your account.

[![a close-up of a red circle](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483181/a15e4655868e0bedd83ab41c/_8qvcK120KKrAXwiFfBiYAem4bv0j3HHnw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=95495b14e8c29fed34f16b0c34c41543c68fb57cc2cc0ca90957aa91ecb56f22&req=fSEuEsF9nIleFb4f3HP0gLt62VE9ZLyulTTO3S5pcaTEThZS9OngZa6Njt5%2F%0AE8QAnwfN7aaqACiXtw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919483181/a15e4655868e0bedd83ab41c/_8qvcK120KKrAXwiFfBiYAem4bv0j3HHnw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=95495b14e8c29fed34f16b0c34c41543c68fb57cc2cc0ca90957aa91ecb56f22&req=fSEuEsF9nIleFb4f3HP0gLt62VE9ZLyulTTO3S5pcaTEThZS9OngZa6Njt5%2F%0AE8QAnwfN7aaqACiXtw%3D%3D%0A)

Once your domain is authenticated in Mailchimp, the emails sent with MailChimp on behalf of your domain will start to be authenticated with DKIM.

* * *

# Klaviyo | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705574-klaviyo#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705574-klaviyo#h_38fa3cc64b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705574-klaviyo#h_f4b653f340)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705574-klaviyo#h_2929cb37cb)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Klaviyo

Klaviyo
=======

How to set up DMARC for Klaviyo in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705574-klaviyo#h_38fa3cc64b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705574-klaviyo#h_f4b653f340)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705574-klaviyo#h_2929cb37cb)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Klaviyo** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages SendGrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Klaviyo as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Klaviyo infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Klaviyo emails
=======================================================

Klaviyo requires 3 CNAME records for email authentication, and one TXT record for domain ownership verification.

Please note that, while getting setup is available to all users, only those with Owner, Admin, Manager, and Campaign Coordinator privileges can make changes.

**1.** Click on your company name in the top right corner of your account.

**2.** Select **Account**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803605/1974e174ac5af158d579c86b/kdbszCIM5vj5wUFkGbAmBbP40sRgtjjUVg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6c495d185f396d73e5a5006afc7b28811186d9da28dec321affe264d2154c586&req=fSAvHsl9m4FaFb4f3HP0gGTRCGq8YtlqFlKd6W6Ot%2FEjwjbG%2BHeeqx9e2t7g%0ADWzj6cQWSDAwN1GkdQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803605/1974e174ac5af158d579c86b/kdbszCIM5vj5wUFkGbAmBbP40sRgtjjUVg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6c495d185f396d73e5a5006afc7b28811186d9da28dec321affe264d2154c586&req=fSAvHsl9m4FaFb4f3HP0gGTRCGq8YtlqFlKd6W6Ot%2FEjwjbG%2BHeeqx9e2t7g%0ADWzj6cQWSDAwN1GkdQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Select the **Settings** dropdown.

**4.** Choose **Domains and Hosting** from the main tab.

**5.** Select **Get Started**.

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803611/e2cfc900308eb8ff5235a6b1/jMChkovsh4I7kcH8e-TD50WaA-cBHzqVOw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b476d89185e874637b27dba68e7cdb7c417ce3da40bf3bd7bc73b5ea710f29f8&req=fSAvHsl9m4BeFb4f3HP0gO1DhUqJwsUhM7zy2UoCyv9pVXy6wpr3FckFShtK%0Atf%2BDa4%2Bvv8tOywrL8Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803611/e2cfc900308eb8ff5235a6b1/jMChkovsh4I7kcH8e-TD50WaA-cBHzqVOw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b476d89185e874637b27dba68e7cdb7c417ce3da40bf3bd7bc73b5ea710f29f8&req=fSAvHsl9m4BeFb4f3HP0gO1DhUqJwsUhM7zy2UoCyv9pVXy6wpr3FckFShtK%0Atf%2BDa4%2Bvv8tOywrL8Q%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Input your root domain (e.g., helloworld.com)

**7.** Specify an arbitrary and unused subdomain (i.e., one that you do not currently have in use elsewhere in your marketing) under **Sending Domain** (e.g., "send")

**8.** Click **Continue**.

**9.** Hover over and click the text to copy the generated TXT and CNAME records to your clipboard.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803617/d60388435ed0c89bd4200d96/DVmO0q1MoYFkY7QRKeYibLdVyqBgRdbWhA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ed0a37ebe54d614d5c2aac2d53c6415588a95a6653a615b68e8b52b86d599aa5&req=fSAvHsl9m4BYFb4f3HP0gASirNlBxHmTLFGe64wcABwku6tEG%2FiphP%2B1L5NP%0AQavG750RfMLsNzt%2Fmg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803617/d60388435ed0c89bd4200d96/DVmO0q1MoYFkY7QRKeYibLdVyqBgRdbWhA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ed0a37ebe54d614d5c2aac2d53c6415588a95a6653a615b68e8b52b86d599aa5&req=fSAvHsl9m4BYFb4f3HP0gASirNlBxHmTLFGe64wcABwku6tEG%2FiphP%2B1L5NP%0AQavG750RfMLsNzt%2Fmg%3D%3D%0A)

In an example where the intended sending domain is send.helloworld.com, with “send” as the subdomain and “helloworld.com” as the root domain, the expected DNS records would be the following:

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Host** | **Value** | **Record Type** |
| send.helloworld.com | u161779.wl030.sendgrid.net | CNAME |
| kl.\_domainkey.helloworld.com | kl.domainkey.u161779.wl030.sendgrid.net | CNAME |
| kl2.\_domainkey.helloworld.com | kl.domainkey.u161779.wl030.sendgrid.net | CNAME |
| helloworld.com | klaviyo-site-verification=public\_API\_key | TXT |

If your brand's intended sending domain has two subdomains, such as send.mail.helloworld.com, “send” would be used for the subdomain and “mail.helloworld.com” for the root domain. The expected DNS records would be the following:

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Host** | **Value** | **Record Type** |
| send.mail.helloworld.com | u161779.wl030.sendgrid.net | CNAME |
| kl.\_domainkey.mail.helloworld.com | kl.domainkey.u161779.wl030.sendgrid.net | CNAME |
| kl2.\_domainkey.mail.helloworld.com | kl.domainkey.u161779.wl030.sendgrid.net | CNAME |
| helloworld.com | klaviyo-site-verification=public\_API\_key | TXT |

**IMPORTANT Note**: _The 1st CNAME record and the TXT record will need to be added directly in your DNS._

_The 2 CNAME DKIM keys will need to be published in **Valimail Enforce**._  
​

**10.** Publish all 4 records in the DNS and Valimail Enforce respectively.

**11.** After all 4 records are published in their respective areas, go back to the Klaviyo account and click **Continue** **to Verify Domain**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803624/5595682832f59ccba11259d1/gTFtDmPn3Nt8dU7s-Qpu2KkZYA4iHwY-Pw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=185ae78729fc3d3fa7a738c3b1a465c85f1e39e1f831e624b322636464a903d7&req=fSAvHsl9m4NbFb4f3HP0gMkSpiyD3FpFc%2Fzkjngv2Vb64KywYYudFvkIOttn%0Aodfxu7MkneeXWSGEUg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803624/5595682832f59ccba11259d1/gTFtDmPn3Nt8dU7s-Qpu2KkZYA4iHwY-Pw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=185ae78729fc3d3fa7a738c3b1a465c85f1e39e1f831e624b322636464a903d7&req=fSAvHsl9m4NbFb4f3HP0gMkSpiyD3FpFc%2Fzkjngv2Vb64KywYYudFvkIOttn%0Aodfxu7MkneeXWSGEUg%3D%3D%0A)

**12.** Review any message that appears. You will see one of the following messages:

*   If a campaign is conflicting, you will see a notification that the deliverability may be impacted. To avoid any conflicting errors, a best practice is to make sure there are no campaigns actively sending or scheduled to go out soon. It is also best practice (but is not required) to pause flows and campaigns until after you apply and test your changes.
    
*   If your records are valid, you will see a success message. This success message may indicate that you need to warm your infrastructure again. Note that this only applies to brand new Klaviyo accounts or newly registered domains (within the last 30 days). If you are an existing account who has at least a 30-day sending history with Klaviyo, you do not have to re-warm.
    
*   If your records are not valid, you will see an error indicating what has not been set up correctly.
    

**13.** Select **Apply Domain**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803630/0feff8c0879732a269ed53c3/Xp4LKMFROnMktrBgvmP-Eo9IRTpMD7rQfA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a72c087d0119fbe692e788d42f02313d12564d941ecd7540a3b748257b14593e&req=fSAvHsl9m4JfFb4f3HP0gCOPBgDTx%2B68cBtD7h%2F3WsJ56Z7exzJ6cJOjsHmv%0AT31jyJDU6YhCLQVYRw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908803630/0feff8c0879732a269ed53c3/Xp4LKMFROnMktrBgvmP-Eo9IRTpMD7rQfA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a72c087d0119fbe692e788d42f02313d12564d941ecd7540a3b748257b14593e&req=fSAvHsl9m4JfFb4f3HP0gCOPBgDTx%2B68cBtD7h%2F3WsJ56Z7exzJ6cJOjsHmv%0AT31jyJDU6YhCLQVYRw%3D%3D%0A)

**14.** Check the box indicating that you understand that you must warm your infrastructure by changing your sending behavior. _If you are an existing Klaviyo account who has a 30 day sending history, you do not need to warm your infrastructure again to move to a dedicated sending domain._

**15.** Click **Apply Domain**.

Klaviyo will now apply your dedicated sending domain to your account, and produce a success message when completed.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM in Klaviyo **[here](https://help.klaviyo.com/hc/en-us/articles/115000357752-How-to-Set-Up-a-Dedicated-Sending-Domain-)
**.

Add a Klaviyo DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Klaviyo emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Klaviyo is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Related Articles

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752173-sophos-email-security#h_50f17655fb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752173-sophos-email-security#h_eece0e9f34)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Sophos Email Security

Sophos Email Security
=====================

How to set up DMARC for Sophos Email Security in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752173-sophos-email-security#h_83f85e15c6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752173-sophos-email-security#h_50f17655fb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752173-sophos-email-security#h_eece0e9f34)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Sophos Email Security** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Sophos Email Security emails
=====================================================================

**1.** Go to **Global Settings** -> **Domains settings/status** and click on the domain you want to add a DKIM key to.

**2.** Click **Add key**.

**3.** Copy the DKIM information that is generated automatically and use it to create a DNS TXT record for your domain.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

**4.** Once your DNS TXT record has been published, click on **Test record** to check that your DNS TXT record matches the information in Sophos Email.

**5.** Activate the DKIM key then click **Save**.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Sophos Email Security, **[here](https://docs.sophos.com/central/customer/help/en-us/ManageYourProducts/GlobalSettings/EmailDomains/DkimKeys/index.html#outbound-dkim-signing)
**.

Add a Sophos Email Security DKIM key in Enforce
===============================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Sophos Email Security emails
====================================================================

Once you establish that Sophos Email Security is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Related Articles

[Cisco Cloud Email Security](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752258-cisco-cloud-email-security)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

SparkPost

SparkPost
=========

How to set up DMARC for Sparkpost in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705813-sparkpost#h_65ac6c2ea9)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705813-sparkpost#h_0435aba20a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705813-sparkpost#h_cb63a4ff0f)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **SparkPost** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your SparkPost emails
=========================================================

**1.** If you didn't add a sending domain during your sign up process, you can **[add one now in the Sparkpost app](https://app.sparkpost.com/domains/create)
**. If you already added a domain, visit the **[Domains](https://app.sparkpost.com/domains/list)
** page to find your existing domain and click on the domain to complete the domain set up.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810804/6fada07aab160e6585db3ec0/OmevjIy8coQFVNXw6xawI5iwqgjI5it7-A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0be9a0e12dd6dd51240040f21402e953f26412c1df348590643d65f3c2dcbcdf&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYFbFb4f3HP0gK4b7cfUCXUNXjKZNINoPOM6wA1s%2B65NjO1I8v0J%0AdcflcSxyY8r0FnSwjg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810804/6fada07aab160e6585db3ec0/OmevjIy8coQFVNXw6xawI5iwqgjI5it7-A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0be9a0e12dd6dd51240040f21402e953f26412c1df348590643d65f3c2dcbcdf&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYFbFb4f3HP0gK4b7cfUCXUNXjKZNINoPOM6wA1s%2B65NjO1I8v0J%0AdcflcSxyY8r0FnSwjg%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Please copy the DKIM key records from the **DNS Verification** section and add the key to Valimail Enforce.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810813/9f5639b697308d24e11b188b/BWuJv7EJNedKsMfragBtqrkTmuO0_DG9ig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e9210a20ddc8e6531f23818d54f87247b48375c898099d94a7b5a4ab045de999&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYBcFb4f3HP0gKh55LEKBV4i3LDWqz39ASStA5mebp%2FQ1VfKaNZK%0ACR9YHP6DF69Y1T8t6w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810813/9f5639b697308d24e11b188b/BWuJv7EJNedKsMfragBtqrkTmuO0_DG9ig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e9210a20ddc8e6531f23818d54f87247b48375c898099d94a7b5a4ab045de999&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYBcFb4f3HP0gKh55LEKBV4i3LDWqz39ASStA5mebp%2FQ1VfKaNZK%0ACR9YHP6DF69Y1T8t6w%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** If you are using a subdomain, we recommend you also add the CNAME record specified under the **Bounce** section, directly in your DNS. That CNAME record can only be managed from your DNS.

If the domain you are setting up DKIM in Sparkpost on is not a subdomain (mail.example.com), you can disregard this step.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810824/ec07c3e5e51f8da0ad481c8d/9R1x3ygShcvqvnazaeSEe6G3afeInqLCmQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=59685f73645de290a948a1e205295d53524e70103db8b5ac5009bfdd6fb586be&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYNbFb4f3HP0gDsx461R1S4i19i8pktoDKZmjB8PSgiy3D9JL4of%0ASezXb2X%2BpkLeYLQ1CQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810824/ec07c3e5e51f8da0ad481c8d/9R1x3ygShcvqvnazaeSEe6G3afeInqLCmQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=59685f73645de290a948a1e205295d53524e70103db8b5ac5009bfdd6fb586be&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYNbFb4f3HP0gDsx461R1S4i19i8pktoDKZmjB8PSgiy3D9JL4of%0ASezXb2X%2BpkLeYLQ1CQ%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** After you add the DKIM key in Valimail Enforce, check the box that says **The TXT record has been added to the DNS provider**. Then click on **Verify Domain**.

If you are using a subdomain and you added the CNAME record to your DNS, do the same for that record too.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810832/b7031974e4d826186ca6929b/WU7hNGOKxV1v7pseBQzapO_3bGfmypMgEg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7439d42b7f7739bb9c8d4c91c2e5448639a2dd72dea5ed4229b57438795e9ada&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYJdFb4f3HP0gGsXLnfAJeLwtNHzQtEbgic8L7u395S4tXidFRiF%0ABz9BF0YJnaEJjSJehg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810832/b7031974e4d826186ca6929b/WU7hNGOKxV1v7pseBQzapO_3bGfmypMgEg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7439d42b7f7739bb9c8d4c91c2e5448639a2dd72dea5ed4229b57438795e9ada&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYJdFb4f3HP0gGsXLnfAJeLwtNHzQtEbgic8L7u395S4tXidFRiF%0ABz9BF0YJnaEJjSJehg%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Your domain's status will change to **Pending**. This means the domain is in the final review process which will verify that the domain meets Sparkpost's best practice requirements. This usually takes just a few minutes, but can sometimes take up to an hour. Once the review is complete, the domain will be ready to send.

[![a screenshot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810841/9cb49bb757636a303b6fb9b6/oNLMYG-2rOdVfrIqPUl1bSdDdHcv1xQHqw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2025b60e95b95b69f055a8049f1e5f8d0c4f197b824bae2b0b3e4027f8932e87&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYVeFb4f3HP0gMk3gqOgEr%2BVOWl3h%2Bzl0rtZaYiQU6wneKWGFnWx%0AmVlr993%2BHswR4Eolkw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908810841/9cb49bb757636a303b6fb9b6/oNLMYG-2rOdVfrIqPUl1bSdDdHcv1xQHqw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2025b60e95b95b69f055a8049f1e5f8d0c4f197b824bae2b0b3e4027f8932e87&req=fSAvHsh%2BlYVeFb4f3HP0gMk3gqOgEr%2BVOWl3h%2Bzl0rtZaYiQU6wneKWGFnWx%0AmVlr993%2BHswR4Eolkw%3D%3D%0A)

You can find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Sparkpost **[here](https://support.sparkpost.com/docs/getting-started/getting-started-sparkpost#sending-domain-step-1-creating-the-domain)
**.

Add a SparkPost DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Sparkpost emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Sparkpost is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Centercode

Centercode
==========

How to set up DMARC for Centercode in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752252-centercode#h_ec51b779ae)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752252-centercode#h_033148b088)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752252-centercode#h_2a5c9f1153)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Centercode** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Centercode emails
==========================================================

In order to set up DKIM for your domain in Centercode , please reach out to Centercode support through the live chat widget located within your Centercode implementation or email **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#8de5e8e1fdcdeee8e3f9e8ffeee2e9e8a3eee2e0)
**. Please include your centercode URL in the request (e.g. yoursite.centercode.com).

Centercode IT will return a public key meant for your IT and your IT must create a DKIM record in the format of:

"**cc.\_domainkey.<your company's domain>.com**"

"**v=DKIM1;k=rsa; p="<the public key provided to you**\>"

You will then need to add that TXT DKIM key in your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Centercode, **[here](https://learn.centercode.com/docs/sending-email-from-your-domain-spfdmarcdkim-setup)
**.

Add a Centercode DKIM key in Enforce
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Centercode emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Centercode is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

CakeMail

CakeMail
========

How to set up DMARC for CakeMail in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752225-cakemail#h_7ed8d00068)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752225-cakemail#h_9de8e75c82)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752225-cakemail#h_86eade09cd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752225-cakemail#h_738390a17a)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)DKIM  
(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **CakeMail** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your CakeMail emails
========================================================

**How to Brand your sender domain**
-----------------------------------

Please send these instructions to your IT support team or your web hosting company.

Branding (please change \*domain\* by your domain):

_link.domain.com CNAME md02.comforward.domain.com CNAME forward.md02.combounce.domain.com MX mail.md02.com (priority 10)bounce.domain.com TXT v=spf1 include:md02.com -all_

**Note**: The records above are just an example.

After the records are published in your DNS, please contact [CakeMail support](https://www.cakemail.com/contact-us/)
 so they can complete the verification.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the CNAME DKIM key/s on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add the rest of the records in your DNS.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for CakeMail, **[here](https://support.cakemail.com/hc/en-us/articles/1260804686649-Branding-and-authenticating-your-sender-domain)
**.

Add a CakeMail DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your CakeMail emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that CakeMail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

CampusLogic StudentForms

CampusLogic StudentForms
========================

How to set up DMARC for CampusLogic StudentForms in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752233-campuslogic-studentforms#h_e5dcc4338a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752233-campuslogic-studentforms#h_2b9ba5ae56)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752233-campuslogic-studentforms#h_fef297be92)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **CampusLogic StudentForms** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **CampusLogic StudentForms** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your CampusLogic StudentForms emails
========================================================================

CampusLogic will need to configure DKIM signing specifically for the institution through our mail vendor (SendGrid) to cause DMARC to pass. Through SendGrid, we will setup 3 CNAME records (6 in total - 3 for sandbox and 3 for production) that you will need to host in your DNS:

One CNAME to point to the mail sending server

Two other CNAMEs used for DKIM keys that would be automatically rotated

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and the 3rd CNAME record in your DNS.

To get the process started, you can open up a ServiceNow case or email **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#54272124243b262014373539242127383b333d377a373b39)
**.

You can also find more on how to set up DKIM and SPF for CampusLogic StudentForms, **[here](https://campuslogicinc.freshdesk.com/support/solutions/articles/5000566195-proper-configuration-for-email-delivery)
**.

Add a CampusLogic StudentForms DKIM key in Valimail
===================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your CampusLogic StudentForms emails
=======================================================================

Once you establish that CampusLogic StudentForms is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705889-marketo#h_8cff9af1d9)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705889-marketo#h_c596e716de)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Marketo

Marketo
=======

How to set up DMARC for Marketo in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705889-marketo#h_751629b8fc)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705889-marketo#h_8cff9af1d9)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8705889-marketo#h_c596e716de)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Marketo** and how are they managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Marketo emails
=======================================================

**1.** Open your Marketo account as an administrator.

**2.** Go to the **Admin** section.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847685/4597c49a579c29299dd98c3e/g52i702rwU0gjS-OEDU4D19jh4C_QAMapA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d9f682c6c8c2457a619550cffec2771ab23677543e7cfcf131d32f0a26ffec2c&req=fSAvHs15m4laFb4f3HP0gL9wXS9ySj56C%2B5Vf6fXNXACKqwMxcXqy5Y6sPfo%0AJHh12G6ZY8uSqXpimQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847685/4597c49a579c29299dd98c3e/g52i702rwU0gjS-OEDU4D19jh4C_QAMapA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d9f682c6c8c2457a619550cffec2771ab23677543e7cfcf131d32f0a26ffec2c&req=fSAvHs15m4laFb4f3HP0gL9wXS9ySj56C%2B5Vf6fXNXACKqwMxcXqy5Y6sPfo%0AJHh12G6ZY8uSqXpimQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Click **Email**, then the **DKIM tab**, and finally **Add Domain**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847689/1bcac54c0db060d19259ac80/LrVfDxZ5jqoYp3_JGyHpN-g_Jpx_eY6wnA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ccf190f362291684cdfe4a78b051c0ce0138eb35c7189972eda745dee0f66065&req=fSAvHs15m4lWFb4f3HP0gKGh7mpVZYLwLqO0axWDAxaX865eyBJy1A2QTUV0%0AIkgGRuV4vpEoKMOQFg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847689/1bcac54c0db060d19259ac80/LrVfDxZ5jqoYp3_JGyHpN-g_Jpx_eY6wnA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ccf190f362291684cdfe4a78b051c0ce0138eb35c7189972eda745dee0f66065&req=fSAvHs15m4lWFb4f3HP0gKGh7mpVZYLwLqO0axWDAxaX865eyBJy1A2QTUV0%0AIkgGRuV4vpEoKMOQFg%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Enter the domain you will be using in Marketo emails as the From Address and click **Add**.

[![a screen shot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847695/f4be57e802e9aa36fd439dea/PoAAGQBVe-HZ1Yg1D3qfjVmMXbowH-uunw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=814099e38671a3e4259af6aa58c3f87e8f6779ba705abc87a97de9a97c643620&req=fSAvHs15m4haFb4f3HP0gFt9CL1prcrkTjqXhhFxPCjgQv57p9eeEtTzF4mq%0ARfRPJ6aGlTVf0HBkWw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847695/f4be57e802e9aa36fd439dea/PoAAGQBVe-HZ1Yg1D3qfjVmMXbowH-uunw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=814099e38671a3e4259af6aa58c3f87e8f6779ba705abc87a97de9a97c643620&req=fSAvHs15m4haFb4f3HP0gFt9CL1prcrkTjqXhhFxPCjgQv57p9eeEtTzF4mq%0ARfRPJ6aGlTVf0HBkWw%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Copy the **Host Record** and **TXT Value** and add the DKIM key in Valimail Enforce.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847702/1e7fb2623ecaab17be8e8855/z7mnWjL3TP00XZT-Nwmw7-KNpYfPCS22gQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ad019f3c9713eb565b9a6e0348726874d473dc5ebd17adfc9b7212a41b51b1c7&req=fSAvHs15moFdFb4f3HP0gEQS5jdAFgoY44iIfIEGerqkS4x5ZLOCaacaoBUm%0Arq%2Fn11tJ%2BRE3V7K6nA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847702/1e7fb2623ecaab17be8e8855/z7mnWjL3TP00XZT-Nwmw7-KNpYfPCS22gQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ad019f3c9713eb565b9a6e0348726874d473dc5ebd17adfc9b7212a41b51b1c7&req=fSAvHs15moFdFb4f3HP0gEQS5jdAFgoY44iIfIEGerqkS4x5ZLOCaacaoBUm%0Arq%2Fn11tJ%2BRE3V7K6nA%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Once the DKIM key is added in Valimail Enforce, come back to Marketo, select your domain, and click **Check DNS**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847706/9079b55c8f198a0eaf82f8cc/ksEMT-hJaez3PGcZIVdoeorH1czWU4NHkQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a2a043459ab161bc243a362896725c77d940d4a2f1df33fe68ddf3f645713f0c&req=fSAvHs15moFZFb4f3HP0gHJB86wHHRBmGr%2FQppxoq%2FVhbI42bfiy6mD5Xcux%0Apep6ezD6Etbqq%2BWoVA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/908847706/9079b55c8f198a0eaf82f8cc/ksEMT-hJaez3PGcZIVdoeorH1czWU4NHkQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a2a043459ab161bc243a362896725c77d940d4a2f1df33fe68ddf3f645713f0c&req=fSAvHs15moFZFb4f3HP0gHJB86wHHRBmGr%2FQppxoq%2FVhbI42bfiy6mD5Xcux%0Apep6ezD6Etbqq%2BWoVA%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Once the DKIM key is validated, you will be ready to send authentication emails on behalf of your domain, using Marketo.

**Note**: The Marketo DNS check may not like the Valimail SPF Macro and display a warning. While Marketo will send in alignment, you may need to contact Marketo Support to have them override the SPF validation script in their UI. The SPF response will work correctly and you can verify this by following the instructions found in this article: [Valimail SPF Records](https://support.valimail.com/support/solutions/articles/48001168380-valimail-spf-records)
.

You can find the instructions on how to turn on DKIM in Marketo **[here](https://experienceleague.adobe.com/docs/marketo/using/product-docs/email-marketing/deliverability/set-up-a-custom-dkim-signature.html)
**.  
​  
​

Add a Marketo DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Marketo emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Marketo is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Campaigner

Campaigner
==========

How to set up DMARC for Campaigner in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752228-campaigner#h_3be86a7c35)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752228-campaigner#h_e9717670be)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752228-campaigner#h_c56e61e30c)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Campaigner** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Campaigner emails
==========================================================

To enable DKIM for Campaigner, add a CNAME record to your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce:

Selector: **em**

Value: **dkim.emailcampaigns.net**

You can then check to see if the DKIM key has been published, using a tool like [Google Admin Toolbox Dig](https://toolbox.googleapps.com/apps/dig/)
.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Campaigner, **[here](https://knowledge.campaigner.com/article/1784-dkim-spf-and-dmarc)
**.

Add a Campaigner DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Campaigner emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Campaigner is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *
