Caspio

Caspio
======

How to set up DMARC for Caspio in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752239-caspio#h_725ed67cee)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752239-caspio#h_46cece325f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752239-caspio#h_8f71fe44e9)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Caspio** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

**Configuring DKIM authentication for your Caspio emails**
----------------------------------------------------------

#### **Configuring and verifying an email address**

**1.** Select **Account** -> **Account settings** from the top menu bar of your Caspio account.

**2.** Select the **Email** tab to view the list of all email addresses and domains used in your apps.

**3.** On the list of Email addresses and domains, select **Add email address**.

[![account settings page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919501631/394f1897eca730e2f0e6d8ea/WBnbt97J0P9nLDDPIOQm0arnAirpSjqROw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1383384a296b14b7967f7a7fdc0b66364bdab6a721da37170867314a4c844598&req=fSEuE8l%2Fm4JeFb4f3HP0gISEgm7GRFZoeWmGdgi%2BuMUe6ErKW8rQXpblqqr3%0AZutJzUuY1wioeb81KQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919501631/394f1897eca730e2f0e6d8ea/WBnbt97J0P9nLDDPIOQm0arnAirpSjqROw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1383384a296b14b7967f7a7fdc0b66364bdab6a721da37170867314a4c844598&req=fSEuE8l%2Fm4JeFb4f3HP0gISEgm7GRFZoeWmGdgi%2BuMUe6ErKW8rQXpblqqr3%0AZutJzUuY1wioeb81KQ%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Enter an email address and optionally a display name. Click **Send verification code**.

**5.** Enter the verification code sent to your email address and click **Verify email address.**

**6.** After verifying your email address, you can proceed to verifying domain to improve email deliverability.

#### **Verifying domain**

Before verifying a domain, you must verify an email address. To learn more, see Configure and verify email.

**1.** On the list of Email addresses and domains, hover over a domain and select **Verify domain**.

[![dkim records verify page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919501633/46caa6c7df43a917deb58315/mX2fPDBlGvK2jTFoRRAnx-IFt8oHcnfOUQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dc163b1c65a2a22660b821ea3d2d7acc8901d90704e08878d03c4c8ffbd8e0ba&req=fSEuE8l%2Fm4JcFb4f3HP0gHMc9tUysuqj8x2TZr%2F7uGx%2B7o8coFQZrjMTfT4P%0AVbGe4jPtLb2BRbFgLg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919501633/46caa6c7df43a917deb58315/mX2fPDBlGvK2jTFoRRAnx-IFt8oHcnfOUQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dc163b1c65a2a22660b821ea3d2d7acc8901d90704e08878d03c4c8ffbd8e0ba&req=fSEuE8l%2Fm4JcFb4f3HP0gHMc9tUysuqj8x2TZr%2F7uGx%2B7o8coFQZrjMTfT4P%0AVbGe4jPtLb2BRbFgLg%3D%3D%0A)

**2\.** Add TXT and CNAME records to your domain’s DNS settings.

*   Copy and paste the TXT and CNAME entries into the DNS records of your email address domain.
    
*   Select **Verify** on the domain verification dialog window.
    
*   Wait until DNS settings are propagated to other DNS.
    

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and the 3rd CNAME or any other record, will go in your DNS.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Caspio, **[here](https://howto.caspio.com/notifications/email-notifications/verifying-email-and-domain/)
**.

**Add a Caspio DKIM key in Valimail**
-------------------------------------

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

**Configuring SPF authentication for your Caspio emails**
---------------------------------------------------------

Once you establish that Caspio is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Combell

Combell
=======

How to set up DMARC for Combell in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754160-combell#h_1e2b6da66d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754160-combell#h_57c4af94ad)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754160-combell#h_620131f37d)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Combell** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Combell emails
=======================================================

In order to set up DKIM for your domain for the Combell emails, you need to create a DKIM key using a DKIM record generator tool.

Once you create the new DKIM key, you will need to add it on your domain's Configuration page in the Valimail platform.

For any help regarding the SPF and DKIM setup, you can contact the [Combell support team](https://www.combell.com/en/support)
.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up SPF for Combell, **[here](https://www.combell.com/en/help/kb/create-spf-record-to-keep-your-e-mails-out-of-the-spam-filter/)
**.

Add a Combell DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Combell emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Combell is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752258-cisco-cloud-email-security#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Cisco Cloud Email Security

Cisco Cloud Email Security
==========================

How to set up DMARC for Cisco Cloud Email Security in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752258-cisco-cloud-email-security#h_503def3ca8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752258-cisco-cloud-email-security#h_d4c1a7e2db)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752258-cisco-cloud-email-security#h_8e8d6aa9d6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752258-cisco-cloud-email-security#h_ea1f78dd79)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752258-cisco-cloud-email-security#h_f446248fad)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for Cisco Cloud Email Security and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Cisco Cloud Email Security emails
==========================================================================

**Create a DKIM signing key**
-----------------------------

**1.** Login to Cisco Cloud Email Security console and go to **Mail Policies** -> **Signing Keys** and select **Add Key...**

**2.** Name the DKIM key and either generate a new private key or paste in an existing one. (Best practice is to select the 2048 option for key encryption).

**3.** Commit the changes.

**Generate a new DKIM signing profile**
---------------------------------------

**4.** Go to **Mail Policies** -> **Signing Profiles** and click **Add Profile...**

a. Give the profile a descriptive name in the field **Profile Name**.

b. Enter your domain in the field **Domain Name**.

c. Enter a new selector string into the field **Selector**.

d. Select the DKIM signing key created in the previous section in the field **Signing Key**.

e. Click **Submit**.

**Note**: The selector is an arbitrary string that is used to allow multiple DKIM DNS records for a given domain.

**5.** From here, click **Generate** in the column **DNS Text Record** for the signing profile you just created.

**6.** Commit the changes.

**7.** Now you will need to add the generated DKIM key in **Valimail Enforce**. (In Enforce you will only be adding the DKIM selector and the exact TXT value of the key - like the bolded italics ones below).

_selector2.\_domainkey.example.com. IN TXT "v=DKIM1; ;"_

**8.** Go to **Mail Policies** -> **Signing Profiles**.

**9.** Under the column **Test Profile**, click **Test** for the new DKIM signing profile.

**Turn DKIM signing on**

**10.** After the test is confirmed successful, you will need to turn DKIM signing on. Go to **Mail Policies** -> **Mail Flow Policies**.

**11.** Go to each mail flow policy that has the **Connection Behavior** of **Relay** and turn **Domain Key/DKIM Signing** to **On**.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM for Cisco Cloud Email Security **[here](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/support/docs/security/email-security-appliance/213939-esa-configure-dkim-signing.html#anc3)
**.

Add a Cisco Cloud Email Security DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Cisco Cloud Email Security emails
=========================================================================

Once you establish that Cisco Cloud Email Security is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752261-cision-pr#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752261-cision-pr#h_45a23f5a56)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752261-cision-pr#h_d8ee2efcbe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752261-cision-pr#h_d78834f8ee)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Cision PR

Cision PR
=========

How to set up DMARC for Cision PR in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752261-cision-pr#h_45a23f5a56)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752261-cision-pr#h_d8ee2efcbe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752261-cision-pr#h_d78834f8ee)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM configuration process for **Cision PR** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages SendGrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Cision PR as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Cision PR infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Cision PR emails
=========================================================

Cision PR uses a dedicated subdomain which will be delegated to Cision PR servers. Cision PR will then publish an SPF record on that subdomain. The subdomain information will be provided in the steps below.

**1.** Activating DKIM signing for Cision PR can be done after contacting Cision PR's Support team to let them know that you would like to enable this feature. Contact them via their Support Email: **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#2f5c5a5f5f405d5b6f4c465c464041014c4042)
** or Support Phone #: **855-204-3479**. Cision PR support will give you the name of the subdomain to use, a CNAME record to put in your DNS and two DKIM key records to add to Valimail Enforce.

**2.** Add the DKIM keys in **Valimail Enforce** for Cision PR.

**3.** After adding the CNAME records in Valimail Enforce, and adding the subdomain CNAME record to your own DNS (example: **em7475.yourdomain.com**), you can validate the setup from the Cision PR Admin Console, or you can reach out to CisionPR Support to have them verify the configuration.

**Note**: This specific sub-domain will not use the Valimail Instant SPF response. The sub-domain will only use the SPF record generated via the Cision PR UI. This will be a CNAME record which you need to add to your DNS for validation purposes.

Additionally, this CNAME record will point that subdomain to Cision PR, which will then publish an SPF record for it.

Add a Cision PR DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Cision PR emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Cision PR is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754166-concep-send#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754166-concep-send#h_78318832a1)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Concep SEND

Concep SEND
===========

How to set up DMARC for Concep SEND in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754166-concep-send#h_1cc0d9c37a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754166-concep-send#h_943a132561)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754166-concep-send#h_78318832a1)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Concep SEND** and how they are managed in Valimail.

DKIM and SPF configuration for Concep SEND is done through a dedicated subdomain, like **news.yourcompany.com** and thus the configuration requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Concep SEND as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Concep SEND infrastructure and typically the DKIM key will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Concep SEND emails
===========================================================

*   1\. Contact **[Concep SEND Support](https://support.concep.com/hc/en-us/articles/support@concep.com)
    ** and ask to have DKIM applied to the domain you use to send from in Concep SEND (e.g. emailcc.com). If you do not have a **[sub-domain or corporate domain](https://support.concep.com/hc/en-us/articles/360022313973)
    ** setup with us, you'll need to do that first.
    

*   You'll receive a DKIM key from us, which you will need to forward onto your IT team and they'll need to apply it to the TXT record of your sending domain.
    

*   Once your IT team have inserted the key, let Support know and we'll confirm when it's applied and when you can send.
    

*   When you submit your ticket, the Support team will work with IT to generate a DKIM key which your IT team will need to add into the TXT record on your sending domain.
    

*   Once your IT team have confirmed the DKIM key has been added and propagated, our team will confirm and switch over your accounts to our dedicated mail server for DKIM's.
    

*   Lastly, we'll send out a test email from one of your accounts and make sure the email header is reading **dkim=pass**.
    

After that, you're all ready and set to send out and be assured your emails are verified as non-spam to the recipient mail server.

You can find the instructions to set up DMARC for Concep SEND **[here](https://support.concep.com/hc/en-us/articles/360004919514-Signing-with-DKIM)
**.

Add a Concep SEND DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Concep SEND emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that Concep SEND is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754156-clickdimensions#h_058896cb82)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754156-clickdimensions#h_e7c5c5c389)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

ClickDimensions

ClickDimensions
===============

How to set up DMARC for ClickDimensions in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754156-clickdimensions#h_576ec89386)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754156-clickdimensions#h_058896cb82)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754156-clickdimensions#h_e7c5c5c389)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **ClickDimensions** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your ClickDimensions emails
===============================================================

**1.** To set up DKIM for your domain in ClickDimenstions, you will first need to navigate to the ClickDimensions **Settings** page and select the **Email Sending Domains** option:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920090742/f440f8ac3d74fc569d549f7f/xWSfPCPgJar5K--g-hsxG1e5u1WVhDTi_Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d4a697391dd76c522e36f67f917aaf4d4675c109dbb867d030148967a148b699&req=fSInFsB%2BmoVdFb4f3HP0gKOFW9VocJaU5EiLFT8BbET5YumbgO%2FJ9WE7Qaee%0AUFpxOxJzZMbf5vObrA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920090742/f440f8ac3d74fc569d549f7f/xWSfPCPgJar5K--g-hsxG1e5u1WVhDTi_Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d4a697391dd76c522e36f67f917aaf4d4675c109dbb867d030148967a148b699&req=fSInFsB%2BmoVdFb4f3HP0gKOFW9VocJaU5EiLFT8BbET5YumbgO%2FJ9WE7Qaee%0AUFpxOxJzZMbf5vObrA%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** This will open the Email Sending Domains area where you can add, edit, and delete your email domains as well as check on their status:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920090752/4101de426913817d78a54bf4/jX4mF98n9-uXMeXcR5NdDR89FFl3qhVRqQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dce53edbdcb70da3cb02cc14f4738293e53b3ffee9ba0b71d1c3e2577231dfc0&req=fSInFsB%2BmoRdFb4f3HP0gOztBKu3Nv6xbocyokO3x2uxpOqRUmXqAkiOnMLS%0AxP8CvmeJKFs2pvPlPA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920090752/4101de426913817d78a54bf4/jX4mF98n9-uXMeXcR5NdDR89FFl3qhVRqQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dce53edbdcb70da3cb02cc14f4738293e53b3ffee9ba0b71d1c3e2577231dfc0&req=fSInFsB%2BmoRdFb4f3HP0gOztBKu3Nv6xbocyokO3x2uxpOqRUmXqAkiOnMLS%0AxP8CvmeJKFs2pvPlPA%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** If you create a new Email Domain, then you will see the following screen:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920090755/d0ea9fa879ed5d80553f21b2/iCd_MJEWeI3vDKPRTrnjOEfmPfQ1OZKHoA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2f8ba16c16bab426b0704740179195545feb3ff8b6eba16d89e1cbe2b49a1b7d&req=fSInFsB%2BmoRaFb4f3HP0gOWcpi7gk1LDBbCF04yedoPthnGChIkd25SJ1BUX%0AzFIIsByraMmH1dSstQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920090755/d0ea9fa879ed5d80553f21b2/iCd_MJEWeI3vDKPRTrnjOEfmPfQ1OZKHoA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2f8ba16c16bab426b0704740179195545feb3ff8b6eba16d89e1cbe2b49a1b7d&req=fSInFsB%2BmoRaFb4f3HP0gOWcpi7gk1LDBbCF04yedoPthnGChIkd25SJ1BUX%0AzFIIsByraMmH1dSstQ%3D%3D%0A)

*   **Domain**: This is the main domain that you would like to send from. For example: yourcompany.com.
    
*   **DKIM Sub Domain**: This is sub domain that you would like to use when sending. Please note that this is only the sub-part of the domain. For instance, if your full sub-domain was "email.yourcompany.com", then you would only enter "email" into this field.
    
*   **Email CNAME**: This is an optional field that will allow you to set a CNAME for this domain. For more details about setting up Email CNAMEs, please see our article here.
    
*   **Contact Email**: This is the email address that we will contact if there are issues when authenticating the domain.
    

**4.** After entering these details and saving, the window will expand to show the DNS records that will need to be setup in your Domain's DNS zone settings. After setting up the DNS records for the domain, the Export to Text File will allow you to export your DNS records for that domain to a .text file to double check them or share them within your organization.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for this domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920090759/35d5636cc45f9ca9dca2f35b/3diwjBkfKJypk6OqIK7xRyC_J_cVYETPCg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a75ad53477d39454e9ba63307ca6c02b91c26aa433291dd53cac60c07936d5b5&req=fSInFsB%2BmoRWFb4f3HP0gBA%2BY1%2FquU2KRYalSbeOyZLUdcJCO%2BKXgusXtokD%0AB2ZRIeP3EOL9dZoGlQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920090759/35d5636cc45f9ca9dca2f35b/3diwjBkfKJypk6OqIK7xRyC_J_cVYETPCg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a75ad53477d39454e9ba63307ca6c02b91c26aa433291dd53cac60c07936d5b5&req=fSInFsB%2BmoRWFb4f3HP0gBA%2BY1%2FquU2KRYalSbeOyZLUdcJCO%2BKXgusXtokD%0AB2ZRIeP3EOL9dZoGlQ%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** You will also have a **Verify** button at the button of the window that will check these fields and ensure that a DNS record exists and that it has content. You will need to use this **Verify** button to manually verify your DNS records after you have set them up with your DNS host.

Once your records are able to be verified, they will need to go through their Change Management process and may take up to a week for the domains to be properly setup and activated.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for ClickDimensions, **[here](https://support.clickdimensions.com/hc/en-us/articles/360054967951)
**.

Add a ClickDimensions DKIM key in Valimail
==========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your ClickDimensions emails
==============================================================

Once you establish that ClickDimensions is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[ARMS](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471009-arms)
[Cision PR](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752261-cision-pr)

Critical Impact

Critical Impact
===============

How to set up DMARC for Critical Impact in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754351-critical-impact#h_fffd5c9d2a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754351-critical-impact#h_34b855f24d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754351-critical-impact#h_64f32e2392)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754351-critical-impact#h_35c84b80b5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754351-critical-impact#h_f1d0442d3d)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Critical Impact** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Critical Impact emails
===============================================================

Generate Sender Authentication Instructions
-------------------------------------------

**1.** Go to **Account** -> **Sender Authentication**.

**Note**: If you do not see this tool, please reach out to your account manager.

**2.** Click the **Add Domain** button.

**3.** Enter the domain or subdomain you want to authenticate (e.g. abc.com) and click **Save**.

**4.** Select the newly added domain in the grid and click the **Authenticate Domain** button. This will generate the unique DNS instructions for your domain.

**5.** Click the **Email Instructions** button to receive a copy of the instructions in email form for you to forward to your IT team.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and any other remaining records in your DNS.

Complete Sender Authentication For Your Domain
----------------------------------------------

**1.** Once you have added the DNS records, select the domain and click the **Authenticate Domain** button.

**2.** When you see green check marks next to the Existing Values and Recommended values, click the **Begin Authentication** button in the top right. (If you see red x marks, it means that the values do not match. Please see notes below.)

**3.** You will receive an email notification confirming that your domain is now successfully authenticated in your account. The status for the domain in the grid will display **Authenticated**.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Critical Impact, **[here](https://support.criticalimpact.com/hc/en-us/articles/4410142435981-Configure-Sender-Authentication)
**.

Add a Critical Impact DKIM key in Valimail
==========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Critical Impact emails
==============================================================

Once you establish that Critical Impact is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Censornet Email Security

Censornet Email Security
========================

How to set up DMARC for Censornet Email Security in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752246-censornet-email-security#h_879984a953)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752246-censornet-email-security#h_024c40b5af)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752246-censornet-email-security#h_90ffb36951)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Censornet Email Security** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Censornet Email Security emails
========================================================================

In your Censornet Email Security dashboard, go to **Products** ⟶ **E-mail Security** ⟶ **Product Configuration** ⟶ **Domains**.

Click the DKIM icon next to the domain you wish to configure.

The full DKIM key will be shown.

[![DKIM key from Censornet Email Security](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919503070/d75308b17190b9d278ab4ebb/image.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a80623464e4200b8bdbd407dac949f68a92395611a1301293df39305e32699d5&req=fSEuE8l9nYZfFb4f3HP0gO7rVmVyUF3CjZ5qTtTcfaZai%2B3jDcEshF0hChz4%0AZKnh8zQ8eGaonpf3EA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919503070/d75308b17190b9d278ab4ebb/image.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a80623464e4200b8bdbd407dac949f68a92395611a1301293df39305e32699d5&req=fSEuE8l9nYZfFb4f3HP0gO7rVmVyUF3CjZ5qTtTcfaZai%2B3jDcEshF0hChz4%0AZKnh8zQ8eGaonpf3EA%3D%3D%0A)

Seen above is an example DKIM key.

You can find the instructions to setup DKIM in Censornet Email Security, **[here](https://help.clouduss.com/ems-knowledge-base/configure-outbound-dkim)
**.

Add a Censornet Email Security DKIM key In Valimail
===================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Censornet Email Security emails
=======================================================================

Once you establish that Censornet Email Security is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470997-activecampaign#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470997-activecampaign#h_19a35d7e0e)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

ActiveCampaign

ActiveCampaign
==============

How to set up DMARC for Active Campaign in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470997-activecampaign#h_fbc3c3723a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470997-activecampaign#h_e9e52aa6ab)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470997-activecampaign#h_19a35d7e0e)

SPF(not supported)
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **ActiveCampaign** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

ActiveCampaign does not support SPF alignment.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your ActiveCampaign emails
==============================================================

**1.** Log in to your **ActiveCampaign account** as your account's admin user.

**2.** Click **Settings**, located on the left menu.

**3.** Click the **Advanced** tab.

**4.** Click the **I will manage my own email authentication** option.

**5.** Type your sending domain into the DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) field and click the **Generate** button.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152195/819de5ae762f996ca3d84b6a/Pe7oxW_u8xuMM_ThQBre5KxoKx_QMAH-UQ.png-1669852407?expires=1755792000&signature=680b3b2d1343f04ec692fe7a65f9abe12e8ce3f566eda5aa4eea4faff5735446&req=fCUlF8x8nIhaFb4f3HP0gHyDs4qBCH%2B4za01CmrRePp1y4sOXW68USqCnQr3%0Ae53r%2BmQEfDUC0bdSKg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-7.com/i/o/852152195/819de5ae762f996ca3d84b6a/Pe7oxW_u8xuMM_ThQBre5KxoKx_QMAH-UQ.png-1669852407?expires=1755792000&signature=680b3b2d1343f04ec692fe7a65f9abe12e8ce3f566eda5aa4eea4faff5735446&req=fCUlF8x8nIhaFb4f3HP0gHyDs4qBCH%2B4za01CmrRePp1y4sOXW68USqCnQr3%0Ae53r%2BmQEfDUC0bdSKg%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** ActiveCampaign will generate a TXT Record Name and TXT Record Value.

**7.** Copy and add the newly generated DKIM key, in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions to set up DKIM and SPF for ActiveCampaign **[here](https://help.activecampaign.com/hc/en-us/articles/206903370-DKIM-SPF-and-DMARC#additional-authentication-methods-0-4)
**.

Add an ActiveCampaign DKIM key in Valimail
==========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your ActiveCampaign emails
=============================================================

Once you establish that ActiveCampaign is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**Note**: Even though ActiveCampaign does not support SPF alignment, you can add this sender as an Enabled Sender for your domain, in Valimail.

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Related Articles

[MailPoet](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757459-mailpoet)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

ConvertKit

ConvertKit
==========

How to set up DMARC for ConvertKit in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754309-convertkit#h_0626d222de)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754309-convertkit#h_b4889cb4e0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754309-convertkit#h_bbb4724bd8)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **ConvertKit** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your ConvertKit emails
==========================================================

**1.** Click your account name at the top right of the ConvertKit dashboard, followed by **Settings**. Then, click on **Domains**.

**2.** Click the **Verify your domain** button.

**3.** Enter the domain that you want to verify for sending, and click **Next**.

**4.** Add the records that appear to your domain's DNS records manager. For more information on this, see the **Configuring your records** section below.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the CNAME DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and any other records (if any), will go in your DNS.

**5.** Once you've added the records, click **Validate**.

**6.** If your records are set up correctly, you will receive a message that your sending domain is verified.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for ConvertKit, **[here](https://help.convertkit.com/en/articles/2502558-using-a-verified-domain-for-email-sending)
**.

Add a ConvertKit DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your ConvertKit emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that ConvertKit is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Conversica

Conversica
==========

How to set up DMARC for Conversica in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754299-conversica#h_1438c34170)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754299-conversica#h_3902c00d32)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754299-conversica#h_cfa264456a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754299-conversica#h_14d9596457)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754299-conversica#h_47f9033d19)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754299-conversica#h_d422c45eb4)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Conversica** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Conversica emails
==========================================================

Creating a Subdomain
--------------------

You will first need to create a subdomain on which you can do the DKIM and SPF authentication for Conversica (usually, cmail.yourdomain.com).

Generate DKIM and SPF records
-----------------------------

After you create the subdomain, you must generate the DKIM and SPF authentication records from Conversica, as shown in the steps below:

**1.** Find **Generate DNS Setup Page Link** in DNS Settings.

[![Generate DNS Setup Page Link button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161049/6dbbae854f313d26d8f7d346/ITudUbwUt2heiyqQtXKLvwaqpD_5SqSIHg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=34ec1992f26d9f23befa0d93a32669942cdac975c1bafe69e435dfa471ea661e&req=fSInF89%2FnYVWFb4f3HP0gNERDUbuHmkH9m6evy7Yzb1d1q%2FBN%2BmwH1ttuRSQ%0AxkLe7fQ8vS9ORzST9w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161049/6dbbae854f313d26d8f7d346/ITudUbwUt2heiyqQtXKLvwaqpD_5SqSIHg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=34ec1992f26d9f23befa0d93a32669942cdac975c1bafe69e435dfa471ea661e&req=fSInF89%2FnYVWFb4f3HP0gNERDUbuHmkH9m6evy7Yzb1d1q%2FBN%2BmwH1ttuRSQ%0AxkLe7fQ8vS9ORzST9w%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click **Generate Link**.

[![generate link button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161058/579b2a80228f2922ed03ab05/RmuqjFnuMynVCsx9l43UHE4_Y1GLynUqFg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=424ffea4f1f088c2fe2a0a87172c1e0a212f31e1eb7891e918d8a66bb821b0a8&req=fSInF89%2FnYRXFb4f3HP0gDgAPTdeGE2hYzEtnjVU7PWIoldCSpk52BeCM%2BhO%0AfCp9LtLI%2FbGZJ%2BKa3w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161058/579b2a80228f2922ed03ab05/RmuqjFnuMynVCsx9l43UHE4_Y1GLynUqFg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=424ffea4f1f088c2fe2a0a87172c1e0a212f31e1eb7891e918d8a66bb821b0a8&req=fSInF89%2FnYRXFb4f3HP0gDgAPTdeGE2hYzEtnjVU7PWIoldCSpk52BeCM%2BhO%0AfCp9LtLI%2FbGZJ%2BKa3w%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Copy and paste the link that is generated, into your DNS to allow them to create and validate the subdomain on your behalf.

[![copy link button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161060/7540a195e520cd23a940dc32/0343VI4yXX0x82dkpzwh8SOOiGaDzEqbVQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=53a8e458e6fbbb911970a7424e8bf9d45ea944a59f7cd5e2a8be4dfb7fc42535&req=fSInF89%2FnYdfFb4f3HP0gCN687DplP8Ub%2F2q57As%2Bx37Ci7hijLpH8ry7hmi%0A0bWnybljxr4NadjezA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161060/7540a195e520cd23a940dc32/0343VI4yXX0x82dkpzwh8SOOiGaDzEqbVQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=53a8e458e6fbbb911970a7424e8bf9d45ea944a59f7cd5e2a8be4dfb7fc42535&req=fSInF89%2FnYdfFb4f3HP0gCN687DplP8Ub%2F2q57As%2Bx37Ci7hijLpH8ry7hmi%0A0bWnybljxr4NadjezA%3D%3D%0A)

Register Subdomain
==================

**1.** Go to the DNS Settings link in your Conversica dashboard, and click **Add Domain**. Then add the desired subdomain to the **Add Domain** section of the DNS Settings. This is where you enter "cmail.yourdomain.com" or any other subdomain name that will be used exclusively by the Conversica assistant.

You can also enter custom selectors for DKIM in the advanced settings of the "Add Domain" interface. Please enter the subdomain and not just the domain.

[![add a domain button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161067/d620c6801ccc07a785bfb368/p1S7szfhEJrugd9qCB8L1tQgNXq6fB2Jig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4f25da33aea571dc0367e022ef920ea541bf1462093b4a565c49a4c8c2b26844&req=fSInF89%2FnYdYFb4f3HP0gPGdF1tcvLPg6q9ho9z46uyf2mKC7gLkK5nDYVDa%0AbMD8ZyXqErDudgtSiA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161067/d620c6801ccc07a785bfb368/p1S7szfhEJrugd9qCB8L1tQgNXq6fB2Jig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4f25da33aea571dc0367e022ef920ea541bf1462093b4a565c49a4c8c2b26844&req=fSInF89%2FnYdYFb4f3HP0gPGdF1tcvLPg6q9ho9z46uyf2mKC7gLkK5nDYVDa%0AbMD8ZyXqErDudgtSiA%3D%3D%0A)

[![add a domain page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161071/765c67b0defd10ed44022428/zHYudub9BT92vf5thj-nkHr64oouLLlLXA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=57da14faaf86835a089c242e722c7e050f7d1432f6318954038411454b96d94b&req=fSInF89%2FnYZeFb4f3HP0gKccPqcIx%2B9hOqKckdiHLqoskzDXfjAWfKuFWQHT%0AGOWC%2BXi9%2FgS2NjiJCA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161071/765c67b0defd10ed44022428/zHYudub9BT92vf5thj-nkHr64oouLLlLXA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=57da14faaf86835a089c242e722c7e050f7d1432f6318954038411454b96d94b&req=fSInF89%2FnYZeFb4f3HP0gKccPqcIx%2B9hOqKckdiHLqoskzDXfjAWfKuFWQHT%0AGOWC%2BXi9%2FgS2NjiJCA%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click into the domain ID you just added and you should see the 9 DNS records that your IT team needs to add to your DNS Hosting Service. There are 2 TXT Records, 3 MX Records, and 4 CNAME records that you will need to add to your DNS Hosting Service.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and the rest of the records will go in your DNS.

[![dkim and SPF records](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161073/008e923ad8fd4199405adb1b/pDdNYTiUCrEpFHbrWkHdz_apZ9tY-gj_MA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=05d4775fb257c718067bd144b219b7e7121a71cd7154179342e23be9b93202bd&req=fSInF89%2FnYZcFb4f3HP0gOtNyz55SZk1hv4IzPEcmePbCS4ZZc9ksR5buMk4%0APZdnAA7R858n%2BRBqwQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161073/008e923ad8fd4199405adb1b/pDdNYTiUCrEpFHbrWkHdz_apZ9tY-gj_MA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=05d4775fb257c718067bd144b219b7e7121a71cd7154179342e23be9b93202bd&req=fSInF89%2FnYZcFb4f3HP0gOtNyz55SZk1hv4IzPEcmePbCS4ZZc9ksR5buMk4%0APZdnAA7R858n%2BRBqwQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Keep this page open in your browser, as you will need to return to **validate** your records once they're created.

**4.** Once all records have been entered in their proper place, click the **Validate** button found on the page from the **DNS Settings** page.

A green checkmark will display for each record that has been entered correctly.

A red ‘X’ indicates there was an error when validating. Please confirm your record entries are correct.

[![dkim and SPF records verified](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161080/44a6e808328bb1f04bac4c87/0Wkg9CzRn7O9AlooyoN9o1LdFwemY6UVLg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=06b5202b3ab118ed8cb34acef14070906a0dee1c0dc2581fca6aced5d1a6875d&req=fSInF89%2FnYlfFb4f3HP0gE2XS4VGJBKpU14PV%2BodWFmVzy7bTohe0wcpqK1l%0AaZzNrMcrwpc70UYCUQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920161080/44a6e808328bb1f04bac4c87/0Wkg9CzRn7O9AlooyoN9o1LdFwemY6UVLg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=06b5202b3ab118ed8cb34acef14070906a0dee1c0dc2581fca6aced5d1a6875d&req=fSInF89%2FnYlfFb4f3HP0gE2XS4VGJBKpU14PV%2BodWFmVzy7bTohe0wcpqK1l%0AaZzNrMcrwpc70UYCUQ%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Conversica, **[here](https://help.conversica.com/hc/en-us/articles/360049186811-Subdomain-Configuration-Instructions)
**.

Add a Conversica DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Conversica emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Conversica is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752263-cleverreach#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752263-cleverreach#h_fd0d12cda8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752263-cleverreach#h_c4625b59c4)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752263-cleverreach#h_573726654a)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

CleverReach

CleverReach
===========

How to set up DMARC for CleverReach in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752263-cleverreach#h_fd0d12cda8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752263-cleverreach#h_c4625b59c4)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752263-cleverreach#h_573726654a)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **CleverReach** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your CleverReach emails
===========================================================

**1.** You can add a new domain for using DKIM in your account: **My Account** -> **Settings** -> **DKIM Domains**. Please insert the domain without http:// or https://:

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919506708/9a428fb463f881ea27b0195c/0vyHBshgxpKXuwcoccRLGbDN_x7E433Naw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b5a7c5fa244ed11996f020c15a363ef3c0f3f6d81b133a6fbeaa0a075b45af09&req=fSEuE8l4moFXFb4f3HP0gO6QkFYKABVkzYsr7mTs0jrwwfrPLju7OGW0xL%2Fu%0AWbibgkM%2BItc39lgfSQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919506708/9a428fb463f881ea27b0195c/0vyHBshgxpKXuwcoccRLGbDN_x7E433Naw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b5a7c5fa244ed11996f020c15a363ef3c0f3f6d81b133a6fbeaa0a075b45af09&req=fSEuE8l4moFXFb4f3HP0gO6QkFYKABVkzYsr7mTs0jrwwfrPLju7OGW0xL%2Fu%0AWbibgkM%2BItc39lgfSQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** You can optionally add the selector. The selector is used to identify the public DKIM key details of the domain.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919506712/ee8ad76ab45b4bef34e2fe43/Rqft5L2Bp7Dl73-pgCJZ_cEuu6xn_2poOQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=21c6409701582a0bfd9eff21eb22055c8d04635aa3ab3eb5c008ecbf5ee49cc0&req=fSEuE8l4moBdFb4f3HP0gNnRnyaMReZRnRhftQpI%2B%2BH74GoXtok4kSTKnG3K%0AhIu0WiQMUOMkahl7DA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919506712/ee8ad76ab45b4bef34e2fe43/Rqft5L2Bp7Dl73-pgCJZ_cEuu6xn_2poOQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=21c6409701582a0bfd9eff21eb22055c8d04635aa3ab3eb5c008ecbf5ee49cc0&req=fSEuE8l4moBdFb4f3HP0gNnRnyaMReZRnRhftQpI%2B%2BH74GoXtok4kSTKnG3K%0AhIu0WiQMUOMkahl7DA%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** When successfully having entered the DKIM key in your account, you have to customize the DNS record for the domain in the next step or have this done by your (domain) provider:

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919506718/09d6ea39b5c745fb45bec25b/ntrwT9RnwydflN-x5g15z7cgfBRMCG8ZaA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=10388be38b83593e07b58878fb81b088fdef04f369f558548f43c3f1ca2cd15c&req=fSEuE8l4moBXFb4f3HP0gM8%2F0okEansAN6j3W4MeTO2%2B4i2C9kox3iikPoIA%0AkHfnZ0NuhEJli7euAg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919506718/09d6ea39b5c745fb45bec25b/ntrwT9RnwydflN-x5g15z7cgfBRMCG8ZaA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=10388be38b83593e07b58878fb81b088fdef04f369f558548f43c3f1ca2cd15c&req=fSEuE8l4moBXFb4f3HP0gM8%2F0okEansAN6j3W4MeTO2%2B4i2C9kox3iikPoIA%0AkHfnZ0NuhEJli7euAg%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Click on **i**, to keep the DNS record as a TXT record for your domain:

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919506725/9be0630f60ac9dbca4b64886/NCCxBBM3Qk0cQKXRphbrpT_nIp8Ovlu_ZA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=742f7700dcecc607c6c42e9d3d5dde1c092c5fe9cc660e9ebe8638dae113b781&req=fSEuE8l4moNaFb4f3HP0gHniWBDFOcQPMAL%2BjNJgRNOTJneA%2FtHed5YAhJ1Y%0A%2Boh%2BtZetRialrA25Tw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/919506725/9be0630f60ac9dbca4b64886/NCCxBBM3Qk0cQKXRphbrpT_nIp8Ovlu_ZA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=742f7700dcecc607c6c42e9d3d5dde1c092c5fe9cc660e9ebe8638dae113b781&req=fSEuE8l4moNaFb4f3HP0gHniWBDFOcQPMAL%2BjNJgRNOTJneA%2FtHed5YAhJ1Y%0A%2Boh%2BtZetRialrA25Tw%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

**6.** After the DKIM key is added in Valimail, go back to the CleverReach setup page and click on **Check**. The system checks in the background if everything has been set up correctly.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for CleverReach, **[here](https://support.cleverreach.de/hc/en-us/articles/360016467539-What-is-DKIM-and-how-do-I-set-it-up-for-my-domain-in-CleverReach-)
**.

Add a CleverReach DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your CleverReach emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that CleverReach is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

  
​  
​

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754536-docusign#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

DocuSign

DocuSign
========

How to set up DMARC for DocuSign in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754536-docusign#h_fe87c340f3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754536-docusign#h_cdfe84cc95)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754536-docusign#h_4d43e05847)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **DocuSign** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your DocuSign emails
========================================================

Before you begin:

*   Log in as a DocuSign Administrator.
    
*   Claim and verify a top-level domain - [Domains](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=jub1589318086105.html&_LANG=enus)
    
*   Have access to edit DNS records in your organization's domain registrar. This is done through a 3rd party site and may require IT assistance from within your organization.
    
*   After claiming a domain, create a subdomain and update your DNS records to verify and configure for CED.
    

**Note**: This process does not use an existing subdomain. A new subdomain is created through the verification process. Subdomains already used for SSO cannot be used for custom email addresses.

**1.** In DocuSign Admin, select **Features**.

**2.** Scroll down to Custom Email Domain, and select **Manage**.

**3.** Select **Actions** -> **Enable**.

The Custom Email Domain menu item is added to DocuSign Admin.

**4.** In DocuSign Admin, select **Custom Email Domain**.

**5.** Select **ADD DOMAIN**.

**6.** Enter a name for the new subdomain.

**Note**: The subdomain name appears in front of the verified top-level domain. For example, if the top-level domain is ‘example.com’ and the subdomain is ‘docusign’, your DocuSign custom email addresses will be \[username\]@docusign.example.com.

**7.** Select an available verified top-level domain, then select **NEXT**.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920241811/1d93338e7ce7273091d27532/GIfV8sv_S9sgtsh1Fa51o2eD6SgjGUyaVQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c57a4690b0d5889eaa48017baaad14fc41ab619f6dfb419c1e00982b06bb4446&req=fSInFM1%2FlYBeFb4f3HP0gFglZYU7SX5DwdTnqoQhh6dgPcSzGWIhSHEzXnUX%0AaU8EmYhhA%2F7%2FMHE1Pg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920241811/1d93338e7ce7273091d27532/GIfV8sv_S9sgtsh1Fa51o2eD6SgjGUyaVQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c57a4690b0d5889eaa48017baaad14fc41ab619f6dfb419c1e00982b06bb4446&req=fSInFM1%2FlYBeFb4f3HP0gFglZYU7SX5DwdTnqoQhh6dgPcSzGWIhSHEzXnUX%0AaU8EmYhhA%2F7%2FMHE1Pg%3D%3D%0A)

**8.** Using the DocuSign-provided values, create two CNAME/DKIM records, one TXT/SPF record, and one MX record in your DNS.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add the TXT and MX records in your DNS.

**9.** Select **VERIFY DNS NOW**.

**Note**: It may take up to 24 hours for changes to your DNS to be published by your domain registrar. If you are unable to verify the domain shortly after updating the DNS records, select **SAVE DOMAIN AND VERIFY DNS LATER**.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for DocuSign, **[here](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&rsc_301&bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=kpy1624481491369.html&_LANG=enus)
**.

Add a DocuSign DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your DocuSign emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that DocuSign is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Docebo

Docebo
======

How to set up DMARC for Docebo in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754531-docebo#h_c9f3545929)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754531-docebo#h_27a0c24494)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754531-docebo#h_4144fbdc10)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Docebo** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Docebo emails
======================================================

**1.** In order to generate the DKIM records for your domain, access the **Admin Menu** by scrolling your mouse over the **gear icon**.

**2.** Click on the **Advanced Settings** option in the **Settings** section.

**3.** Move to the **Email Preferences** settings and click on the **Manage DKIM Keys** button. You will then be redirected main management page of the app, listing the domains already associated with DKIM records. Please note that the DKIM area is fully accessible in the **Email Preferences** area even when the White Label app is active, for all the domains in your Extended Enterprise.

**4.** Press the **plus** button in the top right corner to generate the DKIM records for your domain. _Please note that DKIM records are unique for each domain and it is thus not possible to generate DKIM records for the same domain more than once. If you are using the Extended Enterprise App, you have to generate a DKIM record for every client you defined_.

**5.** Type the domain name of the email sender and press **Generate Keys**. The platform will generate three keys:

*   **Public Key**. DNS public key used by the recipient systems to verify the emails sent from your domain.
    
*   **Selector**. The selector, together with the domain name, is used by recipient servers to verify the email sender.
    
*   **Configuration Text**. For those using a Bind DNS server, this text field shows the configuration in Bind 9 format.
    

**6.** Copy and add the DKIM key in Valimail Enforce. Once DKIM records have been generated, your domain will be listed on the **DKIM Activation** page, together with the corresponding Public Key and the Selector value.

**7.** After adding the DKIM key in Valiamil Enforce, click the **ellipsis icon** at the end of the domain row and select **Verify & Activate** to complete the association between your domain and the DNS server. Remember that the DKIM email security will not be active if you do not execute this step.

**8.** Whenever you need to retrieve the generated keys, click on the **ellipsis icon** and select **View Info**. Always copy the values of the keys associated to your domain from this panel, do not use the values displayed in the DKIM Activation page, since they may not be fully displayed and you may thus copy an incomplete value.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM for Docebo **[here](https://help.docebo.com/hc/en-us/articles/360020125099-Managing-the-DKIM-Feature)
**.

Add a Docebo DKIM key in Valimail
=================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Docebo emails
=====================================================

Once you establish that Docebo is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754519-datamotion-secure-delivery#h_430dfc9939)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754519-datamotion-secure-delivery#h_bd7a0da683)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754519-datamotion-secure-delivery#h_0070d76538)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

DataMotion Secure Delivery

DataMotion Secure Delivery
==========================

How to set up DMARC for DataMotion Secure Delivery in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754519-datamotion-secure-delivery#h_430dfc9939)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754519-datamotion-secure-delivery#h_bd7a0da683)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754519-datamotion-secure-delivery#h_0070d76538)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **DataMotion Secure Delivery** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your DataMotion emails
==========================================================

**1.** Log into the DataMotion SecureMail portal at [https://ssl.datamotion.com](https://ssl.datamotion.com/)
 as the Administrator user.

**2.** Click on **Administrative Tools** -> **DKIM Management**.

[![a screen shot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920237706/ace32d13e667f1e3e1b4801a/6efZPO7LpZKoD4IHLsUDbC_T-0mXXjEuvg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=db0186103e48b2b064435e0068fb6c18774d06b91b30bd03ddd142f83709334e&req=fSInFMp5moFZFb4f3HP0gDmYnny%2BJlaOSQew9ZnfPfl0os3Dd%2FKvwZFFBNnh%0A9pE6lGNwGBDaVi6e8Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920237706/ace32d13e667f1e3e1b4801a/6efZPO7LpZKoD4IHLsUDbC_T-0mXXjEuvg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=db0186103e48b2b064435e0068fb6c18774d06b91b30bd03ddd142f83709334e&req=fSInFMp5moFZFb4f3HP0gDmYnny%2BJlaOSQew9ZnfPfl0os3Dd%2FKvwZFFBNnh%0A9pE6lGNwGBDaVi6e8Q%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Fill in the required fields below and click **Save** to create a public DKIM key.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920237709/bfece2ac791e61b17947831b/SokYLL5u7-LLpVs9dK7ApgAdyyGt95gNhA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=07ebf5d5b8a5f0fd58e746a4483d4d5c578395329f22f97fcf08ae3958192b36&req=fSInFMp5moFWFb4f3HP0gNzcP94bxRpcJ9mBS7sH2IXGEmVVXIFkso%2BGRrDS%0A22DGKCUmKZRhsR3bOQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920237709/bfece2ac791e61b17947831b/SokYLL5u7-LLpVs9dK7ApgAdyyGt95gNhA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=07ebf5d5b8a5f0fd58e746a4483d4d5c578395329f22f97fcf08ae3958192b36&req=fSInFMp5moFWFb4f3HP0gNzcP94bxRpcJ9mBS7sH2IXGEmVVXIFkso%2BGRrDS%0A22DGKCUmKZRhsR3bOQ%3D%3D%0A)

**4\.** Once created, the DKIM key containing the record name and value can be copied and pasted into your DNS records. You will need to create a new TXT record in your DNS administrator portal. This requires some DNS understanding and may require contacting the DNS host support desk for assistance.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for this domain in Valimail, you will need to add the DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920237713/b83839de40685189c357874e/8hLc50n6R6pXlxAL_GgfqH071peaxk4Ubg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=80335e0816b998f6ff716a4334830770c6837181db3007fcae5fb1296a6fc936&req=fSInFMp5moBcFb4f3HP0gDiArfUdtaOLWtbfmxESdI9mJneeD9rsA3UEU7V7%0ANX9hKjdegQqMPq2RVA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920237713/b83839de40685189c357874e/8hLc50n6R6pXlxAL_GgfqH071peaxk4Ubg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=80335e0816b998f6ff716a4334830770c6837181db3007fcae5fb1296a6fc936&req=fSInFMp5moBcFb4f3HP0gDiArfUdtaOLWtbfmxESdI9mJneeD9rsA3UEU7V7%0ANX9hKjdegQqMPq2RVA%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for DataMotion, **[here](https://kb.datamotion.com/?ht_kb=datamotion-dkim-configuration)
**.

Add a DataMotion DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your DataMotion emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that DataMotion is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Customer.io | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754357-customer-io#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754357-customer-io#h_4f35386779)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754357-customer-io#h_bf86aa03f7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754357-customer-io#h_de0958c4fa)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Customer.io

Customer.io
===========

How to set up DMARC for Customer.io in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754357-customer-io#h_4f35386779)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754357-customer-io#h_bf86aa03f7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754357-customer-io#h_de0958c4fa)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM configuration process for **Customer.io** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Mailgun's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Customer.io as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Customer.io infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Customer.io emails
===========================================================

You must set up at least one sending domain before you can send emails from your Customer.io workspace.

**1.** Go to **Settings** -> **Workspace Settings**.

**2.** Click **Email Settings** and then click **Add Sending Domain**.

**3.** Enter the _Domain_, _Display Name_, and _Email Address_ that you want to send messages from, and click **Add Domain**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920180814/df90445a42fbb53645e7c5ca/cA3gZBFgUCQi58Va-_fYbpVBIAsJA9UKJQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f01c30c30cb256868b9afc6649ef8b2589fa9e007d6011c2039e96ccab17131d&req=fSInF8F%2BlYBbFb4f3HP0gOwtF5D8WBfIf67lWEbbQNaAExXbHmq8dHHMBr%2B5%0A6btmMwxo9AIvWw6i9Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920180814/df90445a42fbb53645e7c5ca/cA3gZBFgUCQi58Va-_fYbpVBIAsJA9UKJQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f01c30c30cb256868b9afc6649ef8b2589fa9e007d6011c2039e96ccab17131d&req=fSInF8F%2BlYBbFb4f3HP0gOwtF5D8WBfIf67lWEbbQNaAExXbHmq8dHHMBr%2B5%0A6btmMwxo9AIvWw6i9Q%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Follow the **Workspace Settings** link in the left-hand menu in your Customer.io account, choose **Email** from the list of message types and then select the **Sending Domains** tab.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920180823/8044eef903b761f7d378bab0/GW-dHZill5N34TX5_JQ4846qO8MAyg26wA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9b8bacbc354b07f9a137ead125b03fae763056e7979fbdba49ee41907bde5b8c&req=fSInF8F%2BlYNcFb4f3HP0gPzh0kxXmvvKIMxc2f9hN1FWSNvd4LLt5Fdd19iZ%0AN92l5jD8va9ey3NKWA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920180823/8044eef903b761f7d378bab0/GW-dHZill5N34TX5_JQ4846qO8MAyg26wA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9b8bacbc354b07f9a137ead125b03fae763056e7979fbdba49ee41907bde5b8c&req=fSInF8F%2BlYNcFb4f3HP0gPzh0kxXmvvKIMxc2f9hN1FWSNvd4LLt5Fdd19iZ%0AN92l5jD8va9ey3NKWA%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Click the **Verify domain** button for the domain you would like to authenticate. This is where you will see the MX records that need to be added in your DNS and DKIM records that you need to add in Valimail Enforce in order to authenticate your domain.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920180831/94f65299531f3d4bef7900a0/NWdCSOX6vjwbXMrHOHAtT6fsDlaoPzXe-A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f078f60fb0070907f40fb0c0200347dd833c4ac13ab4033828b74e1a0ab54e0e&req=fSInF8F%2BlYJeFb4f3HP0gNnGLv4JPcxlnP7X7MC7CTrlc9gmLDPEJV4e1taw%0AXHfz8rfezP3Rih%2Bbkg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920180831/94f65299531f3d4bef7900a0/NWdCSOX6vjwbXMrHOHAtT6fsDlaoPzXe-A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f078f60fb0070907f40fb0c0200347dd833c4ac13ab4033828b74e1a0ab54e0e&req=fSInF8F%2BlYJeFb4f3HP0gNnGLv4JPcxlnP7X7MC7CTrlc9gmLDPEJV4e1taw%0AXHfz8rfezP3Rih%2Bbkg%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Please add the MX records in your domain's DNS and the DKIM key in Valimail Enforce. You will also need to add Customer.io in your Enabled Senders list in Enforce, which accounts for adding the Customer.io's SPF include in your SPF record.

**7.** After you are finished with adding the records in DNS and Valimail Enforce, you will need to come back to the Deliverability page and click the **Verify domain** button. We will then check that the records and values are correctly in place. If all three records are verified by green checkmarks, then your domain is verified and you’re all set to start sending from that domain.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920180837/305c30cecd32ee6ccb747cf6/dQbHY65i8RIOnd5UA56R46tkfrApfV3SFQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=177d932878369d45e9c36b37e289da59a212c2d01f850779eeec0bba3b28ee29&req=fSInF8F%2BlYJYFb4f3HP0gF1j9Sn%2Bm1fWm85641OuTekON1AgIZSCob3G0x6r%0A9aC%2FMQHuy%2BSAnnoObg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920180837/305c30cecd32ee6ccb747cf6/dQbHY65i8RIOnd5UA56R46tkfrApfV3SFQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=177d932878369d45e9c36b37e289da59a212c2d01f850779eeec0bba3b28ee29&req=fSInF8F%2BlYJYFb4f3HP0gF1j9Sn%2Bm1fWm85641OuTekON1AgIZSCob3G0x6r%0A9aC%2FMQHuy%2BSAnnoObg%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Customer.io **[here](https://customer.io/docs/authentication/)
**.

Add a Customer.io DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Customer.io emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that Customer.io is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754374-cvent#h_f915f13164)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754374-cvent#h_6c4904526c)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Cvent

Cvent
=====

How to set up DMARC for Cvent in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754374-cvent#h_2e9a64af46)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754374-cvent#h_f915f13164)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754374-cvent#h_6c4904526c)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the DKIM authentication process for **Cvent** and how this is managed in Valimail. Cvent does not currently support SPF-aligned authentication, but DKIM authentication should be enough for most use case scenarios.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Cvent emails
=====================================================

**1.** Open a support ticket with Cvent and ask them to provide DKIM signing keys for your domain.

**2.** Once you obtain the keys, add them to your domain's configuration in the Valimail platform.

**3.** Confirm with Cvent support that DKIM signing is enabled for your domain.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM, **[here](https://support.cvent.com/s/communityarticle/What-is-DKIM)
**.

Add a Cvent DKIM key in Valimail
================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Cvent emails
====================================================

Once you establish that Cvent is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *
