# Doppler | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_cbb95fbcd4)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_c2c954b540)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_f676ffbe28)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_5c8244672f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_35c0067108)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Doppler

Doppler
=======

How to set up DMARC for Doppler in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_cbb95fbcd4)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_c2c954b540)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_f676ffbe28)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_5c8244672f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754541-doppler#h_35c0067108)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Doppler** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Doppler emails
=======================================================

Step One: Create the new Domain Name
------------------------------------

The first thing you need to do is get into your Doppler Relay account and go to **Account Settings** (located on the right side of the navigation bar) and select the **Domain Manager** option.

[![domain manager page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920245285/1d5c3f27500dd5a542f9c32c/duHnzHNX_xXU9SQdBR_nwLLbe3iFWFu7nw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0541ff80b2d2f6bbefd63647db0617a465449760e07ded97cf42a9da241cbe03&req=fSInFM17n4laFb4f3HP0gNV0ZkJSwl6tdGBmUybbKKZfgzvHsZUcUjgMGUT9%0AFOBsuYn8kfSjC%2FQh4g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920245285/1d5c3f27500dd5a542f9c32c/duHnzHNX_xXU9SQdBR_nwLLbe3iFWFu7nw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0541ff80b2d2f6bbefd63647db0617a465449760e07ded97cf42a9da241cbe03&req=fSInFM17n4laFb4f3HP0gNV0ZkJSwl6tdGBmUybbKKZfgzvHsZUcUjgMGUT9%0AFOBsuYn8kfSjC%2FQh4g%3D%3D%0A)

Once there, you have to click on the button called **Add Domain** and then you’ll see the option to write the new Domain, then click the button next to it and the new domain will be successfully added to your list.

[![add a new domain button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920245290/a2b42bd1c30c282ff30aa7c4/nVH6ELdc72RMNHS8NIIcp_dZQpIEAzKwbQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2e064aede5f594088eaa064e41c0880eb87e2a957d16b704a87b9d16dd26e337&req=fSInFM17n4hfFb4f3HP0gCThPLDz7srJfk9I87%2BrwB3n1LUEZrNEihFf89dd%0AXq24%2BZM9sRUqn%2FgMLA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920245290/a2b42bd1c30c282ff30aa7c4/nVH6ELdc72RMNHS8NIIcp_dZQpIEAzKwbQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2e064aede5f594088eaa064e41c0880eb87e2a957d16b704a87b9d16dd26e337&req=fSInFM17n4hfFb4f3HP0gCThPLDz7srJfk9I87%2BrwB3n1LUEZrNEihFf89dd%0AXq24%2BZM9sRUqn%2FgMLA%3D%3D%0A)

Now is time to authenticate your Domain, for that you will need your DKIM Selector and your DKIM Public Key. You can find them on the list below by clicking on the **configuration** icon, you’ll be redirected to a new page with all the information you need:

*   DKIM Selector
    
*   DKIM Public Key
    
*   SPF Field
    

[![DKIM and SPF records page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920245297/4402cce43c55f7f200d864ad/BToRSfZ3Qj9jRQSCzFUTwHLapq3utoKA9Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4e25174960e3a529cc44426a99990006fc778427d038bd7f36f3ae30f880e25c&req=fSInFM17n4hYFb4f3HP0gFHcA7bY%2BN51KRikV94rcpLsn3e3spdxj%2FFwUf%2Bu%0AzomAJxjJSaACRPB45w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920245297/4402cce43c55f7f200d864ad/BToRSfZ3Qj9jRQSCzFUTwHLapq3utoKA9Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4e25174960e3a529cc44426a99990006fc778427d038bd7f36f3ae30f880e25c&req=fSInFM17n4hYFb4f3HP0gFHcA7bY%2BN51KRikV94rcpLsn3e3spdxj%2FFwUf%2Bu%0AzomAJxjJSaACRPB45w%3D%3D%0A)

Second step: Publish the DKIM record
------------------------------------

Create a TXT record with the name and value that appear in the administrator console of your DNS. Hosting providers use different names for the fields associated with a TXT record.

For example, GoDaddy includes fields with the name TXT Name and TXT Value, while Name.com has the same fields with the names Record Host and Record Answer.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add Doppler to your Enabled Senders list.

Once you’ve done these steps, after a while Doppler Relay will show that your DKIM is validated.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Doppler, **[here](https://help.dopplerrelay.com/en/how-to-configure-your-dkim-step-by-step/)
**.

Add a Doppler DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Doppler emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Doppler is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Copernica | Valimail Help Center

Copernica

Copernica
=========

How to set up DMARC for Copernica in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754343-copernica#h_21b13c98af)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754343-copernica#h_4da0919e7a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754343-copernica#h_39a644aded)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754343-copernica#h_eb08d7b9c4)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754343-copernica#h_73e1a36bec)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754343-copernica#h_0122abef29)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Copernica** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Copernica emails
=========================================================

**Step 1. Generate the DKIM key**
---------------------------------

The DKIM key is created in the Publisher.

**1.** Under **E-mailings**, go to **Extra** -> **DKIM Keys**.

**2.** In the dialog window, choose **New DKIM key**.

**3.** Enter your **domain name** (yourdomain.com) in the domain field.

**4.** In the subdomain field enter the name of the selector which will hold the DKIM key later on, without the .\_domainkey part. In this example we use **_dkim_** but you are completely free to use something else.

**5.** Choose the desired key strength. We recommend 2048bit.

**6.** **Store** these settings.

Your key is now generated. The next step is to paste this key into the TXT record subdomain. You may close the DKIM dialog. The key remains available in the software.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920175856/b066a2ceb239ba6e5ac4369e/gekzzZs9AlpuVQ1-V13ostwu78pe7hQLaA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b75462a689b56d0209301a74ac225b948a6dc4c8abd9b49e2a34451da835b3f0&req=fSInF857lYRZFb4f3HP0gPS4z7IR%2BiAE3b9aOnrxKuZBMQNLcQsBK%2F%2BdkEYD%0ADvUlUIFf6S26Jpas8Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920175856/b066a2ceb239ba6e5ac4369e/gekzzZs9AlpuVQ1-V13ostwu78pe7hQLaA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b75462a689b56d0209301a74ac225b948a6dc4c8abd9b49e2a34451da835b3f0&req=fSInF857lYRZFb4f3HP0gPS4z7IR%2BiAE3b9aOnrxKuZBMQNLcQsBK%2F%2BdkEYD%0ADvUlUIFf6S26Jpas8Q%3D%3D%0A)

**Step 2. Add the key into the TXT record on your DNS**
-------------------------------------------------------

In step one, you have generated the key. The next step is to paste the key into the TXT record on your subdomain.

**Note**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail, you will need to add this DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in **Valimail Enforce**.

**Step 3. Validate the DKIM key**
---------------------------------

You have followed the first two steps precisely. Let’s validate if the DKIM has been configured correctly and can be sent along with your mailings.

**1.** Go to **E-mailings** -> **Extra** -> **DKIM keys**.

**2.** Select your DKIM record.

**3.** Click to the **DNS** tab.

Here you will find a list of checks and their results. If there are any errors, solve them before you start sending mails signed with the DKIM.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Copernica, **[here](https://www.copernica.com/en/articles/signing-your-emails-with-dkim)
**.

Add a Copernica DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Copernica emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Copernica is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Related Articles

[Dyn Email Services](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754571-dyn-email-services)
[Netcore](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757552-netcore)
[Symplify](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8759548-symplify)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754513-d2l-brightspace#h_42ab1eb89b)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

D2L Brightspace

D2L Brightspace
===============

How to set up DMARC for D2L Brightspace in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754513-d2l-brightspace#h_00a07513a2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754513-d2l-brightspace#h_c15058c836)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754513-d2l-brightspace#h_42ab1eb89b)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **D2L Brightspace** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your D2L Brightspace emails
===============================================================

Setting up DKIM for D2L Brightspace is fairly easy.

You just need to add 2 CNAME DKIM keys in Valimail Enforce with the following values and selectors:

**d2lmail1.\_domainkey.brightspace.com**

**d2lmail2.\_domainkey.brightspace.com**

A private and public domain key pair is required to allow D2L to sign mail sent on your behalf and enable recipients to decode headers and verify the source of the email.

D2L generates the private and public domain key pair and imports it for use in signing email messages sent from the D2L Cloud on behalf of your domain. D2L provides the public DKIM TXT records to your organization for use in creating the corresponding CNAME records.

D2L will rotate the active DKIM keys every six months. When one key is rotated, the second will become active in signing emails, after which the process will repeat at six-month intervals. For organizations that have DKIM keys enabled and are using a custom mail domain, email delivery may be impacted if both required DKIM CNAME records are not in place at the time of rotation.

The service owner of D2L Brighspace within your organization, will need to contact D2L Brighspace to make sure they DKIM record are validated.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF in D2L Brightspace **[here](https://documentation.brightspace.com/EN/le/email/admin/best_practices_outgoing_mail.htm)
**.

Add a D2L Brightspace DKIM key in Valimail
==========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your D2L Brightspace emails
==============================================================

Once you establish that D2L Brightspace is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Constant Contact

Constant Contact
================

How to set up DMARC for Constant Contact in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754193-constant-contact#h_20c2051014)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754193-constant-contact#h_259f19f1e0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754193-constant-contact#h_3442cbcf2c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754193-constant-contact#h_ea5e7424d8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754193-constant-contact#h_38e9be69a2)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the DKIM authentication processes for **Constant Contact** and how are they managed in Valimail.

Enabling DKIM signing for your custom domain can be done from the Constant Contact admin console in the Constant Contact platform.

Constant Contact can authenticate with either CNAME or TXT DKIM keys.

Configuring DKIM authentication using CNAME records for your Constant Contact emails
====================================================================================

Self-authenticating using DKIM CNAME records is the simplest and most secure way to authenticate your domain email address.

**1.** Click the profile name in the upper-right and select **My Account**.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158427/f2e2e636dc1887dccc2b0475/e7_kir2GQko0RJQj9IyNBzzXgdqn0x6MKA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1d7a5dd1a98ec7ed9c2c5bedcf6eb76f2af9356681956db20b3c3edb0cb03c15&req=fSInF8x2mYNYFb4f3HP0gHAeL5wfTSrvBtUSEE7YYrx%2FqrPknZC0PktvmcVE%0AFiES7YOgmoaWIMDzgw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158427/f2e2e636dc1887dccc2b0475/e7_kir2GQko0RJQj9IyNBzzXgdqn0x6MKA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1d7a5dd1a98ec7ed9c2c5bedcf6eb76f2af9356681956db20b3c3edb0cb03c15&req=fSInF8x2mYNYFb4f3HP0gHAeL5wfTSrvBtUSEE7YYrx%2FqrPknZC0PktvmcVE%0AFiES7YOgmoaWIMDzgw%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click the **Advanced settings** tab.

**3.** Click **Add self-authentication**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158436/c33a94de02066a14f1b791c3/LHl-Mf8FxnGk4l6Uot-0o-ITJgl6u1vw0A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b37a8874082ad37827b93cc48f2f8cb1f157425edf93dd7a586c84c864322461&req=fSInF8x2mYJZFb4f3HP0gHV4MxY%2Bx%2F3enP0gqZFe4mdO4UJCyIr0uoOc%2BrrM%0A8dyP6sfZJvdnKcTyLg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158436/c33a94de02066a14f1b791c3/LHl-Mf8FxnGk4l6Uot-0o-ITJgl6u1vw0A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b37a8874082ad37827b93cc48f2f8cb1f157425edf93dd7a586c84c864322461&req=fSInF8x2mYJZFb4f3HP0gHV4MxY%2Bx%2F3enP0gqZFe4mdO4UJCyIr0uoOc%2BrrM%0A8dyP6sfZJvdnKcTyLg%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Select **Self-authenticate using DKIM CNAME records**.

**5.** Click **Continue**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158442/3bb57cbba2e8807f291b5e07/QLArMbaLHEiDqWqpKNOx4KJo43QI904s1w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ea7f1111bea67b83562cb8dc4a62c386b6e15ce8604c35377845c9d1f4450388&req=fSInF8x2mYVdFb4f3HP0gOK3PuVC%2BsjmtDpZPQWnl0uun2tEUQ7ZejIGJIAj%0AiPnV9mEwW5E89nnqFQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158442/3bb57cbba2e8807f291b5e07/QLArMbaLHEiDqWqpKNOx4KJo43QI904s1w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ea7f1111bea67b83562cb8dc4a62c386b6e15ce8604c35377845c9d1f4450388&req=fSInF8x2mYVdFb4f3HP0gOK3PuVC%2BsjmtDpZPQWnl0uun2tEUQ7ZejIGJIAj%0AiPnV9mEwW5E89nnqFQ%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** From the drop-down, select the domain you want to use for self-authentication. If the custom domain you want to use isn’t listed, choose **Select another domain** from the drop-down to add and verify a new email address.

**7.** Click **Continue**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158446/bb15824156999281b1ff101f/PIrlI6eniHthMjIv7UT69tlpDwqRxLQ4vA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=afe3ce757b0d0e074f887629ecb093c51aaedcc0b935eb6fe80ceafed8246247&req=fSInF8x2mYVZFb4f3HP0gLaoJCs1gDr0sIT%2Fkgc8QGPNUl8XLNlyP4LU16WH%0AhDiGknnnjIjQ11O4rQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158446/bb15824156999281b1ff101f/PIrlI6eniHthMjIv7UT69tlpDwqRxLQ4vA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=afe3ce757b0d0e074f887629ecb093c51aaedcc0b935eb6fe80ceafed8246247&req=fSInF8x2mYVZFb4f3HP0gLaoJCs1gDr0sIT%2Fkgc8QGPNUl8XLNlyP4LU16WH%0AhDiGknnnjIjQ11O4rQ%3D%3D%0A)

**8.** Copy the CNAME record names and values to and add them in **Valimail Enforce.**

**9.** Once you’re done, click **OK**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158454/7656bc470d456fa5c7292e75/zKJUk-0Y03OpNG8JO1EntYW15k3qzbBcTQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=17a29694786a98bf1cb9e335e3a34b83d6ba906a0d9e706c5bfd45797028fb34&req=fSInF8x2mYRbFb4f3HP0gI%2B6abyoLsD0xBUZ0MmMc%2Fmbr1ZznEg3a3aDZS%2F3%0AL4BUJkfwiaeE%2FmqFKQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158454/7656bc470d456fa5c7292e75/zKJUk-0Y03OpNG8JO1EntYW15k3qzbBcTQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=17a29694786a98bf1cb9e335e3a34b83d6ba906a0d9e706c5bfd45797028fb34&req=fSInF8x2mYRbFb4f3HP0gI%2B6abyoLsD0xBUZ0MmMc%2Fmbr1ZznEg3a3aDZS%2F3%0AL4BUJkfwiaeE%2FmqFKQ%3D%3D%0A)

**10.** Click **Got it**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158459/4fdce28c112db78762394e16/3K158IsKQg1kazoEzzlmv76_hcu3NSmZ9Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3951cd83c039251fd1a44adff93a0af6bf644761b28c1e24e65c66b2bd1a7e90&req=fSInF8x2mYRWFb4f3HP0gPA8CtW%2BOPnISmH6mTP8s41NonM46rdRo46hvUbo%0AQ%2Fmx2nzNcAt2QJNgsw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158459/4fdce28c112db78762394e16/3K158IsKQg1kazoEzzlmv76_hcu3NSmZ9Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3951cd83c039251fd1a44adff93a0af6bf644761b28c1e24e65c66b2bd1a7e90&req=fSInF8x2mYRWFb4f3HP0gPA8CtW%2BOPnISmH6mTP8s41NonM46rdRo46hvUbo%0AQ%2Fmx2nzNcAt2QJNgsw%3D%3D%0A)

**11.** Click **OK** to return to your account.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158462/11ebc550f546ca57e16e6730/Ycoa0Sh5MUMsKd9Is4s7RNr3300ogJaq7g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=bc3e0e0c91b3d0812841a51ca10eb60122ff2c4c73f033ed1a55076925571ecb&req=fSInF8x2mYddFb4f3HP0gLwYdMLc39GETRqPtkDZ8mHrioDDg6wa3gygZ%2Box%0AbxhIMkUuOUyCW%2B88NQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158462/11ebc550f546ca57e16e6730/Ycoa0Sh5MUMsKd9Is4s7RNr3300ogJaq7g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=bc3e0e0c91b3d0812841a51ca10eb60122ff2c4c73f033ed1a55076925571ecb&req=fSInF8x2mYddFb4f3HP0gLwYdMLc39GETRqPtkDZ8mHrioDDg6wa3gygZ%2Box%0AbxhIMkUuOUyCW%2B88NQ%3D%3D%0A)

**12.** About 24 hours after you've published the CNAME records into Valimail Enforce, click **Check status** or **Manage** to finish activating your self-authentication.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158465/dda0e42f8fcdbbbeaca7c085/QHsmvSQg0t3Yiy1WoVTc43cP7qhVgYROoQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8e82e9c7fa93771c27bcafe08d1fc2e4d9cb2f0c3bd98f12c950e32492417793&req=fSInF8x2mYdaFb4f3HP0gHFjJEW7uoY%2BvlW8gwgcI7nP2TMD649JQuWn40mj%0AqBDIDZyfTBUeH2XPZw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158465/dda0e42f8fcdbbbeaca7c085/QHsmvSQg0t3Yiy1WoVTc43cP7qhVgYROoQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8e82e9c7fa93771c27bcafe08d1fc2e4d9cb2f0c3bd98f12c950e32492417793&req=fSInF8x2mYdaFb4f3HP0gHFjJEW7uoY%2BvlW8gwgcI7nP2TMD649JQuWn40mj%0AqBDIDZyfTBUeH2XPZw%3D%3D%0A)

13\. Click **Activate**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158475/1cbaca4e55b80429b37d4f53/6HoktCUKju9oIX6FbYGexBto2Nnt863W_g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=87d8e56de411069feddc3b3f8b744a5232755a89cc290098d26642505a415734&req=fSInF8x2mYZaFb4f3HP0gDPBsxATCClLt%2FbjS%2Fj20Br54Cx6zGGxda%2F18pJ3%0AkYznQ9XKzAM7tX2jhA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158475/1cbaca4e55b80429b37d4f53/6HoktCUKju9oIX6FbYGexBto2Nnt863W_g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=87d8e56de411069feddc3b3f8b744a5232755a89cc290098d26642505a415734&req=fSInF8x2mYZaFb4f3HP0gDPBsxATCClLt%2FbjS%2Fj20Br54Cx6zGGxda%2F18pJ3%0AkYznQ9XKzAM7tX2jhA%3D%3D%0A)

You can find these instructions on how to configure DKIM in Constant Contact using CNAME records **[here](https://knowledgebase.constantcontact.com/articles/KnowledgeBase/5932-self-publishing-for-authentication?lang=en_US)
**.

Configuring DKIM authentication using a DKIM TXT record for your Constant Contact emails
========================================================================================

When you self-publish for authentication using a DKIM TXT record, Constant Contact generates a public/private DKIM key pair for you. We use the private key to sign your outgoing emails, while you publish the public key in the DNS records for your domain. This option is best if you have multiple Constant Contact accounts using the same domain.

**1.** Click the profile name in the upper-right and select **My Account**.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158484/a0ea1fbb341235cae36b19c6/e7_kir2GQko0RJQj9IyNBzzXgdqn0x6MKA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0d6daaac15fdd5fa459a236396cdb8070f700c26711be044c145000156e24d1b&req=fSInF8x2mYlbFb4f3HP0gEUcwryfO8%2BqzfTFFqdo9yOv1osXcnDSITY4gn6v%0AEDZopN2lOz9unzXMVA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158484/a0ea1fbb341235cae36b19c6/e7_kir2GQko0RJQj9IyNBzzXgdqn0x6MKA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0d6daaac15fdd5fa459a236396cdb8070f700c26711be044c145000156e24d1b&req=fSInF8x2mYlbFb4f3HP0gEUcwryfO8%2BqzfTFFqdo9yOv1osXcnDSITY4gn6v%0AEDZopN2lOz9unzXMVA%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click the **Advanced settings** tab.

**3.** Click **Add self-authentication**.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158490/df485212db73d11b6065657e/LHl-Mf8FxnGk4l6Uot-0o-ITJgl6u1vw0A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c9a5f91ae8485df6e5d243740b129c29d6835aeb6f42477c1d81f0dc12c8004c&req=fSInF8x2mYhfFb4f3HP0gF84x%2BdaQoRPC3UKXVL3M1CBjDAKam4CkNgaooZd%0AbJdFIbLcbpE4wGWWug%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158490/df485212db73d11b6065657e/LHl-Mf8FxnGk4l6Uot-0o-ITJgl6u1vw0A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c9a5f91ae8485df6e5d243740b129c29d6835aeb6f42477c1d81f0dc12c8004c&req=fSInF8x2mYhfFb4f3HP0gF84x%2BdaQoRPC3UKXVL3M1CBjDAKam4CkNgaooZd%0AbJdFIbLcbpE4wGWWug%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Select **Self-authenticate using DKIM TXT record**.

**5.** Click **Continue**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158501/e45c2919b722eedbf8eeeb8d/H7GXSoHT7P7XDnhTYnkNAhwQ667_Y-zEEA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=573778a00c814af06d7410fcca476f161ea1406f3426a9fd928f0d6c82d69ac6&req=fSInF8x2mIFeFb4f3HP0gCSkLKSSUFN%2Fa60PBT1E1lUS9XEgNzOrfoT9cFl5%0AQ0X3UjVjGa%2B9aCB0Ow%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158501/e45c2919b722eedbf8eeeb8d/H7GXSoHT7P7XDnhTYnkNAhwQ667_Y-zEEA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=573778a00c814af06d7410fcca476f161ea1406f3426a9fd928f0d6c82d69ac6&req=fSInF8x2mIFeFb4f3HP0gCSkLKSSUFN%2Fa60PBT1E1lUS9XEgNzOrfoT9cFl5%0AQ0X3UjVjGa%2B9aCB0Ow%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** From the drop-down, select the domain you want to use for self-authentication. If the custom domain you want to use isn’t listed, choose **Select another domain** from the drop-down to add and verify a new email address.

**7.** Click **Continue**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158504/2c2c8b55342b724831ad1c53/Q6spYQqrYbb5jon7IXy2qhx9N0WXNTL5zg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=73f4fb967faf1358e4c02cedf267dfe03b4c93c99815ed7bcd60bfee0376be2a&req=fSInF8x2mIFbFb4f3HP0gL5h8V5yCkKRFrTDygXrq7oe0mIqgv0Q4Dfe5bL3%0AoDaLNLuzST8Vs2i8Ow%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158504/2c2c8b55342b724831ad1c53/Q6spYQqrYbb5jon7IXy2qhx9N0WXNTL5zg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=73f4fb967faf1358e4c02cedf267dfe03b4c93c99815ed7bcd60bfee0376be2a&req=fSInF8x2mIFbFb4f3HP0gL5h8V5yCkKRFrTDygXrq7oe0mIqgv0Q4Dfe5bL3%0AoDaLNLuzST8Vs2i8Ow%3D%3D%0A)

8\. Click **Generate key**.

[![a screenshot of a message](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158511/04edc478ad04b0c549e7a855/yfHjZbdwp4gREAEBfqaI5zhYKg0m7ftmVA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f15e9f295e3879e440665b9fccbb041a3cdd219129f51e5223c084129f353b6c&req=fSInF8x2mIBeFb4f3HP0gCYwUzD%2BgnaTnlaXHFtovn1TPPvo%2Fcw8OujldrCC%0AZzhEu6zvYXGkjpML8g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158511/04edc478ad04b0c549e7a855/yfHjZbdwp4gREAEBfqaI5zhYKg0m7ftmVA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f15e9f295e3879e440665b9fccbb041a3cdd219129f51e5223c084129f353b6c&req=fSInF8x2mIBeFb4f3HP0gCYwUzD%2BgnaTnlaXHFtovn1TPPvo%2Fcw8OujldrCC%0AZzhEu6zvYXGkjpML8g%3D%3D%0A)

**9.** Copy the DKIM TXT record value and selector and add them in **Valimail Enforce.**

**10.** Once you’re done, click **Ok**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158520/280a9f5f0f96e66d33a2d275/001_GluOvnYKF9uzjxLiUqsGCr9rhXHPVQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e6071b2664d59cc4afd13ad1fba16f64c967cb8d13f3d26048bc3b566d3a249d&req=fSInF8x2mINfFb4f3HP0gLnfPxbKUE1hy6MVT8qlKPVA%2FiruWq0pdoPZsqZe%0ArHSnM2W8D9sH6qC3xA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920158520/280a9f5f0f96e66d33a2d275/001_GluOvnYKF9uzjxLiUqsGCr9rhXHPVQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e6071b2664d59cc4afd13ad1fba16f64c967cb8d13f3d26048bc3b566d3a249d&req=fSInF8x2mINfFb4f3HP0gLnfPxbKUE1hy6MVT8qlKPVA%2FiruWq0pdoPZsqZe%0ArHSnM2W8D9sH6qC3xA%3D%3D%0A)

**Note:** If emails are being sent on behalf of your domain from multiple Constant Contact accounts/instances, each account/instance will need a different private DKIM key, so the procedure described above in the Configuring DKIM authentication using a DKIMTXT record for your Constant Contact emails, will need to be repeated for each separate Constant Contact account/instance. You will have multiple public keys on your DNS to correspond to the private keys. DKIM keys are differentiated by the selector - in the above example, the selector is 10008432. Constant Contact uses numbers for the selector, but that's not always the case. For example, Google uses letters for the selector instead.

You can find these instructions on how to configure DKIM in Constant Contact using a DKIM TXT record **[here](https://knowledgebase.constantcontact.com/articles/KnowledgeBase/5932-self-publishing-for-authentication?lang=en_US)
**.

Add a DKIM key for Constant Contact DKIM in Valimail
====================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Constant Contact emails
===============================================================

Once you establish that Constant Contact is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

SPF alignment for Constant Contact emails
=========================================

Constant Contact does not support SPF alignment for their emails.

However, as long as you have DKIM configured for your domain in Constant Contact , your Constant Contact emails will pass DMARC authenticating with DKIM.

For an email to pass DMARC, it needs to pass with either SPF or DKIM, not both necessarily.

You can find out more information about their support for DMARC alignment **[here](https://knowledgebase.constantcontact.com/articles/KnowledgeBase/34717-SPF-Self-Publishing-for-Email-Authentication?lang=en_US)
**.

emBlue

emBlue
======

How to set up DMARC for emBlue in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754605-emblue#h_12b43fe247)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754605-emblue#h_4130341a7d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754605-emblue#h_6822b664f1)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **emBlue** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your emBlue emails
======================================================

**1.** Add the following TXT DKIM key in your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce:

selector: **epexo**

TXT VALUE: **MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQC92DPEfr8M28KVopiGVgDo3uYLBtSgNIsarAUjOTLiW2LGC+k2WrEBTIyk/XM+N6mUZc9mf7hlrw3zG2T975IPKk1eg4XnqDj45xpKplqyvDFgehuARlSzv1dp2NlqayECXihlFwrtzvXXoEv/9QiH3Efaii/GeMsVqYrxBNSHmQIDAQAB**

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for emBlue, **[here](https://help.embluemail.com/sender/spf-dkim-dmarc-configuration-improving-email-deliverability-prevent-email-spoofing-setup-guide/)
**.

Add a emBlue DKIM key in Valimail
=================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your emBlue emails
=====================================================

Once you establish that emBlue is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754568-duocircle#h_2a98844cde)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

DuoCircle

DuoCircle
=========

How to set up DMARC for DuoCircle in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754568-duocircle#h_1d6f91f838)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754568-duocircle#h_944a3968b3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754568-duocircle#h_2a98844cde)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **DuoCircle** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your DuoCircle emails
=========================================================

To begin signing your outbound email messages with DKIM open the **Sender Domains & DKIM** configuration page for your service, found under your account in the DuoCircle client area. DKIM signing is performed on a per-domain basis, so you must list on this page all domains which will send mail through your Outbound SMTP relay service. Once you have populated this list you may enable DKIM for specific domains by clicking the red "NO" button in the row of each domain.

[![dkim record page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920284677/a9258affe32c7d9bbbd0cf9f/ILll5PQQKcmb2C_0AwcNGsLaz09Y04xS1g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b377050b3a53ba649540fcf1eda8bbb72d9671c3115d8d03e21a6b0a5bd9b002&req=fSInFMF6m4ZYFb4f3HP0gOhdK7OSwk1s2PLPmDzHfluO4BIRf4BS9ay5Ajix%0AEvbPODGyIFtKGTCYTA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920284677/a9258affe32c7d9bbbd0cf9f/ILll5PQQKcmb2C_0AwcNGsLaz09Y04xS1g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b377050b3a53ba649540fcf1eda8bbb72d9671c3115d8d03e21a6b0a5bd9b002&req=fSInFMF6m4ZYFb4f3HP0gOhdK7OSwk1s2PLPmDzHfluO4BIRf4BS9ay5Ajix%0AEvbPODGyIFtKGTCYTA%3D%3D%0A)

This action will enable DKIM signing for that domain and also generate a new DKIM key if no previous key is already known to the system. The red "NO" button will turn to a green "YES" on success, and you will now see two options under the **DKIM tools** heading:

[![dkim record verified page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920284686/3fe723226fca0475cff7a288/040WgkLzKfx0-5V_59Ow6plavUFG_BKl5A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a481e3b1666f5bb8e8e6a4a96640894abcf44bb22790b087ec234884a24a0444&req=fSInFMF6m4lZFb4f3HP0gAAkVMgIRkVzYzyUuGLbER9LOtg81EPT8MZE8jYT%0AxB7Alu5z4IFzrBkbyg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920284686/3fe723226fca0475cff7a288/040WgkLzKfx0-5V_59Ow6plavUFG_BKl5A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a481e3b1666f5bb8e8e6a4a96640894abcf44bb22790b087ec234884a24a0444&req=fSInFMF6m4lZFb4f3HP0gAAkVMgIRkVzYzyUuGLbER9LOtg81EPT8MZE8jYT%0AxB7Alu5z4IFzrBkbyg%3D%3D%0A)

Click the magnifying glass button (left) will display the DKIM settings for this domain. To complete the process of configuring DKIM signing you will need to create a TXT record matching the provided values with your domain's DNS provider. DuoCircle immediately begins DKIM-signing your outbound mail from this domain, however recipients will not be able to verify the signature until this TXT record has been added and propagated through the DNS system.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for DuoCircle, **[here](https://support.duocircle.com/support/solutions/articles/5000713555-domainkeys-identified-mail-dkim-)
**.

Add a DuoCircle DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your DuoCircle emails
========================================================

Once you establish that DuoCircle is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Drip | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754560-drip#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754560-drip#h_04c5195efb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754560-drip#h_c24ae005fb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754560-drip#h_003b470c8b)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Drip

Drip
====

How to set up DMARC for Drip in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754560-drip#h_04c5195efb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754560-drip#h_c24ae005fb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754560-drip#h_003b470c8b)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM configuration process for **Drip**. Because this service leverages SendGrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Drip as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Drip infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Drip emails
====================================================

#### **Generate the CNAME records in the Drip dashboard**

**1.** Sign in to the Drip dashboard, click on **Settings** -> **Email Setup** -> **Sending Domain** -> **Open**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920264013/d3ab91fb8f5b44e873424fbf/yxopLc1B7A4O95K5Ymm_KvEyRb717uGNzA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5cf1da14daf5aacdb92b613a1b89df6e21961efe4e818ddca42b6934fef12794&req=fSInFM96nYBcFb4f3HP0gFzNl%2BIibBQN%2BptQ8OfdUA%2FMHCezeDNQqvxByido%0A9LM7EBY4NM7sNMcqQg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920264013/d3ab91fb8f5b44e873424fbf/yxopLc1B7A4O95K5Ymm_KvEyRb717uGNzA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5cf1da14daf5aacdb92b613a1b89df6e21961efe4e818ddca42b6934fef12794&req=fSInFM96nYBcFb4f3HP0gFzNl%2BIibBQN%2BptQ8OfdUA%2FMHCezeDNQqvxByido%0A9LM7EBY4NM7sNMcqQg%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click on **Change Sending Domain** and enter the domain you wish to configure. Drip will generate 3 CNAME records, **the first one needs to be published in your DNS**, and **the other two are DKIM keys**, **which need to be added in Valimail Enforce.**

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920264025/bc05ae275226de17c54541c6/d_GmfOrK5UX7Jcg8Og93evJeXr0VqqZGDA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=69da9eafc3063ff3fb742bfa8786f0bc661fbc7216944a7e899ffa8cea9f1398&req=fSInFM96nYNaFb4f3HP0gBq%2Bx5f6au%2BYfKXRPrZ79ccVTzNQPlu8DfsX3mYg%0AqfcPbNtOffhboU8UNw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920264025/bc05ae275226de17c54541c6/d_GmfOrK5UX7Jcg8Og93evJeXr0VqqZGDA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=69da9eafc3063ff3fb742bfa8786f0bc661fbc7216944a7e899ffa8cea9f1398&req=fSInFM96nYNaFb4f3HP0gBq%2Bx5f6au%2BYfKXRPrZ79ccVTzNQPlu8DfsX3mYg%0AqfcPbNtOffhboU8UNw%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Add the first CNAME record to **your DNS**. This record will point that subdomain to SendGrid, which will then publish an SPF record for it.

**Add the other two records in the Valimail app**. These are DKIM key records, which need to be added in the DKIM Keys section of your domain.

**Note**: _If the s1 and s2 selector names are already used by a different sender, you will need to reach out to Drip support and ask them to generate CNAME records that use custom selector names for example, drp and drp2._

**Validate the configuration in the Drip dashboard**

**4.** After you added the records in Enforce and your DNS respectively, sign in to the Drip dashboard, click on **Settings** -> **Email Setup** -> **Sending Domain** -> **Open**. Hover over your domain, and click on **Continue Setup**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920264031/53f97d845fc5287cd1806273/rq5mpIhY-jiOnZkCnRZkHTtbzAUUlLhpig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ff117ccfd4784089826e33032a6a0182094792ef420aca6d7ed66bf44b31cee3&req=fSInFM96nYJeFb4f3HP0gLjLm9x1ExhKWurNxGTR7lh4EcwXfx8Qg5pxDgIx%0AlR59u0tymMBYeGgrNQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920264031/53f97d845fc5287cd1806273/rq5mpIhY-jiOnZkCnRZkHTtbzAUUlLhpig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ff117ccfd4784089826e33032a6a0182094792ef420aca6d7ed66bf44b31cee3&req=fSInFM96nYJeFb4f3HP0gLjLm9x1ExhKWurNxGTR7lh4EcwXfx8Qg5pxDgIx%0AlR59u0tymMBYeGgrNQ%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** On the CNAME Records window, click on **I've added these records**.

If the validation is successful, Drip will start sending DMARC compliant mail on behalf of your domain.

You can also find more information on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Drip, **[here](https://help.drip.com/hc/en-us/articles/4424702626829-Email-Setup)
**.

Add a Drip DKIM key in Valimail
===============================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Drip emails
===================================================

Once you establish that Drip is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Emarsys

Emarsys
=======

How to set up DMARC for Emarsys in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754601-emarsys#h_d08dec23cb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754601-emarsys#h_ef16a71b5e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754601-emarsys#h_6128867f2c)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Emarsys** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Emarsys uses a dedicated subdomain setup.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Emarsys emails
=======================================================

**1.** To set up DKIM in Emarsys for your domain, please access this **[link](https://help.emarsys.com/hc/en-us/articles/4402729573777-SAP-Emarsys-DNS-settings#domain-settings)** and fill in the required subdomain and link fields with your correspondent ones as per the example below:

[![a close-up of a chat box](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920294696/909425299b0998cfe85e1ce7/QTi-YnXPzxoqpa8v_O7e0M1on00A5g7Hqg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2ff8b2d558c80475d56edae0fdbba7110e4903db9727468801bf7b357bb83f10&req=fSInFMB6m4hZFb4f3HP0gImq9rdyVuUo%2FVPyd8DWrZLCuD2yKhJNl%2B0h4UUb%0AUB%2Btmy3md02f1Np2GA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920294696/909425299b0998cfe85e1ce7/QTi-YnXPzxoqpa8v_O7e0M1on00A5g7Hqg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2ff8b2d558c80475d56edae0fdbba7110e4903db9727468801bf7b357bb83f10&req=fSInFMB6m4hZFb4f3HP0gImq9rdyVuUo%2FVPyd8DWrZLCuD2yKhJNl%2B0h4UUb%0AUB%2Btmy3md02f1Np2GA%3D%3D%0A)

Name: In the text boxes change the value according to your domain.

**2.** Once you fill in the info, click on **done** to get you settings.

Note: You are of your From domain and link domain that are associated with your organization or brand. Please make sure that your domains are properly specified because the fields in the table will be populated based

Important: If hosting your own replies: you must ensure you can receive all reply emails.

**3.** Please make sure to add all of the ensuing records in your DNS:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920294705/1b4005ce690fe132da18e6d6/iQlLdDmpNR7-f-XukGflqxFn1jDOCJHRoQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d47ff8245941fb16e3b855071a988c336ea9bc0cfca1f2adef58edc35a090a03&req=fSInFMB6moFaFb4f3HP0gBDKCpvS25%2BC0gJHy7%2BLjqM5otoommcWSg1rNvo8%0ATcX9xQZav3GRZE%2FfMA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920294705/1b4005ce690fe132da18e6d6/iQlLdDmpNR7-f-XukGflqxFn1jDOCJHRoQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d47ff8245941fb16e3b855071a988c336ea9bc0cfca1f2adef58edc35a090a03&req=fSInFMB6moFaFb4f3HP0gBDKCpvS25%2BC0gJHy7%2BLjqM5otoommcWSg1rNvo8%0ATcX9xQZav3GRZE%2FfMA%3D%3D%0A)

You can find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Emarsys, **[here](https://help.emarsys.com/hc/en-us/articles/4402729573777-SAP-Emarsys-DNS-settings#domain-settings)
**.

Add an Emarsys DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

**Note**: If the subdomain you have Emarsys configured on uses other services as well, you will need to manage it in Valimail and publish those 2 Emarsys DKIM keys in the platform.

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Emarsys emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Emarsys is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Drift | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754551-drift#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754551-drift#h_9acf4b67c0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754551-drift#h_6ac5df5af7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754551-drift#h_428be233e6)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Drift

Drift
=====

How to set up DMARC for Drift in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754551-drift#h_9acf4b67c0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754551-drift#h_6ac5df5af7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754551-drift#h_428be233e6)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Drift** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Drift emails
=====================================================

**Note**: You can only have one domain per drift instance.

To ensure that emails sent by Drift on your behalf such as Fallback emails and meeting reminders don't get marked as spam or junk you should authenticate those emails with DKIM.

**1.** To begin doing that, navigate into your **Settings** -> **App Settings** -> **Conversations** -> **Email Sending Domains**.

**2.** Click on **Add Domain**.

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260387/6f40aacd7cb04a6b1f4d99fd/WoRnGI1xiTz8LMWoL7wloyvQ8V2Xwh2qYA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=aa0dda6d67e3cbdfb5e51234226ad8eb23286f9983afba5408fffd51a1ba1c07&req=fSInFM9%2BnolYFb4f3HP0gJrXAYBNTj0g0QsF2CWTu7xJhOrYfeBwe0Epyvqz%0AyDGydpAUNMgs6kpyzQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260387/6f40aacd7cb04a6b1f4d99fd/WoRnGI1xiTz8LMWoL7wloyvQ8V2Xwh2qYA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=aa0dda6d67e3cbdfb5e51234226ad8eb23286f9983afba5408fffd51a1ba1c07&req=fSInFM9%2BnolYFb4f3HP0gJrXAYBNTj0g0QsF2CWTu7xJhOrYfeBwe0Epyvqz%0AyDGydpAUNMgs6kpyzQ%3D%3D%0A)

**Note**: We recommend that you setup a 2048 bit key.

**3.** Enter an example email address from the whitelabel domain that you want to add, and select an encryption level. 1024-bit is the default and recommended unless your IT team requires otherwise.

Then you'll see **Successfully created sending domain** after you hit **Save**. But you're not quite done! Above where you added the domain, you should now see that your domain needs verification:

[![a white rectangular object with a black border](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260390/3d243a9ad70dd07e89bd0c3c/D-8WuC1UqAlf2YVEFi8109TEW7T7INt56A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b80dbdc27bb50bcb32d766a807f07766c87f221de59de54293d8a374ad0c3bc5&req=fSInFM9%2BnohfFb4f3HP0gAaCdHXDnQ7XiZylqrosgHlzjr%2FJWXj2BiKGv%2FVF%0Acyr5tyfbq2QxWEnTXw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260390/3d243a9ad70dd07e89bd0c3c/D-8WuC1UqAlf2YVEFi8109TEW7T7INt56A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b80dbdc27bb50bcb32d766a807f07766c87f221de59de54293d8a374ad0c3bc5&req=fSInFM9%2BnohfFb4f3HP0gAaCdHXDnQ7XiZylqrosgHlzjr%2FJWXj2BiKGv%2FVF%0Acyr5tyfbq2QxWEnTXw%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Click the **Needs Verification >.** This will then give you a list of DNS entries that you will need in order to set up authentication.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260395/edfe29ec55d56cad01b08c52/Gq7UkmuV1Ka8SWRvGSfY8c5kCcePKgMcmQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4e014016d065da224abfd459a2b58693f2bdbc9983140a654938fb7aec546d8c&req=fSInFM9%2BnohaFb4f3HP0gEiZOjDVfwBd9P9wz3dgh2e9yokI%2Bqyc26vOXk3b%0AT%2Fb1%2FZsgM57fKa87lA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260395/edfe29ec55d56cad01b08c52/Gq7UkmuV1Ka8SWRvGSfY8c5kCcePKgMcmQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4e014016d065da224abfd459a2b58693f2bdbc9983140a654938fb7aec546d8c&req=fSInFM9%2BnohaFb4f3HP0gEiZOjDVfwBd9P9wz3dgh2e9yokI%2Bqyc26vOXk3b%0AT%2Fb1%2FZsgM57fKa87lA%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Use this information to add the DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce. It will look something like the following:

[![a screenshot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260401/7c4417ee44cac9e2fab035a7/FqfC-37c23n3Sy5jrkAmp-HOtD03A_Np4Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b482501e026f471d128dfa43b9ea1f529601fffd8a57037c0cbe02f6a1ed547c&req=fSInFM9%2BmYFeFb4f3HP0gHNUAkvii5hcWhBBRz3ZBnskJxk%2BQf6z7%2Bdfh36x%0AhbSRA%2FbzTIprB%2Bt8XA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260401/7c4417ee44cac9e2fab035a7/FqfC-37c23n3Sy5jrkAmp-HOtD03A_Np4Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b482501e026f471d128dfa43b9ea1f529601fffd8a57037c0cbe02f6a1ed547c&req=fSInFM9%2BmYFeFb4f3HP0gHNUAkvii5hcWhBBRz3ZBnskJxk%2BQf6z7%2Bdfh36x%0AhbSRA%2FbzTIprB%2Bt8XA%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** After your DKIM key is added in Valimail Enforce, return to Drift and click **Verify**.

[![a blue rectangle with white text](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260403/2ab7f1501dfa077595047cf7/DSe8HXZ-u46NiF63Qv9O7RFtG3cyO1E4eg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=32f43234d18944b1d60be76220b74df994b0cc839238645103553df9b9b5b84f&req=fSInFM9%2BmYFcFb4f3HP0gAOZNujVld%2B4sM4xpjFb512brJdNdI1KfQVSH4GY%0AdPXZLZnt%2BwahK28bPw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260403/2ab7f1501dfa077595047cf7/DSe8HXZ-u46NiF63Qv9O7RFtG3cyO1E4eg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=32f43234d18944b1d60be76220b74df994b0cc839238645103553df9b9b5b84f&req=fSInFM9%2BmYFcFb4f3HP0gAOZNujVld%2B4sM4xpjFb512brJdNdI1KfQVSH4GY%0AdPXZLZnt%2BwahK28bPw%3D%3D%0A)

Now, your domain should read as being Verified, and the TXT record should have a **Verified** status:

[![a white rectangular object with a white border](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260406/74cc72df6dc5e568e2df83e1/iOucr3VG3V9D-HfdMkiuYqpWp86ANgn9ig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=36df60e126f2790802b5ca0bac3608859f6933d4766564303f92350ede09b4b1&req=fSInFM9%2BmYFZFb4f3HP0gNdoyC2pWNYg5MF64eMpUsHpdq2GpxWopyYEBWEC%0AB5FPiW8AkuboFq0NLw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920260406/74cc72df6dc5e568e2df83e1/iOucr3VG3V9D-HfdMkiuYqpWp86ANgn9ig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=36df60e126f2790802b5ca0bac3608859f6933d4766564303f92350ede09b4b1&req=fSInFM9%2BmYFZFb4f3HP0gNdoyC2pWNYg5MF64eMpUsHpdq2GpxWopyYEBWEC%0AB5FPiW8AkuboFq0NLw%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Drift, **[here](https://gethelp.drift.com/s/article/Setting-Up-Email-Sending-Domains-DKIM)
**.

Add a Drift DKIM key in Valimail
================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Drift emails
====================================================

Once you establish that Drift is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Elastic Email | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754592-elastic-email#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754592-elastic-email#h_7327dff87e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754592-elastic-email#h_b5c1a9707b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754592-elastic-email#h_9ffe410913)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Elastic Email

Elastic Email
=============

How to set up DMARC for Elastic Email in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754592-elastic-email#h_7327dff87e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754592-elastic-email#h_b5c1a9707b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754592-elastic-email#h_9ffe410913)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Elastic Email** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your emails with Elastic Email
==================================================================

The first step in this process will be for you to verify your domain.

**1.** In order to verify your domain, navigate to **Settings** -> **Domains** -> **Manage Domains** screen.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920292056/622e824137fdd064a08698fb/V57rukp5B3vhO4pSvy8rnm7dkWNhmy3IpQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=67f99ac5ce12531f660bd0d63e6c7e12585fad6a12e5d90730d2f58cdefd8f02&req=fSInFMB8nYRZFb4f3HP0gDDvR9SfYBqBTmWgmaUreol1XJf5gRzGGOr2P%2BW2%0AFMRD2WpoJ4MJFPM66A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920292056/622e824137fdd064a08698fb/V57rukp5B3vhO4pSvy8rnm7dkWNhmy3IpQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=67f99ac5ce12531f660bd0d63e6c7e12585fad6a12e5d90730d2f58cdefd8f02&req=fSInFMB8nYRZFb4f3HP0gDDvR9SfYBqBTmWgmaUreol1XJf5gRzGGOr2P%2BW2%0AFMRD2WpoJ4MJFPM66A%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click **Verify domain** in order to bring up the **Add domain** popup. In here you can input your domain name and confirm it with the button with the same name at the bottom.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920292061/a8e7979a9915b5d3831e6d81/hfkE4xeclQMb6tk6pml23sOdvqItnQrYyw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4b1bc55df15655e33bd95f9906cbb485c1fffdf377107392efdf897cfc56cfed&req=fSInFMB8nYdeFb4f3HP0gECjZWG15IvFofX2%2BcMSCfAry73MN%2FV5FDYEL4Bb%0Azrbdc6KKTt0JTVWuCA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920292061/a8e7979a9915b5d3831e6d81/hfkE4xeclQMb6tk6pml23sOdvqItnQrYyw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4b1bc55df15655e33bd95f9906cbb485c1fffdf377107392efdf897cfc56cfed&req=fSInFMB8nYdeFb4f3HP0gECjZWG15IvFofX2%2BcMSCfAry73MN%2FV5FDYEL4Bb%0Azrbdc6KKTt0JTVWuCA%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** When your domain is added to the account, it's now visible in the domains list. If a blue star is present next to the domain name, it indicates that it's set as a Default Sender. You can enter a domain's details by clicking the **eye icon**.

[![a white rectangular object with a white rectangle](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920292063/2a9f66d0b6cbd7f2d7b67239/h58IqT3BbmLiszFYiRBDf1HnyrX2TtSixQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9271dde39b2360f4f94fc32ca7f6a18c855936b51dd7e3b239f6632315b70031&req=fSInFMB8nYdcFb4f3HP0gKyaBOsaJ1uj33WwZbwHc3gdjdY6mJZnXE5d9Fjd%0Al%2BZu98ETGmstkSy4LA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920292063/2a9f66d0b6cbd7f2d7b67239/h58IqT3BbmLiszFYiRBDf1HnyrX2TtSixQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9271dde39b2360f4f94fc32ca7f6a18c855936b51dd7e3b239f6632315b70031&req=fSInFMB8nYdcFb4f3HP0gKyaBOsaJ1uj33WwZbwHc3gdjdY6mJZnXE5d9Fjd%0Al%2BZu98ETGmstkSy4LA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** The following screen shows detailed information about your domain's verification status. Here, you can set your domain as default sender and check if your SPF, DKIM, Tracking, MX and DMARC records are properly verified.

**5.** Add in Valimail Enforce, the following DKIM key:

Selector: **api.\_domainkey**

Value: **k=rsa;t=s;p=MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQCbmGbQMzYeMvxwtNQoXN0waGYaciuKx8mtMh5czguT4EZlJXuCt6V+l56mmt3t68FEX5JJ0q4ijG71BGoFRkl87uJi7LrQt1ZZmZCvrEII0YO4mp8sDLXC8g1aUAoi8TJgxq2MJqCaMyj5kAm3Fdy2tzftPCV/lbdiJqmBnWKjtwIDAQAB**

**IMPORTANT**: _You will also need to add a CNAME record for tracking and an MX record, both to your DNS._

_You can only manage the DKIM, SPF and DMARC DNS zones in Valimail Enforce. Any other type of records like MX ones, need to be added in your DNS._

**6.** After you finished publishing all the needed records in both **Valimail Enforce** and your **DNS**, you must complete the verification for these records.

a. Go to your **Settings** -> **Domains** screen.

b. Add your domain (yourdomain.com). Click **Verify Domain**.

c. Green check marks mean that the record has been added correctly.

Please consult all the steps from this setup on how to configure DKIM, SPF, Tracking and MX record in Elastic Email **[here](https://help.elasticemail.com/en/articles/4934400-how-to-verify-your-domain)
**.

Add an Elastic Email DKIM key in Valimail
=========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Elastic Email emails
============================================================

Once you establish that Elastic Email is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Dyn Email Services | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754571-dyn-email-services#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754571-dyn-email-services#h_1785895cdd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754571-dyn-email-services#h_47485f7e1d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754571-dyn-email-services#h_793dd360ae)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Dyn Email Services

Dyn Email Services
==================

How to set up DMARC for Dyn Email Services in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754571-dyn-email-services#h_1785895cdd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754571-dyn-email-services#h_47485f7e1d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754571-dyn-email-services#h_793dd360ae)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Dyn Email Services** and how they are managed in Valimail. Adding Dyn Email Services as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Dyn Email Services infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Dyn Email Services emails
==================================================================

**1.** Once you login to your Dyn account as an admin, open the **Approved Senders** view.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920286635/c9cb868d7e85e85a97c1043b/lOX6AjzK0G22m5w51N-ARwROHQa6gP85hQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=880b11679f594cd908110743155017000406ab24ad2bbf68d78ddf795a29571b&req=fSInFMF4m4JaFb4f3HP0gAluGyEK7aFZjgszQxQ8DdOOUHXS%2FLoehxYYXEH4%0A5tuK%2FibRe2%2BBdVJ0vQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920286635/c9cb868d7e85e85a97c1043b/lOX6AjzK0G22m5w51N-ARwROHQa6gP85hQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=880b11679f594cd908110743155017000406ab24ad2bbf68d78ddf795a29571b&req=fSInFMF4m4JaFb4f3HP0gAluGyEK7aFZjgszQxQ8DdOOUHXS%2FLoehxYYXEH4%0A5tuK%2FibRe2%2BBdVJ0vQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Select the approved sender to add DKIM settings.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920286649/a3d963d73fd5bba8a1be815b/Ld4MmkA4vCKEeMJG5RDK_dlU0YR1IWfkPQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=861e62da73be947258549052a70af5602d087b9774979e6d768c33675b51a6b4&req=fSInFMF4m4VWFb4f3HP0gOlOFKkV%2BJBmG6eLt5uFM9CmvJIMK3ScMcOVK7dc%0AZMmSjSjwKHSR%2BC7A7g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920286649/a3d963d73fd5bba8a1be815b/Ld4MmkA4vCKEeMJG5RDK_dlU0YR1IWfkPQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=861e62da73be947258549052a70af5602d087b9774979e6d768c33675b51a6b4&req=fSInFMF4m4VWFb4f3HP0gOlOFKkV%2BJBmG6eLt5uFM9CmvJIMK3ScMcOVK7dc%0AZMmSjSjwKHSR%2BC7A7g%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Select **No** in the DKIM column to open the DKIM setup window.

You will be asked to enter a domain selector. A domain selector allows you to create and differentiate multiple DKIM records for one domain, giving you the ability to have separate DKIM records for each type of mail that you send, such as marketing, corporate, or transactional mail.

Follow the instructions in the DKIM setup window and click **Continue**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920286660/bd458e039a9dd7115899e7a0/RRhUYaM478N9wSNSCcBo-fLpu7vRY7L5TQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=53c7f7ac4419e1f9facca466a4384c50694f1034b2bb6380a48734ceb3c358ac&req=fSInFMF4m4dfFb4f3HP0gFcHynXSUnmT1V6UXphnRFSJV6joEMnsZn53jZ3X%0A6%2FbJfBQ094w%2BD2Sxkg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920286660/bd458e039a9dd7115899e7a0/RRhUYaM478N9wSNSCcBo-fLpu7vRY7L5TQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=53c7f7ac4419e1f9facca466a4384c50694f1034b2bb6380a48734ceb3c358ac&req=fSInFMF4m4dfFb4f3HP0gFcHynXSUnmT1V6UXphnRFSJV6joEMnsZn53jZ3X%0A6%2FbJfBQ094w%2BD2Sxkg%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** DKIM requires two TXT records to be created in your DNS configuration.

Use the generated hostnames in the DNS TXT Record 1 and DNS TXT Record 2 fields to create two new nodes in your DNS configuration.

For example, if you own the domain “yourdomain.com” and are trying to add a DKIM record for the approved sender “[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#91f5fef2e4fcf4ffe5f0e5f8feffd1e8fee4e3f5fefcf0f8ffbff2fefc)
” and you are using the domain selector “support”, the information in DNS TXT Record 1 should look like this: support.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com

In your DNS setup, create a new node (or cut zone) with the hostname “support.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com”.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920286664/8d11de1c8b940aa8992b426b/klGJvmpWfs6fgJvWTOZ8RHHohlFOVbY0mw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8c7d9ce02534cc25336d983b126315ebf1d17d9b3dacdd9cf521eb4f26430e47&req=fSInFMF4m4dbFb4f3HP0gFaVwPmtjUcLH6BdW38ah1lVDzCiEJ31NVEuwnaF%0AL0RQ3UkRscG0mh9cFQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920286664/8d11de1c8b940aa8992b426b/klGJvmpWfs6fgJvWTOZ8RHHohlFOVbY0mw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8c7d9ce02534cc25336d983b126315ebf1d17d9b3dacdd9cf521eb4f26430e47&req=fSInFMF4m4dbFb4f3HP0gFaVwPmtjUcLH6BdW38ah1lVDzCiEJ31NVEuwnaF%0AL0RQ3UkRscG0mh9cFQ%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Within each new node in your DNS setup, use the information in the Value/Data fields to create a TXT record within their respective nodes.

**6.** Add the newly created DKIM key in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM in Dyn Email Services **[here](https://help.dyn.com/dkim-records-for-approved-senders/)
**.

Add a Dyn Email Services DKIM key in Valimail
=============================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Dyn Email Services emails
=================================================================

Once you establish that Dyn Email Services is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Emfluence

Emfluence
=========

How to set up DMARC for Emfluence in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754609-emfluence#h_4054118f24)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754609-emfluence#h_facd661061)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754609-emfluence#h_2fc612cbaa)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Emfluence** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Emfluence emails
=========================================================

Emfluence issues a 2048bit TXT DKIM key to sign emails on behalf of your domain.

In order to setup DKIM in Emfluence for your domain, please reach out to **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#8bf8fefbfbe4f9ffcbeee6ede7feeee5e8eea5e8e4e6)
** to get started.

You can also find more on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Emfluence, **[here](https://emarketingplatform.com/blog/what-is-email-authentication-spf-dkim/)
**.

Add an Emfluence DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Emfluence emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Emfluence is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

FactuurSturen.nl

FactuurSturen.nl
================

How to set up DMARC for FactuurSturen.nl in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754660-factuursturen-nl#h_ac9f415256)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754660-factuursturen-nl#h_778b454318)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754660-factuursturen-nl#h_c9dd0b260c)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **FactuurSturen.nl** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your FactuurSturen.nl emails
================================================================

**1.** Log into the **FactuurSturen.nl** portal

**2\.** Navigate to **Settings** -> **Profiles** -> **Add DKIM**

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920332215/f28d5fab75fc77320125d02b/SI1zcy_9NPuGPy6gWG9P_YTo8JNzNPtzcg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2bd04f8b8439cc9932ba7ad555e3d424cbd5c58989e6e47e15dc73c47071d99c&req=fSInFcp8n4BaFb4f3HP0gM4151Ho4cocS2tPjhKRnBS58O66BAVfXuG3EWfn%0AI%2BgQfI6c2s2zCiJcIg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920332215/f28d5fab75fc77320125d02b/SI1zcy_9NPuGPy6gWG9P_YTo8JNzNPtzcg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2bd04f8b8439cc9932ba7ad555e3d424cbd5c58989e6e47e15dc73c47071d99c&req=fSInFcp8n4BaFb4f3HP0gM4151Ho4cocS2tPjhKRnBS58O66BAVfXuG3EWfn%0AI%2BgQfI6c2s2zCiJcIg%3D%3D%0A)

**3\.** Copy the DKIM key information and add it to the Valimail Enforce app.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM **[here](https://www.factuursturen.nl/help/106/hoe-schakel-ik-dkim-in/&q=dkim)
**.

Add a FactuurSturen.nl DKIM key in Valimail
===========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your FactuurSturen.nl emails
===============================================================

Once you establish that FactuurSturen.nl is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Emma

Emma
====

How to set up DMARC for Emma in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754611-emma#h_641228f084)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754611-emma#h_040d3d95c8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754611-emma#h_0af78be1ea)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(supported)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Emma** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

In this case, Emma **does not** send out SPF aligned emails however, we advise you to add the Emma service to your Enabled Senders list in Valimail, if Emma is an authorized sender for your domain.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Emma emails
====================================================

Before you can set up DKIM in your Emma account, you will need to add three CNAME records in **Valimail**.

Your three CNAME records should be associated with your sending domain and look like this:

Record Name: **e2ma-k1**

Record Value: **e2ma-k1.dkim.e2ma.net**

Record Name: **e2ma-k2**

Record Value: **e2ma-k2.dkim.e2ma.net**

Record Name: **e2ma-k3**

Record Value: **e2ma-k3.dkim.e2ma.net**

You will also need a TXT record to set up DKIM, which you can create from the validation hash code in your Emma account by following these steps:

**1.** Log into Emma and select the **Account** page from the dropdown menu next to your name.

**2.** Click on the last tab for **DKIM** to reveal your validation hash code.

[![a screenshot of a account](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920298559/aabd23310e0a2665f59f489d/MuMYvZ7SFQKdBvy19eNu8jD0qLaxN4boMA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=469c49996217a640398987a6dfd18f271aa226102082747308806a9cddc290d7&req=fSInFMB2mIRWFb4f3HP0gKqGBMRLdzPsPqP9SMDEeO9oZM%2B3Q0C3NGLHkGvB%0AnXJcWXwz8HvxxlDyww%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920298559/aabd23310e0a2665f59f489d/MuMYvZ7SFQKdBvy19eNu8jD0qLaxN4boMA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=469c49996217a640398987a6dfd18f271aa226102082747308806a9cddc290d7&req=fSInFMB2mIRWFb4f3HP0gKqGBMRLdzPsPqP9SMDEeO9oZM%2B3Q0C3NGLHkGvB%0AnXJcWXwz8HvxxlDyww%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Once you have that code, add in your **DNS** a TXT record that contains the following value for your domain: e2ma-verification=xxxx and be sure to replace “xxxx” with your validation hash code.

**4.** Once this is done, your CNAME records added in Valimail Enforce will have propagated.

**5.** The DKIM keys will need to be activated now. Please do so, by following the instructions below:

*   Navigate back to your **Account** page and select the **DKIM** tab.
    
*   Enter your sender domain (_mydomain.com_) in the **DKIM domain** field.
    
*   Clicking to **Save** will kick off a validation process where Emma checks to see if your CNAME and TXT records are active.
    
*   If everything aligns correctly, the **Save** button will turn blue for you to click on again to finalize the process.
    

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM in Emma **[here](https://support.myemma.com/s/article/DomainKeys-Identified-Mail-DKIM)
**.

Add an Emma DKIM key in Valimail
================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Emma emails
===================================================

Once you establish that Emma is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *
