Engaging Networks

Engaging Networks
=================

How to set up DMARC for Engaging Networks in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754619-engaging-networks#h_eef10855c9)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754619-engaging-networks#h_a42c23b548)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754619-engaging-networks#h_83f26ea5b2)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Engaging Networks** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Engaging Networks emails
=================================================================

**1.** Log in to your **Engage Networks** account.

**2.** Click on **Email** -> **Settings**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920304265/12bf2521f072f457577860f1/oIFq5rvJK5k_jtuuPFLrYsKzeGmPYkMYvw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=56cc11d20a0d838494b7fdacf8daf3dfa9a9c1f9408e6af70b57c3919c045259&req=fSInFcl6n4daFb4f3HP0gFXZ9dvRRfSxEwu%2BdVqkzZVs2NteOMXpPlOdFyVz%0AN8MU%2FrobPsH9YNY3Qg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920304265/12bf2521f072f457577860f1/oIFq5rvJK5k_jtuuPFLrYsKzeGmPYkMYvw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=56cc11d20a0d838494b7fdacf8daf3dfa9a9c1f9408e6af70b57c3919c045259&req=fSInFcl6n4daFb4f3HP0gFXZ9dvRRfSxEwu%2BdVqkzZVs2NteOMXpPlOdFyVz%0AN8MU%2FrobPsH9YNY3Qg%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Click on the **Domain Authentication** tab.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920304270/deb9d9137703033dbf04542d/d3RO9hphNLu_vthwYUAAbdjtF4h45vQdIA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=76085667f65b91b9ffa05689ec89ab5c3cf0e646948c9d5ca539edb711fbfcbe&req=fSInFcl6n4ZfFb4f3HP0gEg28umC9K4Ax3mG7HIXnsowGTO4PtXjQ%2BJv%2BqhW%0A7jcnFpMd0knNXFvDeQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920304270/deb9d9137703033dbf04542d/d3RO9hphNLu_vthwYUAAbdjtF4h45vQdIA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=76085667f65b91b9ffa05689ec89ab5c3cf0e646948c9d5ca539edb711fbfcbe&req=fSInFcl6n4ZfFb4f3HP0gEg28umC9K4Ax3mG7HIXnsowGTO4PtXjQ%2BJv%2BqhW%0A7jcnFpMd0knNXFvDeQ%3D%3D%0A)

This will list any sending domains you are using. Sending domains are the domains in the email addresses you use for thank you emails or email campaigns. For example, if you send an email from the sender [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#d2bab3a0a0ab92bfabb1bab3a0bba6abfcbda0b5)
, then the sending domain will be listed as mycharity.org.

**4.** Click on **Validate** next to the domain you wish to authenticate, in order to see your DKIM records.

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920304275/53b25d6a0e3335e53ecbc72d/ZcQiM49pzEbSal8m2ccF6KpetHKWXV44HQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=cc3fe48469529242e2121a011f2ef7362fd5a9b04c1724e6e10dd114d8aeb8e4&req=fSInFcl6n4ZaFb4f3HP0gAbolj5CN%2BUtKKgwZZuxWJyoL2%2BMUzyjARzoQPcJ%0A1Bn9wSW%2F40isRNeZ3w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920304275/53b25d6a0e3335e53ecbc72d/ZcQiM49pzEbSal8m2ccF6KpetHKWXV44HQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=cc3fe48469529242e2121a011f2ef7362fd5a9b04c1724e6e10dd114d8aeb8e4&req=fSInFcl6n4ZaFb4f3HP0gAbolj5CN%2BUtKKgwZZuxWJyoL2%2BMUzyjARzoQPcJ%0A1Bn9wSW%2F40isRNeZ3w%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys you will see, to your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add Engaging Networks to your Enabled Senders list.

**6.** After the CNAME DKIM keys are added in Valimail and the Engaging Networks service in your Enabled Senders list, come back to this window and click **Validate** again. If proper validated, you will get a green checkmark next to the DKIM and SPF records, like shown below.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920304285/1bff57b6cfa2194a1b66e68b/olbEYJgReLG8dhvly5roF5Fz5_mxlgUxBw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=75ef12faf26a2f4fb19a149641d0d08a97aa2fb0c9becbfbe4f9a9a714d0daa4&req=fSInFcl6n4laFb4f3HP0gH2cl8gr2cWrbx373e1LrrPKKXLqUdpqxWWOErFZ%0AkraOc4UU3G7yypq71w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920304285/1bff57b6cfa2194a1b66e68b/olbEYJgReLG8dhvly5roF5Fz5_mxlgUxBw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=75ef12faf26a2f4fb19a149641d0d08a97aa2fb0c9becbfbe4f9a9a714d0daa4&req=fSInFcl6n4laFb4f3HP0gH2cl8gr2cWrbx373e1LrrPKKXLqUdpqxWWOErFZ%0AkraOc4UU3G7yypq71w%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Engaging Networks, here.

Add an Engaging Networks DKIM key in Valimail
=============================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Engaging Networks emails
================================================================

Once you establish that Engaging Networks is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

FormAssembly

FormAssembly
============

How to set up DMARC for FormAssembly in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754716-formassembly#h_963fc46308)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754716-formassembly#h_977ef4341f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754716-formassembly#h_ccd625789c)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **FormAssembly** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your FormAssembly emails
============================================================

DKIM can be set up for **Enterprise Cloud** and **Compliance Cloud** accounts.

Please reach out to FormAssembly Support if you would like to have DKIM set up for your instance. The setup process may require 1-2 days to complete. With your support request, you will need to provide your EC/CC instance URL, the email domain for which you are requesting DKIM setup as well as the DKIM selector name (which must be a single alphanumeric string ex: mydkim1, no special characters) that you would like to use.

When setting up DKIM, it is recommended that you review the following FormAssembly Admin Dashboard settings:

**Admin Dashboard | Settings | General (Administrator)** - Support Email and Bounce Email

**Admin Dashboard | Settings | Miscellaneous (Notification Settings**) - Default Notification Email and Default Notification Sender

You can also find information about this procedure to set up DKIM on FormAssembly **[here](https://help.formassembly.com/help/email-notification-security-and-authentication)
**.

Add a FormAssembly DKIM key in Valimail
=======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your FormAssembly emails
===========================================================

Once you establish that FormAssembly is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754735-freshdesk#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754735-freshdesk#h_d1c46ed196)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754735-freshdesk#h_f827dda522)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Freshdesk

Freshdesk
=========

How to set up DMARC for Freshdesk in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754735-freshdesk#h_d1c46ed196)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754735-freshdesk#h_c3ce3b54de)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754735-freshdesk#h_f827dda522)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Freshdesk** and how are they managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

DKIM and SPF configuration for Freshdesk is done through a dedicated subdomain, like **fddkim.yourdomain.com**.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Freshdesk emails
=========================================================

**1.** Navigate to **Admin** from the menu. Select **Channels** and click on **Email**.

**2.** Click on the **Advanced settings** option towards the top right menu.

**3.** Select the **Configure DKIM** option.

**4.** Click on the **Configure** button next to support domain name.

**5.** Copy the system-generated settings (4 CNAME records). The **3 DKIM keys** will have to be published in **Valimail Enforce** and the **4th CNAME** record will have to be published in your **DNS**.

**6.** Atfer the 3 DKIM keys and the 4th CNAME record are published in Enforce and your DNS respectively, return to this Freshdesk configuration window and click the **Verify** button.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920374883/a582c186e51f812819951960/jwzovAhwV5sZ2bgp_viB2CBsbDdowPHI4A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f39424be5a639d102b678a7794da90f4477ff69b9f6b640fa861420711d2beb1&req=fSInFc56lYlcFb4f3HP0gAKvnR9KUKXcmKhgGnvI59PnE3n%2FZxgo1AxqVUVW%0A2nKAiUoo4OwCyZJgAg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920374883/a582c186e51f812819951960/jwzovAhwV5sZ2bgp_viB2CBsbDdowPHI4A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f39424be5a639d102b678a7794da90f4477ff69b9f6b640fa861420711d2beb1&req=fSInFc56lYlcFb4f3HP0gAKvnR9KUKXcmKhgGnvI59PnE3n%2FZxgo1AxqVUVW%0A2nKAiUoo4OwCyZJgAg%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Once verified, the system will send an email to the Admin stating that the DNS settings were verified for the particular domain, and below the Status column, each record will show the green verified checkmark.

[![a screenshot of a survey](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920374888/1b35a4690c86921bd49d53b7/Dl0wYJDctLWXTPM5_IAPbEU1GfspnNVQEw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e4c7b72eaf5ea8c5d45fe1015ed3f11440883995ebfabc7bc0c15c5335f1837d&req=fSInFc56lYlXFb4f3HP0gG1ZCLCasArZJ67vyHig09gIC3eZGYZ75abAt7W5%0ABlokvvcnDxCoMndfHA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920374888/1b35a4690c86921bd49d53b7/Dl0wYJDctLWXTPM5_IAPbEU1GfspnNVQEw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e4c7b72eaf5ea8c5d45fe1015ed3f11440883995ebfabc7bc0c15c5335f1837d&req=fSInFc56lYlXFb4f3HP0gG1ZCLCasArZJ67vyHig09gIC3eZGYZ75abAt7W5%0ABlokvvcnDxCoMndfHA%3D%3D%0A)

**8.** The account admin will receive an email after backend verification is complete for every domain.

You can find the instructions to set up DKIM for Freshdesk **[here](https://support.freshdesk.com/en/support/solutions/articles/50000005425-enable-dkim)
**.

Add a Freshdesk DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Freshdesk emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Freshdesk is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Everlytic | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754630-everlytic#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754630-everlytic#h_e42d218097)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754630-everlytic#h_3f359bf46e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754630-everlytic#h_09edba557a)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Everlytic

Everlytic
=========

How to set up DMARC for Everlytic in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754630-everlytic#h_e42d218097)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754630-everlytic#h_3f359bf46e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754630-everlytic#h_09edba557a)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Everlytic** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Everlytic emails
=========================================================

To verify and authenticate your domain using DNS records:

**1.** Select **Verify** and **Authenticate Domain by DNS** and click **Generate Records**.

**2.** On the following page, copy and paste the generated TXT and CNAME fields into the DNS section in your domain hosting settings.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add that TXT record in your DNS.

**3.** Once you’ve done this, select the **I have added these records** checkbox and click **Verify**.

**4.** Configuration will reflect on the Domains dashboard under the Verification tab once the relevant domain has been correctly configured.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Everlytic, **[here](https://help.everlytic.com/knowledgebase/my-profile/)
**.

Add an Everlytic DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Everlytic emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Everlytic is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

FortiMail Cloud Email Security

FortiMail Cloud Email Security
==============================

How to set up DMARC for FortiMail Cloud Email Security in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754719-fortimail-cloud-email-security#h_59d9e0a6ed)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754719-fortimail-cloud-email-security#h_9ce0202c76)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754719-fortimail-cloud-email-security#h_18606dc9ea)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **FortiMail Cloud Email Security** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your FortiMail Cloud Email Security emails
==============================================================================

In order to sign outgoing mail with DKIM signatures, you must configure the protected domain. In this example, a selector and DKIM key is generated for fortinet.com.

Note that DKIM signing settings only appear when configuring an existing protected domain.

**1.** Go to **Domain & User** -> **Domain** -> **Domain** and edit an existing profile.

**2.** Under Advanced Setting, click **DKIM Setting**.

**3.** Click **New**.

**4.** Enter a name in the New selector field.

Note that the selector name must match its corresponding domain name (in this example, fortinet.com)

**5.** Set **DKIM key** to **Auto Generation**. The key pair will be automatically generated and the public key exported for publication on a DNS server.

**6.** Click **OK**.

**7.** The new selector appears. Select the newly created selector and click **Download** to download the domain key DKIM file.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920371004/61d8d0c68e61cec139783be7/mn6CTL88SK_wsE2SNuAVhVU4-MD_ZQhctQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1f463ae676bbc9f7ac3748cff88921d8795539aeafe4e70d61e5b50e2fb81642&req=fSInFc5%2FnYFbFb4f3HP0gMnQ38LvrmOiPgmt5jLy%2FWguYU0P1h%2BOFJIowDuo%0AtYBc38m6npDZrb578A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920371004/61d8d0c68e61cec139783be7/mn6CTL88SK_wsE2SNuAVhVU4-MD_ZQhctQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1f463ae676bbc9f7ac3748cff88921d8795539aeafe4e70d61e5b50e2fb81642&req=fSInFc5%2FnYFbFb4f3HP0gMnQ38LvrmOiPgmt5jLy%2FWguYU0P1h%2BOFJIowDuo%0AtYBc38m6npDZrb578A%3D%3D%0A)

**8.** From the DKIM Setting window in FortiMail, select the newly created selector and click **Activate**.

**9.** Click **Close**, and click **OK**.

**10.** Add the DKIM key on your domain's configuration page in **Valimail Enforce**.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for FortiMail Cloud Email Security, **[here](https://docs.fortinet.com/document/fortimail/7.2.2/cookbook/776656/enabling-dkim-signing-on-fortimail)
**.

Add a FortiMail Cloud Email Security DKIM key in Valimail
=========================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your FortiMail Cloud Email Security emails
=============================================================================

Once you establish that FortMail Cloud Email Security is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

  
​

* * *

FastMail

FastMail
========

How to set up DMARC for FastMail in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754662-fastmail#h_a93ce3ef26)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754662-fastmail#h_75cd87fde7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754662-fastmail#h_270807db78)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **FastMail** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your FastMail emails
========================================================

**1.** When your domain is not correctly signed with DKIM, you may see a warning banner when you attempt to send mail from this domain, and you will see your domain listed as **Needs attention** on the **Settings → Domains** page.

**2.** If you click **Edit** on your domain, and then on **Get Instructions**, we will give you a list of DNS records to use to sign your domain.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the 2 FastMail CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for FastMail, **[here](https://www.fastmail.help/hc/en-us/articles/4627098536207-Why-do-I-need-DKIM-)
**.

Add a FastMail DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your FastMail emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that FastMail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Gainsight

Gainsight
=========

How to set up DMARC for Gainsight in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754770-gainsight#h_80363b7f29)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754770-gainsight#h_3b8ed4b1ad)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754770-gainsight#h_511b374757)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM configuration process for **Gainsight**. Because this service leverages SendGrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Gainsight as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Gainsight infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Gainsight emails
=========================================================

To set up DKIM in Gainsight for your domain, please reach out to Gainsight Support so they can provide your DNS settings. For more information on entering a support ticket, refer to the **[How to Submit a Gainsight Support Ticket](https://support.gainsight.com/SFDC_Edition/Administration_and_Permissions/Gainsight_Product_Support/How_to_Submit_a_Gainsight_Support_Ticket)
** article.

In order to generate these DNS settings, the support team will need to know the following things:

*   What sending domain would you like to use?
    
*   What is the Tenant ID for your production instance of Gainsight NXT? You can find Tenant ID at **Administration** -> **Application Settings**.
    

After this information has been collected, the support team will generate Domain & Email allow list in the form of CNAME records similar to the examples below:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920382856/bff64d8a235ce5dbe3ac50ac/4QG67ftUO4DtlXn2wK3A-vxSboi47fornA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b7bc6d847b20cc616279b0d8327d77723eac3e4f5fff22275dc256f7904469f0&req=fSInFcF8lYRZFb4f3HP0gMqQMLK6VsvSy9WuCp3xfuajdKgC1yxp%2BP4kM5pG%0AyES0JJcyeWlH1QEt4Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920382856/bff64d8a235ce5dbe3ac50ac/4QG67ftUO4DtlXn2wK3A-vxSboi47fornA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b7bc6d847b20cc616279b0d8327d77723eac3e4f5fff22275dc256f7904469f0&req=fSInFcF8lYRZFb4f3HP0gMqQMLK6VsvSy9WuCp3xfuajdKgC1yxp%2BP4kM5pG%0AyES0JJcyeWlH1QEt4Q%3D%3D%0A)

You will need to enter these settings in your DNS with the help of your IT team and communicate back to your support representative for confirmation. The 2 DKIM keys will need to be published in **Valimail Enforce**, and the rest of the records will need to be published in your **DNS**.

Once confirmation that the records have been properly published has been provided, our team will run a validation test to resolve these values. If this is completed without any problems, then no other steps will need to be taken. However, if issues do arise, then our team will work with your IT team to investigate further.

You can find more info about the DKIM set up process for Gainsight **[here](https://support.gainsight.com/Gainsight_NXT/01Onboarding_and_Implementation/Onboarding_for_Gainsight_NXT_in_Salesforce/Login_and_Permissions/Email_Service_Configuration)
**.

Add a Gainsight DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Gainsight emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Gainsight is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Delivra

Delivra
=======

How to set up DMARC for Delivra in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754526-delivra#h_f679e3ac93)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754526-delivra#h_6d8587bf47)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754526-delivra#h_bfe63b0163)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Delivra** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.  
Delivra does not send SPF alignment emails by default, so DKIM is the best way to configure this sender.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Delivra emails
=======================================================

**1.** In your ESP account, click on **Settings** icon at the top right, then **Account Management**,

then **Domain Keys**.

**2.** On the Domain Keys page, click the yellow **Create Domain Key** button.

**3.** Enter domain name, select key size, click **OK**.

**4.** Hover over the new record and click the blue **View** button on the right.

**5.** Copy and send the Domain Name, DKIM TXT Record Address, and DNS TXT Record values to the

individual who manages your DNS records (likely your IT department) to publish the record.

**Important**: If you manage your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail.

**6.** Enable signing once the TXT DKIM key/record has been published in Valimail/DNS.

**7.** Click on **Settings** icon at the top right, then **Account Management**, then **Domain Keys**.

**8.** Hover over your domain’s record, click the drop-down arrow next to **View** and click

**Enable Signing**.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Delivra, **[here](https://support.delivra.com/hc/en-us/articles/4403340180891-DKIM-Instructions-and-FAQ)
**.

Add a Delivra DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Delivra emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Delivra is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

  
​

* * *

# Front | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754762-front#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754762-front#h_30d9866b6e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754762-front#h_c8e1e00dee)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754762-front#h_7eedbacb6f)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Front

Front
=====

How to set up DMARC for Front in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754762-front#h_30d9866b6e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754762-front#h_c8e1e00dee)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754762-front#h_7eedbacb6f)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM configuration process for **Front**. Because this service leverages SendGrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Front as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Front infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Front emails
=====================================================

There is one deliverability settings page visible to Company Admins where you can configure records for all your email domains in one place. If you are not a Company Admin, you will not see this settings page and will need to get your admin’s help.

Follow these steps to add your SPF/DKIM records for each domain you use to send emails from Front.

**1.** Click the **gear icon** on the top right of Front and into the **Company settings** tab.

**2.** Click on the **Deliverability** tab. Here, company admins can configure SPF/DKIM records. _This page will only be visible to Company Admins after they add at least one team SMTP channel._

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381709/9c0a1a4812f25506e498df99/mcEM9JiW-e-ENKiRb8XxpQmRKA4nfeQDLg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f71b310b588390d2904ecc5ddd0e19014df6bce3698ef19602a8d59010ca3af0&req=fSInFcF%2FmoFWFb4f3HP0gJ9HD04H1BJQ8JDSvQXfh9qmJHBoMNRBhOxxEMNw%0AWD0ynlF%2FJ4SH9oPbRg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381709/9c0a1a4812f25506e498df99/mcEM9JiW-e-ENKiRb8XxpQmRKA4nfeQDLg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f71b310b588390d2904ecc5ddd0e19014df6bce3698ef19602a8d59010ca3af0&req=fSInFcF%2FmoFWFb4f3HP0gJ9HD04H1BJQ8JDSvQXfh9qmJHBoMNRBhOxxEMNw%0AWD0ynlF%2FJ4SH9oPbRg%3D%3D%0A)

**Note**: If you do not see the Deliverability tab, it is likely because only individual SMTP inboxes have been added. If this is the case, please follow the instructions **[here](https://help.front.com/t/80249r#individual-channels)
** to add SPF/DKIM records for individual SMTP channels.

**3.** You will see a list of your SMTP channel domains under **Domains** on the left. Any email domains with missing SPF/DKIM records will have a yellow warning icon. Select a domain to view its DNS records.

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381710/933e4a2169319571f6f0dfa5/i1m9oLnYMZjPAuEiYbxmxTOv4yK1nyeqmA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9824d2dfe5c062bc5d35bfc1e5cfee2fbe1eec51bb1d6f171c9d286badb5eadb&req=fSInFcF%2FmoBfFb4f3HP0gFfOqFFbmr9YlIboHBgT2Myn5bRRqosaRZw74TJM%0ANCbWUmAcP7r%2FTujMpA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381710/933e4a2169319571f6f0dfa5/i1m9oLnYMZjPAuEiYbxmxTOv4yK1nyeqmA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9824d2dfe5c062bc5d35bfc1e5cfee2fbe1eec51bb1d6f171c9d286badb5eadb&req=fSInFcF%2FmoBfFb4f3HP0gFfOqFFbmr9YlIboHBgT2Myn5bRRqosaRZw74TJM%0ANCbWUmAcP7r%2FTujMpA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Copy the Values from the DNS records in Front, and add them as a DNS record in your DNS provider settings. Each DNS provider offers a different UI to add these entries to the DNS. Please ask your IT department if you're unsure how to do this.

**Note**: If you want to manage the DKIM key in Valimail, the TXT DKIM key needs to be added in **Valimail Enforce**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381712/7a4d2975bcd088a178ed0f6a/aD3ofAOJALS2RRlzACId_vRGV2oa4AYhow.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f3690fead1918fd53be1e2c0739a63ee9df196badde1cd7abcfe68acc78b7657&req=fSInFcF%2FmoBdFb4f3HP0gM7Gjvurlz7rU0e8dh7Cyl5KY6VHM%2BvLBBaIQWOq%0AZR%2FMVkQ%2B5fSeN02qQw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381712/7a4d2975bcd088a178ed0f6a/aD3ofAOJALS2RRlzACId_vRGV2oa4AYhow.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f3690fead1918fd53be1e2c0739a63ee9df196badde1cd7abcfe68acc78b7657&req=fSInFcF%2FmoBdFb4f3HP0gM7Gjvurlz7rU0e8dh7Cyl5KY6VHM%2BvLBBaIQWOq%0AZR%2FMVkQ%2B5fSeN02qQw%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Once you've added the records in Valimail Enforce and in your DNS, you can come back to Front and click **Check DNS settings**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381720/fd5407a7126cad760bc2c4b8/opkqARkDPq9PwCsJxYpqU-ISTTaGlqV0Cg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ca9bb1dc879c9d8630c129abeda3884745c60754143ed7ece44a1997ed8c80ed&req=fSInFcF%2FmoNfFb4f3HP0gHruL%2BRhjjFPoE1ZFS887gaBlLuBgz8b2%2FIPmX4D%0AVXcOelYW8XghbcjGiw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381720/fd5407a7126cad760bc2c4b8/opkqARkDPq9PwCsJxYpqU-ISTTaGlqV0Cg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ca9bb1dc879c9d8630c129abeda3884745c60754143ed7ece44a1997ed8c80ed&req=fSInFcF%2FmoNfFb4f3HP0gHruL%2BRhjjFPoE1ZFS887gaBlLuBgz8b2%2FIPmX4D%0AVXcOelYW8XghbcjGiw%3D%3D%0A)

This will trigger a check by SendGrid to verify that your DNS configuration is correct. Be aware that SendGrid might not see your changes before the time specified in your DNS TTL settings. Wait a few seconds and the validation result will appear (with the encountered errors if any).

**6.** When you’ve successfully added the SPF/DKIM records, the warning icon will be removed from the channel name in the list and the DNS records will say **Verified**.

[![a screenshot of a phone](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381721/158dfe0ee2927456484fc857/9AFmvb72eXf-NIroWMYvL5ZDn9BLpOayOg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3e3280e70e5711e4ea22297b0390123263b4db4ce62bf871bb856551853c84b5&req=fSInFcF%2FmoNeFb4f3HP0gArBhF3O%2FHSW1im0YvC36aXpfi%2BlCLp9Cnnk%2F3an%0AfdwEOnJpbnKPuY6fsw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920381721/158dfe0ee2927456484fc857/9AFmvb72eXf-NIroWMYvL5ZDn9BLpOayOg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3e3280e70e5711e4ea22297b0390123263b4db4ce62bf871bb856551853c84b5&req=fSInFcF%2FmoNeFb4f3HP0gArBhF3O%2FHSW1im0YvC36aXpfi%2BlCLp9Cnnk%2F3an%0AfdwEOnJpbnKPuY6fsw%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions to set up DKIM and SPF for Front **[here](https://help.front.com/t/80249r/deliverability-settings-and-spfdkim)
**.

Add a Front DKIM key in Valimail
================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Front emails
====================================================

Once you establish that Front is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Flywheel | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754708-flywheel#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754708-flywheel#h_5fc723aacf)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754708-flywheel#h_efa592ff25)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754708-flywheel#h_82ec5a0a17)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Flywheel

Flywheel
========

How to set up DMARC for Flywheel in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754708-flywheel#h_5fc723aacf)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754708-flywheel#h_efa592ff25)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754708-flywheel#h_82ec5a0a17)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Flywheel** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Flywheel** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Flywheel emails
========================================================

**1.** Go to the **Advanced** tab and look for the **Email Deliverability** card.

**Note**: If you don’t see the Email Deliverability card you will need to [reach out to support](http://app.getflywheel.com/help)
 for help.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367898/2d57f903dd418ecfb016c436/yJzbSX4sBe9GwiwqF-KTIQ-_9mUYsFlWEA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3d4aa5ca450ffc39bac6222ba19767f77df8a1e96e70842b84eb07e2d768305b&req=fSInFc95lYhXFb4f3HP0gFlpoD6IHeOwK2ossk2deLSK8IdNYTxIgNRh0NQ3%0AevTi%2FyBlLQSDI%2BPSMg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367898/2d57f903dd418ecfb016c436/yJzbSX4sBe9GwiwqF-KTIQ-_9mUYsFlWEA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3d4aa5ca450ffc39bac6222ba19767f77df8a1e96e70842b84eb07e2d768305b&req=fSInFc95lYhXFb4f3HP0gFlpoD6IHeOwK2ossk2deLSK8IdNYTxIgNRh0NQ3%0AevTi%2FyBlLQSDI%2BPSMg%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click **Set up email**.

[![a screenshot of a website](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367905/7a021ce749455d08703413fc/BvrIMjJOQ_kHoIxlSqAFKey-sh0OY-j1jQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=664ec50c8d54904509af1847fd6ea0fa2fc966d80c80b99b89460caefd90ff91&req=fSInFc95lIFaFb4f3HP0gBo5V22Rassi4M8EBHWekfirH6XwsIQvATrWU7zZ%0ASuI9PpsjFb%2FASimATQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367905/7a021ce749455d08703413fc/BvrIMjJOQ_kHoIxlSqAFKey-sh0OY-j1jQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=664ec50c8d54904509af1847fd6ea0fa2fc966d80c80b99b89460caefd90ff91&req=fSInFc95lIFaFb4f3HP0gBo5V22Rassi4M8EBHWekfirH6XwsIQvATrWU7zZ%0ASuI9PpsjFb%2FASimATQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Select the domain you want to authenticate. A pop-up will appear asking you to select the domain that you want to authenticate. Simply choose the domain from the dropdown and then click **Set Domain and Begin Setup**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367909/f7a9b0184594203ee855268f/QhuNG8uHm4RcxymR8eGMo8VkIDO_LUn1Ng.png?expires=1755792000&signature=da7aec77d1e7f53d5dccd761b7ee5bcc018365b8f5a354543a09064e19efb60d&req=fSInFc95lIFWFb4f3HP0gHWO6FrI7AyUd15SvGlFZ11JwJULTqDbzlHG6%2B85%0AOrgnxbjiyQKE3Alskg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367909/f7a9b0184594203ee855268f/QhuNG8uHm4RcxymR8eGMo8VkIDO_LUn1Ng.png?expires=1755792000&signature=da7aec77d1e7f53d5dccd761b7ee5bcc018365b8f5a354543a09064e19efb60d&req=fSInFc95lIFWFb4f3HP0gHWO6FrI7AyUd15SvGlFZ11JwJULTqDbzlHG6%2B85%0AOrgnxbjiyQKE3Alskg%3D%3D%0A)

**Note**: Only the domains that have been added to your site will appear in the domain drop down. If you have not added your domain to Flywheel, you will not be able to authenticate that domain with SendGrid.

**4.** Add CNAME records to your DNS provider. After you select your domain, you’ll be redirected to a page that will explain how to add the CNAME records. Follow the instructions to add them, then come back to this page and click **Check Validation**.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add the 3rd CNAME record in your DNS.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367915/5f0641411612f39879dd326d/JnPS-fjgtByoWa3TtFgPZgM1Ks0AADT09Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=94a1cd5f5a1aa3a5bf959d83225b69ce765e50367701727e48dad5a82c578ba4&req=fSInFc95lIBaFb4f3HP0gPEt60P7%2FyGoGrwJdTlFzrfwVyqsZYdroMFzVySR%0AGR2AcPW5EZXClCDKIA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367915/5f0641411612f39879dd326d/JnPS-fjgtByoWa3TtFgPZgM1Ks0AADT09Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=94a1cd5f5a1aa3a5bf959d83225b69ce765e50367701727e48dad5a82c578ba4&req=fSInFc95lIBaFb4f3HP0gPEt60P7%2FyGoGrwJdTlFzrfwVyqsZYdroMFzVySR%0AGR2AcPW5EZXClCDKIA%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** We’ll make sure the validation worked. After you add the CNAME records in Valimail and to your DNS provider and click **Ok, I’m Done**, we’ll do some verifying on our end and then let you know whether or not the CNAME records were successfully added. If they weren’t, we’ll show you which records errored out so you can try and fix those with your DNS provider.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367920/85a2bb99256f886fbfc09bdf/IV7gloysXCDkdZ3qzfJPshsyQ2uGJFcT4g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dd23f48e006b58f00f679c4649e7f50d17130785558edfc026dd9dadcda47614&req=fSInFc95lINfFb4f3HP0gO7c%2BDmKHmcBIUgytGZbYH%2FtakzZL1UsIZ%2FqxoX5%0AaGrUy9CLcShxkjST8g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367920/85a2bb99256f886fbfc09bdf/IV7gloysXCDkdZ3qzfJPshsyQ2uGJFcT4g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dd23f48e006b58f00f679c4649e7f50d17130785558edfc026dd9dadcda47614&req=fSInFc95lINfFb4f3HP0gO7c%2BDmKHmcBIUgytGZbYH%2FtakzZL1UsIZ%2FqxoX5%0AaGrUy9CLcShxkjST8g%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** When everything validates successfully, you’ll be taken back to the Advanced page with a success banner and an indicator in the card that your domain was successfully authenticated.

[![Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367922/b924293e755c79ba73b94bf3/DdPRwKXz5wM6FAPpifdrBVThFgHyiSPygA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=cda63877a67a2b27e431156ca024faee310a9ff1f602dafd152b0b59e575758a&req=fSInFc95lINdFb4f3HP0gHqKtuDT6rbx%2BSrfyxWOZhqHTM2FPkk8U6Ipv39A%0AEnUeRmacROKZo8%2FyKQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://intercom.help/valimail-4dffb6ef6e39/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

**Note**: You can always check the status of your domain authentication set-up in the Email Deliverability card on the Advanced tab. An orange banner means that you didn’t finish the set-up and a red banner means that your records didn’t validate correctly with your DNS provider. Double check that the CNAME records are correct and then try again.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367929/262638e8bb7663ae44bf37fc/NID8LGOptqFsfo6_nkGgeiYb0F4TjPRyHg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=80b7f54c922837fd7d822a9170a2057d92b99fc17c3ef52df9443e846a8d1df5&req=fSInFc95lINWFb4f3HP0gCLJ2rb4%2BG6rFeeOr5AKOsDXQEV%2FfZMoNPqWAJAg%0AMisjCtL7wZDdOBrEsw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920367929/262638e8bb7663ae44bf37fc/NID8LGOptqFsfo6_nkGgeiYb0F4TjPRyHg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=80b7f54c922837fd7d822a9170a2057d92b99fc17c3ef52df9443e846a8d1df5&req=fSInFc95lINWFb4f3HP0gCLJ2rb4%2BG6rFeeOr5AKOsDXQEV%2FfZMoNPqWAJAg%0AMisjCtL7wZDdOBrEsw%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Flywheel, **[here](https://getflywheel.com/wordpress-support/sending-email-on-flywheel/)
**.

Add a Flywheel DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Flywheel emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Flywheel is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Greenvelope | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754793-greenvelope#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754793-greenvelope#h_195bb36e19)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754793-greenvelope#h_f980a273bc)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754793-greenvelope#h_eb1bce3838)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Greenvelope

Greenvelope
===========

How to set up DMARC for Greenvelope in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754793-greenvelope#h_195bb36e19)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754793-greenvelope#h_f980a273bc)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754793-greenvelope#h_eb1bce3838)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the process of authenticating emails sent from the **Greevelope** service on behalf of your organization's domain(s) and how the authentication records are managed in Valimail.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Greenvelope emails
===========================================================

Greenvelope uses a single standard TXT DKIM key:

**gv**.\_domainkey

"v=DKIM1; k=rsa; p=**MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQCl43nwaq3SW8v7Bu5OWdVfWlfGg7tDC3OUqoldlUwW1dZH1DCbYXUBoI7aKtAdlbU8+4DUK53vTUrb6rQVbeoL9lQ9596e/Vt3HwqViOTSfM8WQuCXqkDN2D+q73wJ6y9DgpAFVONwIIVWd8jZQ32+AAGiB7FJngjXyyA6Y97dCwIDAQAB**"

Once the DKIM key has been added to the Valimail Platform please perform these steps within **Greenvelope Admin Console**:

**1.** Go to the **Details** tab.

**2.** Go to **Information**.

**3.** Open the **Advanced Options** dropdown.

**4.** Click to **purchase/turn on** this feature.

[![Advanced Options menu](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920388144/0ed7663b90ebe04e8f1ed6fa/mGvP2Nlrzm4TNa0PrpeXMxh-1JAwHOyBMA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=db98e25d347eab6b5a15e9e8aa4ba33b7cb33988609c2b0973c8e6792273a0fc&req=fSInFcF2nIVbFb4f3HP0gLix%2BhtEKnOiRB5k0KHefREMjzml2%2FphWSsjW02W%0AwDBqNxqTCKlnvbVXjg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920388144/0ed7663b90ebe04e8f1ed6fa/mGvP2Nlrzm4TNa0PrpeXMxh-1JAwHOyBMA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=db98e25d347eab6b5a15e9e8aa4ba33b7cb33988609c2b0973c8e6792273a0fc&req=fSInFcF2nIVbFb4f3HP0gLix%2BhtEKnOiRB5k0KHefREMjzml2%2FphWSsjW02W%0AwDBqNxqTCKlnvbVXjg%3D%3D%0A)

**Note**: Screenshot from the Vendor's configuration page.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM **[here](https://support.greenvelope.com/hc/en-us/articles/360022921914-Set-Up-DKIM-Business-Accounts-Only-Premium-Feature-)
**.

Add a Greevelope DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Greenvelope emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that Greenvelope is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

GetResponse

GetResponse
===========

How to set up DMARC for GetResponse in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754772-getresponse#h_6a394c1843)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754772-getresponse#h_fc614e082a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754772-getresponse#h_89ba2793fe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754772-getresponse#h_855cdcf8eb)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **GetResponse** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your GetResponse emails
===========================================================

Generate a DKIM key in GetResponse
----------------------------------

**1.** In the GetResponse Dashboard, click on the **profile icon** -> **Manage account** -> **Email addresses**.

**2.** Click on **Authenticate** next to the domain name you would like to generate the DKIM key for.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920383620/5aacecccfee6afb66a260e38/16Bklh3azXrDYJis68KCCkdJMXM8nr2SuQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dd879965a7322db58aef92a05fd01cad8e7d249ff5deb1eee2a704acaae84ede&req=fSInFcF9m4NfFb4f3HP0gMHtUkw8%2FAQNSSA333y1SMDeDElYtfECDGxzYZMK%0Amo%2FgwTc7ekUsrHTpRg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920383620/5aacecccfee6afb66a260e38/16Bklh3azXrDYJis68KCCkdJMXM8nr2SuQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dd879965a7322db58aef92a05fd01cad8e7d249ff5deb1eee2a704acaae84ede&req=fSInFcF9m4NfFb4f3HP0gMHtUkw8%2FAQNSSA333y1SMDeDElYtfECDGxzYZMK%0Amo%2FgwTc7ekUsrHTpRg%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Copy TXT host and value from the pop-up window:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920383622/a04b8c66105d7cf76140db28/Qh1PXqNKayqCoKzrfVaNeVGcpWf9Fcp9Cw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7e6285b75f6ff2be448db2a10acaad9ccbc87fee850004789ba759cf472e23b5&req=fSInFcF9m4NdFb4f3HP0gAPmjmD94cgt9LxeOmdebgzafYzMRLNKnCPRI4rh%0Azdj7H1QWnBBjrVfnDQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920383622/a04b8c66105d7cf76140db28/Qh1PXqNKayqCoKzrfVaNeVGcpWf9Fcp9Cw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7e6285b75f6ff2be448db2a10acaad9ccbc87fee850004789ba759cf472e23b5&req=fSInFcF9m4NdFb4f3HP0gAPmjmD94cgt9LxeOmdebgzafYzMRLNKnCPRI4rh%0Azdj7H1QWnBBjrVfnDQ%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Add the TXT DKIM key in Valimail Enforce using the instructions in the next article chapter.

**5.** Once the DKIM key is added in Valimail Enforce, make sure the DKIM record shows as verified/enabled in GetResponse.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for GetResponse **[here](https://www.getresponse.com/help/what-is-dkim-and-how-do-i-configure-it.html)
**.

Add a GetResponse DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your GetResponse emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that GetResponse is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754739-freshservice#h_bb0df08084)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Freshservice

Freshservice
============

How to set up DMARC for Freshservice in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754739-freshservice#h_716da95259)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754739-freshservice#h_94260ee149)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754739-freshservice#h_bb0df08084)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Freshservice** and how are they managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

DKIM and SPF configuration for Freshservice is done through a dedicated subdomain, like **fsdkim.yourdomain.com**

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Freshservice emails
============================================================

**1.** Log in to your Freshservice account as an admin.

**2.** Go to **Admin** -> **Support Channels** -> **Email** -> **Set up SPF and DKIM email authentication**.

**3.** If you've set up an email address that uses a custom domain name and choose to send emails via Freshservice Mail Server, the domain name will appear on this page automatically if the email address is verified. You cannot add or update the domain name here.

**4.** Click on **Get Started** for the email domain(s) present on this page.

**5.** Copy the 3 DKIM key records and add them in **Valimail Enforce**. Additionally, copy and publish the 4th CNAME record in your **DNS** directly - this will allow Freshservice to publish an SPF record for the selected subdomain.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| **hostname** | **value** |
| fs.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com | wl\*\*\*\*\*\*s1.domainkey.freshemail.io |
| fs2.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com | wl\*\*\*\*\*\*s2.domainkey.freshemail.io |
| fs3.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com | wl\*\*\*\*\*\*s3.domainkey.freshemail.io |
| fsdkim.yourdomain.com | spfmx3.domainkey.freshemail.io |

**6.** Once you have completed adding the records, you need to return to Freshservice and verify your domain. Expand the domain settings on the same page and click on **Verify**. If the DNS settings are published correctly, it will show the status as **Verified**. The status of each record will also be reflected by a tick/cross.

**After Verification in Freshservice**

*   An email is sent to the **account** and **super admins** when the verification is complete. One email is sent for each domain name you have configured and verified.
    

You can find the instructions on how to setup DKIM in Freshservice **[here](https://support.freshservice.com/en/support/solutions/articles/50000003933-step-by-step-guide-to-add-spf-and-dkim-email-authentication-in-freshservice)
**.

Add a Freshservice DKIM key in Valimail
=======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Freshservice emails
===========================================================

Once you establish that Freshservice is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

GoodData

GoodData
========

How to set up DMARC for GoodData in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754777-gooddata#h_01d528d0ef)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754777-gooddata#h_f3fdc307c6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754777-gooddata#h_f24a61a57c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754777-gooddata#h_6b09ef6e87)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **GoodData** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your GoodData emails
========================================================

Please reach out to GoodData Customer Support or to your Account Manager on their side for providing you a DKIM key that can sign emails for your domain.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF **[here](https://help.gooddata.com/doc/free/en/expand-your-gooddata-platform/gooddata-integration-into-your-application/white-label-your-domain/configure-dkim-for-outgoing-email/?scroll-translations:language-key=ja_jp&search=spf)
**.

Add a GoodData DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Marketo emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Marketo is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in GoodData for your domain
================================================

You may notice that GoodData emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added GoodData to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by GoodData not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by configuring a CNAME record.

More details on how to achieve this are found **[here](https://support.gooddata.com/hc/en-us)
**.

* * *

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Freshmail

Freshmail
=========

How to set up DMARC for Freshmail in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754737-freshmail#h_9e30fe1738)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754737-freshmail#h_8535845875)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754737-freshmail#h_26e30404fb)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **FreshMail** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your FreshMail emails
=========================================================

**1.** Select **Settings** in the top right corner of the screen.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920375466/e15eb4f912c3029dd4b9d852/UX2rTH_foVdn9Q9NvfPZ80RdaOOumAx8aw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8c3d1dc9e03c365677ef81da0a583c0203f9b59d809225aa48e11f3cc1a727d2&req=fSInFc57mYdZFb4f3HP0gOzG5EJLacrhnCy%2BN63lmXkgVmTJWTyH8Y9vnl2V%0Aal3mONtZ28gdLXG1eA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920375466/e15eb4f912c3029dd4b9d852/UX2rTH_foVdn9Q9NvfPZ80RdaOOumAx8aw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8c3d1dc9e03c365677ef81da0a583c0203f9b59d809225aa48e11f3cc1a727d2&req=fSInFc57mYdZFb4f3HP0gOzG5EJLacrhnCy%2BN63lmXkgVmTJWTyH8Y9vnl2V%0Aal3mONtZ28gdLXG1eA%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Next, click on the **DKIM verification** in the menu on the left.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920375468/27ad6c56799941ba22464727/xuAdPqvzwCU96yWWgSszH7BF0aBdOEqn1g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=df88524155932a6bfe8a409f7a285f8e0678b0493cc7f427be2dd3779c062139&req=fSInFc57mYdXFb4f3HP0gNJEDsbl4M%2FTcddUEbqffD8bw8jkvJbz9OWNJmUN%0Ac7Oz2jNk%2F4%2FWxr2pTg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920375468/27ad6c56799941ba22464727/xuAdPqvzwCU96yWWgSszH7BF0aBdOEqn1g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=df88524155932a6bfe8a409f7a285f8e0678b0493cc7f427be2dd3779c062139&req=fSInFc57mYdXFb4f3HP0gNJEDsbl4M%2FTcddUEbqffD8bw8jkvJbz9OWNJmUN%0Ac7Oz2jNk%2F4%2FWxr2pTg%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** You then need to click the **Authenticate new domain**.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920375469/77042e873c3d9e018108339d/XFdkU3OUGXg1C5t0XgQlKuGiZ7F6GUkYfw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4fa4709b515ceab2bbccb070aaca562341ef3abe6559acf6ffbf083a28101b52&req=fSInFc57mYdWFb4f3HP0gM29FYALhhLC6AaihdWdiT9uWY%2FsLNdkc3FgZXwt%0A6OnwW7sJ8%2Fx5PdYW0w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920375469/77042e873c3d9e018108339d/XFdkU3OUGXg1C5t0XgQlKuGiZ7F6GUkYfw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4fa4709b515ceab2bbccb070aaca562341ef3abe6559acf6ffbf083a28101b52&req=fSInFc57mYdWFb4f3HP0gM29FYALhhLC6AaihdWdiT9uWY%2FsLNdkc3FgZXwt%0A6OnwW7sJ8%2Fx5PdYW0w%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** This will open a domain authentication window for DKIM settings. Enter the address of the domain to which you wish to add the public key.

If everything is set correctly, your domain will be authenticated.

However, if you the status shows an unauthenticated domain, it is possible that either the domain was entered with an error (check again if there is no typo) or the security feature was set incorrectly.

In this case, click **Verify** and then copy the record displayed and place it in your server within the DNS zone.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920375473/cacc8fa726eaa4f6a9d7e34b/Tifx5CjBMK3c-qgEghmZDHe0SESHtI4AnA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a061479efcd9b40e9d20b7191fe78cda1c9b6251ecd3bb19ba09df2ddd06374b&req=fSInFc57mYZcFb4f3HP0gEU4LYHPysne0kmXDrJJdAiAmX%2BjISHUsat%2Bj3CX%0A%2BHdihAUQhBz8XbSjeg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920375473/cacc8fa726eaa4f6a9d7e34b/Tifx5CjBMK3c-qgEghmZDHe0SESHtI4AnA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a061479efcd9b40e9d20b7191fe78cda1c9b6251ecd3bb19ba09df2ddd06374b&req=fSInFc57mYZcFb4f3HP0gEU4LYHPysne0kmXDrJJdAiAmX%2BjISHUsat%2Bj3CX%0A%2BHdihAUQhBz8XbSjeg%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** That done, click the **Verify** button and confirm to authenticate the domain once again. If everything is fine, your domain will be displayed as authenticated.

If you have any problems or questions, write to: **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#0f7c7a7f7f607d7b4f697d6a7c67626e6663217f63)
**

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for FreshMail, **[here](https://freshmail.com/help-and-knowledge/help/what-is-dkim-and-how-to-set-it-up/)
**.

Add a FreshMail DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your FreshMail emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Freshmail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Hushmail

Hushmail
========

How to set up DMARC for Hushmail in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754827-hushmail#h_5cc3398750)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754827-hushmail#h_83a428d6d5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754827-hushmail#h_40a48915f4)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Hushmail** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Hushmail emails
========================================================

To setup DKIM for your Hushmail emails, please add a CNAME DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

The key should be this format:

DKIM Selector: **hush**

CNAME Target: **\_dkim.hushmail.com**

[Husmail Customer Care](https://www.hushmail.com/contact/support/)
 will also be happy to answer your questions is your DKIM key will not begin to sign emails.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Hushmail, **[here](https://help.hushmail.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043601032-Setting-DKIM-and-DMARC-for-your-domain)
**.

Add a Hushmail DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Hushmail emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Hushmail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Groove HQ

Groove HQ
=========

How to set up DMARC for Groove HQ in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754800-groove-hq#h_cf759a1438)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754800-groove-hq#h_2f194f587d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754800-groove-hq#h_603a7e0fc3)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Groove HQ** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Groove HQ** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Groove HQ emails
=========================================================

**1.** In Groove, you need to go to the Inbox you would like to add the CNAME records for:

**2.** Go to **Settings** -> **Channels** -> **Email**.

**3.** Select which inbox you would like and click the **pencil icon** to edit.

**4.** Go to the SMTP configuration tab, you will see the 3 CNAME records that you will need to add to your DNS.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920388976/25fa52d3c1eb42a07684a4a8/zF0LXLafkJ2EuWw9hyyO5FZnGCh3uHgyXA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=313d9f51d51d809639baca76585f0757545f9f121b95860836bf8a9e1dd784a7&req=fSInFcF2lIZZFb4f3HP0gETDpW105YS5HkrXyn31Az82%2FNAvn0pK1kg6mXVy%0AIyLyV2Ioci%2Fhg4fCeg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920388976/25fa52d3c1eb42a07684a4a8/zF0LXLafkJ2EuWw9hyyO5FZnGCh3uHgyXA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=313d9f51d51d809639baca76585f0757545f9f121b95860836bf8a9e1dd784a7&req=fSInFcF2lIZZFb4f3HP0gETDpW105YS5HkrXyn31Az82%2FNAvn0pK1kg6mXVy%0AIyLyV2Ioci%2Fhg4fCeg%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the CNAME DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add that TXT record and the other CNAME record in your DNS. In the example above, you need to add in Valimail the first CNAME DKIM key.

**6.** Go back to Groove and to your Inbox and SMTP tab. Here you will be able to test that your CNAME records have published. Just under the CNAME records listing is a **Test DNS** button that will show you if the records are set up correctly or not. For each CNAME record there are 2 possible states:

Invalid: The records are set up incorrectly or haven't been setup

Valid: You're good to go!

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920388978/c0c5938eb80983128020b922/EWlX0yHeFBb1eTOy3GLyLoYHbVh0nGaG1g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b5b7021802e65d5ff82caf768cdab20ded484569c46539f6100ba908216faed6&req=fSInFcF2lIZXFb4f3HP0gCz5M%2B6YxlM4KgRkqIe8CNT7pwGODGnf2cpwXN1R%0A9LFLm%2FA0sE3DWgIByg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920388978/c0c5938eb80983128020b922/EWlX0yHeFBb1eTOy3GLyLoYHbVh0nGaG1g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b5b7021802e65d5ff82caf768cdab20ded484569c46539f6100ba908216faed6&req=fSInFcF2lIZXFb4f3HP0gCz5M%2B6YxlM4KgRkqIe8CNT7pwGODGnf2cpwXN1R%0A9LFLm%2FA0sE3DWgIByg%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Groove HQ, **[here](https://help.groovehq.com/help/white-labeling-email-to-avoid-spam-filters)
**.

Add a Groove HQ DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Groove HQ emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Groove HQ is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Help Scout

Help Scout
==========

How to set up DMARC for Help Scout in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754806-help-scout#h_9fec395104)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754806-help-scout#h_035ed59e84)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754806-help-scout#h_e77e36a3c5)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Help Scout** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Help Scout emails
==========================================================

**1.** You need 2 CNAME records at your DNS hosting provider. When a recipient server checks for the DKIM key at your domain, these records will forward that check over to Help Scout servers for the key.

strong1.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com will point to strong1.\_domainkey.helpscout.net

strong2.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com will point to strong2.\_domainkey.helpscout.net

**2.** If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add these 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

**3.** After you've added the records in Valimail, open your Help Scout mailbox and head over to **Settings** -> **Outgoing Email** and click the **Test Settings** button. If a valid CNAME record is found for your domain, you'll see the DKIM status indicator change to _Active_.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920389503/60860d72ca0b7828d185a18b/U5o2rizRhHpXtWPuS22o3jC_d2u9s0eRGA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0b93079dabe17992054efcfeab8dd9dfe201a005af8d69b6b80116da28455f35&req=fSInFcF3mIFcFb4f3HP0gEOLYckXnkpaWpXBnK7Z%2Blbd6q%2ByF6U60NnuemCw%0Ag1yN1JsAk5ovPE%2FDdA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920389503/60860d72ca0b7828d185a18b/U5o2rizRhHpXtWPuS22o3jC_d2u9s0eRGA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0b93079dabe17992054efcfeab8dd9dfe201a005af8d69b6b80116da28455f35&req=fSInFcF3mIFcFb4f3HP0gEOLYckXnkpaWpXBnK7Z%2Blbd6q%2ByF6U60NnuemCw%0Ag1yN1JsAk5ovPE%2FDdA%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Help Scout, **[here](https://docs.helpscout.com/article/666-use-dkim-to-help-with-email-delivery)
**.

Add a Help Scout DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Help Scout emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Help Scout is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *
