[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755623-icims#h_af8f1ef2d7)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

iCIMS

iCIMS
=====

How to set up DMARC for iCIMS in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755623-icims#h_fe460989d0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755623-icims#h_a6e9cdedb1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755623-icims#h_af8f1ef2d7)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **iCIMS** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

iCIMS does not sent SPF aligned emails by default.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your iCIMS emails
=====================================================

To enable DKIM in iCIMS fro your domain(s), you will need to contact iCIMS by submitting a case to iCIMS Technical Support.

iCIMS will then provide a pair of 2048 bit rate CNAME records to facilitate automatic key rotation. A DKIM key will be hosted in the iCIMS system's DNS.

Specific email domains for sending must be identified. DKIM keys are not automatically portable to just any email domain.

**Note**: You must specify the email domain(s) the DKIM key will be applied to in your authentication request.

**Important Note**: Emails will continue to send from the system email domain (e.g., [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#99e0f6ecebb4faf6f4e9f8f7e0b4f7f8f4fcb2f8ecedf6ebfce9f5e0d9f8fefcf7edeab7f0faf0f4eab7faf6f4)
) until all email authentication methods have been implemented and validations checks have been passed.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for iCIMS, **[here](https://community.icims.com/s/article/Configuring-Email-Authentication)
**.

Add an iCIMS DKIM key in Valimail
=================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your iCIMS emails
====================================================

Once you establish that iCIMS is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Hornetsecurity](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8835060-hornetsecurity)
[QuoteValet](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/9105009-quotevalet)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Informz (Higher Logic)

Informz (Higher Logic)
======================

How to set up DMARC for Informz (Higher Logic) in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755700-informz-higher-logic#h_ebc36aa096)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755700-informz-higher-logic#h_029d6450b3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755700-informz-higher-logic#h_0abbd7c75e)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Informz** (offered by Higher Logic) and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Informz does not support SPF alignment.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Informz emails
=======================================================

**1.** To begin the DKIM authentication process for your Higher Logic Thrive Marketing Professional (Informz) account sending domains, log into your Thrive Marketing Professional account and navigate to: **Admin** -> **System Settings** -> **Setup** -> **Email Authentication**.

**2.** Enter your sending domain into the text field and click **GET INSTRUCTIONS** for customized DNS instructions.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465290/3546f8b1a296c52fc5c7df58/3E9Wf3kxERLeGN-G8LiIrC9EeNkWZV1W_Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4cc8cae6b882cce9797db8d0be656fd78dd17d222c7eb650a3a4cdd1c5680f30&req=fSInEs97n4hfFb4f3HP0gBGSgXYMRWTSChkGWiGglDLMlLVYkvkwvSfSSGlN%0AhbQnOtLS%2FHWTMsLy1Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465290/3546f8b1a296c52fc5c7df58/3E9Wf3kxERLeGN-G8LiIrC9EeNkWZV1W_Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4cc8cae6b882cce9797db8d0be656fd78dd17d222c7eb650a3a4cdd1c5680f30&req=fSInEs97n4hfFb4f3HP0gBGSgXYMRWTSChkGWiGglDLMlLVYkvkwvSfSSGlN%0AhbQnOtLS%2FHWTMsLy1Q%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys in the Configuration page for your domain in Valimail Enforce.

**4.** After the CNAME records are added in Valimail Enforce, return to the **Email Authentication** page to run a test.

**5\.** Click **TEST** to see if the domain has passed authentication. If so, you'll see a message indicating the domain has passed the authentication test.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Informz, **[here](https://support.higherlogic.com/hc/en-us/articles/360033054071-Email-Authentication)
**.

Add an Informz DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Informz emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Informz is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

# Hubspot | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754819-hubspot#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754819-hubspot#h_13d38df060)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754819-hubspot#h_391a96dc49)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754819-hubspot#h_0fc8b2fa61)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Hubspot

Hubspot
=======

How to set up DMARC for Hubspot in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754819-hubspot#h_13d38df060)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754819-hubspot#h_391a96dc49)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754819-hubspot#h_0fc8b2fa61)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Hubspot** and how are they managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Hubspot supports both SPF and DKIM, but they do not send emails in SPF alignment by default.

DKIM and SPF configuration for Hubspot is done through a dedicated subdomain.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Hubspot emails
=======================================================

**1.** In your HubSpot account, click the **settings icon** in the main navigation bar.

**2.** In the left sidebar menu, navigate to **Website** -> **Domains & URLs**.

**3.** Click **Connect a domain**.

**4.** In the dialog box, select **Email Sending**, then click **Connect**. You'll then be directed to the domain connection screen.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920392922/093a4ced046cbc46f5a2c3e8/x-HaTPierDifg_LdgQ7TAdqPBtFoFFSaCA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7103a79b5f5f71d4836fe9044a002ac8b5890c942439dd8cba89c877d29115c8&req=fSInFcB8lINdFb4f3HP0gARf40%2FblSTshRKHEk5XRNtcsb4mTI7at7ePifFf%0Ac5q%2B8culFvUGLphhlw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920392922/093a4ced046cbc46f5a2c3e8/x-HaTPierDifg_LdgQ7TAdqPBtFoFFSaCA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7103a79b5f5f71d4836fe9044a002ac8b5890c942439dd8cba89c877d29115c8&req=fSInFcB8lINdFb4f3HP0gARf40%2FblSTshRKHEk5XRNtcsb4mTI7at7ePifFf%0Ac5q%2B8culFvUGLphhlw%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** On the domain connection screen, enter the email address you use to send emails from this domain, then click **Next**.

**6.** On the next screen, verify that the email sending domain is correct, then click **Next**.

**7.** Copy the DKIM key records and add them in **Valimail Enforce**. Additionally, copy and publish the first CNAME record in your DNS directly - this will allow HubSpot to publish an SPF record for the selected subdomain.

**8.** When you're done updating the values in Enforce and your DNS provider, select the **Done** checkbox in HubSpot.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920392925/e3a3625ec419e4cd14941854/maPNy67FNAqWyoJ-UsrVUFV1OwUpGrq82Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a3e97936263060d7e5174e6b3cb6b6fb3f55c6e5a096dc2caa0639b8c483b62a&req=fSInFcB8lINaFb4f3HP0gCsQ69586GYcNDQLUMuKdcK%2BH2AHcLGyfNIc2LkK%0Almus0s8Lo39UFQco1g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920392925/e3a3625ec419e4cd14941854/maPNy67FNAqWyoJ-UsrVUFV1OwUpGrq82Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a3e97936263060d7e5174e6b3cb6b6fb3f55c6e5a096dc2caa0639b8c483b62a&req=fSInFcB8lINaFb4f3HP0gCsQ69586GYcNDQLUMuKdcK%2BH2AHcLGyfNIc2LkK%0Almus0s8Lo39UFQco1g%3D%3D%0A)

**9.** After you add the DKIM keys in Valimail Enforce and the other CNAME record in your DNS, you'll see a **Verified** message letting you know your email sending domain is verified. Once you see the **Verified** message, click **Done**.

**10.** If you are in the process of adding the CNAME records in Enforce and/or in your DNS, you'll might see a _Record invalid_ error displayed to the right of one or all of your records. Click **check them again** to see if the changes have propagated.

You can find the instructions on how to setup DKIM in Hubspot **[here](https://knowledge.hubspot.com/domains-and-urls/connect-your-email-sending-domain)
**.

Add a Hubspot DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Hubspot emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Hubspot is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: If the Hub ID you were provided with is for example: **123456.spf00.hubspotemail.net**, fill in the HubSpot ID and SPF ID fields when you add Hubspot as an Enabled Sender on your domain in Valimail.

**_Note_:** We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755637-icontact#h_898382f793)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

iContact

iContact
========

How to set up DMARC for iContact in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755637-icontact#h_4390c1a143)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755637-icontact#h_55e47ef9b0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755637-icontact#h_898382f793)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the DKIM authentication processes for **iContact** and how is that managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

iContact does not send SPF aligned emails by default, so DKIM is the best option to configure this service.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your iContact emails
========================================================

**1.** Login to your domain hosting account. (i.e.; GoDaddy, Wix, Network Solutions, etc.)

**2.** Locate the Domain DNS settings. (if you are not familiar with editing your domain DNS - we recommend requesting help from your domain hosting provider)

**3.** Enter the CNAME records specified below.

TYPE: CNAME

NAME: icontact.k1.\_domainkey.<yourdomain.com>

POINTS TO: **k1.dkim.icontact.com**

TYPE: CNAME

NAME: icontact.k2.\_domainkey.<yourdomain.com>

POINTS TO: **k2.dkim.icontact.com**

**Note**: If you want to manage these DKIM keys in Valimail, you will need to add them in the **Valimail platform**.

**4.** Confirm the CNAME is set up correctly.

*   Go **[here](https://tools.wordtothewise.com/authentication)
    **.
    
*   Scroll down to the section for DKIM.
    
*   Check for "icontact.k1" and "icontact.k2" for your domain. You will have to do each individually. See the example below for icontact.k1:
    

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920448813/07a1d162b46ff4ddc4fc0802/KhV0urIZf7J6LKxqSXK1R9z7qycEQuDp7A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=60e1e94ccae5c2ae1d5f845162dd346182b6386a4669169060f8351057bf105f&req=fSInEs12lYBcFb4f3HP0gPLJ6trQPSyaFKk41eqxpl80PyS%2Feu%2F1wUrcR7IB%0AR2gURGE%2FDhzZ1P6ZHg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920448813/07a1d162b46ff4ddc4fc0802/KhV0urIZf7J6LKxqSXK1R9z7qycEQuDp7A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=60e1e94ccae5c2ae1d5f845162dd346182b6386a4669169060f8351057bf105f&req=fSInEs12lYBcFb4f3HP0gPLJ6trQPSyaFKk41eqxpl80PyS%2Feu%2F1wUrcR7IB%0AR2gURGE%2FDhzZ1P6ZHg%3D%3D%0A)

*   When you click Check, if it is green, then it is set up. If it is red, then it is not set up correctly.
    

**5.** **Contact iContact support**. You must contact Support or your Account Advisor and advise you have performed the above DKIM steps and request that we turn on custom DKIM for your account. Do not skip this step as it can affect future sends.

**6.** **Wait for domain to update**. The recommended time to wait for the change to be active for each record is 1 hour.

**7.** **Send a test message**. Check the email headers to confirm that DKIM specifies your domain.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM for iContact **[here](https://kb.icontact.com/hc/en-us/articles/360057099892)
**.

Add an iContact DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your iContact emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that iContact is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754810-hennge-one#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754810-hennge-one#h_71305649f1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754810-hennge-one#h_90f69a9930)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754810-hennge-one#h_ff2caceb9a)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

HENNGE One

HENNGE One
==========

How to set up DMARC for HENNGE One in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754810-hennge-one#h_71305649f1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754810-hennge-one#h_90f69a9930)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754810-hennge-one#h_ff2caceb9a)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **HENNGE One** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your HENNGE One emails
==========================================================

**1.** Click on the \[ **Digital Signature** \] → \[ **DKIM Setting** \] in HENNGE Email DLP Admin Console and choose the \[ **+** \] button beside the **Selector** box.

[![HENNGE One Internal Configuration Screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391202/3a6ed9a6543e34c0898afad5/EUyX2G44AFB7fms3GISEJKo0SAT7axKRMg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=21cf0760d1b27a440624fc3c6ed06378a4a59a4a0f216d0defa0b73daf215f75&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4FdFb4f3HP0gHi1bmXWxUWrayPe3dD4KcAjOAXocX3FwhA4Stfk%0AhSWEsJhvhjVWuhzovQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391202/3a6ed9a6543e34c0898afad5/EUyX2G44AFB7fms3GISEJKo0SAT7axKRMg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=21cf0760d1b27a440624fc3c6ed06378a4a59a4a0f216d0defa0b73daf215f75&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4FdFb4f3HP0gHi1bmXWxUWrayPe3dD4KcAjOAXocX3FwhA4Stfk%0AhSWEsJhvhjVWuhzovQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** The **Create DKIM Selector** Window will appear and please enter the Selector Name and chose Key Length 2048 Bits.

[![HENNGE One Internal Configuration Screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391207/8063df91172e0323f4ddeb21/_cQxc43YGFUGfJKCFmrRm000U5h1DphZNQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3b94e6713c44742d2abffab57b0e3958553d28402cfd7eaa27239bcb55433d11&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4FYFb4f3HP0gP6lEgbh6oT%2Bjulcazydqc%2BuRUWj3lmC8mMy740t%0A0Nytv4E4pvVzPDKDeA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391207/8063df91172e0323f4ddeb21/_cQxc43YGFUGfJKCFmrRm000U5h1DphZNQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3b94e6713c44742d2abffab57b0e3958553d28402cfd7eaa27239bcb55433d11&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4FYFb4f3HP0gP6lEgbh6oT%2Bjulcazydqc%2BuRUWj3lmC8mMy740t%0A0Nytv4E4pvVzPDKDeA%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Once the DKIM Selector has been saved, please select the selector created, the TXT Record to be added to DNS will appear.

**Note\***: By default, the value \[ t=y; \] tag will appear in the test mode, when utilizing this feature for production, please remove \[ t=y; \] from the value.

**Note\*\***: To enable the DKIM-Signature for Multiple domains including sub-domain,

the TXT Record needs to be registered in DNS record for each domain.

[![HENNGE One Internal Configuration Screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391213/54f8e7fd39a6fc41672d8663/mdNeeAkcWmFdCnJRoRG-un7rDAQcLHMP5A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4a29eec9b2ed3e15ea55dc79467a12c6ab59aca86fc42c2a7965fef6b748e2cc&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4BcFb4f3HP0gMxtSuD2WvjzdcTKUzslfxKCRSMPOkAyWWG3Wc4q%0As0lGAUdqY43JFjm6QA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391213/54f8e7fd39a6fc41672d8663/mdNeeAkcWmFdCnJRoRG-un7rDAQcLHMP5A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4a29eec9b2ed3e15ea55dc79467a12c6ab59aca86fc42c2a7965fef6b748e2cc&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4BcFb4f3HP0gMxtSuD2WvjzdcTKUzslfxKCRSMPOkAyWWG3Wc4q%0As0lGAUdqY43JFjm6QA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** After the TXT Record has been registered to the DNS, please enter the domain that the DNS was registered for into the **Domain Names** and press **Apply**.

**Note**: DKIM-Signature will be applied for the domains which is registered in the "Domain Names" field.

To enable the DKIM-Signature for multiple domains including sub-domain,

Please register the additional domain in the "Domain Names" field with a new line as attached image.

[![HENNGE One Internal Configuration Screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391214/268f55efe42649e8c3f8534b/YnCbXH0IyVyqa1uFiRIZtnbRA9j17R7dAw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=336181695f4d96471747fa0f33e770263c50281054be54538d7e6fd93cc9b77f&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4BbFb4f3HP0gGnNBd%2BgqMyFisPRMZGKT9BUmOwm9l6SbOX4%2BX2P%0ATlOkCXQZ0cOoeJIaxA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391214/268f55efe42649e8c3f8534b/YnCbXH0IyVyqa1uFiRIZtnbRA9j17R7dAw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=336181695f4d96471747fa0f33e770263c50281054be54538d7e6fd93cc9b77f&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4BbFb4f3HP0gGnNBd%2BgqMyFisPRMZGKT9BUmOwm9l6SbOX4%2BX2P%0ATlOkCXQZ0cOoeJIaxA%3D%3D%0A)

[![HENNGE One Internal Configuration Screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391217/39861a4cc1ee5e2078b73dab/VFVvoFziSVhr6MvJJvFBhdRlMPuVa9abKA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0c31e725bd5a7fa4adb23b20b045e9a0c3a4a80ec300f70542e190b2f565ae2c&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4BYFb4f3HP0gJe8ncAsNaclLMHMEWSj8zauoK2163oLcXmrtsFt%0AyqklTxXw%2FQzrPzUZXg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920391217/39861a4cc1ee5e2078b73dab/VFVvoFziSVhr6MvJJvFBhdRlMPuVa9abKA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0c31e725bd5a7fa4adb23b20b045e9a0c3a4a80ec300f70542e190b2f565ae2c&req=fSInFcB%2Fn4BYFb4f3HP0gJe8ncAsNaclLMHMEWSj8zauoK2163oLcXmrtsFt%0AyqklTxXw%2FQzrPzUZXg%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF **[here](https://support.hdeone.com/hc/en-us/articles/360008742693-DKIM-Configuration)
**.

Add a HENNGE One DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your HENNGE One emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that HENNGE One is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

The sender had no direct instructions related to SPF Alignment. In case SPF alignment is not activated automatically, please reach out to HENNGE One for further instructions.

* * *

Influitive

Influitive
==========

How to set up DMARC for Influitive in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755641-influitive#h_9c103d7d6a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755641-influitive#h_bddae7e560)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755641-influitive#h_5f26332cd8)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Influitive** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Influitive emails
==========================================================

Follow these instructions to configure your DKIM:

**1.** Add this TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce:

selector: **scph0416**

TXT Value:

**MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQDih5pVeAPRFdDHY+14sCpx2Alpv6Fa4TpfuTuvahTlynaArXq7NjLef5VaxM/qZ0jRzSK+9YSWW4ZgX56Oz/2rM+aZGcIQQeY9fnboRqOvj5yEUZE8Lb0TLOJ8LvwkI5gKn/T5soRcVcX9DQKmn/NbaRjXx1zSzEANc6NyxOfMDQIDAQAB**

**2.** After you added this key in Valimail Enforce, please notify Influitive Support at **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#51222421213e232511383f373d2438253827347f323e3c)
** that you are enabling this feature, as there is one setting that they need to enable in the backend of their system.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Influitive, **[here](https://support.influitive.com/article/351-configuring-spf-dkim-settings)
**.

Add an Influitive DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Influitive emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Influitive is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755711-intercom#h_fdba92137b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755711-intercom#h_731e2681e3)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Intercom

Intercom
========

How to set up DMARC for Intercom in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755711-intercom#h_7022bcdb4d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755711-intercom#h_fdba92137b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755711-intercom#h_731e2681e3)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Intercom** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Intercom emails
========================================================

**1.** Visit the **Domains** page in email settings. This page contains the information you'll need to configure the DNS records for each of the domains you use as part of your sender addresses. It'll look like this:

[![a screen shot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920468497/91f25dae0559d65426b8b2cc/FZncov7vwJYXOEnNC-DvAQx6hD7_srI4Fg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=53bde7398fbf2f66c7f0b5b6f7d7fa85390ef3707ba5644351009ff03384394b&req=fSInEs92mYhYFb4f3HP0gJ0ZJBHr7IileKyH3pG9CGAIuBE%2F8snDCxaACxj0%0AoE5k7AYpqxuhS7q93g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920468497/91f25dae0559d65426b8b2cc/FZncov7vwJYXOEnNC-DvAQx6hD7_srI4Fg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=53bde7398fbf2f66c7f0b5b6f7d7fa85390ef3707ba5644351009ff03384394b&req=fSInEs92mYhYFb4f3HP0gJ0ZJBHr7IileKyH3pG9CGAIuBE%2F8snDCxaACxj0%0AoE5k7AYpqxuhS7q93g%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click **Next** to move to step 2 - **Copy your records**. Here you can copy the names and values of the domain-key and bounce CNAME entries to update your external DNS configuration.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920468500/c3e113254e47feda9ac110fb/vBGegZgffb_WjDap-4OeirV-A8kfTrItNQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4071a434020bbb32049290a28f25bc71a72128528c68576a1efa334bd6b41323&req=fSInEs92mIFfFb4f3HP0gFTST9bQsNyt25sy0Swhk0U%2B8je%2BVRkpbpWHWNn7%0ALl9ElBCF8dQuj%2FD1kw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920468500/c3e113254e47feda9ac110fb/vBGegZgffb_WjDap-4OeirV-A8kfTrItNQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=4071a434020bbb32049290a28f25bc71a72128528c68576a1efa334bd6b41323&req=fSInEs92mIFfFb4f3HP0gFTST9bQsNyt25sy0Swhk0U%2B8je%2BVRkpbpWHWNn7%0ALl9ElBCF8dQuj%2FD1kw%3D%3D%0A)

**Important**: The second CNAME record is used to customize the return-path address on emails you send from Intercom. This is an optional step, and not performing this step will not prevent you sending messages, however, it is highly recommended to ensure delivery of your messages.

Your domain will show as unauthenticated until both CNAME records have been added.

If two or more of your sender addresses use the same domain, you will only need to configure one DNS entry to enable DKIM signing for emails sent using any of these addresses.

**3.** Add the first CNAME record which is the DKIM key, in Valimail Enforce. Add the second CNAME record only in your DNS.

**4.** After you added the DKIM key in Enforce and the second CNAME record in your DNS, the final step is to click on **Check status**. If configured correctly your status will change for each CNAME will change to Authenticated and your domain will be fully validated.

[![a black line with numbers and a curved line](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920468503/8aecee3f44732a6914e49e49/E0GquqG2TM32YKaFrDTDWaoy7CZgnQYYTw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5b23ab72ddfa5befbaed69121719bf58a1177721f13dc7da3484c0ea69810541&req=fSInEs92mIFcFb4f3HP0gBl3fPrYy%2BBA9f0S4VnRRbIlG%2FnW37GgTRUhRZAB%0A46u5iQysTUtqoqbO7g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920468503/8aecee3f44732a6914e49e49/E0GquqG2TM32YKaFrDTDWaoy7CZgnQYYTw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=5b23ab72ddfa5befbaed69121719bf58a1177721f13dc7da3484c0ea69810541&req=fSInEs92mIFcFb4f3HP0gBl3fPrYy%2BBA9f0S4VnRRbIlG%2FnW37GgTRUhRZAB%0A46u5iQysTUtqoqbO7g%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instruction on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Intercom **[here](https://www.intercom.com/help/en/articles/182-a-guide-to-sending-email-from-your-own-address#verify-your-domain)
**.

Add an Intercom DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Intercom emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Intercom is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Forcepoint Email Security Cloud

Forcepoint Email Security Cloud
===============================

How to set up DMARC for Forcepoint Email Security Cloud in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_0657d0801c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_8828497702)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_5aae615b0d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_a9df4d3de6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_a2bf2aa1f0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_0ae0f906bc)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_dd485eae37)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_1648ad86d1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_32dc59f56c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_f8d2a8b397)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754712-forcepoint-email-security-cloud#h_ff6f10804c)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for the **Forcepoint Email Security Cloud** service and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Forcepoint Email Security Cloud emails
===============================================================================

Adding a DKIM key
-----------------

**1.** Log in to your ForcePoint account as an administrator and go to **Settings** -> **Inbound/Outbound** -> **DKIM Settings** page:

**2.** Click **Add** in the DKIM Signing Keys section to open the Add Signing Key page.

**3.** Enter a name for your key in the **Key name** entry field.

**4.** Select one of the following options for creating your key:

*   **Generate key** (default) to create the private key. Only 1024-bit keys are supported.
    
*   **Private key** to enter a key you have already created. Paste the key in the entry box.
    

**5.** Click **OK**.

Importing or exporting a DKIM key
---------------------------------

*   To import a DKIM signing key in the **Settings** -> **Inbound/Outbound** -> **DKIM Settings** page, click **Import** to open a browser window. Navigate to the desired key file and click **Open**. You cannot import a duplicate key file.
    
*   To export a key, select the desired key in the signing keys table by marking its associated check box and click **Export** to open a browser window. Navigate to the desired directory location and click **Save**.
    

Creating a DKIM signing rule
----------------------------

A DKIM signing rule associates a private/public key pair with a set of domains and email addresses. Signing rule options let you determine which message headers to sign, how much of the message body to sign, and whether to attach additional signature tags for such items as signature date/time or expiration time.

You may create a signing rule, import an existing rule from a local directory, or export a rule to a local directory on the **Settings** -> **Inbound/Outbound** -> **DKIM Settings** page.

You may also delete a signing rule. Select the desired rule by marking its associated check box and click **Delete**.

The DKIM Signing Rules section contains a table of rule information. You can configure the number of signing rule entries per page, between 25 and 100, in the **Per page** drop-down list at the top of the table.

You can perform a keyword search by entering a term in the entry field at the top right of the table and clicking **Search**. Click **Show all rules** to clear the Search field and refresh the signing rules list.

Adding a signing rule
---------------------

Use the following steps to create a DKIM signing rule in the **Settings** -> **Inbound/Outbound** -> **DKIM Settings** page:

**1.** Click **Add** in the DKIM Signing Rules section to open the Add Signing Rule page.

**2**. Enter a name for your rule in the **Rule name** entry field.

**3.** Enter the name of the domain to which this signing rule applies.

**4.** If desired, mark the **Include user identifier** check box to include the identity of the user or agent for whom the message is signed.

**5.** Enter the user identifier in the **User identifier** entry field (optional). This field is not enabled if the **Include user identifier** check box is not marked.

**6.** Enter the domain name selector in the **Selector** entry field. A selector is a name component provided in addition to the domain name used in the DNS public key query. A given domain may have multiple selectors.

**7.** Select the signing key you want to associate with this rule from the **Signing key** drop-down list of existing keys.

**8.** Click **Advanced Options** to open a box with additional optional rule settings:

*   Select an encryption algorithm from the **Algorithm** drop-down list. Options include RSA-SHA-1 (default) or RSA-SHA-256.
    
*   Specify a canonicalization method for message header and body in the **Canonicalization** section. The canonicalization process prepares a message header and body before email is signed. Canonicalization is required because email processing may introduce minor changes to a message.
    
*   Indicate the message headers you want to sign from the list of standard headers. You can include other headers as a comma-separated list in the **Additional headers** field.
    
*   Specify whether you want the entire message body signed or only a portion of it signed. For the latter selection, enter the maximum number of Kbytes you want signed (default is 1024).
    
*   Select any optional signature tags for the signing rule:
    

\* **t** lets you add a signature creation timestamp

\* **x** lets you specify a signature expiration time in seconds (default is 3600)

\* **z** adds the list of signed header fields to the signature

**9.** From the Signing rule options drop-down list, select either **Sign email messages** or **Do not sign email messages**. Then create a list of email addresses to which this option applies.

For example, if you select **Sign email messages**, then email from the addresses in the list are signed. Email from other addresses is not signed.

If you select **Do not sign email messages**, then email from the addresses in the list are not signed, and email from all other users is signed.

You may search the email address list by entering a keyword in the search entry field and clicking **Search**.

You may remove an email address from the list by selecting it and clicking **Remove**.

**10.** Click **OK**.

Importing or exporting a rule
-----------------------------

To import a DKIM signing rule in the **Settings** -> **Inbound/Outbound** -> **DKIM Settings** page, click **Import** to open a browser window. Navigate to the desired rule file and click **Open**. You cannot import a duplicate key rule.

To export a rule, select the desired rule in the signing rules table by marking its associated check box and click **Export** to open a browser window. Navigate to the desired directory location and click **Save**.

Generating a DNS text record (public key)
-----------------------------------------

Generate a public key for a rule from the DKIM Signing Rules table by clicking the link for the desired rule in the DNS Text Record column. A Generate DNS Text Record box that contains the new public key appears.

You can view a public key by clicking **View** for a particular private key in the DKIM Signing Keys table Public Key column.

Testing a rule
--------------

Ensure that you have created a valid rule by clicking the **Test** link in the **Test Rule** column of the DKIM Signing Rules table for the desired signing rule. The test performs a DNS lookup query. You receive confirmation of success or failure when the test is complete.

You must have performed a successful rule test before a rule can be enabled.

Enabling DKIM verification
--------------------------

The DKIM validation method uses the message header digital signature to associate a domain name with the email. The DKIM signature verification function retrieves signer information, including the public key, from the DNS. This signer information is analyzed and verified to determine message legitimacy.

You can enable DKIM verification in the **Settings** -> **Inbound/Outbound** -> **DKIM Settings** page, in the DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) Verification section. Mark any of the following check boxes to activate DKIM verification:

*   **Enable DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) verification for inbound messages**
    
*   **Enable DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) verification for outbound messages**
    
*   **Enable DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) verification for internal messages**
    

By default, these check boxes are not marked.

You can also find the instructions and complete Forcepoint Email Security Cloud DKIM setup up procedure, **[here](https://www.websense.com/content/support/library/email/v82/esg_help/enable_dkim_verification_explain_esg.aspx)
**.

Add a Forcepoint Email Security Cloud DKIM key in Valimail
==========================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Forcepoint Email Security Cloud emails
==============================================================================

Once you establish that Forcepoint Email Security Cloud is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Related Articles

[Sophos Email Security](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752173-sophos-email-security)
[Cisco Cloud Email Security](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752258-cisco-cloud-email-security)
[DuoCircle](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754568-duocircle)
[FortiMail Cloud Email Security](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754719-fortimail-cloud-email-security)
[Trend Micro Email Security](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758133-trend-micro-email-security)

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_63bc02ce6a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_0b79abd14d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_69b56c19de)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_9fa28e22ce)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_2b5fa51100)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Sage Intacct

Sage Intacct
============

How to set up DMARC for Sage Intacct in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_63bc02ce6a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_0b79abd14d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_69b56c19de)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_9fa28e22ce)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755708-sage-intacct#h_2b5fa51100)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Sage Intacct** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Sage Intacct** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Sage Intacct emails
============================================================

Authenticate your domain
------------------------

1.  Go to **Company > Setup > Company**.
    
2.  Select the **Security** tab.
    
3.  Select **Edit**.
    
4.  Scroll down to **Email sender domain settings**.
    
5.  Enter your **Email sender domain**.
    
    For example, if you enter **example.com**, then emails will be sent from **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#acc5c2d8cdcfcfd881c1cdc5c0dfc9dedac5cfc9ecc9d4cdc1dcc0c982cfc3c1)
    **.
    
6.  Select **Authenticate domain**.
    

If you want to authenticate a different domain, select **Delete domain** and start again.

Enter DNS Keys
--------------

When the authentication process is complete, a DNS key table appears. Six total DNS keys must be added to the DNS table.

1.  Enter DNS keys.
    
    *   **Row 1:** Enter the CNAME used to configure the SPF record.
        
    *   **Row 2 and 3:** Enter the two CNAMEs used to configure DKIM authentication.
        
    *   **Row 4 and 5:** Enter one or two TXT strings that are used to configure EKS.
        
    *   **Row 6:** Enter one TXT string used to configure SPF for the previous email delivery service (for backwards compatibility).
        
    
2.  Select **Validate domain**.
    

Below is an example of DNS keys:

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1008544005/3836d943eb4e9ab4c378350d/UI-DNS-keys-table.png?expires=1755792000&signature=bd65cc95408642b49dcc27c50fd779983c6d65b6082c2700c2c8fb0cb1be06ec&req=dSAnHsx6mYFfXPMW1HO4zfCyCgkgv%2Bt5riSePjPcDGcmncdyGFqrnnDtttUb%0ASJwbIe1rbRFLNJexFk4%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/1008544005/3836d943eb4e9ab4c378350d/UI-DNS-keys-table.png?expires=1755792000&signature=bd65cc95408642b49dcc27c50fd779983c6d65b6082c2700c2c8fb0cb1be06ec&req=dSAnHsx6mYFfXPMW1HO4zfCyCgkgv%2Bt5riSePjPcDGcmncdyGFqrnnDtttUb%0ASJwbIe1rbRFLNJexFk4%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Sage Intacct, [here](https://www-p02.intacct.com/ia/docs/en_US/help_action/Company/Company_setup/Company_Information/Security/company-DKIM-email-sender-domain-settings.htm)
.

Configuring SPF authentication for your Sage Intacct emails
===========================================================

Once you establish that Sage Intacct is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in Sage Intacct for your domain
====================================================

You may notice that Intacct emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added Intacct to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by Intacct not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by configuring a CNAME record.

In Sage Intacct, complete the following steps:

1.  Go to **Company** from the navigation bar.
    
2.  Select the **Setup** tab.
    
3.  Select **Company** under **Configuration** within the menu.
    
4.  Click the **Security** tab.
    
5.  Scroll down to **Email sender domain settings**.  
    ​
    
    [![a screenshot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920467874/78ae493f957c77fc4c7509c4/dNl2Ls-4MWKD6nhHWIEXb0690Sg1N-c1CA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6fd48ab18c59b769cd15289195eeb8afbeeb073385addfe6087fa8e254805e28&req=fSInEs95lYZbFb4f3HP0gCH%2FsTqDwrOrdOvTljMFbZvHEYQij%2BH2awwcrOFs%0A8N0%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920467874/78ae493f957c77fc4c7509c4/dNl2Ls-4MWKD6nhHWIEXb0690Sg1N-c1CA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6fd48ab18c59b769cd15289195eeb8afbeeb073385addfe6087fa8e254805e28&req=fSInEs95lYZbFb4f3HP0gCH%2FsTqDwrOrdOvTljMFbZvHEYQij%2BH2awwcrOFs%0A8N0%3D%0A)
    
6.  Fill in the **domain** (for example: [yourdomainname.com](https://yourdomainname.com/)
    ).
    
7.  Check the box **Enforce DNS Validation**.
    
    *   If it is correctly setup you will receive a popup that states, the configuration is valid.
        
    *   If it is not correctly setup the popup will show below, and you will need to review the configuration:
        
    

[![a black screen with white text](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920467876/9eb586f66bdde98da1662354/z7wSKFd5yGHdw6OJAQ7OhpW6G0LsStfXsQ.jpeg?expires=1755792000&signature=ef24cca7ca47bbf7885de5c6fac5fbe90361ab72aa7d5d9fc45d43e259a231eb&req=fSInEs95lYZZFb4f3HP0gBOc8uveD3thT79yIn9BKn6OfH5HSUU3MRtYthjS%0ApY7ybPp0qpIAcNNstw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920467876/9eb586f66bdde98da1662354/z7wSKFd5yGHdw6OJAQ7OhpW6G0LsStfXsQ.jpeg?expires=1755792000&signature=ef24cca7ca47bbf7885de5c6fac5fbe90361ab72aa7d5d9fc45d43e259a231eb&req=fSInEs95lYZZFb4f3HP0gBOc8uveD3thT79yIn9BKn6OfH5HSUU3MRtYthjS%0ApY7ybPp0qpIAcNNstw%3D%3D%0A)

# Greenhouse | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_735ebe71a7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_33bd0dbd40)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_bc20386d60)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_66e0cee6a0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_3078c06945)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_4d26b1dcfe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_2f615ef6cd)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Greenhouse

Greenhouse
==========

How to set up DMARC for Greenhouse in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_735ebe71a7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_33bd0dbd40)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_bc20386d60)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_66e0cee6a0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_3078c06945)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_4d26b1dcfe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754779-greenhouse#h_2f615ef6cd)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Greenhouse** and how are they managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

DKIM and SPF configuration for Greenhouse is done through a dedicated subdomain, like **gh-mail.yourcompany.com**

This Greenhouse dedicated subdomain will not be managed from Valimail, but from your DNS directly.

Configuring DKIM and SPF authentication for your Greenhouse emails
==================================================================

Because DKIM and SPF configuration for Greenhouse is done through a dedicated subdomain, the SPF, DKIM management of this subdomain will only be done in your DNS. Please follow the instructions below to make sure you set up DKIM and SPF authentication for your domain in Greenhouse.

Verify email domain in Greenhouse Recruiting
============================================

**1.** Click the **Configure** icon from the navigation bar and select **Email Settings** on the left.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386947/8bea4b87cecf5dd3d8c7846d/RFnARTbG1VFzi64iI9CWZj0Pgk-bZs7qtA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6082451177f9443bef81f27bce4a55f87891d20e2de990e78642d763995a5461&req=fSInFcF4lIVYFb4f3HP0gNlZ10dciRt9cMxUKuys8VE0EGqk4Uf71T50j2xv%0AP0KpOEOXHbok17izzQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386947/8bea4b87cecf5dd3d8c7846d/RFnARTbG1VFzi64iI9CWZj0Pgk-bZs7qtA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6082451177f9443bef81f27bce4a55f87891d20e2de990e78642d763995a5461&req=fSInFcF4lIVYFb4f3HP0gNlZ10dciRt9cMxUKuys8VE0EGqk4Uf71T50j2xv%0AP0KpOEOXHbok17izzQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Enter your organization's email domain in the Email Settings page and click **Register**.

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386949/392429040b84493e3984b221/Syqd04FblIIdt1HNoWrh4BfZZyWqTgXoAw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=da2aedd787a05cb853623aaccfaab74ffe244578e8ac2a18aff96e011138f800&req=fSInFcF4lIVWFb4f3HP0gP2GyN0oxQA%2BRTU8ZthHQq45ZT%2F%2F1ePVoAYfeGzT%0At2U2C75%2FjxR2CJGyeg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386949/392429040b84493e3984b221/Syqd04FblIIdt1HNoWrh4BfZZyWqTgXoAw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=da2aedd787a05cb853623aaccfaab74ffe244578e8ac2a18aff96e011138f800&req=fSInFcF4lIVWFb4f3HP0gP2GyN0oxQA%2BRTU8ZthHQq45ZT%2F%2F1ePVoAYfeGzT%0At2U2C75%2FjxR2CJGyeg%3D%3D%0A)

Ask your IT team add the files to your DNS setup
================================================

Once you've registered your company's email domain, your IT team will need to add special files (called SPF, DKIM, MX, and CNAME records) to your email configuration to complete the setup.

To automatically send directions and the files to your IT team, click **Email Your I.T. Dept** and enter their address in the field.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386951/57bc9238cc2bf46bf17afa4b/nfhlu2jXq4rtF3opxTUg5BoT0hQqdvGxGg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=61e47fd67f16241de33d74ee7f873cc29fabd5a5b632437ac987f3bfe86871be&req=fSInFcF4lIReFb4f3HP0gGi2XVAI%2FLY8THMchfPlP2T1M2xY64%2FreIziBqU1%0A%2FqFqArBkpW2HObWOrw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386951/57bc9238cc2bf46bf17afa4b/nfhlu2jXq4rtF3opxTUg5BoT0hQqdvGxGg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=61e47fd67f16241de33d74ee7f873cc29fabd5a5b632437ac987f3bfe86871be&req=fSInFcF4lIReFb4f3HP0gGi2XVAI%2FLY8THMchfPlP2T1M2xY64%2FreIziBqU1%0A%2FqFqArBkpW2HObWOrw%3D%3D%0A)

**Note**: _If you don't have an IT team, look for whoever manages the DNS for your email domain. Most likely, you can forward these records to someone in your IT department, and they should know what to do._

_For organizations using web hosting services with the domain implied (such as GoDaddy or SquareSpace), the records populated after clicking **Email your IT Department** must be edited further to successfully verify your domain._

Verify an email domain using a web host with domain implied
===========================================================

Certain web hosting services like GoDaddy or SquareSpace imply your organization's domain when adding DNS records. This means that your web host service will automatically append your organization's domain to all added DNS records.

**Note**: _Please check with your IT team to see if your web hosting service implies your organization's domain when adding DNS records._

For organizations using a web hosting service that implies your domain for DNS records, you'll need to edit the **Email Your IT Department** email as part of the email verification process.

**1.** Once you've registered your domain with Greenhouse Recruiting under **Configure** -> **Email Settings**, click **Email Your I.T. Department**.

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386952/6d26c3f9205dcd2129380098/acCMWnG7QWRD7MGLXskc_Uu-4JTVOiaFfg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2304ac1bb3775d9d4ece3d85d2e901a89d46dbe98546bea1f27c7eed7256af38&req=fSInFcF4lIRdFb4f3HP0gIS8XlYWRmbGGJn%2F2DtFiZX36XYsfNYp67dm4mNn%0AY2hiKiLDwKnGmyzeYA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386952/6d26c3f9205dcd2129380098/acCMWnG7QWRD7MGLXskc_Uu-4JTVOiaFfg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2304ac1bb3775d9d4ece3d85d2e901a89d46dbe98546bea1f27c7eed7256af38&req=fSInFcF4lIRdFb4f3HP0gIS8XlYWRmbGGJn%2F2DtFiZX36XYsfNYp67dm4mNn%0AY2hiKiLDwKnGmyzeYA%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Greenhouse Recruiting will populate the information needed by your IT team to verify your domain in an email. However, since your organization is using a web host with the domain implied, the **Hostname** column needs to be edited.

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386953/2aa6c4a7e5524ee67dc2245f/dxT7dIcbSYVx9Tr0DpYWRR2wKIMgYK4LcA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0e667259d5e699f91bedf912cf3f90c8818295ac04d2c5dd4f39944413113a6c&req=fSInFcF4lIRcFb4f3HP0gC1lVJh91YX9e%2Fz7wLCKtqpbR9R%2FiwnHTRS5fpuG%0A9kGJEU1%2BlTFukgq9kg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386953/2aa6c4a7e5524ee67dc2245f/dxT7dIcbSYVx9Tr0DpYWRR2wKIMgYK4LcA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0e667259d5e699f91bedf912cf3f90c8818295ac04d2c5dd4f39944413113a6c&req=fSInFcF4lIRcFb4f3HP0gC1lVJh91YX9e%2Fz7wLCKtqpbR9R%2FiwnHTRS5fpuG%0A9kGJEU1%2BlTFukgq9kg%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Remove your domain from each DNS record in the Hostname column (everything after gh-mail).

**Note**: _Because your web host already appends your domain, if these entries are not edited you'll essentially be trying to verify a duplicative record, like gh-mail.redesign.greenhouse.io.redesign.greenhouse.io, that will cause your DNS settings to fail._

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386956/0e00cc8699764ae6456fffb8/OKdQR6NzByse-01OE8XuTlUEV0fq3QGJWA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a178173a5e8b53b1bf81abbb28979d044307089322ea15a9a2f1b459fc4f51ce&req=fSInFcF4lIRZFb4f3HP0gLTRN3l35yEtv9DLnMwDze8Ktg2xn4YOd7bHUjQN%0AA5cbLqX59rnwamInEw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920386956/0e00cc8699764ae6456fffb8/OKdQR6NzByse-01OE8XuTlUEV0fq3QGJWA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a178173a5e8b53b1bf81abbb28979d044307089322ea15a9a2f1b459fc4f51ce&req=fSInFcF4lIRZFb4f3HP0gLTRN3l35yEtv9DLnMwDze8Ktg2xn4YOd7bHUjQN%0AA5cbLqX59rnwamInEw%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Once the entries are edited, click **Send**. Your IT Team will use these edited records to verify your domain.

You can find the instructions for the steps above **[here](https://support.greenhouse.io/hc/en-us/articles/201111684-Email-Verification)
** and **[here](https://support.greenhouse.io/hc/en-us/articles/360027019452)
**.

Adding the Greenhouse dedicated subdomain to Valimail
=====================================================

Although the DMARC authentication is done in your DNS for this Greenhouse dedicated subdomain, we also recommend that you add the subdomain to your domain's Configuration page in Enforce. This is needed for tracking and classification purposes with respect to the DKIM keys and future email traffic associated with this subdomain.

**1.** On the Configuration page in Enforce, click on the **Add Email Subdomain** button on the far left side of the your Email Domains section.

[![](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/920386854/fdbd56edda17dcf3171b71c5/image.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c30a65bf88aa4acdff4ebc0ba5f4cca16284d81fac5b93d4d4ed6a70da21e813&req=fSInFcF4lYRbFb4f3HP0gIPZCGfeAYA99TvWiv72M59Ggt6MS6XuUGAzJU2f%0AowddFN3Db38khP7dIQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/920386854/fdbd56edda17dcf3171b71c5/image.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c30a65bf88aa4acdff4ebc0ba5f4cca16284d81fac5b93d4d4ed6a70da21e813&req=fSInFcF4lYRbFb4f3HP0gIPZCGfeAYA99TvWiv72M59Ggt6MS6XuUGAzJU2f%0AowddFN3Db38khP7dIQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Add the Greenhouse subdomain in the **Name** field, select Greenhouse as the **Email service provider** and make sure you check the box for **This domain will only be used for s single sender**.

Add a Greenhouse DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Greenhouse emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Greenhouse is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

**Note:** _Some customers who use Greenhouse on the top level, or org domain only, have reported that they have seen Greenhouse messages stop being sent in an aligned manner. This can happen because Greenhouse has an automated script to check for a particular include value being set in your SPF record. They cannot parse the standard SPF record we provide our customers and one of two things needs to happen:_

*   _You can explicitly add their include before our standard record until the validation step has occurred_
    
*   _You can contact Greenhouse Support and they will override this check._
    

* * *

Related Articles

[Beamery](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471015-beamery)
[Conversica](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754299-conversica)

# Infusionsoft | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755703-infusionsoft#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755703-infusionsoft#h_19f1b6e9ec)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755703-infusionsoft#h_f87530188a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755703-infusionsoft#h_9681c2306b)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Infusionsoft

Infusionsoft
============

How to set up DMARC for Infusionsoft in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755703-infusionsoft#h_19f1b6e9ec)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755703-infusionsoft#h_f87530188a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755703-infusionsoft#h_9681c2306b)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Infusionsoft** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Infusionsoft emails
============================================================

**1.** Navigate to **Marketing** -> **Settings**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465971/6721afbab8f1af1f68c1ecb9/RyWeH0sZa69CriX49uwhU-S4IDFunTUE0g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0ca51c706352fd5eacf925c98233d904d66937a6a06dc01f50d31def755d9365&req=fSInEs97lIZeFb4f3HP0gPFNAB0272tXHnmZRx%2F94uaGgSP%2FKVWtUnq8VvwX%0ACe5mCnOtVU21kyjXRw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465971/6721afbab8f1af1f68c1ecb9/RyWeH0sZa69CriX49uwhU-S4IDFunTUE0g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0ca51c706352fd5eacf925c98233d904d66937a6a06dc01f50d31def755d9365&req=fSInEs97lIZeFb4f3HP0gPFNAB0272tXHnmZRx%2F94uaGgSP%2FKVWtUnq8VvwX%0ACe5mCnOtVU21kyjXRw%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click **Email Authentication**.

[![a white and grey rectangular object with text](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465978/e543f095d1a6d9c4f7fc888a/nuGjRlG1HVpLhnCE9O6euqhVbBrepkIq6w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=05f240fc3499192b892c84387ebbb5d01f8864f4f6131c79331ebf647a4343ba&req=fSInEs97lIZXFb4f3HP0gJbwYnquBgQNhtTF4askl79oU38Ui%2BMttbgQK00g%0AllkR951YaPyyRmpeBQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465978/e543f095d1a6d9c4f7fc888a/nuGjRlG1HVpLhnCE9O6euqhVbBrepkIq6w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=05f240fc3499192b892c84387ebbb5d01f8864f4f6131c79331ebf647a4343ba&req=fSInEs97lIZXFb4f3HP0gJbwYnquBgQNhtTF4askl79oU38Ui%2BMttbgQK00g%0AllkR951YaPyyRmpeBQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Click **Add a Domain**.

**Note**: This button is only available to admin users.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465983/423e9257b5c0e28df2451daa/HbirwEYu44Hn_7wzEjuCIw_D6AdVN3ywgA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6623a988d0e041e1a5c14fdf7b2aefa1337e0add035e3220037b976399de56f7&req=fSInEs97lIlcFb4f3HP0gP30fMiZvXnDIobhgqD%2B3y4EjDjMj2x5EviyaRSb%0AojyZqAtJiEiUk4er1w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465983/423e9257b5c0e28df2451daa/HbirwEYu44Hn_7wzEjuCIw_D6AdVN3ywgA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6623a988d0e041e1a5c14fdf7b2aefa1337e0add035e3220037b976399de56f7&req=fSInEs97lIlcFb4f3HP0gP30fMiZvXnDIobhgqD%2B3y4EjDjMj2x5EviyaRSb%0AojyZqAtJiEiUk4er1w%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Select a domain to authenticate.

**Note**: You can add additional email domains by adding that email domain to a User Record.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465987/347af3a54f32ee55cf69cd7f/hEtqJzEc4SmOXJefBtw3vF0GkQyNP0W-qQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6ea6dadd9ea59b4342a11de17663b6adda386ac080f883c76db0cf3c165df4a8&req=fSInEs97lIlYFb4f3HP0gDFl4TLSxon1C9wnG5fkmr6CXWOV3m4bqWhcKptP%0Af7APCKGyY8trR%2Fxi3A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465987/347af3a54f32ee55cf69cd7f/hEtqJzEc4SmOXJefBtw3vF0GkQyNP0W-qQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6ea6dadd9ea59b4342a11de17663b6adda386ac080f883c76db0cf3c165df4a8&req=fSInEs97lIlYFb4f3HP0gDFl4TLSxon1C9wnG5fkmr6CXWOV3m4bqWhcKptP%0Af7APCKGyY8trR%2Fxi3A%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Create a new CNAME record using the text below as the **Name** or **Host** (the actual name depends on your provider).

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465993/4c2fe2f2de4fd5b0c4822a22/630iCrvBjD2ylV4sDjKxd0NvvnoTxPOSjQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b8b134a7bcea505d5955fa3ee279cbe72127ca7ccdda9ca72935976c214f170f&req=fSInEs97lIhcFb4f3HP0gILCUza6boNUKr37T624QCStOKk21NFdKQIP7TGD%0AyVPkKTWaaPRjeGcd9g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465993/4c2fe2f2de4fd5b0c4822a22/630iCrvBjD2ylV4sDjKxd0NvvnoTxPOSjQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b8b134a7bcea505d5955fa3ee279cbe72127ca7ccdda9ca72935976c214f170f&req=fSInEs97lIhcFb4f3HP0gILCUza6boNUKr37T624QCStOKk21NFdKQIP7TGD%0AyVPkKTWaaPRjeGcd9g%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Copy and paste the provided text into the DNS "value" or "points to" field (the actual name depends on your provider) within your new CNAME record.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465995/31144fa6c5845107bcf0bd92/RHbNoJUuwJ_mUDLpyMwjNz3UYXNFtGWUrQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=daa91d26e08fa27eb272e0f9c8dfd85f1377a388f583153901aef1f716dbbb97&req=fSInEs97lIhaFb4f3HP0gKeFp%2FR0y4D8%2F%2Fhd5imNDJwuIskvPq%2FLk5iyfj7H%0ATiKrjnOg0sRutZycPA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465995/31144fa6c5845107bcf0bd92/RHbNoJUuwJ_mUDLpyMwjNz3UYXNFtGWUrQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=daa91d26e08fa27eb272e0f9c8dfd85f1377a388f583153901aef1f716dbbb97&req=fSInEs97lIhaFb4f3HP0gKeFp%2FR0y4D8%2F%2Fhd5imNDJwuIskvPq%2FLk5iyfj7H%0ATiKrjnOg0sRutZycPA%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Click the **Verify** button.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465996/112dd87eba841b6091e27562/1MdYE75Dz4BsY51KGezYR7_qpt_rMapIAw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d532a388c2f6901f17ed36d44229423a7396c8dd240b59b98d2165ccc2b4e40e&req=fSInEs97lIhZFb4f3HP0gPk8cpNctfsjHZftx4fiYS5pTWFa7bf%2BRRTk4cLs%0AZq%2B0Q656FSQAGWa4AA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920465996/112dd87eba841b6091e27562/1MdYE75Dz4BsY51KGezYR7_qpt_rMapIAw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d532a388c2f6901f17ed36d44229423a7396c8dd240b59b98d2165ccc2b4e40e&req=fSInEs97lIhZFb4f3HP0gPk8cpNctfsjHZftx4fiYS5pTWFa7bf%2BRRTk4cLs%0AZq%2B0Q656FSQAGWa4AA%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Infusionsoft **[here](https://help.infusionsoft.com/help/dkim)
**.

Add an Infusionsoft DKIM key in Valimail
========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Infusionsoft emails
===========================================================

Once you establish that Infusionsoft is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Jive

Jive
====

How to set up DMARC for Jive in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755720-jive#h_6a279a1173)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755720-jive#h_325ec6dd8a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755720-jive#h_c099c54531)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Jive** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Jive** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

_Jive Hosted authenticates using a TXT DKIM key_

_Jive Cloud authenticates using a pair of CNAME DKIM keys_

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Jive emails
====================================================

In order to configure DKIM, you first need to request a new TXT DKIM public key (for Jive Hosted) or DKIM CNAME Records (for Jive Cloud). To do this, open a ticket with Jive Support with the subject **DKIM configuration required**. Please also include:

*   The email domain (typically this is the same email domain used for the Server Admin Email setting in **Admin Console** -> **System** -> **Settings** -> **Email Server**).
    

**Note**: If you are a Cloud Customer, you will not have access to the above setting. You can simply mention this on your support ticket. Customer Support will be able to get this information for you, via the backend.

*   The "selector" - a simple identifier that can be anything, even something as simple as "jmx". The selector has to be exactly 3 characters long.
    

Our Jive Infrastructure Operations team will perform the implementation on the Jive side and your assigned support agent will provide you the entries that you need to add to your domain's DNS record.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Jive, **[here](https://helpdesk.aurea.com/hc/en-us/articles/360013374599-Setting-up-SPF-DKIM-and-DMARC-Email-Authentication-with-Jive#h_c11074f3-8af8-45ef-9489-2d2896dda291)
**.

Add a Jive DKIM key in Valimail
===============================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://intercom.help/valimail-4dffb6ef6e39/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Jive emails
===================================================

Once you establish that Jive is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Inxmail

Inxmail
=======

How to set up DMARC for Inxmail in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755714-inxmail#h_e25b21b0a6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755714-inxmail#h_e7215b9473)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755714-inxmail#h_be5e8bcaff)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Inxmail** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Inxmail emails
=======================================================

In general, Inxmail recommends using a separate subdomain of your main domain as a sending domain.

Example:

domain: [example.com](https://example.com/)

subdomain: [mail.example.com](https://mail.example.com/)

The Brand Identity Protection agent helps you to correctly set up DKIM signing for your own sender domain, see: [Initial set-up: Implementation scenarios](https://help.inxmail.com/en/content/xpro/brand_identity_ersteinrichtung.htm)
.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Inxmail, **[here](https://help.inxmail.com/en/content/xpro/mailings_per_dkim_signieren.htm)
** and **[here](https://help.inxmail.com/en/content/xpro/brand_identity_szenario_3.htm)
**.

Add an Inxmail DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Inxmail emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Inxmail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

LianaMailer
===========

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **LianaMailer** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your LianaMailer emails
===========================================================

LianaMailer automatically sends information about needed actions regarding DKIM and SPF for every [Liana®Cloud Email Marketing](https://www.lianatech.com/solutions/email-marketing/overview.html)
 user after their account is established.

If you need more assistance in setting DKIM up in LianaMailer, please contact their Support.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for LianaMailer, **[here](https://www.lianatech.com/resources/blog/how-dkim-and-spf-improve-the-delivery-rate-of-your-newsletter.html)
**.

Add a LianaMailer DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your LianaMailer emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that LianaMailer is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.
