[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755719-jangomail#h_266ab85e14)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755719-jangomail#h_fff9e894cb)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

JangoMail

JangoMail
=========

How to set up DMARC for JangoMail in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755719-jangomail#h_c3af595814)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755719-jangomail#h_266ab85e14)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8755719-jangomail#h_fff9e894cb)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **JangoMail** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your JangoMail emails
=========================================================

To set up DKIM on your own domain, you must first create the unique key inside your account.

**1.** Log into your JangoMail account.

**2.** Go to **Settings** → **Email Settings** → **DKIM**.

**3.** Add your domain by entering your domain and a selector for your domain and then click **Save**. _A good default selector value is to simply use "**jm**", though any single-word selector will work. Selectors must be no longer than 64-bits. Keep in mind that the selector you choose will correspond to the DNS record you must add for your domain (TXT record for **selector.\_domainkey.yourdomain.com**)._

**4.** _You must now add the public key to your DNS server. The DKIM will be **disabled** until you add the public key into your DNS server._

**5.** To view the keys, click the **Details** icon next to your domain.

**6.** A new window will launch showing you the DNS record, the Value, and the Public Key for the DKIM. Ensure that you copy the entire contents of the Value field in the popup window.

**7.** Once the DNS record is in place, you must go back to JangoMail to enable the key pair. Follow the instructions in step 2 to get to **DKIM**, and click the **Enable** icon next to your domain. JangoMail will then verify that your DNS TXT record is in place and correct, and if so, it will enable the DKIM. Your email campaigns will now be signed with DKIM.

**Note**: It may take up to 48 hours for the new TXT record to be visible in DNS, so you may not be able to enable the key right away.

You can also find the instructions to set up DKIM for JangoMail **[here](https://jangomail.zendesk.com/hc/en-us/articles/200660154-DKIM-Record)
**.

Add a JangoMail DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your JangoMail emails
========================================================

Once you establish that JangoMail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Listrak

Listrak
=======

How to set up DMARC for Listrak in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757268-listrak#h_9bf62aae3f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757268-listrak#h_8b7be27841)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757268-listrak#h_ded864eb3c)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Listrak** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Listrak emails
=======================================================

To enable DKIM for your outbound messages, contact Listrak Support at **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#1c6f696c6c736e685c70756f686e7d77327f7371)
**. Please be prepared with the following information:

*   What is the **from domain** that you will be using to send mail?
    

Listrak will then provide you with a TXT DKIM key which you will need to add on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Listrak, **[here](https://help.listrak.com/en/articles/1505383-branding-your-listrak-account)
**.

Add a Listrak DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Listrak emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Listrak is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Key Survey by WorldAPP

Key Survey by WorldAPP
======================

How to set up DMARC for Key Survey by WorldAPP in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757123-key-survey-by-worldapp#h_6c696d3f87)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757123-key-survey-by-worldapp#h_08f4d07636)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757123-key-survey-by-worldapp#h_e9af32f3b8)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Key Survey by WorldAPP** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Key Survey by WorldAPP emails
======================================================================

**1.** Navigate to the **My account** page and go to the **Security settings** tab.

[![security settings tab](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920889098/371810a597b4ee96d971e146/SdLabomMMBhurwdwSLMSgGwpZu_Tt6GouQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=da9f000aefd9a1e0e046a61e092f851cd52e8c348cf02b4f46b1c6c129b77e26&req=fSInHsF3nYhXFb4f3HP0gEwj3nmzpJQMXKdCvZLcccL2iOsv61XFdy6KwXgA%0AaA6ZtxsgCX12E0XHHQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920889098/371810a597b4ee96d971e146/SdLabomMMBhurwdwSLMSgGwpZu_Tt6GouQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=da9f000aefd9a1e0e046a61e092f851cd52e8c348cf02b4f46b1c6c129b77e26&req=fSInHsF3nYhXFb4f3HP0gEwj3nmzpJQMXKdCvZLcccL2iOsv61XFdy6KwXgA%0AaA6ZtxsgCX12E0XHHQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Find the **Registered Email Domains** section and click **Add email domain** button.

[![add email domain button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920889103/b8425414a9a21aeb0b2c7504/ZOQzJtS7cMDYO2Hkp5QG_7kZmRq5-eHAag.png?expires=1755792000&signature=15aeae6bf1803559a9effa39110b3bb29b644fff72f6b2684653dc487bb527ef&req=fSInHsF3nIFcFb4f3HP0gJOiGeJDl7LozBTs09%2BZpzKL9F0ifmVUlLnC%2FewS%0AfCCI17geam7yGhawQA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920889103/b8425414a9a21aeb0b2c7504/ZOQzJtS7cMDYO2Hkp5QG_7kZmRq5-eHAag.png?expires=1755792000&signature=15aeae6bf1803559a9effa39110b3bb29b644fff72f6b2684653dc487bb527ef&req=fSInHsF3nIFcFb4f3HP0gJOiGeJDl7LozBTs09%2BZpzKL9F0ifmVUlLnC%2FewS%0AfCCI17geam7yGhawQA%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Type in a domain name you would like to use in "From" field for emails that will be send from your account and then click the **Add domain**.

[![add a domain page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920889112/c1c12b6704bc3b37620e3cf1/sS-8t1UV7bIGf5mLlDzG2GpZ-FmIXn_1aw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=293563455624dcf8b8951e1b43e489087473df9184c361cff7c83f5f75eb06d0&req=fSInHsF3nIBdFb4f3HP0gD448gjJMBLTx8J2Ixdkht8qP1s9jUTh%2FX3cRcEQ%0A2UchdMzE1QdwWLk3wQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920889112/c1c12b6704bc3b37620e3cf1/sS-8t1UV7bIGf5mLlDzG2GpZ-FmIXn_1aw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=293563455624dcf8b8951e1b43e489087473df9184c361cff7c83f5f75eb06d0&req=fSInHsF3nIBdFb4f3HP0gD448gjJMBLTx8J2Ixdkht8qP1s9jUTh%2FX3cRcEQ%0A2UchdMzE1QdwWLk3wQ%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** FORM system will generate DKIM and SPF records for your domain.

[![dkim key details](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920889118/2a762982334f872ff51f8143/bjaEu62lnNf0pryNXTnF-x9-T7dExu4dbA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d1ec72a178021e5d7f654dda82efdb7a77b7726bdd72fa7ea89da96741faad27&req=fSInHsF3nIBXFb4f3HP0gJ9l0BSjrJSO%2FNVodH5no2kvwbwDyP5kOX530IzT%0AEVTG98Ete%2BVgdvlCKQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920889118/2a762982334f872ff51f8143/bjaEu62lnNf0pryNXTnF-x9-T7dExu4dbA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d1ec72a178021e5d7f654dda82efdb7a77b7726bdd72fa7ea89da96741faad27&req=fSInHsF3nIBXFb4f3HP0gJ9l0BSjrJSO%2FNVodH5no2kvwbwDyP5kOX530IzT%0AEVTG98Ete%2BVgdvlCKQ%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Key Survey by WorldAPP, **[here](https://help.worldapp.com/display/FHC/Registered+Email+Domains)
**.

Add a Key Survey by WorldAPP DKIM key in Valimail
=================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://intercom.help/valimail-4dffb6ef6e39/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Key Survey by WorldAPP emails
=====================================================================

Once you establish that Key Survey by WorldAPP is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://intercom.help/valimail-4dffb6ef6e39/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

LibraryH3lp

LibraryH3lp
===========

How to set up DMARC for LibraryH3lp in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757205-libraryh3lp#h_d64dfe91b7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757205-libraryh3lp#h_36f1ef8d8f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757205-libraryh3lp#h_5139a31e8b)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the DKIM authentication process for **LibraryH3lp** and how this is managed in Valimail. LibraryH3lp does not currently support SPF-aligned authentication, but DKIM authentication should be enough for most use case scenarios.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your LibraryH3lp emails
===========================================================

**1.** Contact LibraryH3lp support([\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#1d6e686d6d726f695d71747f6f7c6f64752e716d337e7270)
) and ask them to provide a DKIM signing key for your domain.

**2.** Once LibraryH3lp support provides a DKIM key, add it to your domain's configuration in the Valimail Enforce app

**3.** Confirm with LibraryH3lp support that DKIM signing is enabled for your domain

You can also find the instructions on how to obtain the DKIM key from LibraryH3lp **[here](https://docs.libraryh3lp.com/3mail-forwarding)
**.

Add a LibraryH3lp DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your LibraryH3lp emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that LibraryH3lp is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

**Note**: Even though LibraryH3lp does not support SPF alignment, you can still enable it for your domain, by adding it in your Enabled Sender's list in Valimail.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Lessonly

Lessonly
========

How to set up DMARC for Lessonly in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757187-lessonly#h_5e1e048a47)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757187-lessonly#h_3aa590c0d0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757187-lessonly#h_a0b5ad132f)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM configuration process for **Lessonly** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages SendGrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Lessonly as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Lessonly infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Lessonly typically uses one dedicated IP, therefore emails sent with this service will come from the following IP address: **167.89.73.35 o1.m.lessonly.com.**

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Lessonly emails
========================================================

To begin the process of authenticating a domain, please reach out to Lessonly Support at **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#0b787e7b7b64797f4b676e78786465677225686466)
**.

A member of the Support team can then begin the process of authenticating the domain through Lessonly's email provider, Sendgrid. Lessonly needs to be provided a main point of contact during this process to ensure the authentication is verified from the customer's end.

**Important Note:** _By default, Lessonly automatically sets the DKIM to "**lly**." If a different DKIM is preferred, please provide a three-letter or numerical value for the Support member to input._

Once all steps have been taken on Lessonly's end, an email is sent to the point of contact with the below message:

_Hi, this is the Lessonly Support team. We are trying to authenticate your domain with SendGrid, but I don't have the ability to modify your DNS records. Can you help me add these records so that I can complete the process?_

The point of contact has 48 hours to confirm the DNS records or the link will expire. Once confirmed, then Lessonly Support verifies the set-up in Sendgrid and it should be all set.

You can find these instructions and more information on how to set up DMARC for Lessonly **[here](https://intercom.help/lessonly/en/articles/5653016-how-to-authenticate-a-domain)** and **[here](https://university.lessonly.com/preview/lesson/22575-ensuring-email-deliverability?section_id=135751)
**.

Add a Lessonly DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Lessonly emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Lessonly is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Mailerlite

Mailerlite
==========

How to set up DMARC for Mailerlite in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757444-mailerlite#h_3d35d2a643)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757444-mailerlite#h_aa47cab1c1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757444-mailerlite#h_40e053e80a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757444-mailerlite#h_403af0ce90)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757444-mailerlite#h_13df820bae)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757444-mailerlite#h_903cbc7800)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Mailerlite** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Mailerlite emails
==========================================================

Before you can set up DKIM for Mailerlite, you will need to verify your email address.

How to verify your email address
--------------------------------

**1.** Head to the **Settings** page select the **Domains** tab.

**2.** Click **Add domain**.

**3.** Enter the email address you wish to use to send emails from and click **Save**.  
​

[![a screenshot of a account settings](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920969873/0fdc6712fe49ec188502e0e3/RT0IjyvIMnnzunpPiAXnJZsBkZHf84aJpQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6b656fb4835b4d48e193e574a5282ac58cea2292c748f24e37a62939ea6ee044&req=fSInH893lYZcFb4f3HP0gIhh%2F65sHknu4SZdbRJIxNqNQluO4HsNLCK71Lci%0AvNNydXc1%2FiWp%2FpMtjQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920969873/0fdc6712fe49ec188502e0e3/RT0IjyvIMnnzunpPiAXnJZsBkZHf84aJpQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6b656fb4835b4d48e193e574a5282ac58cea2292c748f24e37a62939ea6ee044&req=fSInH893lYZcFb4f3HP0gIhh%2F65sHknu4SZdbRJIxNqNQluO4HsNLCK71Lci%0AvNNydXc1%2FiWp%2FpMtjQ%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Once you receive the confirmation email, click on the **Confirm my email address** link.

**5.** You will see a success message confirming the domain verification.

How to authenticate your domain
-------------------------------

**1.** Head to the **Settings** page select the **Domains** tab.

**2.** Click on **Authenticate** for a verified domain, or **Add domain** for an unverified one (and then click on **Send confirmation email**).

**3.** You’ll get the **Name** and **Value** fields for the **DKIM** and **SPF** records of this domain. Keep this page open.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920969885/8668ce1fab0604c009d2fdb2/z3U7qBt5gmHnK4jP9ddyf7a0UyIL89VqDw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=380e0882875189572280bbb6c5afc17d1d89064aa5cbd15c4b2438c2e608edc5&req=fSInH893lYlaFb4f3HP0gHoKZ1MR%2BWlbkQk1XJDJ6yEond5mf8iSYQV%2FSedl%0AelOPjrM4pCS%2BDP2HQQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920969885/8668ce1fab0604c009d2fdb2/z3U7qBt5gmHnK4jP9ddyf7a0UyIL89VqDw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=380e0882875189572280bbb6c5afc17d1d89064aa5cbd15c4b2438c2e608edc5&req=fSInH893lYlaFb4f3HP0gHoKZ1MR%2BWlbkQk1XJDJ6yEond5mf8iSYQV%2FSedl%0AelOPjrM4pCS%2BDP2HQQ%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Copy the DKIM key record by using the **Copy** button on the Mailerlite account window and add it to Valimail Enforce. Additionally, you will need to also add the **Mailerlite** service to your Enabled Senders list which is also on the Configuration page in Enforce.

**5.** After you add the DKIM key and Mailerlite service to your Enabled Senders list, head back to your MailerLite Domains tab and click on the **Authenticate** button for the domain you want to authenticate.

**6.** Click on the green **Check DNS Records** button to check if the DKIM and SPF records match.

If they match, the records will be approved and the domain will be authenticated.

[![a screenshot of a account settings](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920969889/21b319be28c80717d9334aa9/W8FKikYjXIBRTfovQtL6g8Oonpd4jSITdw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=196e45a668e9552e855817e11f7253a499d2c0583323517d97c3e284553a9be8&req=fSInH893lYlWFb4f3HP0gC4QyCBQIOhAeVYX1qriNLGYkQbpNu397dsGapZz%0AYwHq8oaByZD7okf%2Bdg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920969889/21b319be28c80717d9334aa9/W8FKikYjXIBRTfovQtL6g8Oonpd4jSITdw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=196e45a668e9552e855817e11f7253a499d2c0583323517d97c3e284553a9be8&req=fSInH893lYlWFb4f3HP0gC4QyCBQIOhAeVYX1qriNLGYkQbpNu397dsGapZz%0AYwHq8oaByZD7okf%2Bdg%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Mailerlite **[here](https://www.mailerlite.com/help/how-to-verify-and-authenticate-your-domain)
**.

Add a Mailerlite DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Mailerlite emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Mailerlite is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in Mailerlite for your domain
==================================================

You may notice that Mailerlite emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added Mailerlite to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by Mailerlite not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by configuring a CNAME record.

More details on how to achieve that are found in the article **[here](https://www.mailerlite.com/help/how-to-verify-and-authenticate-your-domain#new/align-domain)
.**

MailPoet

MailPoet
========

How to set up DMARC for MailPoet in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757459-mailpoet#h_eb5c75d147)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757459-mailpoet#h_ac258f44ed)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757459-mailpoet#h_67ec56c67a)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the DKIM authentication process for **MailPoet** and how this is managed in Valimail. MailPoet does not currently support SPF-aligned authentication, but DKIM authentication should be enough for most use case scenarios.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your MailPoet emails
========================================================

Adding a Sender Domain and setting up DKIM will help assure your subscribers that emails MailPoet sends on your behalf were indeed sent by you and thus may improve deliverability of your emails.

**1.** Add your domain to the [My Sender Domains](https://account.mailpoet.com/sender_domains)
 list by typing your domain to the **Domain** input and click **Add a new Sender Domain**.

[![a screenshot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920978074/27c3d0f36fe3f0f476eefb26/MG-sW3pYotDWVs5oSR9LrUA7RjJlBDmcbQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8771ffcf61fa788d709f8b2d8942a4ad9a2db6b5b51671888ea1712b86ee8647&req=fSInH852nYZbFb4f3HP0gIq31VcSrE3XHKah3AW6zKZQG3dsDVWyuFcneukN%0AfajYic9JBuxhh4gWyw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920978074/27c3d0f36fe3f0f476eefb26/MG-sW3pYotDWVs5oSR9LrUA7RjJlBDmcbQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8771ffcf61fa788d709f8b2d8942a4ad9a2db6b5b51671888ea1712b86ee8647&req=fSInH852nYZbFb4f3HP0gIq31VcSrE3XHKah3AW6zKZQG3dsDVWyuFcneukN%0AfajYic9JBuxhh4gWyw%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Add the requested DNS records to your domain's DNS server.

**Note**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add that TXT record in your DNS.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920978080/1eac4a3ee85804061a7ed5f6/3y81at4SGu90aI4DqtpVjtXP0hJ799wyCA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=fef36409cb531eb6abb8a87d723edd34af40bbad4ea2b93a07821da2e1fe1902&req=fSInH852nYlfFb4f3HP0gARUe6RZLoqTybcJ6urqq%2By6%2FR0%2F2xb1m8v2Nfwb%0AQ0EYQAzf0SUlJ2TF0Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920978080/1eac4a3ee85804061a7ed5f6/3y81at4SGu90aI4DqtpVjtXP0hJ799wyCA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=fef36409cb531eb6abb8a87d723edd34af40bbad4ea2b93a07821da2e1fe1902&req=fSInH852nYlfFb4f3HP0gARUe6RZLoqTybcJ6urqq%2By6%2FR0%2F2xb1m8v2Nfwb%0AQ0EYQAzf0SUlJ2TF0Q%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Click **Verify DNS records**.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for MailPoet, **[here](https://kb.mailpoet.com/article/188-how-to-set-up-mailpoet-sending-service#dns)
**.

Add a MailPoet DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your MailPoet emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that MailPoet is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757113-kayako#h_f61a99a0c0)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Kayako

Kayako
======

How to set up DMARC for Kayako in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757113-kayako#h_9f3d8d0ea0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757113-kayako#h_eb849947e0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757113-kayako#h_f61a99a0c0)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Kayako** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Kayako** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Kayako emails
======================================================

If you want to enable DKIM for a custom domain or your own company's email address, you need to add some CNAME entries to your DNS records first.

**1.** If you are adding DKIM for a custom domain, start by signing in to your domain's DNS provider.

**Note**: For help enabling DKIM for your default Kayako domain (e.g., support@\[yourcompany\].Kayako.com), skip to step 5.

**2.** Go to your DNS records.

**3.** Add three new CNAME records that read as follows:

email.\[YOURDOMAINNAME\].com, pointing to email.kayako.com

s1.\_domainkey.\[YOURDOMAINNAME\].com, pointing to s1.\_domainkey.kayako.com

s2.\_domainkey.\[YOURDOMAINNAME\].com, pointing to s2.\_domainkey.kayako.com

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail fro your domain, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and the 3rd CNAME record in your DNS.

**NOTES**:

*   Replace \[YOURDOMAINNAME\] with your custom domain.
    
*   We recommend that you wait for 24 hours to allow your updated DNS records to propagate. If you enable DKIM in Kayako before the changes have taken effect, your emails may not send correctly.
    
*   If any of the required CNAME records are already in use by other services, you need to contact the **Kayako support team** asking to set up sender authentication manually via our SendGrid account.
    

**4.** Once your changes have taken effect, sign in to Kayako and go to the **admin area**.

**5.** Click **Email** on the sidebar and switch to the **Settings** tab. (see screenshot below for steps 5-7).

**6.** Check the **Use DKIM** to authenticate box.

**7.** Click the **Save** button to enable DKIM.

[![a screenshot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920887387/da7f0b7c99a3961060f0ecd9/ZATcW13XmdNNWPWweDYryapzstqFHokFfg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=cea98a62bf0f6a1858cc05d96dfdb5e483dabffbdee4c46ff249e28c273c6f45&req=fSInHsF5nolYFb4f3HP0gDSuZXdKPsQ3wDUs%2BGX7MBSWhrdqQGe1bw1kEOEt%0AHrlgECPTjyxe6Nf%2FiA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920887387/da7f0b7c99a3961060f0ecd9/ZATcW13XmdNNWPWweDYryapzstqFHokFfg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=cea98a62bf0f6a1858cc05d96dfdb5e483dabffbdee4c46ff249e28c273c6f45&req=fSInHsF5nolYFb4f3HP0gDSuZXdKPsQ3wDUs%2BGX7MBSWhrdqQGe1bw1kEOEt%0AHrlgECPTjyxe6Nf%2FiA%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Kayako, **[here](https://help.kayako.com/hc/en-us/articles/360006457279-Ensuring-Your-Emails-Are-Received-and-Do-Not-Go-Into-Your-Customer-s-Spam-Folder)
**.

Add a Kayako DKIM key in Valimail
=================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Kayako emails
=====================================================

Once you establish that Kayako is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus

ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus
=============================

How to set up DMARC for ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757463-manageengine-servicedesk-plus#h_b334a133b0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757463-manageengine-servicedesk-plus#h_f857adbf1a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757463-manageengine-servicedesk-plus#h_b698501862)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus emails
=============================================================================

**1.** Navigate to the **Settings** page -> **Email tab**, and click **Add Domain**.

[![add domain button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920976554/d40e2b1bab0a2a86c0d966c3/pozE2J--mp5zLh4PjkDydYtsWqzaXJ-jqw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=32d586b59df6996a70d1caf182594d1b630eda7941397bce276995753ed9bb50&req=fSInH854mIRbFb4f3HP0gH3L5o1LubMCDYxVJcWxfCv5d7M4WAf%2B9pTmfnd%2F%0AdL54ncMG%2FDAKkv9epA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920976554/d40e2b1bab0a2a86c0d966c3/pozE2J--mp5zLh4PjkDydYtsWqzaXJ-jqw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=32d586b59df6996a70d1caf182594d1b630eda7941397bce276995753ed9bb50&req=fSInH854mIRbFb4f3HP0gH3L5o1LubMCDYxVJcWxfCv5d7M4WAf%2B9pTmfnd%2F%0AdL54ncMG%2FDAKkv9epA%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** In the dialog that appears, provide the domain name, and click **Add**.

[![add domain window](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920976564/0009eb0ea5b2663df0c5740c/F1g1jxQGwsOx4J1hVwnLZwhVGigHEDkYMw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2733347212b137e6cde9360b1c3194738f55bc3281a268e330f23e1cbe6df88b&req=fSInH854mIdbFb4f3HP0gJaL%2BtDEbx8RDOLewa1K4ko756h5KT6xcdeTcucO%0ATXgvh%2FkqKg90ehpH3w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920976564/0009eb0ea5b2663df0c5740c/F1g1jxQGwsOx4J1hVwnLZwhVGigHEDkYMw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2733347212b137e6cde9360b1c3194738f55bc3281a268e330f23e1cbe6df88b&req=fSInH854mIdbFb4f3HP0gJaL%2BtDEbx8RDOLewa1K4ko756h5KT6xcdeTcucO%0ATXgvh%2FkqKg90ehpH3w%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Click the **Get Code** link and copy the generated SPF and DKIM values.

[![spf and dkim records window](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920976568/4930be2bfe39a1caf671dba7/xiuGsJFjRlsEeSFnOJvHHgAD-21APvEC2g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=443558d7a0bdb03d084052026c07c0f240e13d3f41b5a326e8ee917284398f17&req=fSInH854mIdXFb4f3HP0gI0MX2yNf4YIRY7eURkf3QYlqgAh5Cp8KTbEd4er%0ANwXG1JIpVr4HPAj1Jw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920976568/4930be2bfe39a1caf671dba7/xiuGsJFjRlsEeSFnOJvHHgAD-21APvEC2g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=443558d7a0bdb03d084052026c07c0f240e13d3f41b5a326e8ee917284398f17&req=fSInH854mIdXFb4f3HP0gI0MX2yNf4YIRY7eURkf3QYlqgAh5Cp8KTbEd4er%0ANwXG1JIpVr4HPAj1Jw%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Add the SPF and DKIM values in your DNS settings.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and also add ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus in your Enabled Senders list.

**5.** Return to your ServiceDesk Analytics Plus console and click the **Validate** button to verify your domain and start authenticated sending emails on behalf of your domain.

[![dkim and spf records validation](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920976573/80e4ebde1541b6ccc7fbed16/yPfP-tVADLmpqMDnU_ATvqHZZT4t_PFNsw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=445a4cfda3e016963abf0b9aaeceaac5df7cf47404092b6460de5a2034dbc32c&req=fSInH854mIZcFb4f3HP0gMmaH%2Bn1wIRi46%2F9AizyYO1SjI7LAxX9d8zvZLGo%0A1f1iuinAxMCtEJ7RdQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920976573/80e4ebde1541b6ccc7fbed16/yPfP-tVADLmpqMDnU_ATvqHZZT4t_PFNsw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=445a4cfda3e016963abf0b9aaeceaac5df7cf47404092b6460de5a2034dbc32c&req=fSInH854mIZcFb4f3HP0gMmaH%2Bn1wIRi46%2F9AizyYO1SjI7LAxX9d8zvZLGo%0A1f1iuinAxMCtEJ7RdQ%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus, **[here](https://www.manageengine.com/analytics-plus/cloud-help/mail-configuration.html)
**.

Add a ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus DKIM key in Valimail
========================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus emails
============================================================================

Once you establish that ManageEngine ServiceDesk Plus is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

# Campaign Monitor by Marigold | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757492-campaign-monitor-by-marigold#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757492-campaign-monitor-by-marigold#h_f4a0769308)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757492-campaign-monitor-by-marigold#h_7e1697103f)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Campaign Monitor by Marigold

Campaign Monitor by Marigold
============================

How to set up DMARC for Campaign Monitor in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757492-campaign-monitor-by-marigold#h_c4659ca87a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757492-campaign-monitor-by-marigold#h_f4a0769308)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757492-campaign-monitor-by-marigold#h_7e1697103f)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Campaign Monitor** and how they are managed in Valimail Enforce. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

**Note**: Campaign Monitor has been rebranded as _Marigold_ at the beginning of 2023. For more info on that, you can visit **[here](https://meetmarigold.com/)
**.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Campaign Monitor emails
================================================================

1\. Set up the domain you want to send from in your web host.

2\. In Marigold/Campaign Monitor, click your profile image at the top right, then click **Account settings**.

3\. Click **Sending Domains**.

4\. Click **Add domain**.

5\. In the **Domain** field, enter a fully qualified domain name, for example, mail.example.com. Authenticating a second level domain (for example, example.com) will not authenticate its subdomains. Once you have entered your domain, Continue.

6\. You now need to add the generated DKIM key in **Valimail**.

7\. Once you have added the records into your DNS, go back into Marigold/Campaign Monitor and click **Authenticate now** at the bottom of the screen.

You will see a confirmation message once the domain has been successfully authenticated. The next time you create an email campaign, you will be able to select your authenticated domain when defining the sender.

You can find the instructions on how to set up DKIM for Campaign Monitor by Marigold, **[here](https://help.campaignmonitor.com/manage-your-own-authentication)
**.

Add a Campaign Monitor DKIM key in Valimail
===========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Campaign Monitor emails
===============================================================

Once you establish that Campaign Monitor is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

**Note**: Marigold does not send SPF aligned emails by default.

* * *

Related Articles

[Campaigner](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8752228-campaigner)
[Zoho Campaigns](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758256-zoho-campaigns)

Mailjet by Sinch

Mailjet by Sinch
================

How to set up DMARC for Mailjet in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757452-mailjet-by-sinch#h_32ec805bb6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757452-mailjet-by-sinch#h_db04c99544)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757452-mailjet-by-sinch#h_eac31c8dfb)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Mailjet by Sinch** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Mailjet emails
=======================================================

**1.** Log in your Mailjet account and go to **Mailjet** -> **Account** -> **Domain**.

From your **Sending Domain Authentication** page, you will find all your sending domains and their SPF & DKIM statuses. (Every time you add a sending email address or domain, the domain will be added to the **Sending Domain Authentication** page).

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971630/d2f43f594a8d333e59435507/n9GclyVfejp0rRr1dBfmj6oepZ98YxdaTg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3aff064d4349479f6cf116d71b0cf05fb3de38375990f09b47858d2c694b442d&req=fSInH85%2Fm4JfFb4f3HP0gCUIlpRjcrvpB%2FsfONKYMk7Ob%2BV3WpbxKMDwTaPD%0AT5eqwWAYRSJLhqAkBQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971630/d2f43f594a8d333e59435507/n9GclyVfejp0rRr1dBfmj6oepZ98YxdaTg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3aff064d4349479f6cf116d71b0cf05fb3de38375990f09b47858d2c694b442d&req=fSInH85%2Fm4JfFb4f3HP0gCUIlpRjcrvpB%2FsfONKYMk7Ob%2BV3WpbxKMDwTaPD%0AT5eqwWAYRSJLhqAkBQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** For the domain you want to authenticate, click on **Manage** to view the SPF & DKIM values.

[![a screenshot of a computer login](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971637/c8003bbc2f7d03a981b4881d/bVLsRTGjH8d3rO_RcsFc7EHnG8-iddaqEQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=692ebaffe52ccf36c1b15b64c5e04dcad0e7c24f826e10f79e6d38a97c864dcb&req=fSInH85%2Fm4JYFb4f3HP0gKKStSfqbF%2FhwFbRsX%2F%2B5LE9em6SPoZdbWWVnjl6%0A38z7iyuU6SwB%2BUBZxg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971637/c8003bbc2f7d03a981b4881d/bVLsRTGjH8d3rO_RcsFc7EHnG8-iddaqEQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=692ebaffe52ccf36c1b15b64c5e04dcad0e7c24f826e10f79e6d38a97c864dcb&req=fSInH85%2Fm4JYFb4f3HP0gKKStSfqbF%2FhwFbRsX%2F%2B5LE9em6SPoZdbWWVnjl6%0A38z7iyuU6SwB%2BUBZxg%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Copy the DKIM selector and DKIM XTXT value of the key from the Manage window.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971643/5ddd3ba0213e6d19e7405c16/GGU1ceDL2G79PQyFtQCqWyjnbauWpy6BwQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6dd767c09620dd58525b027c19d8ca0db30b0b546f1025ee091a5144fd20e240&req=fSInH85%2Fm4VcFb4f3HP0gOKuNf2JBid8BeiBqtnjb1VFYdm4iAemlCdM3vtP%0AV279%2F6kPIypIWrplUQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971643/5ddd3ba0213e6d19e7405c16/GGU1ceDL2G79PQyFtQCqWyjnbauWpy6BwQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6dd767c09620dd58525b027c19d8ca0db30b0b546f1025ee091a5144fd20e240&req=fSInH85%2Fm4VcFb4f3HP0gOKuNf2JBid8BeiBqtnjb1VFYdm4iAemlCdM3vtP%0AV279%2F6kPIypIWrplUQ%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Add the DKIM key in Valimail Enforce and also add Mailjet to your Enabled Senders list in Enforce.

**5.** Once you have added the DKIM key and the Mailjet service on your Configuration page in Enforce, go back to your Mailjet page and click the **Force Refresh** button.

[![a screenshot of a web page](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971647/41c37128034ec0393017b410/54QZzfAGYtT03ClbM6I5yPuvVasGntq_nQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=52811a0ab6b83974eb2aa3951f464ca61a1642f6935873a74f6bdb0818c07080&req=fSInH85%2Fm4VYFb4f3HP0gNgzgQAQnpDsC4odjpAJWcxP2KHgCvyovhy6lsw7%0AaquLdxNjTja%2B4sXlVA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971647/41c37128034ec0393017b410/54QZzfAGYtT03ClbM6I5yPuvVasGntq_nQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=52811a0ab6b83974eb2aa3951f464ca61a1642f6935873a74f6bdb0818c07080&req=fSInH85%2Fm4VYFb4f3HP0gNgzgQAQnpDsC4odjpAJWcxP2KHgCvyovhy6lsw7%0AaquLdxNjTja%2B4sXlVA%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Once your domain has been authenticated, you will see the green your record looks good message.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971656/8d9e8b82f73eb62fa340a8f5/03Oouowi4rw1Id9mkO-WNJNRfzhQUP7CMw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3f807dfb74fc5b0eabe5add16ace4fc446a7dbb5b31e414af431d0cc65b08e1e&req=fSInH85%2Fm4RZFb4f3HP0gJ%2FUIDQaALAuNnmTXvIbH7U9uzDeF3CzNVS6D5WK%0Ao5%2B41VXqrYKz1E75ug%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920971656/8d9e8b82f73eb62fa340a8f5/03Oouowi4rw1Id9mkO-WNJNRfzhQUP7CMw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3f807dfb74fc5b0eabe5add16ace4fc446a7dbb5b31e414af431d0cc65b08e1e&req=fSInH85%2Fm4RZFb4f3HP0gJ%2FUIDQaALAuNnmTXvIbH7U9uzDeF3CzNVS6D5WK%0Ao5%2B41VXqrYKz1E75ug%3D%3D%0A)

**Note**: You need to force a refresh to check the status as Mailjet does not automatically check for SPF & DKIM changes.

You can find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Mailjet **[here](https://documentation.mailjet.com/hc/en-us/articles/360042412734-Authenticating-Domains-with-SPF-DKIM)
**.

Add a Mailjet DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Mailjet emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Mailjet is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757470-marchingorder#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757470-marchingorder#h_df4f939ebf)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757470-marchingorder#h_b493adb2f2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757470-marchingorder#h_d212714d97)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

MarchingOrder

MarchingOrder
=============

How to set up DMARC for MarchingOrder in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757470-marchingorder#h_df4f939ebf)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757470-marchingorder#h_b493adb2f2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757470-marchingorder#h_d212714d97)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **MarchingOrder** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

MarchingOrder typically uses a dedicated subdomain configuration setup and they are leveraging Elastic Email as their infrastructure provider.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your MarchingOrder emails
=============================================================

**1.** Please add a DKIM record (TXT record) of the public key so that the From domain can sign emails with DKIM for security and deliverability purposes. The d= domain would be the From domain. The selector will be "**api**.\_domainkey" in all cases.

Name (if using primary domain): api.\_domainkey.yourdomain.edu

Or

Name (if using subdomain): api.\_domainkey.subdomain.yourdomain.edu

Value:

k=rsa;t=s;p=MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQCbmGbQMzYeMvxwtNQoXN0waGYaciuKx8mtMh5czguT4EZlJXuCt6V+l56mmt3t68FEX5JJ0q4ijG71BGoFRkl87uJi7LrQt1ZZmZCvrEII0YO4mp8sDLXC8g1aUAoi8TJgxq2MJqCaMyj5kAm3Fdy2tzftPCV/lbdiJqmBnWKjtwIDAQAB

**Important**: If you are managing this domain/subdomain in Valimail, you will need to add this DKIM in the Configuration page for your domain in Valimail Enforce, like presented below.

**2.** After you add the DKIM key, you will need to contact MarchingOrder for them to verify the setup.

Notify MarchingOrder of the sending email address so that it may be added and verified in the system.

After adding the sender email address, please copy/paste the information below and instruct the service owner to forward it to MarchingOrder.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Hello,

The email deliverability steps are complete. The sender email address for MarchingOrder will be <insert sender email address>. Please forward this information to your MarchingOrder customer success manager so that they can add / verify this email within their system.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

Sometimes, they may encounter an error with the SPF record. See the notes below:

**Notes:**

We have heard from various customers that it is preferable to set up MarchingOrder from a specific subdomain for example, [graduationnews.mydomain.edu](https://graduationnews.mydomain.edu/)
 instead of setting it up at the Org domain level (ie instead of [mydomain.edu](https://mydomain.edu/)
). This is due to a few reasons:

*   MarchingOrder leverages Elastic Email as their infrastructure provider and has limited control over Elastic Email's verification process.
    
*   Elastic Email is incapable of parsing our customers' standard SPF record. Instead their platform is doing a simple regex check against the text record to find an include and not a real SPF query. (we have reached out to Elastic Email to improve their verification process). Using a dedicated subdomain as noted above can help with this problem.
    
*   Another workaround is to specifically list the Elastic Email SPF include in your SPF record directly after the Valimail record. This is not ideal as the Elastic Email record is overly permissive. But some customers have said this worked for them.
    

Please be sure to contact Marching Order's Support Team for additional information.

Add a MarchingOrder DKIM key in Valimail
========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your MarchingOrder emails
============================================================

Once you establish that MarchingOrder is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Related Articles

[AfterShip](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8470999-aftership)
[Beamery](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8471015-beamery)
[Elastic Email](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754592-elastic-email)
[Netcore](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757552-netcore)

Marquis Documatix

Marquis Documatix
=================

How to set up DMARC for Marquis Documatix in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757497-marquis-documatix#h_5ebbdf94cf)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757497-marquis-documatix#h_92196003f7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757497-marquis-documatix#h_ca6acddc81)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757497-marquis-documatix#h_c0cb4d3aac)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757497-marquis-documatix#h_c2e3372483)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757497-marquis-documatix#h_914b247eb1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757497-marquis-documatix#h_6f3c20250e)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Marquis Documatix** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Marquis Documatix uses a set of 2 CNAME DKIM keys and a dedicated subdomain configuration setup.

Setup a dedicated subdomain
===========================

In order for Marquis Documatix to send emails using your domain name, we will need to have a sub-domain created, e.g., ‘email’ (email.yourdomain.com). IT and marketing will most likely coordinate with each other to determine a sub-domain name. To set up a sub-domain, you will need to do the following:

STEP 1: **CREATE A CNAME FOR YOUR SUB-DOMAIN**
----------------------------------------------

The CNAME will brand your email “from” name, as well as all email links and images, while allowing

DocuMatix to report back about email deliverability and interaction. The CNAME will point your subdomain to the DocuMatix service. Create a CNAME for your external subdomain with the value of documatix.com. Example below:

[![a close up of a logo](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920985328/3d1039ba20ed089bd7aef2cc/-6SVkWvlUD88-SN3Vb_mSQoil7NI_M_GEA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1fd1e626fa06d4ee51a3b090ce872e61df22e6da967bd5b19916499fe5e05503&req=fSInH8F7noNXFb4f3HP0gHN3T%2FBy1K%2BQGyWGfRkKG%2ByB6cbTvYQJBi2ytaG%2B%0A9eMiOsdVjAdD2ftpYQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920985328/3d1039ba20ed089bd7aef2cc/-6SVkWvlUD88-SN3Vb_mSQoil7NI_M_GEA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1fd1e626fa06d4ee51a3b090ce872e61df22e6da967bd5b19916499fe5e05503&req=fSInH8F7noNXFb4f3HP0gHN3T%2FBy1K%2BQGyWGfRkKG%2ByB6cbTvYQJBi2ytaG%2B%0A9eMiOsdVjAdD2ftpYQ%3D%3D%0A)

STEP #2: **CREATE AN INTERNAL A RECORD FOR YOUR SUB-DOMAIN**
------------------------------------------------------------

If your internal DNS does not resolve the sub-domain record from the external DNS, you will need to create an internal A record pointing to 206.71.80.80. This will allow images and links to work on emails that are sent to email addresses with your domain. Important: ensure that you do not have an A record for your sub-domain on your external DNS. An external A record would conflict with the CNAME.

**AFTER SUB-DOMAIN CREATION**
-----------------------------

Once these steps are completed you will need to determine a username for each account that you have.

This will be your new “From” email address. For example, a general marketing account may have ‘marketing’ as the username ([\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#177a76657c72637e797057727a767e7b396e78626573787a767e79)
. com). You will pick the username you would like to use, but we will create it for you. Please note: The above steps are required in order for Marquis to deliver emails on your behalf.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Marquis Documatix emails
=================================================================

If your organization has DMARC turned on for your emails, DKIM adds a digital signature to the email header which is used to verify the authenticity of the email. If you have DMARC turned on, let a Marquis Client setup team member know that you want to use DKIM. If you choose to add DMARC later, contact DocuMatix support to add DKIM.

You will need to create two new sub-domains with the CNAME below:

**key1.\_domainkey.email.yourdomain.com CNAME key1.documatix.com.**

**key2.\_domainkey.email.yourdomain.com CNAME key2.documatix.com.**

In case you need more assistance in setting this up, you can contact Marquis Documatix customer support directly at **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#6b0f181e1b1b04191f2b0c04060a191a1e021845080406)
**

Add a Marquis Documatix DKIM key in Valimail
============================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Marquis Documatix emails
================================================================

Once you establish that Marquis Documatix is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

As you develop emails within DocuMatix, you can send tests to yourself. There may also be times when you use DocuMatix for internal emails to be sent to your staff. In both cases, whitelisting will ensure that the emails go through seamlessly. To help with email delivery inside your company it is highly recommended that you Whitelist the DocuMatix IP Address Ranges and/or the below DocuMatix Email Servers.

You need to make sure that you have the Marquis Documatix SPF IP range added in your SPF record.

**Marquis Documatix SPF IP range** - 206.71.80.64/27

By adding **Marquis Documatix** in your Enabled Senders list in Valimail, you would ensure the above whitelisting requirement is met.

The whitelisting procedure will vary, depending on your email service. Just provide this information to your IT department and they will be able to accomplish this quite easily. In addition, it is recommended that you whitelist your new “From” email address. Example: [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#f19c90839a9485989f96b1949c90989ddf889e8483959e9c90989fdf929e9c)

* * *

MarketingPlatform

MarketingPlatform
=================

How to set up DMARC for MarketingPlatform in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757495-marketingplatform#h_6682bcc8db)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757495-marketingplatform#h_74ec4c988e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757495-marketingplatform#h_7f0cf85a5f)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **MarketingPlatform** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your MarketingPlatform emails
=================================================================

If there is no DKIM for MarketingPlatform, you will receive an error message on this.

If you click on the **info** button, you will get the option to create a DKIM value for your domain.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920983565/fb2ae6a884b6f5c7897c3b31/OD9D_b-F-kW6kOHkoMv8UFeIRLwaf3fAJg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=04ee80b00039ec5127af348ba0d85bb2c760d39dd502e2f515ac7a45846c4d7d&req=fSInH8F9mIdaFb4f3HP0gFxJC4xhTrJRL%2FBtVvwlCfxK3jeNk4QdeNCHRxSV%0AJSf4fLaeltVN89dYdA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920983565/fb2ae6a884b6f5c7897c3b31/OD9D_b-F-kW6kOHkoMv8UFeIRLwaf3fAJg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=04ee80b00039ec5127af348ba0d85bb2c760d39dd502e2f515ac7a45846c4d7d&req=fSInH8F9mIdaFb4f3HP0gFxJC4xhTrJRL%2FBtVvwlCfxK3jeNk4QdeNCHRxSV%0AJSf4fLaeltVN89dYdA%3D%3D%0A)

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920983570/08321c7e3ea38f840edffdc5/ugly4C7Tyhuqd7lSgRBRnWw_auyPCcGt-g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=bc3738ba157e844ca20861affdfaea7f526d639c1e67b838f09a4e735f491471&req=fSInH8F9mIZfFb4f3HP0gCLmUneClxLGaZGCVtHAzqDYqXSiHDUWZF7wZY8Z%0ATKD8pVIW%2BNTgq42MKw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920983570/08321c7e3ea38f840edffdc5/ugly4C7Tyhuqd7lSgRBRnWw_auyPCcGt-g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=bc3738ba157e844ca20861affdfaea7f526d639c1e67b838f09a4e735f491471&req=fSInH8F9mIZfFb4f3HP0gCLmUneClxLGaZGCVtHAzqDYqXSiHDUWZF7wZY8Z%0ATKD8pVIW%2BNTgq42MKw%3D%3D%0A)

DKIM also needs to be created as a TXT-record in your DNS administration. However, it is important to note that the domain/hostname in this setup contains nothing but just the domain – most often you will always see emp.\_domainkey. in front of your domain when DKIM is used for MarketingPlatform.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add this DKIM TXT key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for MarketingPlatform, **[here](https://marketingplatform.com/wiki/spf-dkim-setup/)
**.

Add a MarketingPlatform DKIM key in Valimail
============================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your MarketingPlatform emails
================================================================

Once you establish that MarketingPlatform is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757467-mandrill#h_e236e687c4)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Mandrill

Mandrill
========

How to set up DMARC for Mandrill in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757467-mandrill#h_ffc03e924e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757467-mandrill#h_84aef705ff)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757467-mandrill#h_e236e687c4)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Mandrill** and how are they managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Mandrill emails
========================================================

**Note**: You will first need to add your domain to Mandrill.

**1.** Open your Mandrill account and go to **Settings** > **Domains**.

**2.** Enter your domain name and click on **Add** button.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920975751/bf0c454c164cdb9e9baaabf0/MV9w6Vl4430M310qDUfpRa0DDGMzyb7TPw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=80fbc4d76d93445215614519e8d18bf48d4ae5018cf828403918d63ba9e156a9&req=fSInH857moReFb4f3HP0gA2IAXMBrDBMty5%2FXtDAO1m%2FJ4i5Bu30JuOAgjnb%0AXyPQf0d7JYzTPi%2BcBg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920975751/bf0c454c164cdb9e9baaabf0/MV9w6Vl4430M310qDUfpRa0DDGMzyb7TPw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=80fbc4d76d93445215614519e8d18bf48d4ae5018cf828403918d63ba9e156a9&req=fSInH857moReFb4f3HP0gA2IAXMBrDBMty5%2FXtDAO1m%2FJ4i5Bu30JuOAgjnb%0AXyPQf0d7JYzTPi%2BcBg%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** After adding your domain, click on **View DKIM Settings**. You will find the DKIM record that contains the DKIM selector (mandrill) and the designated key value that you need to publish in **Valimail Enforce**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920975760/110393f1fedca670293f6a69/AdwE8f8aWbs1i_-2CkWXq--UiFRHAB-Zvw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b677d90c97254f3ac0b43bb48214b444cb13fc1f498b4c9401ceb5692157b209&req=fSInH857modfFb4f3HP0gKsNyKJdkCieU%2F7ilsuUwAdnHzT5jE6tQ0pTKgOz%0AG5SQdeiLz%2BuYlD%2FX3w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920975760/110393f1fedca670293f6a69/AdwE8f8aWbs1i_-2CkWXq--UiFRHAB-Zvw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b677d90c97254f3ac0b43bb48214b444cb13fc1f498b4c9401ceb5692157b209&req=fSInH857modfFb4f3HP0gKsNyKJdkCieU%2F7ilsuUwAdnHzT5jE6tQ0pTKgOz%0AG5SQdeiLz%2BuYlD%2FX3w%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** The next step is to add the Mandrill DKIM key in **Valimail Enforce**.

**5.** After publishing the TXT record in Valimail Enforce, click on the **Test DNS Settings** button in the Mandrill management console and check for a verification mark against your record.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920975764/11627c1d3ca96aa90d047629/nNFdDI0SxX7I0b-7wjcBmcYPHRlAYqI7Yw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=329c004b2b6ab9ca7359b441932746a4d5bdbfe7057abd10ed94885f3e5cbb89&req=fSInH857modbFb4f3HP0gAr3%2BEjVTqIxuz3Hr261%2B2F8tpugqgaSEWUrO0Tq%0AHPZVrmsZFRSPeDio8Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920975764/11627c1d3ca96aa90d047629/nNFdDI0SxX7I0b-7wjcBmcYPHRlAYqI7Yw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=329c004b2b6ab9ca7359b441932746a4d5bdbfe7057abd10ed94885f3e5cbb89&req=fSInH857modbFb4f3HP0gAr3%2BEjVTqIxuz3Hr261%2B2F8tpugqgaSEWUrO0Tq%0AHPZVrmsZFRSPeDio8Q%3D%3D%0A)

You can find the instructions on how to setup a DKIM key in Mandrill **[here](https://mailchimp.com/developer/transactional/docs/authentication-delivery/#configure-your-dns)
**.

Add a Mandrill DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Mandrill emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Mandrill is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

MailUp

MailUp
======

How to set up DMARC for MailUp in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757461-mailup#h_bd41aaf102)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757461-mailup#h_ad1744cf6c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757461-mailup#h_1ab5a71535)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **MailUp** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your MailUp emails
======================================================

**1.** Please create the following two CNAMEs (replace "mydomain.com" with your domain)

Record Name: ml01.\_domainkey.mydomain.com

Record Value: ml01.dkim.musvc.com.

Record Name: ml02.\_domainkey.mydomain.com

Record Value: ml02.dkim.musvc.com.

**Note**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail Enforce, you will just need to add these CNAME keys in your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

2\. After the DKIM keys were added in Valimail Enforce, you can run the DKIM test in [Senders authentication](https://help.mailup.com/display/MUG/Senders+authentication)
 to confirm that the CNAMEs have been successfully updated.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for MailUp, **[here](https://help.mailup.com/display/mailupQuickStart/Maximizing+deliverability+for+your+emails)
**.

Add a MailUp DKIM key in Valimail
=================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your MailUp emails
=====================================================

Once you establish that MailUp is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Magnews

Magnews
=======

How to set up DMARC for Magnews in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757441-magnews#h_78e62bd19c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757441-magnews#h_28dd995b2a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757441-magnews#h_1096ae165e)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Magnews** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

  
​

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Magnews emails
=======================================================

Getting better brand protection for your sending domain takes just a few steps .

*   Access magnews and enter the **Sender domains** section. Enter the domain of the email address set as the sender of your communications.
    
*   Then go to the **Management** section , click on **Deliverability** and then on **Sender domains**: add your domain here.
    
*   Magnews generates the public key to be entered in your domain's DNS by simply creating a TXT type DNS record. To do this, contact your IT department or your domain hosting manager.
    
*   Now all you have to do is access magnews again , in the previously created sender domain tab, to activate the DKIM signing .
    

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Magnews, **[here](https://www.magnews.it/blog/il-mondo-di-magnews/dkim-proteggi-il-tuo-dominio-mittente-dallo-spoofing/)
**.

Add a Magnews DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Magnews emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Magnews is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

MessageGears

MessageGears
============

How to set up DMARC for MessageGears in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757502-messagegears#h_6299f1acfe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757502-messagegears#h_cf213a821b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757502-messagegears#h_7987447b33)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **MessageGears** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your MessageGears emails
============================================================

If you’d like to implement DKIM for your domain, reach out to MessageGears Support Team to get started. You can [open a support ticket](https://support.messagegears.com/hc/en-us/requests/new)
, or email them directly at **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#23505653534c5157634e46505042444644464251500d404c4e)
**

They will provide you with a TXT DKIM key record that you will need to add in your DNS.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

Once the key is added, reach out back to MessageGears Support Team so they can verify the key.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for MessageGears, **[here](https://support.messagegears.com/hc/en-us/articles/115001011687-Email-Authentication-and-DMARC)
**.

Add a MessageGears DKIM key in Valimail
=======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your MessageGears emails
===========================================================

Once you establish that MessageGears is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *
