[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757556-newton-paycor#h_e00d5057b0)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Newton (Paycor)

Newton (Paycor)
===============

How to set up DMARC for Newton in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757556-newton-paycor#h_badcfc0524)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757556-newton-paycor#h_7ac5181deb)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757556-newton-paycor#h_e00d5057b0)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Newton** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Newton** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Website: [https://www.paycor.com/recruiting-software](https://www.paycor.com/recruiting-software)

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Newton (Paycor) emails
===============================================================

**1\.** Newton (now owned by Paycor) uses SendGrid under the hood. The setup process is like a standard SendGrid activation. This is done by opening a ticket with Newton's Customer Support.

**2\.** Newton will generate the proper DNS instructions for the activation. This will be a CNAME record hosted in your DNS and two CNAME DKIM keys. The DKIM keys can be published through Enforce since the DKIM keys will be on the top-level domain.

**3\.** The DKIM keys can be distinguished from having "\_domainkey." in the hostname.

**Ensure that Newton generates unique DKIM selectors and does not use s1/s2**

**4.** Once the DNS changes are in place, notify Newton support and they can activate on their end.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Newton (Paycor), **[here](https://support.paycor.com/s/article/Recruiting-Add-SPF-Record-Enable-DKIM-Signing-to-Improve-Email-Delivery-and-Security#dkim)
**.

Add a Newton (Paycor) DKIM key in Valimail
==========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Newton emails
=====================================================

Once you establish that Newton is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757500-meltwater#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757500-meltwater#h_d432eab24b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757500-meltwater#h_5b60c262c1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757500-meltwater#h_858eeecd74)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Meltwater

Meltwater
=========

How to set up DMARC for Meltwater in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757500-meltwater#h_d432eab24b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757500-meltwater#h_5b60c262c1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757500-meltwater#h_858eeecd74)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Meltwater** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Meltwater** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Meltwater emails
=========================================================

There are three main steps to set up these records:

**1.** Request your records within the platform.

**2.** Ask your IT Specialist to update the records within your email.

**3.** Your Domain status within the platform has changed to **Verified**.

*   Within the platform, select **Settings** from the left-hand side navigation bar.
    
*   Select **Approved Senders**.
    

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920986319/2d1ce855f7abe6817513eb7b/D2cuS4fsA3bGz8ZtGmk-FbzAbqbBp-wKcQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f8399af8c340a252457524f20446986e743658ecc2da08675734b2a11485437c&req=fSInH8F4noBWFb4f3HP0gLmBhuXAAPYuwH5qgcnlm3TiF9Hu1Sb3VxqO%2FT5A%0AUOuoyO2GfPoKBRKfeg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920986319/2d1ce855f7abe6817513eb7b/D2cuS4fsA3bGz8ZtGmk-FbzAbqbBp-wKcQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f8399af8c340a252457524f20446986e743658ecc2da08675734b2a11485437c&req=fSInH8F4noBWFb4f3HP0gLmBhuXAAPYuwH5qgcnlm3TiF9Hu1Sb3VxqO%2FT5A%0AUOuoyO2GfPoKBRKfeg%3D%3D%0A)

*   If you do not have any Approved Senders listed and no details under the **Domains** section, please add your work email address as an **Approved Sender**.
    
*   If you do have 'activated' Approved Senders, select the **Configure Domain** button on your Domain which is unverified.
    
*   Your specific CNAME records will open in a new page, and the URL can be sent to your IT specialist. CNAME records will house both SPF and DKIM records.
    

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920986329/b782a22860d94b1589637d39/Wrfd28UHkme1cHhgwA05dHzxmxe1bXg9-w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6c4ff22c12821ac248bbf5dee45e12526a969862e57e457e119af4e89b60a449&req=fSInH8F4noNWFb4f3HP0gOIoJAHeJDurNQmDOLtYSNzC5FsWs3R5xC4oqjW%2F%0AJbNTKTLQzszCf3IJmg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920986329/b782a22860d94b1589637d39/Wrfd28UHkme1cHhgwA05dHzxmxe1bXg9-w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6c4ff22c12821ac248bbf5dee45e12526a969862e57e457e119af4e89b60a449&req=fSInH8F4noNWFb4f3HP0gOIoJAHeJDurNQmDOLtYSNzC5FsWs3R5xC4oqjW%2F%0AJbNTKTLQzszCf3IJmg%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Meltwater will issue 3 CNAME records.

If you manage your DKIM keys in Valimail Enforce, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys in your domain's Configuration page in Valimail. The 3rd CNAME record needs to be added in your DNS. See example below:

CNAME em1199.example.com

Value **u21413664.wl167.sendgrid.net**

CNAME **mlt**.\_domainkey.example.com

Value **mlt.domainkey.u21413664.wl167.sendgrid.net**

CNAME **mlt2**.\_domainkey.example.com

Value **mlt2.domainkey.u21413664.wl167.sendgrid.net**

**5.** After all 3 DKIM records have been properly added in their respective management zones, check the status within Meltwater. Please note it can take 24-48 hours to update.

**6.** Within the platform, select **Settings** from the left-hand side navigation bar.

**7.** Select **Approved Senders**. Your domain will change to **This Domain has been verified.**

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920986340/4738f0b0f8e013d8fbda7920/wq-tmZoNGfxw5w-cm9oI_vDOXdecD6VPUQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3a71827fd368a3a3e7a8eacb25e8a80da8be2201a16296e0ad80eea97fd4bdb7&req=fSInH8F4noVfFb4f3HP0gAKYrFxScmB0ZT%2B8afZ1bDjAHNroz0QlXtg0sYXv%0ATAd5C8VF05cpF871Vw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920986340/4738f0b0f8e013d8fbda7920/wq-tmZoNGfxw5w-cm9oI_vDOXdecD6VPUQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3a71827fd368a3a3e7a8eacb25e8a80da8be2201a16296e0ad80eea97fd4bdb7&req=fSInH8F4noVfFb4f3HP0gAKYrFxScmB0ZT%2B8afZ1bDjAHNroz0QlXtg0sYXv%0ATAd5C8VF05cpF871Vw%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Meltwater **[here](https://help.meltwater.com/en/articles/4064638-how-to-add-an-approved-sender-email-delivery-settings)
**.

Add a Meltwater DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Meltwater emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Meltwater is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Netcore

Netcore
=======

How to set up DMARC for Netcore in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757552-netcore#h_48ac21e854)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757552-netcore#h_ee988fe24d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757552-netcore#h_c4a91bebda)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Netcore** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Netcore uses a dedicated subdomain setup for DKIM and SPF configuration.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Netcore emails
=======================================================

**1.** You will first need to add a sender domain in Netcore Email API.

**2.** Once you have added a sender domain in Netcore Email API, the system asks you to configure two TXT records on your domain's DNS settings. The primary objective of adding these records is to authenticate Netcore Email API servers to send your emails.

**3.** The first TXT record is to verify the ownership of your domain and authenticate your domain to send emails using Netcore Email API. **You will need to add the Netcore service to your domain's Configuration in Valimail Enforce.**

**4.** The second TXT record which the system asks to add is primarily for DKIM validation. It is a method to validate the authenticity of the emails. **You will need to add the DKIM key in Valimail Enforce**.

**5.** Once you have added these values in your settings, Press the **Check DNS Records** button to verify.

You can find the instructions to set up DKIM and SPF for Netcore **[here](https://emaildocs.netcorecloud.com/docs/what-is-spf-and-dkim-and-why-these-are-important-to-have)
**.

Add a Netcore DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Netcore emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Netcore is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Odoo

Odoo
====

How to set up DMARC for Odoo in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757600-odoo#h_005ba887bc)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757600-odoo#h_d43e2380dd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757600-odoo#h_09464f51a4)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the DKIM authentication processes for **Odoo** and how it is managed in Valimail.

Odoo does not support SPF alignment.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Odoo emails
====================================================

When sending an email, the Odoo server includes a unique DKIM signature in the headers. The recipient’s server decrypts this signature using the DKIM record in the database’s domain name. If the signature and the key contained in the record match, this guarantees that the message is authentic and has not been altered during transport.

To enable DKIM, add a CNAME record to your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce:

Selector: **odoo**

Value: **odoo.\_domainkey.odoo.com.**

You can then check to see if the DKIM key has been published, using a tool like [Google Admin Toolbox Dig](https://toolbox.googleapps.com/apps/dig/)
.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM for Odoo **[here](https://www.odoo.com/documentation/16.0/applications/general/email_communication/email_domain.html)
**.

Add an Odoo DKIM key in Valimail
================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Odoo emails
===================================================

Once you establish that Odoo is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Moosend

Moosend
=======

How to set up DMARC for Moosend in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757512-moosend#h_eb60f76ab8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757512-moosend#h_fcd20a3ac6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757512-moosend#h_0bee5f4b50)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Moosend** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Moosend emails
=======================================================

Steps to set up DKIM/Domain key for Moosend:

**1.** Log in to your **Moosend** account.

**2.** In the top navigation bar, click the **Account Icon** and click **Settings**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920988468/54156e69aac80a90cb0e8fc6/1Ow7PPNy6Y0yk5OrePGgYqoJXwCfESR2ng.png?expires=1755792000&signature=fa95bed2f42dce3d920041607c1962d8c6fc0ff0144f6f1ec7f8b780f139ccbc&req=fSInH8F2mYdXFb4f3HP0gJte1j4CVj0CjSe6r39ChViQXWPFJhzNwCWnAwk3%0AXa8ar9YUeyUb9rR%2BUQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920988468/54156e69aac80a90cb0e8fc6/1Ow7PPNy6Y0yk5OrePGgYqoJXwCfESR2ng.png?expires=1755792000&signature=fa95bed2f42dce3d920041607c1962d8c6fc0ff0144f6f1ec7f8b780f139ccbc&req=fSInH8F2mYdXFb4f3HP0gJte1j4CVj0CjSe6r39ChViQXWPFJhzNwCWnAwk3%0AXa8ar9YUeyUb9rR%2BUQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** In the menu on the left, click **Senders**.

**4.** In the **SPF/DKIM** column, you can see which senders have a valid DKIM signature indicated by a green check icon.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920988473/85c457cc9b71697ae2df91d6/Qffad-fM96LA3J6JtgGBOIxGCWn8Qq1pBQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9ec5f777f53efd6ec4dc20080a6826b3660fa890312858090563e226905a3c5d&req=fSInH8F2mYZcFb4f3HP0gKyeH%2BxyCFyB0ae%2BPbUo3xqBcbmSpF2kcgch6MtB%0Axn9oZeYYfd%2F95%2BIUlQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920988473/85c457cc9b71697ae2df91d6/Qffad-fM96LA3J6JtgGBOIxGCWn8Qq1pBQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9ec5f777f53efd6ec4dc20080a6826b3660fa890312858090563e226905a3c5d&req=fSInH8F2mYZcFb4f3HP0gKyeH%2BxyCFyB0ae%2BPbUo3xqBcbmSpF2kcgch6MtB%0Axn9oZeYYfd%2F95%2BIUlQ%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Click **Set up SPF/DKIM** for the sender address for which you want to set up a DKIM record.

**6.** Click the **Domain keys identified mail (DKIM)** drop-down menu to display the DKIM records.

**7.** Copy and paste the information in the TXT record name and TXT record value fields to your domain's Configuration page in Enforce.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920988475/025d64a96e485b322dc359cf/aSVf4w1gNsuRdJexhQt37zn3E5Vt6z419Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e6f95d37cf7cfc08d1bd2c4a5dfb069f320e29cf6c85db3e587f1157f12037e6&req=fSInH8F2mYZaFb4f3HP0gLyUjoLQvcdmSIZSefMLkOF8xz2jKFMXZci3UTQ8%0AY9SQ%2FSEFf5vgzuoFdQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920988475/025d64a96e485b322dc359cf/aSVf4w1gNsuRdJexhQt37zn3E5Vt6z419Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e6f95d37cf7cfc08d1bd2c4a5dfb069f320e29cf6c85db3e587f1157f12037e6&req=fSInH8F2mYZaFb4f3HP0gLyUjoLQvcdmSIZSefMLkOF8xz2jKFMXZci3UTQ8%0AY9SQ%2FSEFf5vgzuoFdQ%3D%3D%0A)

**Important**: It takes between 30 minutes to 48 hours to finalize the process before you can move to the next step.

**8.** Click **Verify DNS record** to complete the verification.

Example DKIM TXT record name for Moosend:

**ms.\_domainkey.mydomain.com**

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Moosend **[here](https://help.moosend.com/hc/en-us/articles/4409209163282-What-is-DKIM-and-why-you-should-care)
**.

Add a Moosend DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Moosend emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Moosend is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure#h_8642b45907)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure#h_80da03c5e3)

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
===========================

How to set up DMARC for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure#h_8e340d9c10)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure#h_0c54f37b00)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure#h_8642b45907)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure#h_80da03c5e3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure#h_6665e4145d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure#h_222e46891a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure#h_99d6c24b69)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Oracle Cloud Infrastructure** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure does not send SPF aligned emails by default, so DKIM would be the best option to have for this service.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Oracle Cloud Infrastructure emails
===========================================================================

Step 1: Confirm your sending domain and provider
------------------------------------------------

To add a DKIM record, you need to determine what sending domain or subdomain that you want to set up DKIM for. For this exercise, we use example.com. Determine what email sending service provider or conduit you’re looking to authenticate. In this case, we use OCI Email Delivery.

If you send from different domains or subdomains, each requires their own DKIM record to be created. You can reuse selector records, however.

Step 2: Create a selector
-------------------------

Create a selector record for that domain, which acts as your public key. This record is unique to that domain and can be whatever you choose. For this example, we use **1234-oracle**.

Step 3: Implement the record
----------------------------

You will need to publish the newly created DKIM key in **Valimail Enforce**. The example domain and selector record results in the following CNAME record for implementation:

**1234-oracle.\_domainkey.example.com** IN CNAME **1234-oracle.example.com.dkim.yyz1.oracleemaildelivery.com**

OCI uses standard 2048-bit encryption. Valimail Enforce supports the 2048 bit encryption.

Following the publishing of the record and propagation time, your DKIM record is functional, providing a private key in sent emails that shows mailbox providers that email coming from that email provider is legitimate.

Step 4: Trust but verify
------------------------

After everything is saved, it’s important to verify that your new DKIM record is set up correctly. You can most easily check through a third-party source for example the **[Google Admin Toolbox Dig](https://toolbox.googleapps.com/apps/dig/)
**. All you need is the domain and selector record.

You can also find the instructions to set up DKIM for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure **[here](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/integration-cloud/oracle-integration-oci/configure-email-authentication-settings-spf-and-dkim.html)
**.

Add an Oracle Cloud Infrastructure DKIM key in Valimail
=======================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Oracle Cloud Infrastructure emails
==========================================================================

Once you establish that Oracle Cloud Infrastructure is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757601-okta#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757601-okta#h_493d2c18d8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757601-okta#h_03d7af42fe)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Okta

Okta
====

How to set up DMARC for Okta in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757601-okta#h_f743f95ec4)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757601-okta#h_493d2c18d8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757601-okta#h_03d7af42fe)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM configuration process for **Okta** and they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages SendGrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Okta as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Okta infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Okta emails
====================================================

**1.** In the Admin Console, go to **Customizations** -> **Emails**. (If you enabled multibrand customization, go to **Customizations** -> **Brands**, and then select the brand you want. In the **Domains** tab -> **Email** section, click **Add domain**).

**2.** In the **Email address** field, enter the email address that you want to send the system notification emails from. This email address appears in the emails sent to your users.

**3.** In the **Name of email sender** field, enter the name of sender. This name appears as the sender in the emails sent to your users.

**4.** Click **Continue**.

**5.** Update your DNS records using the provided values.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys (Those are **s1** and **s2** in the example below) in Valimail Enforce and then add the TXT and the first CNAME record to your DNS.

**6.** After you've updated your DNS records through your domain provider, click I've updated the DNS records. Okta begins polling your DNS records until it detects your updates (this may take up to 24 hours). Your configuration is pending until the DNS updates are detected.

Alternatively, you can click **I will update the DNS records later**. Your records aren't polled and your configuration is incomplete until you update the relevant DNS records and click **I've updated the DNS records**. You can view the list of records that require an update at any time.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921004901/95becd71cb06b0a2335defdf/0vsZrXMy8TIZSma8ZE7bBcoc2jwO_5M9OA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=25f916779a968b7490dddc26f840b7c1f299904080c4e636aa05a5f593f2913f&req=fSImFsl6lIFeFb4f3HP0gKeRHqsQt%2BhPbB1y1sFOpTdfCeUbP7rF%2BJzWceTO%0AW6ZDS2bmjYF5HgiGkg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921004901/95becd71cb06b0a2335defdf/0vsZrXMy8TIZSma8ZE7bBcoc2jwO_5M9OA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=25f916779a968b7490dddc26f840b7c1f299904080c4e636aa05a5f593f2913f&req=fSImFsl6lIFeFb4f3HP0gKeRHqsQt%2BhPbB1y1sFOpTdfCeUbP7rF%2BJzWceTO%0AW6ZDS2bmjYF5HgiGkg%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Select a DNS update option:

*   **I've updated the DNS records** — Okta begins polling your DNS records until it detects your updates (up to 24 hours). Your configuration is pending until the DNS updates are detected.
    
*   **I will update the DNS records later** — Your records aren't polled. Your configuration is incomplete until you update the relevant DNS records and click **I've updated the DNS records**. You can update the records at any time.
    

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF **[here](https://developer.okta.com/docs/guides/custom-url-domain/main/#about-custom-email-addresses)
**.

Add an Okta DKIM key in Valimail
================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Okta emails
===================================================

Once you establish that Okta is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757557-ngp-van#h_fad03adde5)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

NGP VAN

NGP VAN
=======

How to set up DMARC for NGP VAN in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757557-ngp-van#h_173c3e0131)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757557-ngp-van#h_701cbfe7fe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757557-ngp-van#h_fad03adde5)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **NGP VAN** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your NGP VAN emails
=======================================================

In order to set up DKIM for your domain in NGP VAN, please follow the instructions in this screenshot:

[![NGP VAN DKIM key details](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920998035/c1ce0573251f793685f7345f/t9eOqb6N_SKI2qxaCzaPmcNnT6tAqsrn0Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a8f5eabc955dc504818204072dae55d428162927ed1ea03e9696eb1cde7d7096&req=fSInH8B2nYJaFb4f3HP0gGwcyYECXngcfKKpc9aNxjDLWemH9Kgm9aYzKli9%0A%2FpRmI0eLmWkXuEHIGg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920998035/c1ce0573251f793685f7345f/t9eOqb6N_SKI2qxaCzaPmcNnT6tAqsrn0Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a8f5eabc955dc504818204072dae55d428162927ed1ea03e9696eb1cde7d7096&req=fSInH8B2nYJaFb4f3HP0gGwcyYECXngcfKKpc9aNxjDLWemH9Kgm9aYzKli9%0A%2FpRmI0eLmWkXuEHIGg%3D%3D%0A)

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

After the DKIM key is added on your domain in Valimail Enforce you need to contact NGP VAN Support for further verification/validation of the TXT DKIM key record at: **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#e9818c8599a98c9f8c9b90888a9d808687c78a8684)
**

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for NGP VAN, **[here](https://help.everyaction.com/s/article/2906018-howdo-i-configure-my-dkim-spf)
**.

Add an NGP VAN DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your NGP VAN emails
======================================================

Once you establish that NGP VAN is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

**Important Note**: In order to validate the configuration you may need to append NGP VAN's include mechanism to your SPF record, after the macro directive. Your SPF record should look as follows: **v=spf1 include:%{i}.\_ip.%{h}.\_ehlo.%{d}.\_spf.vali.email include:\_spfprod.ngpvan.com ~all**

For more detailed info on why an action such as in the above note might be required, please consult our articles regarding **[How to work around Vendor Verification for SPF](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466463-working-around-vendor-verification-of-spf)
** and **[Trailing SPF includes in Valimail delegated domains](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466464-trailing-spf-includes-in-valimail-delegated-domains)
**.

# Wordpress (WPEngine) | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758023-wordpress-wpengine#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Wordpress (WPEngine)

Wordpress (WPEngine)
====================

Recommendations for Using Wordpress (WPEngine) as a Sender

Updated over a year ago

Wordpress made a change to their default blog mailing infrastructure at the end of 2020.

[Due to the updates they no longer send in alignment via SPF.](https://wpengine.com/support/dmarc-best-practices-get-email-inbox/)
 Their instructions may state otherwise, but we have yet to see evidence of alignment from our customers who used the _standard/default_ setup in the past.

Our recommendation is that customers who wish to send via Wordpress as their domain leverage one of the many plugins available in the Wordpress repository. Wordpress themselves also [recommend using a third-party host instead of the default service](https://wpengine.com/support/using-3rd-party-email-provider-send-mail-wordpress/)
.

We don't have an official recommendation but most customers prefer the plugins which relay out of their corporate infrastructure and can be DKIM signed, or plugins such as "[WP Mail SMTP" from WPForms.](https://wpmailsmtp.com/)

* * *

Related Articles

[Hosted Servers](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466473-hosted-servers)
[DuoCircle](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754568-duocircle)
[Front](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8754762-front)
[MailChannels SMTP Relay](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8771208-mailchannels-smtp-relay)
[Sender Requirements Failure Report](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/11964171-sender-requirements-failure-report)

Namecheap

Namecheap
=========

How to set up DMARC for Namecheap in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757550-namecheap#h_198694cdff)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757550-namecheap#h_592651a7d7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757550-namecheap#h_57b076d509)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Namecheap** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Namecheap emails
=========================================================

1\. Sign in to your **Namecheap** account.

2\. Go to **Private Email** menu:

[![a screen shot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995208/e7fedd6fb87376da305637ae/RkbCeabYZyzRVheHnnow_W_X5TOcKRGYvA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=be90d4fd25c49817e88ce5ddd4ea5be9bcb1d77ccfa51cf6a863343892decf68&req=fSInH8B7n4FXFb4f3HP0gOrItWmW5QbnI0n%2BX9XlYiF%2B28mI0j5VZNUXLBhv%0AZ%2F%2BeRJKhh18R4QQPZg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995208/e7fedd6fb87376da305637ae/RkbCeabYZyzRVheHnnow_W_X5TOcKRGYvA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=be90d4fd25c49817e88ce5ddd4ea5be9bcb1d77ccfa51cf6a863343892decf68&req=fSInH8B7n4FXFb4f3HP0gOrItWmW5QbnI0n%2BX9XlYiF%2B28mI0j5VZNUXLBhv%0AZ%2F%2BeRJKhh18R4QQPZg%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Then, look for your Private Email subscription in the list and click on **Manage** next to it:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995214/455a54ee2f53b21279235553/k7JZI6mrAKb8KrTha6pczr3n6zb_8qM3Aw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=eeeac64e74cd55eb95735be31b5e0300ed7186e0ef7fb8cd6616e524a3d6a738&req=fSInH8B7n4BbFb4f3HP0gOMmDq%2F19ppgPti4BsidOC9j5aWte3QMFpAnuW3k%0APbII0%2FLn%2FVFA%2BzPUCA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995214/455a54ee2f53b21279235553/k7JZI6mrAKb8KrTha6pczr3n6zb_8qM3Aw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=eeeac64e74cd55eb95735be31b5e0300ed7186e0ef7fb8cd6616e524a3d6a738&req=fSInH8B7n4BbFb4f3HP0gOMmDq%2F19ppgPti4BsidOC9j5aWte3QMFpAnuW3k%0APbII0%2FLn%2FVFA%2BzPUCA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Click on the **Generate** button under DKIM:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995221/b09d001ebe8da4be3bef664f/_Rc4ke-G_Ke_7DgADXxMWauMKLmwEW_0_A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2821fd131cc86d648db42e30e35d06b507624f7f6e5a23fbb1c00e746cf087c1&req=fSInH8B7n4NeFb4f3HP0gHKsvRb8r%2FlXTNiMHbg%2FUlRAMJsHfqCUdf4EoKmT%0AeDXzYKgQhUFrf9TAnA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995221/b09d001ebe8da4be3bef664f/_Rc4ke-G_Ke_7DgADXxMWauMKLmwEW_0_A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2821fd131cc86d648db42e30e35d06b507624f7f6e5a23fbb1c00e746cf087c1&req=fSInH8B7n4NeFb4f3HP0gHKsvRb8r%2FlXTNiMHbg%2FUlRAMJsHfqCUdf4EoKmT%0AeDXzYKgQhUFrf9TAnA%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Once done, you will get the **DKIM has been generated** message. To check the host and value for the TXT record, click on **Show DKIM**:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995226/6853e3168480aee756308669/_sV1YkwOnXgXRevB04sp3s3P6Mt4TJZs8Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=175908238ea8baa5c32df0d77420ebf03d907f2db3e3bc903ad0126bcafc6392&req=fSInH8B7n4NZFb4f3HP0gGN2eJBAarnykqP3wef%2BRG4EbgBfn6YnDj42Jcvu%0AkCmXK1sEkH2MHaCp9w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995226/6853e3168480aee756308669/_sV1YkwOnXgXRevB04sp3s3P6Mt4TJZs8Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=175908238ea8baa5c32df0d77420ebf03d907f2db3e3bc903ad0126bcafc6392&req=fSInH8B7n4NZFb4f3HP0gGN2eJBAarnykqP3wef%2BRG4EbgBfn6YnDj42Jcvu%0AkCmXK1sEkH2MHaCp9w%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Here, you can see the fields with the **DNS record** as well as **Public** and **Private** keys values:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995232/17ffe9682a31685c1fd5a316/-V5gEb5XDIy8t4g5_mb9id2RLzezZ2RCzA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=cb06a0a03bbafe37a2af94b6beefba5c4f8ecf5027062b95026a38c5dae1d07c&req=fSInH8B7n4JdFb4f3HP0gIzWZ2yDWnH2SVuoDh87wI2Tv3MYAKUPHl1X9Wxo%0AtAOeN81ckSLlNLagRQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920995232/17ffe9682a31685c1fd5a316/-V5gEb5XDIy8t4g5_mb9id2RLzezZ2RCzA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=cb06a0a03bbafe37a2af94b6beefba5c4f8ecf5027062b95026a38c5dae1d07c&req=fSInH8B7n4JdFb4f3HP0gIzWZ2yDWnH2SVuoDh87wI2Tv3MYAKUPHl1X9Wxo%0AtAOeN81ckSLlNLagRQ%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Add the TXT DKIM on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce. The TXT DKIM key is the one in the **DNS Record** field.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF, **[here](https://www.namecheap.com/support/knowledgebase/article.aspx/10383/2176/how-to-set-up-a-dkim-record-for-private-email/)
**.

Add a Namecheap DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Namecheap emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Namecheap is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Microsoft Dynamics 365

Microsoft Dynamics 365
======================

How to set up DMARC for Microsoft Dynamics 365 in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757505-microsoft-dynamics-365#h_73fc8492e2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757505-microsoft-dynamics-365#h_f81b5157e7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757505-microsoft-dynamics-365#h_40ed2ba0d7)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Microsoft Dynamics 365** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Microsoft Dynamics 365 does not send SPF aligned emails by default.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Microsoft Dynamics 365 emails
======================================================================

**1.** Go to **Settings** -> **Email marketing** -> **Domain authentication**. A list of existing authenticated domains opens.

[![a screenshot of a search box](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987879/8756622fd0783cf2cad4fc10/9uWyz2BSzP6lXob7wd5GbTlafLP5JuSBcw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=88a41c8daa5c8cf92f6f59c645abbbfad5c98c04d7f749fdb62a445ad5f56e68&req=fSInH8F5lYZWFb4f3HP0gGKztY%2FXss4NjBqwCqoCkNCGYZj7tFHxkoB5nnQ2%0AF1A8PVop5g0eQAw6hQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987879/8756622fd0783cf2cad4fc10/9uWyz2BSzP6lXob7wd5GbTlafLP5JuSBcw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=88a41c8daa5c8cf92f6f59c645abbbfad5c98c04d7f749fdb62a445ad5f56e68&req=fSInH8F5lYZWFb4f3HP0gGKztY%2FXss4NjBqwCqoCkNCGYZj7tFHxkoB5nnQ2%0AF1A8PVop5g0eQAw6hQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Select **New** on the command bar to add a new domain. The wizard will guide you through the whole domain authentication process step by step. On the first step you will need to enter the domain name that you want to authenticate and select if it will be used for forms hosting and email sending capabilities.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987884/0a53b4ce1104c05d02c212e9/P3Iwr3UIyiRiLyiGg5whlyFLmpa8L5gksA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=54d21208a60d3533d3cff1f00461e7e574b390296629535ba66439a747f6eae3&req=fSInH8F5lYlbFb4f3HP0gP0NiPRdnrUKvkQslpEaxHvTJQ378CXbixokpYLC%0AJjdz3Lr5M4hSSqCC2Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987884/0a53b4ce1104c05d02c212e9/P3Iwr3UIyiRiLyiGg5whlyFLmpa8L5gksA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=54d21208a60d3533d3cff1f00461e7e574b390296629535ba66439a747f6eae3&req=fSInH8F5lYlbFb4f3HP0gP0NiPRdnrUKvkQslpEaxHvTJQ378CXbixokpYLC%0AJjdz3Lr5M4hSSqCC2Q%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** On the next step you will be asked to add your first DNS record, which will check and confirm the ownership of your domain. Use “Copy” buttons to accurately copy the values of TXT record to avoid typos.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987888/d1f7f75cf57d49e9dd45abae/2TzWYJzII0FCTo__Jku5wOf8jXzWjIgTww.png?expires=1755792000&signature=abc0b74f0e118859dda778efcdcf9d9630654073e8c1a4461c8c9a526a3134d1&req=fSInH8F5lYlXFb4f3HP0gOmf5xwcgXEF37hijq48P32QIR%2F1dPcxduglwx9j%0AZYvwVBfPPmCEx3ZkWA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987888/d1f7f75cf57d49e9dd45abae/2TzWYJzII0FCTo__Jku5wOf8jXzWjIgTww.png?expires=1755792000&signature=abc0b74f0e118859dda778efcdcf9d9630654073e8c1a4461c8c9a526a3134d1&req=fSInH8F5lYlXFb4f3HP0gOmf5xwcgXEF37hijq48P32QIR%2F1dPcxduglwx9j%0AZYvwVBfPPmCEx3ZkWA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** The next 2 steps will guide you through the setup process of CNAME records (CNAME1 and CNAME2) that stand for DKIM protection functionality. These 2 DKIM CNAME records must be published on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987895/5841f54fcaa4bcafa4c9bdd2/Fh1ouI4uzWas-vAv-CEA4We62Y9FZ6XuMg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f9dcf47f205c8bc800e7eeeac2404bbaf428fd82d0a8539579f8dd6a92048430&req=fSInH8F5lYhaFb4f3HP0gH2m1JMsk1X6hE%2Fj5R0sgoZi46rcT%2BKkxXMKnKRD%0A%2BEo0St%2FGcJhmkW24Bg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987895/5841f54fcaa4bcafa4c9bdd2/Fh1ouI4uzWas-vAv-CEA4We62Y9FZ6XuMg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f9dcf47f205c8bc800e7eeeac2404bbaf428fd82d0a8539579f8dd6a92048430&req=fSInH8F5lYhaFb4f3HP0gH2m1JMsk1X6hE%2Fj5R0sgoZi46rcT%2BKkxXMKnKRD%0A%2BEo0St%2FGcJhmkW24Bg%3D%3D%0A)

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987900/59d202a07ea839748c5b37fd/qNn0TWCymjAp21kwWx7VK7ZrDXEQJlHjhA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8d613458196a4d3491db6cc5c6be6ac62712a0651a3fdbbaff9f1302ae0e0f60&req=fSInH8F5lIFfFb4f3HP0gIJa9xFzX69zJaeblzGQJDHyWm6pIIQoEF6EepMP%0ANr43MerBtTBiJoMdPw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987900/59d202a07ea839748c5b37fd/qNn0TWCymjAp21kwWx7VK7ZrDXEQJlHjhA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8d613458196a4d3491db6cc5c6be6ac62712a0651a3fdbbaff9f1302ae0e0f60&req=fSInH8F5lIFfFb4f3HP0gIJa9xFzX69zJaeblzGQJDHyWm6pIIQoEF6EepMP%0ANr43MerBtTBiJoMdPw%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** The next step stands for the last required CNAME DNS record, which is for Envelope-From (Return-Path) domain alignment and SPF protection functionality. This last CNAME record needs to be added in your DNS.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987909/aa43451d71689f54e73bd786/QR8O3SwdQqKw4TrCwk_InGMRHJfUxqYoWg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8c855bc9846abcd2f2bb01a47562ebfcc41d065747ca8826e50e79e8aa4ad539&req=fSInH8F5lIFWFb4f3HP0gP5fWJpglQ%2Bz3zf46UO%2BFxjClZjnUXUh7JGzlp3Q%0ACPtVOc8g0ieEkBpSAQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987909/aa43451d71689f54e73bd786/QR8O3SwdQqKw4TrCwk_InGMRHJfUxqYoWg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8c855bc9846abcd2f2bb01a47562ebfcc41d065747ca8826e50e79e8aa4ad539&req=fSInH8F5lIFWFb4f3HP0gP5fWJpglQ%2Bz3zf46UO%2BFxjClZjnUXUh7JGzlp3Q%0ACPtVOc8g0ieEkBpSAQ%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** On the last step you will be able to review and check your published DNS records. After your checkup is finished, select **Verify**. The system will check and validate all published DNS records and will show you the result summary on the dashboard. If there’s something wrong with your DNS records, you will see which exact record fails on the dashboard.

Here’s an example of an error message that states that TXT Ownership key was not found published in the DNS, which means either the record was not yet published, or it has some mistake/typo.

[![a screenshot of a computer screen](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987915/58e6a08745211939c919aa9a/Fzqr6tDzUfcwj8QeucThulqqzQh-Mb5lGQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a0e4a856bce75a386c1babb8a2a0ec0a4151d0c98dd9733362bac55817c1e9b8&req=fSInH8F5lIBaFb4f3HP0gAnfO9oxnwLvLbS8U1ct5AXipY4pqj5wknyGjs1E%0AM2RCnYw4UNP97Pr9cA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920987915/58e6a08745211939c919aa9a/Fzqr6tDzUfcwj8QeucThulqqzQh-Mb5lGQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a0e4a856bce75a386c1babb8a2a0ec0a4151d0c98dd9733362bac55817c1e9b8&req=fSInH8F5lIBaFb4f3HP0gAnfO9oxnwLvLbS8U1ct5AXipY4pqj5wknyGjs1E%0AM2RCnYw4UNP97Pr9cA%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Microsoft Dynamics 365, **[here](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dynamics365/marketing/domain-authentication)
**.

Add a Microsoft Dynamics 365 DKIM key in Valimail
=================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Microsoft Dynamics 365 emails
=====================================================================

Once you establish that Microsoft Dynamics 365 is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757602-omnisend#main-content)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Omnisend

Omnisend
========

How to set up DMARC for Omnisend in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757602-omnisend#h_d04b1550ca)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757602-omnisend#h_d3b3d72c27)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757602-omnisend#h_26ef82e18d)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Omnisend** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Omnisend leverages Mailgun's email instrastructure.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Omnisend emails
========================================================

**Custom domain setup process**

**1.** You will find Sender's domain settings in **Store settings** -> **Sender's domains**:

[![a screenshot of a website](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005511/212f1a257bce2cdec72707a1/zB-bHyotMqBoYAONHk_h6-QnjgRSE5wtKw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f21b8aeaac9e37f3bafb0632d85dbe5a86a109d86951e5cdbef1e67b98259c61&req=fSImFsl7mIBeFb4f3HP0gJyMAKpZK2xZUUVVBZN1kpaI2wq7CKYLUIpyeS3Z%0AKkvh9jA7licr8FDk8A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005511/212f1a257bce2cdec72707a1/zB-bHyotMqBoYAONHk_h6-QnjgRSE5wtKw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f21b8aeaac9e37f3bafb0632d85dbe5a86a109d86951e5cdbef1e67b98259c61&req=fSImFsl7mIBeFb4f3HP0gJyMAKpZK2xZUUVVBZN1kpaI2wq7CKYLUIpyeS3Z%0AKkvh9jA7licr8FDk8A%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** In order to sign your own domain, click + **Add domain** button:

[![a screenshot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005514/d3e543325500d966855ae253/YMh8BJpWemupvYj5OWT0HHDA1DUsWc2F0A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=57a4958e05c121a2ccf510c83caca5008749ab5a47c68d76e121fceb6d58883e&req=fSImFsl7mIBbFb4f3HP0gEL8cdKXwvCBJwCewApqKsR%2BEveHAgJXeELbfIMh%0A9c8ZcXiK9qH2El1%2BuA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005514/d3e543325500d966855ae253/YMh8BJpWemupvYj5OWT0HHDA1DUsWc2F0A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=57a4958e05c121a2ccf510c83caca5008749ab5a47c68d76e121fceb6d58883e&req=fSImFsl7mIBbFb4f3HP0gEL8cdKXwvCBJwCewApqKsR%2BEveHAgJXeELbfIMh%0A9c8ZcXiK9qH2El1%2BuA%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Provide the exact domain (or sub-domain) you want to use for your Campaigns and/or Automation.

[![a screen shot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005518/72233f9adebcdf5c55db2f44/50a0V46nQ2RBSvkuJX-y1erFG6cUxQ8cWQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=67945dbbf45c20c5a0711e3334effb7e4c1679d8051d63892ad29e4dc1a13e89&req=fSImFsl7mIBXFb4f3HP0gHiZb0rMxGiUIkrABQkFRsxxiTsiKKMYh5%2BHfH6X%0AscYfQpX2IbfNme%2BmHA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005518/72233f9adebcdf5c55db2f44/50a0V46nQ2RBSvkuJX-y1erFG6cUxQ8cWQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=67945dbbf45c20c5a0711e3334effb7e4c1679d8051d63892ad29e4dc1a13e89&req=fSImFsl7mIBXFb4f3HP0gHiZb0rMxGiUIkrABQkFRsxxiTsiKKMYh5%2BHfH6X%0AscYfQpX2IbfNme%2BmHA%3D%3D%0A)

**Note**: You will need to add Omnisend's provided records to your DNS, so we recommend using a domain name that is the same as your store web, for example, @**storename.com**. Consequently, the right choice for the subdomain contains your store name: @**info.storename.com**; @**news.storename.com**; @**email.storename.com**, etc.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for this domain/subdomain in Valimail, you will need to add the DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail.

**4.** You will need to add Omnisend to your Enabled Senders in Valimail for your domain/subdomain. Once added, click **Verify** records.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005529/75f268404099dba7134ab654/MA7yWzLUDgqYKSnqa-JAN6MbyyRpTQjw6g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3815be4eb3579c7a7773b4a71540ca51a17ba609b80fab71051057cd2cc8ab14&req=fSImFsl7mINWFb4f3HP0gGfoctkALW7Tg3XLkyeMFTzdkMTw6U505pA3%2BJur%0AhcRKqyAiuIHq9Bng0A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005529/75f268404099dba7134ab654/MA7yWzLUDgqYKSnqa-JAN6MbyyRpTQjw6g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3815be4eb3579c7a7773b4a71540ca51a17ba609b80fab71051057cd2cc8ab14&req=fSImFsl7mINWFb4f3HP0gGfoctkALW7Tg3XLkyeMFTzdkMTw6U505pA3%2BJur%0AhcRKqyAiuIHq9Bng0A%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** On the Domain authentication page, you will be provided with the records you need to add to the settings page of your DNS provider.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005537/9ad6d447dd9c0802f4aeddc3/y1Mb-LLXH6olx1sTWQ9AOGlYQa718ujb4w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=85c2466168eca0a079d7a581c097cfa458b02020ff86fa743a304523776f8044&req=fSImFsl7mIJYFb4f3HP0gDH0vk7nJJ0Mcx3t2DPXOMdsmDNDyuL%2B3H1ed76Z%0AacDJl%2F%2FkqR2FacWFCg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005537/9ad6d447dd9c0802f4aeddc3/y1Mb-LLXH6olx1sTWQ9AOGlYQa718ujb4w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=85c2466168eca0a079d7a581c097cfa458b02020ff86fa743a304523776f8044&req=fSImFsl7mIJYFb4f3HP0gDH0vk7nJJ0Mcx3t2DPXOMdsmDNDyuL%2B3H1ed76Z%0AacDJl%2F%2FkqR2FacWFCg%3D%3D%0A)

**Note!** Once the custom sender domain is set up, [warm-up](https://support.omnisend.com/en/articles/2939571-warm-up-with-omnisend)
 is essential. Otherwise, your deliverability might get much worse than the one you had on a shared sender domain.

**6.** After the DKIM key is added in Valimail, as the final step you must assign the verified domain/subdomain to your **Campaigns** or **Automations**. Click on the dropdown and select both the domain and the subdomain for your Campaigns and Automations.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005542/e2a89f34fd735ebc871f0fd9/mkI_9ZoW8h3obuCYuK_vdHRedNNQ26sb3w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2627b052493eef784cd6b44505009cec7bc647d1d445f2b5ff2846329abd0437&req=fSImFsl7mIVdFb4f3HP0gNgyxON3kln%2BxcFPAh0R%2BVxbYGIZkqBw6y%2BZqbcj%0AXSLU8%2FhGYHXcXzLSOg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921005542/e2a89f34fd735ebc871f0fd9/mkI_9ZoW8h3obuCYuK_vdHRedNNQ26sb3w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2627b052493eef784cd6b44505009cec7bc647d1d445f2b5ff2846329abd0437&req=fSImFsl7mIVdFb4f3HP0gNgyxON3kln%2BxcFPAh0R%2BVxbYGIZkqBw6y%2BZqbcj%0AXSLU8%2FhGYHXcXzLSOg%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Omnisend, **[here](https://support.omnisend.com/en/articles/3385292-domain-signature-in-omnisend)
**.

Add an Omnisend DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Omnisend emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Omnisend is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Oracle Eloqua | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757605-oracle-eloqua#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757605-oracle-eloqua#h_02899a25cc)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757605-oracle-eloqua#h_6ac904571f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757605-oracle-eloqua#h_ec9528616e)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Oracle Eloqua

Oracle Eloqua
=============

How to set up DMARC for Oracle Eloqua in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757605-oracle-eloqua#h_72052ed12a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757605-oracle-eloqua#h_02899a25cc)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757605-oracle-eloqua#h_6ac904571f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757605-oracle-eloqua#h_ec9528616e)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Oracle Eloqua** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Oracle Eloqua emails
=============================================================

To use DKIM, your company must acquire a **Branding & Deliverability** package from Oracle Eloqua and submit a DKIM signing request to them. Depending on the plan you've purchased, you can request a DKIM signature for 3-20 domains.

To use your corporate domain with Eloqua signature rules which sends emails on behalf of your domain, your IT team will need to add the public key to the corporate domain.

As part of the initial setup, you/your IT team will receive a confirmation email as part of this process. This email will include a link that you need to click as part of the setup process.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Oracle Eloqua, **[here](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/marketing/eloqua-user/Help/Deliverability/EmailSendingDomainSubdomain.htm#4_DKIM)
**.

Add an Oracle Eloqua DKIM key in Valimail
=========================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Hushmail emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Hushmail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in Oracle Eloqua for your domain
=====================================================

You may notice that Oracle Eloqua emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added Oracle Eloqua to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by Oracle Eloqua not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by configuring a CNAME record.

More details on how to achieve that are found in the article, **[here](https://support.oracle.com/epmos/faces/DocumentDisplay?id=2133235.1)
**.

* * *

Related Articles

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757604-oracle-cloud-infrastructure)
[Oracle Fusion](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8771290-oracle-fusion)
[Oracle Taleo](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8772456-oracle-taleo)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757652-quentn#h_0f6b9f7c03)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757652-quentn#h_e440aa0694)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Quentn

Quentn
======

How to set up DMARC for Quentn in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757652-quentn#h_36fa5912f5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757652-quentn#h_cfdab70d5c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757652-quentn#h_bb69e9939a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757652-quentn#h_0f6b9f7c03)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757652-quentn#h_e440aa0694)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Quentn** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Quentn emails
======================================================

**Step 1: Create the domain**
-----------------------------

**1.** In the menu, click on **Email** on the item **Verified domains**.

**2.** Click on the button **\+ Create domain**.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011461/6a49287317ba4746376f38ac/07VhCafQfwylln9HJZDp7eyp5L6FS0At6Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9a5101004dbb4309de9672cf3f5e6eafc7c26046cb48acefafc11ae44441b054&req=fSImFsh%2FmYdeFb4f3HP0gM4WLKeGvyp4v4fSBSiPBNm25Lzh3Yw6IqQA2ZT3%0A5vaj5tZKyIfChIQE9Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011461/6a49287317ba4746376f38ac/07VhCafQfwylln9HJZDp7eyp5L6FS0At6Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9a5101004dbb4309de9672cf3f5e6eafc7c26046cb48acefafc11ae44441b054&req=fSImFsh%2FmYdeFb4f3HP0gM4WLKeGvyp4v4fSBSiPBNm25Lzh3Yw6IqQA2ZT3%0A5vaj5tZKyIfChIQE9Q%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Enter your domain here and click on **Create**.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011463/b4fdd941fda69f0ebda4ee50/kajwy84sONxRgou0Yx68ruEt6KBa5G4gtg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6bc522c12279782c35a4f4417e284f80250376e8e76915607751bc6034375eb8&req=fSImFsh%2FmYdcFb4f3HP0gLY29UxAmfBFW7ajFJQonetbPiggeoA3ivs1DSLM%0AYxfTsuzO4jScEli3FA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011463/b4fdd941fda69f0ebda4ee50/kajwy84sONxRgou0Yx68ruEt6KBa5G4gtg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6bc522c12279782c35a4f4417e284f80250376e8e76915607751bc6034375eb8&req=fSImFsh%2FmYdcFb4f3HP0gLY29UxAmfBFW7ajFJQonetbPiggeoA3ivs1DSLM%0AYxfTsuzO4jScEli3FA%3D%3D%0A)

**Step 2: Verify the domain**
-----------------------------

**1.** In the overview of the verified sender domains for the domain you just created, click on **Actions** -> **Verification info**.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011471/7a47351b0cff14eb1e118977/sB2E9xGQaX6qpU8ZJGIiLC_Z0yRcisiWDA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ffdaf58dafcea83b213676ba88f310640acc32ec9a12afa625c66f7a0c6bddce&req=fSImFsh%2FmYZeFb4f3HP0gBYZyj6uQCFlTXDTlvmx8pBwljkVxH%2BjYxgOQSsU%0AJ1KesuwNOdhBUyuhQQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011471/7a47351b0cff14eb1e118977/sB2E9xGQaX6qpU8ZJGIiLC_Z0yRcisiWDA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ffdaf58dafcea83b213676ba88f310640acc32ec9a12afa625c66f7a0c6bddce&req=fSImFsh%2FmYZeFb4f3HP0gBYZyj6uQCFlTXDTlvmx8pBwljkVxH%2BjYxgOQSsU%0AJ1KesuwNOdhBUyuhQQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** You will now be shown DNS entries. If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail and add Quantn in your Enabled Senders list.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011476/6e160042c0ce76339563acc1/RLnDLj469CIiUlA-ZZPA_I7VS7peZRoX7Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=26a07901219606c05711e7d77e543b84374b16fcc333d1d56c8e39c9a4502f0d&req=fSImFsh%2FmYZZFb4f3HP0gK3OO0nnkVkhkrSknbLWRO%2FYTw5M1ijrY%2F%2BZzB5T%0A1f%2FJ7ZDs1VCFMKKWag%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011476/6e160042c0ce76339563acc1/RLnDLj469CIiUlA-ZZPA_I7VS7peZRoX7Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=26a07901219606c05711e7d77e543b84374b16fcc333d1d56c8e39c9a4502f0d&req=fSImFsh%2FmYZZFb4f3HP0gK3OO0nnkVkhkrSknbLWRO%2FYTw5M1ijrY%2F%2BZzB5T%0A1f%2FJ7ZDs1VCFMKKWag%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** If the domain has been verified, a note will appear in the overview of your verified sender domains.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011479/dd5dbeaca93bd55e75d6a362/RPkr32xN7xwgU6Y2Hhsvi5vEBNCz5Zmmww.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c0e824639c36270595ba5bbb250a062d0328cf485c7ec21643d74b1f0705c3b0&req=fSImFsh%2FmYZWFb4f3HP0gHL6QA3AYqVeGOUlRQalyeoyqdzGHHQfE7ydRRoF%0AHEYKsq4567SWtO1ERA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921011479/dd5dbeaca93bd55e75d6a362/RPkr32xN7xwgU6Y2Hhsvi5vEBNCz5Zmmww.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c0e824639c36270595ba5bbb250a062d0328cf485c7ec21643d74b1f0705c3b0&req=fSImFsh%2FmYZWFb4f3HP0gHL6QA3AYqVeGOUlRQalyeoyqdzGHHQfE7ydRRoF%0AHEYKsq4567SWtO1ERA%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Quentn, **[here](https://help.quentn.com/hc/en-150/articles/4518762767249-Verify-email-sender)
**.

Add a Quentn DKIM key in Valimail
=================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Quentn emails
=====================================================

Once you establish that Quentn is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

  
​

# Mimecast | Valimail Help Center
