Mimecast

Mimecast
========

How to set up DMARC for Mimecast in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757509-mimecast#h_ba50f7dcbd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757509-mimecast#h_1ace351d43)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757509-mimecast#h_5326bbca44)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757509-mimecast#h_18f4db731c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757509-mimecast#h_fa61969c52)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

**Mimecast** is an international company specializing in cloud-based email management.

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Mimecast** and how to manage this configuration in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

In order to setup DKIM for Mimecast, you will have to **Setup an** **Outbound Signing Definition** and **Setup an Outbound Policy** to apply DKIM to your outbound emails.

Setup an Outbound Signing Definition
====================================

**1.** Once logged in, click the **Administration** dropdown, select **Gateway** and click **Policies**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989936/3bb0f696bc0de6d7106c813e/ZUVPom6pV5q901bW2fWdZR8pnZ8aTo4jGA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2fb693d47e4983e7148d4198006ff13c3271f01bbd154c13f5ae94457a367eb3&req=fSInH8F3lIJZFb4f3HP0gMWc6uKf4OeZ8dqtmXORAr2fxAyrkhBQVg522%2Fws%0AeiITBufT9mCG4CTJhQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989936/3bb0f696bc0de6d7106c813e/ZUVPom6pV5q901bW2fWdZR8pnZ8aTo4jGA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2fb693d47e4983e7148d4198006ff13c3271f01bbd154c13f5ae94457a367eb3&req=fSInH8F3lIJZFb4f3HP0gMWc6uKf4OeZ8dqtmXORAr2fxAyrkhBQVg522%2Fws%0AeiITBufT9mCG4CTJhQ%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Click the **Definitions** dropdown and select **DNS Authentication - Outbound**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989940/7c79dd4df655bb64c71d0450/qC2Y7FfakNWKjszYMUhWGBsjrGcbNhm6Bg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6725441d4def1fc9078ecbbaec40b9f439bbefa0e6681b28b2f70e0739ae886a&req=fSInH8F3lIVfFb4f3HP0gDyF7vgX0ASCpOrvM2mCytuTCt10bHsIN95GukO2%0AXsY9kgQDaP95bnmO9w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989940/7c79dd4df655bb64c71d0450/qC2Y7FfakNWKjszYMUhWGBsjrGcbNhm6Bg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=6725441d4def1fc9078ecbbaec40b9f439bbefa0e6681b28b2f70e0739ae886a&req=fSInH8F3lIVfFb4f3HP0gDyF7vgX0ASCpOrvM2mCytuTCt10bHsIN95GukO2%0AXsY9kgQDaP95bnmO9w%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Click “**New DNS Authentication – Outbound Signing**” to create a new DKIM policy.

[![a screenshot of a browser window](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989951/28face023fccec2d14de0f0e/IADXNTmYmu9MG4wH6DDUAJBnotQKb7zsaQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=955012757aff028d3e9342e23243d1defcf89db1b29813cf62f6a03dee991197&req=fSInH8F3lIReFb4f3HP0gM5yWPrwRlTUn1tQ81FEc9PpFcFBADyFJXtI5I0j%0AmGdRG0y3aG4WwQucrQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989951/28face023fccec2d14de0f0e/IADXNTmYmu9MG4wH6DDUAJBnotQKb7zsaQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=955012757aff028d3e9342e23243d1defcf89db1b29813cf62f6a03dee991197&req=fSInH8F3lIReFb4f3HP0gM5yWPrwRlTUn1tQ81FEc9PpFcFBADyFJXtI5I0j%0AmGdRG0y3aG4WwQucrQ%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Fill in a description and select **Sign outbound mail with DKIM**. A domain needs to be selected and so click **Lookup** next to **Domain**.

[![a screenshot of a browser](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989954/39f937491c257e9a0cc59268/4ICbZsiUwRBZyf4CETUXuDiZCKPIktFxug.png?expires=1755792000&signature=610a4729e12a1805c2c9d8b57dfb458460308bf734936da67dfe90372521e771&req=fSInH8F3lIRbFb4f3HP0gBYc6Lv9xbgWwY4u5%2F4rkg1Y4IUWp00qHVCCOxIG%0A7rhxCSSkCpH%2FON26IA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989954/39f937491c257e9a0cc59268/4ICbZsiUwRBZyf4CETUXuDiZCKPIktFxug.png?expires=1755792000&signature=610a4729e12a1805c2c9d8b57dfb458460308bf734936da67dfe90372521e771&req=fSInH8F3lIRbFb4f3HP0gBYc6Lv9xbgWwY4u5%2F4rkg1Y4IUWp00qHVCCOxIG%0A7rhxCSSkCpH%2FON26IA%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Select your domain by clicking **Select** in front of your chosen domain.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989959/b4516d8f088bed7365d0c2e1/0QcjCYp7kqU_lDqqhjYt0mDKcrcKXhaLRw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=339ed89ea061fab6a1663dc60d99591ff18862c91e1e1480a7233587598f6590&req=fSInH8F3lIRWFb4f3HP0gAyqO9NEDExAVyOKhBN6nRYkx%2BaftF24VULHSBhy%0ApQjMsSbpWE1b4GPLCg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989959/b4516d8f088bed7365d0c2e1/0QcjCYp7kqU_lDqqhjYt0mDKcrcKXhaLRw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=339ed89ea061fab6a1663dc60d99591ff18862c91e1e1480a7233587598f6590&req=fSInH8F3lIRWFb4f3HP0gAyqO9NEDExAVyOKhBN6nRYkx%2BaftF24VULHSBhy%0ApQjMsSbpWE1b4GPLCg%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Select either **1024 bits** or **2048 bits** as your DKIM Key Length. We recommend choosing **2048 bits** for more secure encryption.

Do not change the **Selector**. Click **Generate**.

[![a screenshot of a browser](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989965/6f60a10510689a5e646d4ab0/o2aHiWMFLQJejBkieKzsdqeg0Vs2I1FCvw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=95244ef8abb8395c647cbc68e3aaca600384de133a0849cf871f70d1e48792b9&req=fSInH8F3lIdaFb4f3HP0gOV%2FL2ShKZ6GJzBOFOl09gRQXJ41rqJD0HGyJbD%2B%0Adw3kScWgVLE8eRlu2w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989965/6f60a10510689a5e646d4ab0/o2aHiWMFLQJejBkieKzsdqeg0Vs2I1FCvw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=95244ef8abb8395c647cbc68e3aaca600384de133a0849cf871f70d1e48792b9&req=fSInH8F3lIdaFb4f3HP0gOV%2FL2ShKZ6GJzBOFOl09gRQXJ41rqJD0HGyJbD%2B%0Adw3kScWgVLE8eRlu2w%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Your new DKIM key has been generated successfully.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989969/fa864202fafbffa31dd80cdd/_9xxduDzGm1uxklFTvp2YHvrfm1uw0cD9w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f435e5f86eb58aca904d9ecf1141a6f9728e7255b28f68bfa039c4253b8b9cb8&req=fSInH8F3lIdWFb4f3HP0gFEL5sBEWz0tz042MG00UHr1otBgZPh0Fdzrlm5l%0AEIsnlMywRdVzDF569A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989969/fa864202fafbffa31dd80cdd/_9xxduDzGm1uxklFTvp2YHvrfm1uw0cD9w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f435e5f86eb58aca904d9ecf1141a6f9728e7255b28f68bfa039c4253b8b9cb8&req=fSInH8F3lIdWFb4f3HP0gFEL5sBEWz0tz042MG00UHr1otBgZPh0Fdzrlm5l%0AEIsnlMywRdVzDF569A%3D%3D%0A)

**8.** The newly created DKIM key will need to be published in Valimail Enforce.

Add a Mimecast DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://intercom.help/valimail-4dffb6ef6e39/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Setup an Outbound policy
========================

**1.** Once done with your outbound signing definition, next you need to create your outbound policy.

Under **Policies** select **DNS Authentication – Outbound**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989976/21d9e2ed560e33e6a6c518c5/U3VTX-WeZhNcrVQBu4ckO47KUDNzn_SIZg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3919a773ee7fed1dabd3e3a2b231b8fdd9da443ecbdb65874a91e03e6ac6e2fa&req=fSInH8F3lIZZFb4f3HP0gL9hM0%2BDGXeqzw3obIVWUjBIXBNtRYsGtE%2BNQ72t%0AjiZnIDSTbBMhG95f8w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989976/21d9e2ed560e33e6a6c518c5/U3VTX-WeZhNcrVQBu4ckO47KUDNzn_SIZg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3919a773ee7fed1dabd3e3a2b231b8fdd9da443ecbdb65874a91e03e6ac6e2fa&req=fSInH8F3lIZZFb4f3HP0gL9hM0%2BDGXeqzw3obIVWUjBIXBNtRYsGtE%2BNQ72t%0AjiZnIDSTbBMhG95f8w%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Add a new Policy for Outbound Signing and use the following values:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989982/44c41215bb98fa0a74c17f22/VHGcmsAe9NJjYX4cuoSR6aAvVGTzxvD2Lw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e009653c29dfbd7a6fafe0b1f3f79ac0f8707172b21455cfdd823a49c6f45b67&req=fSInH8F3lIldFb4f3HP0gCsxwR5qi9FwcPUW9zktEOLcSZyWk5r4dAyBC2a2%0Al%2FcJ0L7vHVvZd8BJsQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989982/44c41215bb98fa0a74c17f22/VHGcmsAe9NJjYX4cuoSR6aAvVGTzxvD2Lw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e009653c29dfbd7a6fafe0b1f3f79ac0f8707172b21455cfdd823a49c6f45b67&req=fSInH8F3lIldFb4f3HP0gCsxwR5qi9FwcPUW9zktEOLcSZyWk5r4dAyBC2a2%0Al%2FcJ0L7vHVvZd8BJsQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Go back into Mimecast and click **Check DNS** to verify that the Mimecast DKIM key was properly published in **Valimail**.

[![a screenshot of a browser](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989984/30f8abcb1d37a31a9f10527f/8HZW1JkcAx1C3Zq6xEowxdxprN-egZlkFg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=37f14ed1bf490d1fb1c163e4f17163b3b8f65f759b050622d0407b08ad7e3ae0&req=fSInH8F3lIlbFb4f3HP0gG%2FJG8Zci9RqjIGBvS2NL541Y%2BfOMsdEKHF5OK4A%0AMPnV1PxaCHQ%2BlVSGWA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920989984/30f8abcb1d37a31a9f10527f/8HZW1JkcAx1C3Zq6xEowxdxprN-egZlkFg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=37f14ed1bf490d1fb1c163e4f17163b3b8f65f759b050622d0407b08ad7e3ae0&req=fSInH8F3lIlbFb4f3HP0gG%2FJG8Zci9RqjIGBvS2NL541Y%2BfOMsdEKHF5OK4A%0AMPnV1PxaCHQ%2BlVSGWA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** After the validation of the DKIM key was done successfully in Mimecast, you can now send DKIM authenticated email for your domain using Mimecast.

Configuring SPF authentication for your Mimecast emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Hushmail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in Mimecast for your domain
================================================

You may notice that Mimecast emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added Mimecast to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by Mimecast not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by making sure SPF alignment is turn on in Mimecast for your domain.

You can find more information on how to set up SPF and SPF alignment for Mimecast **[here](https://community.mimecast.com/s/article/Connect-Application-Implementing-SPF-for-Outbound-Email-Delivery-731048977)
**.

Outreach Systems Neoserra

Outreach Systems Neoserra
=========================

How to set up DMARC for Outreach Systems Neoserra in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757607-outreach-systems-neoserra#h_f7a01517d0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757607-outreach-systems-neoserra#h_876f4894dd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757607-outreach-systems-neoserra#h_dd38059282)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Outreach Systems Neoserra** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Outreach Systems Neoserra emails
=========================================================================

In order to allow Neoserra to send emails that look like they came from your email servers, we recommend that you set up custom DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) authentication for your domain and include Neoserra in your Sender Policy Frameworks (SPF) record. Both are necessary because SPF uses rule sets to determine authorized IP addresses from which you will be sending emails and DKIM uses public key cryptography to authenticate individual email messages.

To set up DKIM in Outreach Systems Neoserra for your domain, you must add the following CNAME DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce:

DKIM Selector: **osc1**

CNAME Value: **osc1.\_domainkey.outreachsystems.com.**

Once you added the CNAME DKIM key and added Outreach Systems Neoserra in your Enabled Sender list, please **contact OutreachSystems** so that we can clear all email addresses that were rejected because they could not be authenticated.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Outreach Systems Neoserra, **[here](https://support.outreachsystems.com/resources/help/neoserra/faq.jsp?id=n359)
**.

Add an Outreach Systems Neoserra DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Outreach Systems Neoserra emails
========================================================================

Once you establish that Outreach Systems Neoserra is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

QuestionPro

QuestionPro
===========

How to set up DMARC for QuestionPro in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757654-questionpro#h_2dfde750b1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757654-questionpro#h_8df246201c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757654-questionpro#h_34f485d099)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **QuestionPro** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your QuestionPro emails
===========================================================

QuestionPro will generate a public and private key pair. Please add a TXT record of the public key that will be shared with you to your domain. This step needs to be performed by your IT team as QuestionPro does not have access to your domain settings.

Once QuestionPro will generate a DKIM key for you, you will need to add that in Valimail Enforce.

After the DKIM TXT key is published in Enforce, QuestionPro will activate the DKIM keys to complete the set up. QuestionPro will verify email delivery status and DKIM key signature on the email.

In order to set up DKIM with QuestionPro, please contact QuestionPro directly on chat or contact your QuestionPro service administrator (person/team that has set up QuestionPro to send emails on behalf of your domain) to initiate DKIM set up.

You can find more information about this procedure **[here](https://www.questionpro.com/help/dkim.html)
**.

Add a QuestionPro DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your QuestionPro emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that QuestionPro is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

KnowBe4

KnowBe4
=======

How to set up DMARC for KnowBe4 in Valimail

Updated over 10 months ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757129-knowbe4#h_dc60fe4541)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757129-knowbe4#h_d9083cab48)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757129-knowbe4#h_5937830503)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757129-knowbe4#h_ac29f93be8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757129-knowbe4#h_a92b129301)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757129-knowbe4#h_cee47b3e57)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757129-knowbe4#h_ea7c17dc98)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757129-knowbe4#h_dac1825d8b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757129-knowbe4#h_2087eccc67)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

KnowBe4 is a training and compliance service. They typically will send spoofing emails as your domain to your employees to test to see who opens the message(s) and to provide metrics on this. They then offer follow-up training as needed for your employees.

In order to prevent these training emails from going to the recipient's Spam or Junk folder, you will need to set up SPF and DKIM authentication for KnowBe4. The steps below require access to the KnowBe4 Account Settings page, so make sure that you have an account with proper access.

FYI: On the KnowBe4 help page they will instruct you to add their "include" statement to your SPF record. That is not needed when you use Valimail since we have automation in place that already takes care of that by using our SPF Macro:

[Valimail SPF Records](https://support.valimail.com/a/solutions/articles/48001168380?portalId=48000011765)

Configuring DKIM authentication for your KnowBe4 emails
=======================================================

The steps below outline the process involved in creating a custom DKIM signature for your domain, which will sign the emails send by KnowBe4 on your behalf. DKIM is configured separately for the **Phishing**, **Training** and **PhishER** emails.

Enabling DKIM Signatures for Phishing Emails
============================================

By default, all KnowBe4 training emails contain a DKIM signature but phishing emails require the account owner to enable this feature first. See below for steps on how to enable DKIM signatures for phishing emails.

**1.** Log in to your KnowBe4 account.

**2.** Click on your email in the upper-right corner and click **Account Settings**.

**3.** Under the **Phishing Settings** section, click the checkbox labeled **Enable DKIM signature**.

[![a screenshot of a email signature](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892317/4722f51d0b17b58e6000ce49/sFQ7mRdLS97-3TB9f7kgk_OtOgH42kumJw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=75429e413d93c79b4146d68c9ca00d596d9131ccd7a6c3df849391e8fbf5cffe&req=fSInHsB8noBYFb4f3HP0gNJFibPwVj2LoMnGgbFazT%2Bt3JTQIehMeE7%2BDIPF%0AGVhPj7Ks7%2FhvDD8%2B9w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892317/4722f51d0b17b58e6000ce49/sFQ7mRdLS97-3TB9f7kgk_OtOgH42kumJw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=75429e413d93c79b4146d68c9ca00d596d9131ccd7a6c3df849391e8fbf5cffe&req=fSInHsB8noBYFb4f3HP0gNJFibPwVj2LoMnGgbFazT%2Bt3JTQIehMeE7%2BDIPF%0AGVhPj7Ks7%2FhvDD8%2B9w%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Click **Save Changes**.

All KnowBe4 phishing emails will now contain KnowBe4’s signing domain and can be used to verify if a phishing email is from KnowBe4 or a real phishing attack. See the section below for information on how to customize the signing domain for phishing and training emails.

Using Custom DKIM Signatures in KnowBe4 Phishing Emails
=======================================================

After enabling DKIM signatures, your organization can adjust the signing domain for your organization’s needs. See the instructions below to use your own signing domain for phishing emails.

**1.** Log in to your KnowBe4 account.

**2.** Click on your email in the top-right corner and click **Account Settings**.

**3.** Under the **Phishing Settings** section, click the checkbox labeled **Enable DKIM signature** if you haven’t enabled DKIM signatures already.

**4.** Click the button labeled **Use your own signing domain**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892327/1df6fd00597152bb188765d2/IeZtuHAOBKUgsdrIdGmc7dOcRXcAN_JamQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ddeff2d2477db42ccba8a26847e1fbd4e27438078ed81399e61790b197ed8d83&req=fSInHsB8noNYFb4f3HP0gNfsCmSm0fO5DxVxFOd4unoN1WwOR0p63OpDSGJ2%0Ahqm3RiimjGDfd1dT9A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892327/1df6fd00597152bb188765d2/IeZtuHAOBKUgsdrIdGmc7dOcRXcAN_JamQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ddeff2d2477db42ccba8a26847e1fbd4e27438078ed81399e61790b197ed8d83&req=fSInHsB8noNYFb4f3HP0gNfsCmSm0fO5DxVxFOd4unoN1WwOR0p63OpDSGJ2%0Ahqm3RiimjGDfd1dT9A%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Choose the domain you wish to use. To add a domain to this drop-down, you will first need to add the domain as an **Allowed Domain** in your KnowBe4 account. For more information on this topic, please see **[How to Add and Verify Allowed Domains](https://support.knowbe4.com/hc/en-us/articles/360013430414-How-to-Add-and-Verify-Allowed-Domains)
.**

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892329/ad8fae6009879799d5c5171c/R3K7FihMDsyPaUU5PSgLJ6UWN94eYMA6ww.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7d9f845204a57cb78d73608221cfec651de9687b5cbe0c01dc2f8bab52bedc71&req=fSInHsB8noNWFb4f3HP0gGmsfGz%2FKbCISt39Nrjojbhe4dqAIUhpALZNGqvM%0ACtsfLRjQ3%2BS1QUMW7A%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892329/ad8fae6009879799d5c5171c/R3K7FihMDsyPaUU5PSgLJ6UWN94eYMA6ww.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7d9f845204a57cb78d73608221cfec651de9687b5cbe0c01dc2f8bab52bedc71&req=fSInHsB8noNWFb4f3HP0gGmsfGz%2FKbCISt39Nrjojbhe4dqAIUhpALZNGqvM%0ACtsfLRjQ3%2BS1QUMW7A%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Click **Create a DKIM selector for this domain**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892336/a6e7c9d72eee3b9efd5329a5/42OSyCMW9lnBWUZ_UkLPjMsnxyRmrz0Ncg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c0ae10bc5c92eeedb9495b92f344504c42bb53a27ca2e926a5668f853c0105da&req=fSInHsB8noJZFb4f3HP0gA%2BIPCTgMG7jGNn3z7W8%2BPeaPKP1jmphsp5notaW%0AZP8HJfAW0mbcwjiMKw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892336/a6e7c9d72eee3b9efd5329a5/42OSyCMW9lnBWUZ_UkLPjMsnxyRmrz0Ncg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c0ae10bc5c92eeedb9495b92f344504c42bb53a27ca2e926a5668f853c0105da&req=fSInHsB8noJZFb4f3HP0gA%2BIPCTgMG7jGNn3z7W8%2BPeaPKP1jmphsp5notaW%0AZP8HJfAW0mbcwjiMKw%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Copy the host name and values provided in the pop-up.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892346/40ba05526730e47bb2204ad7/QqH2dgHdE-BQLXvjYLkrfXZhGfAWMw36qQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c343de7d1114f97ce4b09f563a9807e98a0843b6914ae872499ef7fbda51a9b1&req=fSInHsB8noVZFb4f3HP0gAegfQBV5git8Ec3KIhquDfj9J42Tc1auAA9%2FUMV%0AzYCKqu6dJAH2GTE%2BQA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892346/40ba05526730e47bb2204ad7/QqH2dgHdE-BQLXvjYLkrfXZhGfAWMw36qQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c343de7d1114f97ce4b09f563a9807e98a0843b6914ae872499ef7fbda51a9b1&req=fSInHsB8noVZFb4f3HP0gAegfQBV5git8Ec3KIhquDfj9J42Tc1auAA9%2FUMV%0AzYCKqu6dJAH2GTE%2BQA%3D%3D%0A)

**8.** Add the DKIM key in **Valimail**.

**9.** Once you’ve added the DKIM TXT record in Valimail, click on the **Validate the DNS TXT record for this DKIM selector** button to make sure the validation of the published record is done.

**10.** Click **OK** in the DKIM Selectors Details window in your KMSAT console.

**11.** Click **Save Changes** at the bottom of your **Account Settings**.

Using Custom DKIM Signatures in KnowBe4 Training Emails
=======================================================

You can also use custom DKIM signatures for your training emails. See the instructions below to use your own signing domain in training emails.

**1.** Log in to your KnowBe4 account.

**2.** Click on your email in the upper-right corner and click **Account Settings**.

**3.** Under the **Training Settings** section, click the checkbox labeled **Enable Custom DKIM Signature** if you haven’t enabled DKIM signatures already.

[![a screenshot of a email signature](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892356/fc35381e75c6a2cb6d7bc851/hE5mIKZtlFqArVV17ZKJWbva-iqESw9rmg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dde6dff5c66f4b23c4ce34113f5f1991f3ee4c55ddd20d5ce5464579538eefc3&req=fSInHsB8noRZFb4f3HP0gA94HvQqbAyBuRZlC6r8GMEb6d9Bos6PEgfqCW8V%0AOfzZBU5%2Fh5PRhhMspg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892356/fc35381e75c6a2cb6d7bc851/hE5mIKZtlFqArVV17ZKJWbva-iqESw9rmg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dde6dff5c66f4b23c4ce34113f5f1991f3ee4c55ddd20d5ce5464579538eefc3&req=fSInHsB8noRZFb4f3HP0gA94HvQqbAyBuRZlC6r8GMEb6d9Bos6PEgfqCW8V%0AOfzZBU5%2Fh5PRhhMspg%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Repeat all the next steps from the **Using Custom DKIM Signatures in KnowBe4 Phishing Emails** section presented above.

You can also find the instructions for how to set up DKIM for KnowBe4 Phishing and Training **[here](https://support.knowbe4.com/hc/en-us/articles/1500010872521-How-to-Enable-and-Customize-DKIM-Signatures)
**.

Enabling and Customizing DKIM Signatures in PhishER
===================================================

PhishER notifications contain a line of text called a DKIM signature that proves it is an authentic KnowBe4 or custom email. This guide will show you how to enable DKIM signatures for PhishER from KnowBe4 and how to use an allowed domain as a custom DKIM signature.

To enable your DKIM Signatures in PhishER, follow the steps below:

**1.** Open the **PhishER platform** and then navigate to the **Settings** page.

**2.** Go to **Email Server**.

**3.** Customize the **Email Server** settings and enable the **DKIM Signature toggle**. When you enable the toggle, you will see the **Signing Domain**(s) and the **Selector** from your KMSAT Account Settings page.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/992862797/98205265f69be5fd2038ecef/emailServerSettings.png?expires=1755792000&signature=00ce24822ba4e91f0383722c854725bab987e763278c5c40a2ce1828c8284c9c&req=fSklHs98mohYFb4f3HP0gFB6mA8mraLvNRRD90GhYAT6kNRv%2B177nLRHPZsJ%0Az4R7Sm4ef07rGTY%2F0Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/992862797/98205265f69be5fd2038ecef/emailServerSettings.png?expires=1755792000&signature=00ce24822ba4e91f0383722c854725bab987e763278c5c40a2ce1828c8284c9c&req=fSklHs98mohYFb4f3HP0gFB6mA8mraLvNRRD90GhYAT6kNRv%2B177nLRHPZsJ%0Az4R7Sm4ef07rGTY%2F0Q%3D%3D%0A)

4\. Click **Save**.

Using Custom DKIM Signatures in PhishER
=======================================

After enabling DKIM signatures, your organization can adjust the signing domain for your organization’s needs.

**1.** Log in to your KnowBe4 account and navigate to PhishER.

**2.** Click on the **Settings** icon and go to **Email Server** page.

**3.** Under the **Configure DKIM section**, click the button labeled **Add Custom Signing Domain**.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/992862816/e04c4297acfe638242518a05/AddCustomDomain.png?expires=1755792000&signature=cf0e06ce74b2e86844c6db28e685de3737de5e412f122b517b2b0928f6e7a273&req=fSklHs98lYBZFb4f3HP0gPP8XZgk4BdvlSOlwH7nG10S3vJBf6ALUS1fq75a%0Apwp5Smcb2MbEjBqPYw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/992862816/e04c4297acfe638242518a05/AddCustomDomain.png?expires=1755792000&signature=cf0e06ce74b2e86844c6db28e685de3737de5e412f122b517b2b0928f6e7a273&req=fSklHs98lYBZFb4f3HP0gPP8XZgk4BdvlSOlwH7nG10S3vJBf6ALUS1fq75a%0Apwp5Smcb2MbEjBqPYw%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Choose the domain you wish to use. To add a domain to this drop-down menu, you will first need to add the domain as an **Allowed Domain** in your KnowBe4 account. See their [How to Add and Verify Allowed Domains](https://support.knowbe4.com/hc/en-us/articles/360013430414-How-to-Add-and-Verify-Allowed-Domains)
 article for more information.

5\. Click **Add** and the **Configure Custom Signing Domain** window will open.

6\. Copy the host name and values provided in the pop-up.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/992862831/9b115ee3289e991acf4e16bc/ConfigureCustomDomain.png?expires=1755792000&signature=31757348cb874718551ca6f0a866f0d1cd65e01575472f561a2a775e5ae411bf&req=fSklHs98lYJeFb4f3HP0gA6L%2ByJND65cdN9pBEDBPy6ux5Mv6IqbYFdeThM%2B%0AYBQwtC352FUX2Q0eZw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/992862831/9b115ee3289e991acf4e16bc/ConfigureCustomDomain.png?expires=1755792000&signature=31757348cb874718551ca6f0a866f0d1cd65e01575472f561a2a775e5ae411bf&req=fSklHs98lYJeFb4f3HP0gA6L%2ByJND65cdN9pBEDBPy6ux5Mv6IqbYFdeThM%2B%0AYBQwtC352FUX2Q0eZw%3D%3D%0A)

**7.** Navigate to your DNS provider and add a TXT record containing the copied information.

**NOTE**: If you manage you domain in Valimail, you will need to add the DKIM key/s on your domain's configuration page in the Valimail platform.

**8.** Once you’ve created the TXT record in your DNS provider, select the domains that you want to use the new DKIM record.

**9.** Click **Save**.

You can also find the instrucxtions on how to set up DKIM in KnowBe4 PhishER, **[here](https://support.knowbe4.com/hc/en-us/articles/4403660264339-How-to-Enable-and-Customize-PhishER-DKIM-Signatures)
**.

Add a KnowBe4 DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://intercom.help/valimail-4dffb6ef6e39/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your KnowBe4 emails
======================================================

Once you establish that KnowBe4 is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://intercom.help/valimail-4dffb6ef6e39/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in KnowBe4 for your domain
===============================================

This section explains what steps you need to follow in order to configure [SPF alignment](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466453-dkim-and-spf-alignment-what-it-is-why-it-matters-and-how-to-fix-it)
 for the emails sent by KnowBe4 on behalf of your domain.

Align the Return-Path domain on your Phishing Emails
====================================================

**1.** Log in to your KnowBe4 admin account.

**2.** Click your email address on the top-right of the screen, then click **Account Settings**.

**3.** Navigate to the **Phishing Settings** section.

**4.** Under the Phishing Email Headers subsection, click the checkbox next to **Overwrite Fixed Return-path Address with Sender Address**.

[![a screen shot of a email](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892372/fbe463d6ffa1568a668edfa0/Yrv3qv-GP7yMwl5rM-sVa_cvgJiM59ZhqA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c43202dca7cb4e8d3851d2157168242dfa2cd31b1c92b66b9622d0984195ae1d&req=fSInHsB8noZdFb4f3HP0gHalUWk%2Bq%2BmfDvddQXa2F6kLbfGZR0rRTNrz7H8T%0AologKJg4PyWN4mHIZA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892372/fbe463d6ffa1568a668edfa0/Yrv3qv-GP7yMwl5rM-sVa_cvgJiM59ZhqA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c43202dca7cb4e8d3851d2157168242dfa2cd31b1c92b66b9622d0984195ae1d&req=fSInHsB8noZdFb4f3HP0gHalUWk%2Bq%2BmfDvddQXa2F6kLbfGZR0rRTNrz7H8T%0AologKJg4PyWN4mHIZA%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Save your settings by clicking **Save Changes** at the bottom of the page.

Align the Return-Path domain on your Training Emails.
=====================================================

**1.** Log in to your KnowBe4 admin account.

**2.** Click your email address on the top-right of the screen, then click **Account Settings**.

**3.** Navigate to the **Training Settings** section.

**4.** Under the Training Email Headers subsection, click the checkbox next to **Overwrite Fixed Return-path Address with Sender Address**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892386/6c422d271af0d7b59423fa24/1yYS-W1dSzfMpB0rWJW4Z0rGMyQlvKlyfA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=18e9ef7eaf8fcf3ec0e8311f1b839ec1e0c494d5f0cc8a317be0d7184f60f63b&req=fSInHsB8nolZFb4f3HP0gPby%2BAc8GelkXVDCl2u%2BJ1p3edQqkmMsRB39HZvN%0AnsL%2BPAdvqhToWIhy9g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920892386/6c422d271af0d7b59423fa24/1yYS-W1dSzfMpB0rWJW4Z0rGMyQlvKlyfA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=18e9ef7eaf8fcf3ec0e8311f1b839ec1e0c494d5f0cc8a317be0d7184f60f63b&req=fSInHsB8nolZFb4f3HP0gPby%2BAc8GelkXVDCl2u%2BJ1p3edQqkmMsRB39HZvN%0AnsL%2BPAdvqhToWIhy9g%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Save your settings by clicking **Save Changes** at the bottom of the page.

**Note**: _If you have a free KnowBe4 account, you will need to contact their support and ask them to make these changes for your account._

Retarus

Retarus
=======

How to set up DMARC for Retarus in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758044-retarus#h_a72b952ac4)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(not supported)

This article covers the SPF authentication processes for **Retarus** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

For now, Retarus does not support DKIM signing for the customer's domain.

Configuring SPF authentication for your Retarus emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Retaurus is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

That's it! Starting now all new Retarus emails will pass DMARC authentication via SPF as long as they are being sent in an aligned manner.

* * *

Related Articles

[CookiePro by OneTrust](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8772470-cookiepro-by-onetrust)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757650-qualtrics#h_ee05878180)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757650-qualtrics#h_30daee9f86)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Qualtrics

Qualtrics
=========

How to set up DMARC for Qualtrics in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757650-qualtrics#h_2e80107c13)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757650-qualtrics#h_ee05878180)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757650-qualtrics#h_30daee9f86)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Qualtrics** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Qualtrics emails
=========================================================

**1.** Go to the **Admin** page.

**2.** Go to the **Organization Settings** tab.

**3.** Navigate to the **Email** section.

**4.** Click **Add domain**.

[![a screenshot of a email address](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921012037/03daf83f11fde97ea228e68e/9dKtXPCi756gsV4Ja4hlj-i4HEyaI6-9-w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=40c594ff5a843ba4a08f94d2a20e31c560df21e8faa9124e14f3d5bca032f4b2&req=fSImFsh8nYJYFb4f3HP0gITYi3hpB2Ceoqhu569gMoAc0csxCP6QOcd2XoP9%0An85jsawMWq2pDJYFQw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921012037/03daf83f11fde97ea228e68e/9dKtXPCi756gsV4Ja4hlj-i4HEyaI6-9-w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=40c594ff5a843ba4a08f94d2a20e31c560df21e8faa9124e14f3d5bca032f4b2&req=fSImFsh8nYJYFb4f3HP0gITYi3hpB2Ceoqhu569gMoAc0csxCP6QOcd2XoP9%0An85jsawMWq2pDJYFQw%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Enter the **Domain** you wish to use.

**6.** Choose the **Selector** for your key. We recommend avoiding special characters like the underscore which can cause problems with certain mail receivers. As an example, you can use “qualtrics” for the selector.

**7.** Choose the **Key size** which determines how long the key is. We recommend you to choose the 2048 bits encryption option.

**8.** Click **Add**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921012038/dd6e738b1d6740e1aaaa46f3/GkrQbxH6D423Kt9KETGH-XZBnmRGVM1Gdg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=78c2c4b14775a36ebc3876a8bd7e9676815d9b6f34b986f958f6750dc24dd2f8&req=fSImFsh8nYJXFb4f3HP0gH4jXWe1AhOkkWDmYiB24h0JRDU3%2FJmPSerNoBeE%0Ao7w4YdP7nJCywEyVLg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921012038/dd6e738b1d6740e1aaaa46f3/GkrQbxH6D423Kt9KETGH-XZBnmRGVM1Gdg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=78c2c4b14775a36ebc3876a8bd7e9676815d9b6f34b986f958f6750dc24dd2f8&req=fSImFsh8nYJXFb4f3HP0gH4jXWe1AhOkkWDmYiB24h0JRDU3%2FJmPSerNoBeE%0Ao7w4YdP7nJCywEyVLg%3D%3D%0A)

**9.** The window will display the MX/DKIM/SPF records. Copy the DNS configuration displayed in the pop-up; make sure you copy the information included in the Domain, Type, and Value columns.

[![a screenshot of a email address](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921012042/329f915a4c05010bf34e0396/tRR9P4JeojEnDJIaNG1Ygq1_jBx679ZqNg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0dc12589484eaf1e186797f37e540ce72de7b23e4b39c521f569b93b92bb35a6&req=fSImFsh8nYVdFb4f3HP0gHJDn8cyJjg6VkmvKE%2B%2F6qr03onYmzVRIQMjmbd0%0Aq2ntWiQk53P1vrEceg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921012042/329f915a4c05010bf34e0396/tRR9P4JeojEnDJIaNG1Ygq1_jBx679ZqNg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0dc12589484eaf1e186797f37e540ce72de7b23e4b39c521f569b93b92bb35a6&req=fSImFsh8nYVdFb4f3HP0gHJDn8cyJjg6VkmvKE%2B%2F6qr03onYmzVRIQMjmbd0%0Aq2ntWiQk53P1vrEceg%3D%3D%0A)

**10.** The TXT DKIM key needs to be added in **Valimail** and the MX record needs to be added in the **DNS**.

**11.** After you added both records in their respective areas, come back to this page and click **Activate**.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF in Qualtrics **[here](https://www.qualtrics.com/support/survey-platform/distributions-module/email-distribution/using-a-custom-from-address/)
**.

Add a Qualtrics DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Qualtrics emails
========================================================

Once you establish that Qualtrics is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Poppulo

Poppulo
=======

How to set up DMARC for Poppulo in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757812-poppulo#h_6d47874819)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757812-poppulo#h_48181b1e18)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757812-poppulo#h_559c9cd999)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the DKIM authentication process for **Poppulo** and how this is managed in Valimail. Poppulo does not currently support SPF-aligned authentication, but DKIM authentication should be enough for most use case scenarios.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Poppulo emails
=======================================================

In order to turn on DKIM configuration in Poppulo for your domain, you will need to contact [Poppulo support](https://www.poppulo.com/contact-us)
.

Poppulo should provide you with a 2048 bit TXT DKIM key record which you will need to add on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce, like in the example provided in the next chapter.

**[Here](https://knowledgebase.poppulo.com/articles/Best_Practice_Guide/Whitelisting/?q=dkim&l=en_US&fs=Search&pn=1&topicBC=)
** is an article that references Poppulo's support of DMARC and DKIM.

Add a Poppulo DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Poppulo emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Poppulo is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

NetcoreCloud

NetcoreCloud
============

How to set up DMARC for NetcoreCloud in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757811-netcorecloud#h_48d1eacf0d)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757811-netcorecloud#h_3385e2f48a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757811-netcorecloud#h_ce64c06336)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **NetcoreCloud** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your NetcoreCloud emails
============================================================

To validate your domain with a NetcoreCloud DKIM key, just add the following TXT record in your DNS.

Name:

pepipost.\_domainkey.mydomain.com

Value:

k=rsa; p=MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQDLMMExLiGRqzJkNdNIjUnLX7JL0wjbwwENDoXgJIBisIsrofLPetZM401dioNU8k/Qp6nIyi5oioyZh+1jDXoCPFa4nLGRNj3Nz785N7b76aTtHmy2wTgR2LFS/Yw5/iyzhyrWsIyINyyHs77EoCwSOQjJfhFxb6SmifLN51IIvwIDAQAB

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

After you added the TXT DKIM key, click on verify **DKIM** option, and you will find that it is verified.

[![dkim verification button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921046308/cbf291eb74628ecfaf71cb00/RYmOEH7ZHifKr_OD5zRZabWJpO0_3vjC7A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=01b7e607a083f940041734f28a073d3dcb455a192588b1a56c168d972d06860f&req=fSImFs14noFXFb4f3HP0gHXWu57Ml7mrUXu90RDvurqUttwq%2Brs%2Fdjnh6nrJ%0AOXWeyKj02Tv703hcbA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921046308/cbf291eb74628ecfaf71cb00/RYmOEH7ZHifKr_OD5zRZabWJpO0_3vjC7A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=01b7e607a083f940041734f28a073d3dcb455a192588b1a56c168d972d06860f&req=fSImFs14noFXFb4f3HP0gHXWu57Ml7mrUXu90RDvurqUttwq%2Brs%2Fdjnh6nrJ%0AOXWeyKj02Tv703hcbA%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Pepipost, **[here](https://pepipost.com/blog/add-verify-domain/)
**.

Add a NetcoreCloud DKIM key in Valimail
=======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your NetcoreCloud emails
===========================================================

Once you establish that NetcoreCloud is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757555-netsuite#h_339ffa043e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757555-netsuite#h_a6d7a08070)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Netsuite

Netsuite
========

How to set up DMARC for Netsuite in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757555-netsuite#h_db18fae7a5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757555-netsuite#h_f0a1fe1869)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757555-netsuite#h_9bac00d443)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757555-netsuite#h_339ffa043e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757555-netsuite#h_a6d7a08070)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Netsuite** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Netsuite emails
========================================================

**1.** Log in to your NetSuite account as an administrator and go to **Setup** -> **Company** -> **Email** -> **Email Preferences**.

**2.** Click the **Domain Keys** subtab.

**3.** In the **Domain Selector** field, enter the first domain selector. The number of DKIM selectors is limited to one selector per email domain. If you are sharing an email domain between multiple production accounts, use one selector for the shared email domain. Configure that same selector in each account that uses the same email domain.

**Note**: When naming domain selectors, follow the specifications outlined in **[RFC 6376 Section 3.1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6376#section-3.1)
** Selectors and **[RFC 1035 Section 2.3.1](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1035#section-2.3.1)
** **Preferred name syntax**. A few suggestions for naming domain selectors based on these RFCs:

*   The domain selector name (label) must start with a letter, end with a letter or digit, and have as interior characters only letters, digits, and a hyphen.
    
*   Attempting to name a selector beginning with a digit results in an error message that the DKIM selector name is invalid. The record cannot be saved.
    
*   Valid digits (numbers) are 0 through 9.
    
*   Both uppercase and lowercase letters are allowed, but no significance is attached to the case of the letter.
    
*   If using a hyphen as an interior character in the domain selector name, ensure that the character is a hyphen (Unicode U+2010) and not a different character that may look similar to a hyphen.
    
*   The domain selector name must be 63 characters or less.
    

A suggested best practice is to structure the domain selector name to include information such as the purpose, the owner, and the creation date.

For example, to meet the criteria specified in the RFCs, **dec2020-netsuite** is a name you could enter in the Domain Selector field in NetSuite.

**4.** In the Domain Name field, enter the domain name you are using to send DKIM-signed email from NetSuite. For example, if the email address from which you are sending DKIM signed email is [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#5d372a32313b381d2a32313b383831383e292f3233343e2e733e3230)
, the domain is wolfeelectronics.com. An email address from this domain can appear in the From header.

**5.** Enter the **Private** and **Public** domain keys (in PEM format) used for signing in one of the following ways:

*   If you have used the same domain keys with another application, enter the domain keys manually.
    
*   If you have not generated a domain key for this domain previously, click **Generate Key Pairs** to have NetSuite generate them for you.
    

You need the public domain key to set up your domain with a domain hosting service.

**6.** After entering the domain keys, click **Generated DNS Entry**. Your complete, properly formatted DNS entry is shown in a popup window. Copy this DNS entry. Do not close the browser window.

**7.** Add the DKIM TXT key/s in **Valimail Enforce**.

**Note**: When a domain is shared by more than one NetSuite account, a message is displayed on the Domain Keys subtab indicting that the email domain is used by other NetSuite accounts. If the DKIM configuration needs to be modified, the customer must contact NetSuite Customer Support.

**8.** Make sure you add the generated DKIM key in Valimail Enforce procedure within 14 days after entering domain keys in NetSuite.

**Important**: If you do not complete the Set Up a DNS Text Record within 14 days, the From header of email sent from that particular domain will continue to be rewritten. For more information, please see **[From Headers in Email Can Be Rewritten](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/subsect_1536091586.html)
**.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM for Netsuite **[here](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_163604950862.html)
**.

Activate DKIM keys in NetSuite
==============================

To activate DKIM keys in your NetSuite account:

**1.** In your NetSuite account on the **Email Preferences** page, check the **Active** box for each key you have set up.

**2.** Click **Save**. All outgoing email messages sent from NetSuite using the domain or subdomain you entered will have a DKIM header.

This code header contains the domain authentication information but does not add any text to your messages.

**3.** Test your DKIM setup.

Verify your DKIM setup
======================

After you have entered your Domain Keys in NetSuite, published the DKIM key in Valimail Enforce, and activated your DKIM keys, you can test your DKIM setup.

To verify your DKIM Setup:

**1.** Inside your Netsuite account, go to **Setup** -> **Company** -> **Email** -> **Email Preferences**.

**2.** On the **Domain Keys** subtab, select the domain key you want to verify and click **Verify DNS Entry**.

NetSuite checks to make sure the public domain key in the DNS record matches the public domain key entered in NetSuite.

**3.** The results of the test are displayed in a popup window. Possible results from this test include the following:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920996758/673e93adcee8072a1f1cd039/N1XcqxnNwZQKZTL1NsMFl2aCAvkNEFtuOQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=19186dcfdd585f39d47e48b19c5793b88a79e814dbae859e2919f5fba66d6e3a&req=fSInH8B4moRXFb4f3HP0gF5SvFC6WPzfYz85Yz3m7rBrqk21OA66QTl9vJzD%0AF2KMgihbellMgGKc4g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920996758/673e93adcee8072a1f1cd039/N1XcqxnNwZQKZTL1NsMFl2aCAvkNEFtuOQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=19186dcfdd585f39d47e48b19c5793b88a79e814dbae859e2919f5fba66d6e3a&req=fSInH8B4moRXFb4f3HP0gF5SvFC6WPzfYz85Yz3m7rBrqk21OA66QTl9vJzD%0AF2KMgihbellMgGKc4g%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** If you receive a message that your DNS entry for DKIM has been verified, click **Send Test Email to DKIM Reflector** to send a test email message.

A DKIM reflector is a service set up to receive and analyze email. This reflector then forwards a report on your domain key setup. The report is sent to the address shown in the **Email Address to Receive Test Response** field.

Add a Netsuite DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Netsuite emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Netsuite is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

Paubox Encrypted Email

Paubox Encrypted Email
======================

How to set up DMARC for Paubox Encrypted Email in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757809-paubox-encrypted-email#h_553e02cae7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757809-paubox-encrypted-email#h_914f85303e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757809-paubox-encrypted-email#h_47abb39c01)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Paubox Encrypted Email** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Paubox Encrypted Email emails
======================================================================

**1.** Log in to your Paubox [dashboard](https://www.paubox.com/users/sign_in)
.

(don't know your password? Click the "Forgot your password?" link)

**2\.** In the left-hand navigation menu, click **Overview**.

**3.** In the DKIM Configuration row, click **Open Settings**.

**4.** Select your domain in the dropdown menu and click **Generate DKIM Key**.

**5.** Add a new DNS record to your domain host with the values from your Paubox dashboard.

(the values in your dashboard are unique to you and your domain)

**Note**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

**6.** Toggle **DKIM Enabled** to **Yes** (make sure Step 5 is completed before toggling to Yes!).

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Paubox Encrypted Email, **[here](https://support.paubox.com/hc/en-us/articles/360056545434-What-is-DKIM-How-do-I-enable-it-)
**.

Add a Paubox Encrypted Email DKIM key in Valimail
=================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Paubox Encrypted Email DKIM emails
==========================================================================

Once you establish that Paubox Encrypted Email DKIM is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Peoplevine

Peoplevine
==========

How to set up DMARC for Peoplevine in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757810-peoplevine#h_5e33c294b5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757810-peoplevine#h_537d91e2c0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757810-peoplevine#h_3eaa909f76)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Peoplevine** and how they are managed in Valimail. Because this service leverages Sendgrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding **Peoplevine** as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Sendgrid infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Peoplevine emails
==========================================================

Setting up a Domain Key (DKIM) you further identify your e-mails that they came securely from your server. In your DNS, we recommend you setup the following DNS entries:

CNAME - Host: sender - VALUE: sender.peoplevine.net

CNAME - Host: **s1**.\_domainkey - VALUE: **s1.\_domainkey.peoplevine.net**

CNAME - Host: **s2**.\_domainkey - VALUE: **s2.\_domainkey.peoplevine.net**

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys (s1, and s2) on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and the 3rd CNAME record in your DNS.

Once you have this in place, please send a request to **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#3f4c4a4f4f504d4b7f4f5a504f535a4956515a115c5052)
** to setup your DKIM. Simply provide us with the email address your emails are being sent from.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Peoplevine, **[here](https://help.peoplevinehelp.com/en/articles/1042305-how-do-i-prevent-my-newsletters-and-emails-from-going-to-spam-delivery-settings)
**.

Add a Peoplevine DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Peoplevine emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Peoplevine is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Oracle Right Now | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757606-oracle-right-now#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757606-oracle-right-now#h_87993f0480)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757606-oracle-right-now#h_c04895de70)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Oracle Right Now

Oracle Right Now
================

How to set up DMARC for Oracle Right Now in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757606-oracle-right-now#h_db8ff88a67)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757606-oracle-right-now#h_87993f0480)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757606-oracle-right-now#h_c04895de70)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Oracle Right Now** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Oracle Right Now emails
================================================================

To enable DKIM for your outbound email using your organization’s domain name in the Friendly From/Branded Address field (email are signed based on the value configured in the outgoing email settings), use the following checklist:

1.  Submit a **[service request](https://cx.rightnow.com/app/ask)
    ** for this information requesting DKIM signing. Specify the email address you intend on using as the Friendly From/Branded Address in your mailings. Oracle Right Now's standard key length is 1024 bit. If you would like a 2048 bit key please add that information to your request. We recommend you chose the 2048 bit.
    
2.  Oracle will generate a public-private key pair for use in signing these messages, and will generate a unique selector that will be used for signing your messages, and will update the incident with the selector in a form suitable for use with BIND DNS servers. If you have multiple domains we can specify a key which can be used for multiple domains on one site.
    
3.  Your organization’s IT administrators must add this selector within the domain desired to be signed. If your IT department uses a DNS infrastructure other than BIND, they must consult their documentation or vendors on the appropriate form to be used. Update the incident when this step has been completed. The DKIM key will need to be published in **Valimail Enforce**.
    
4.  Oracle will verify that the DKIM key has been published correctly, and that the published selector in your domain validates against DKIM standards.
    
5.  Oracle will configure their mail servers to sign messages matching the domain name portion of your “From:” address, and will close the incident after testing. Subsequently, all your email using that domain name delivered from our hosting environment will contain a DKIM signature. If you have added a domain key for multiple domains then the process repeats with (3) you publishing the key for the new domain, then we will verify (4) and enable (5) the key for that domain on our servers.
    

You can find more info about the whole DKIM set up process in Oracle Right Now **[here](https://cx.rightnow.com/app/answers/detail/a_id/2701/track/AvOCywofDv8S~WO0Gqly~6L0RXcqCi75Mv_i~zj~PP9r)
**.

Add an Oracle Right Now DKIM key in Valimail
============================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Oracle Right Now emails
===============================================================

Once you establish that Oracle Right Now is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# L-Soft EASE | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757141-l-soft-ease#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757141-l-soft-ease#h_5e9355c0ec)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757141-l-soft-ease#h_32281593b8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757141-l-soft-ease#h_34ed624b14)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

L-Soft EASE

L-Soft EASE
===========

How to set up DMARC for L-Soft EASE in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757141-l-soft-ease#h_5e9355c0ec)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757141-l-soft-ease#h_32281593b8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757141-l-soft-ease#h_34ed624b14)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **L-Soft EASE** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

L-Soft EASE uses a TXT DKIM key and a dedicated subdomain configuration setup.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your L-Soft EASE emails
===========================================================

To set up DKIM for your L-Soft EASE emails, you will need to create and publish a DKIM TXT key in your DNS and also add it on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce, you manage the DKIM in Valimail for that subdomain.

Creating a DKIM TXT record can be done in various ways. If you run your own DNS, simply edit your forward zone file to include a TXT record. We will assume for this exercise that the LISTSERV host name is “listserv.example.com”, and we will enter the following information:

**Host**: default.\_domainkey.listserv

**TXT Value**: v=DKIM1;k=rsa;p=MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQDcARWuStG7G33L+M5jqjiCbhfKBlgxIMC8Of5ODONOTUSETHISKEYITISANEXAMPLEONLY91O1RigBB/C+UXzPO+N1+hZ55ZXS8MPGPgaV9VM1EysEdyfm2Y/rn935GGJwtm67fz+6dyKkCAzLsMjR5DvcxxlMzf6Gs9TrX7PBNwIDAQAB

**TTL**: Your preference, but typically 1 hour.

**Notes**:

*   1\. When creating a new record in a DNS zone file, the host name normally is not fully qualified. If you are editing the zone file for the example.com zone, it should not be necessary to enter the fully-qualified domain name in the “host” section. Be sure to check the documentation for whatever DNS you are running if you are unsure of this.
    
*   2\. We are assuming a DKIM selector value of “default”. For the purposes of DKIM authentication, external sites will always check DNS for a TXT record belonging to “selector”.\_domainkey.”hostname”. In our example, external sites would be looking for the TXT record belonging to default.\_domainkey.listserv.example.com .
    
*   3\. The TXT value should not break and wrap as displayed above. It should be one continuous line of text. The value of “p=” is the text of the public key from between the lines
    

Once you have created your DNS entries and LISTSERV configuration for DKIM, you will want to test it.

Listserv has a built-in Deliverability Assessment report which can be reached in the web interface at Server Administration -> Site Configuration -> Deliverability Assessment. The first screen looks like the following:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920894315/13bff17d6db6c6f939825622/tHlqOkAzMToEPS1r5EFxfdn1zFbAZAYXXw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f6b1e095cb86e466f4626c8b19fa5234829e20c3afdab2ff62ed6d065fbb7953&req=fSInHsB6noBaFb4f3HP0gOLYpUNZ6Xh%2BnZoh820%2FzoahA%2F8%2BaevhPrPK%2FCd9%0AZpi49oGkHFuioyYO%2Bw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920894315/13bff17d6db6c6f939825622/tHlqOkAzMToEPS1r5EFxfdn1zFbAZAYXXw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f6b1e095cb86e466f4626c8b19fa5234829e20c3afdab2ff62ed6d065fbb7953&req=fSInHsB6noBaFb4f3HP0gOLYpUNZ6Xh%2BnZoh820%2FzoahA%2F8%2BaevhPrPK%2FCd9%0AZpi49oGkHFuioyYO%2Bw%3D%3D%0A)

Unless you have multiple domains set up in LISTSERV, there should be no reason to change the pre-populated values. If they are correct, simply click Submit. This will yield the report:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920894322/bdf55720af38e2f3af4de6fe/zvvhae_lcGZTRVD3lZeybXJK5AK9tlzUFg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d34fb9249d03b78c7c8ab4d7d0f4173991f13180d7599ff2bea1a8a8206d7f71&req=fSInHsB6noNdFb4f3HP0gL9t3OWVP4mgRBCXA5jpn%2FRW6wfxZRlG9c%2F3Vcda%0ABD4tkqYOmaK%2B3ERRsA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/920894322/bdf55720af38e2f3af4de6fe/zvvhae_lcGZTRVD3lZeybXJK5AK9tlzUFg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=d34fb9249d03b78c7c8ab4d7d0f4173991f13180d7599ff2bea1a8a8206d7f71&req=fSInHsB6noNdFb4f3HP0gL9t3OWVP4mgRBCXA5jpn%2FRW6wfxZRlG9c%2F3Vcda%0ABD4tkqYOmaK%2B3ERRsA%3D%3D%0A)

The green shields indicate that, so far as LISTSERV is concerned, you have properly configured the DKIM DNS entry, and LISTSERV itself is properly configured to sign outbound messages with DKIM. If either or both of the shields are not green, you need to recheck your DNS entry and LISTSERV configuration, and correct any errors before running the report again.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF FOR L-Soft EASE, **[here](https://www.lsoft.com/manuals/17.0/LISTSERV_DKIM_Config.pdf)
**.

Add an L-Soft EASE DKIM key in Valimail
=======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your L-Soft EASE emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that L-Soft EASE is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://intercom.help/valimail-4dffb6ef6e39/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Pure360

Pure360
=======

How to set up DMARC for Pure360 in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757970-pure360#h_6914251ef0)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757970-pure360#h_c925333ca5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757970-pure360#h_ef127d1f65)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Pure360** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Pure360 emails
=======================================================

In order to set up DKIM in Pure360 for your domain, you will need to contact Pure360 Support so they can generate like us to generate a DKIM key.

The best way to get in touch is to raise a support ticket through your account. Use the support widget you can find to the right of your screen while logged in. Alternatively, you can contact Pure360 via **0844 5877 360** or **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#6e0f0d0d011b001a030f000f090b1c1d2e1e1b1c0b5d585e400d0103)
**

If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Pure360, **[here](http://support.pure360.com/knowledge-base/domain-masking/)
**.

Add a Pure360 DKIM key in Valimail
==================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Pure360 emails
======================================================

Once you establish that Pure360 is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# Promoter.io | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757967-promoter-io#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757967-promoter-io#h_6da2022026)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757967-promoter-io#h_6f75425d2e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757967-promoter-io#h_c7b6340af1)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Promoter.io

Promoter.io
===========

How to set up DMARC for Promoter.io in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757967-promoter-io#h_6da2022026)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757967-promoter-io#h_6f75425d2e)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757967-promoter-io#h_c7b6340af1)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM configuration process for **Promoter.io**. Because this service leverages SendGrid's email infrastructure, it requires additions to your DNS records outside of the Valimail platform. Adding Promoter.io as an authorized sender in the Valimail platform usually requires a dedicated subdomain to be pointed to the Promoter.io infrastructure and typically the DKIM keys will be added in the Valimail platform.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Promoter.io emails
===========================================================

If you are already a current user of SendGrid or have records added for Sendgrid due to another service, you'll need to follow these steps below in order to link your domain. In short, you will need to add a third level domain (subdomain) to differentiate between your existing records and the record for **Promoter**.

(Suppose your domain is **example.com**)

**1.** Visit your DNS and add a subdomain to your account. You can name this whatever you'd like in your DNS. In the example below, we used the name **survey**.

Your Domain

**example.com**

Subdomain

**survey.example.com**

The survey portion of the domain is what helps us for the CNAME dkim1 and dkim2 records that will need to be added.

**2.** Visit your **Deliverability Settings** in Promoter and proceed with the rest of the steps by entering in your subdomain and click **Add**. With the subdomain example, SendGrid would generate the following CNAME **DKIM** and **mail\_name** records:

p.survey.example.com

**s1\_domainkey.survey.example.com**

**s2\_domainkey.survey.example.com**

**3.** Add the 2 DKIM keys in Valimail Enforce and the other record in your DNS.

**4.** Once the DKIM records have been added, go back to the Promoter account page and make sure the records are validated.

You can also find the instructions to set up DKIM for Promoter.io **[here](https://help.promoter.io/en/articles/537899-existing-account-with-sendgrid)
**.

Add a Promoter.io DKIM key in Valimail
======================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Promoter.io emails
==========================================================

Once you establish that Promoter.io is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *
