[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757968-proofpoint-essentials#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757968-proofpoint-essentials#h_0dc25df6a8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757968-proofpoint-essentials#h_8a440e5d7c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757968-proofpoint-essentials#h_45d84b6e32)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Proofpoint Essentials

Proofpoint Essentials
=====================

How to set up DMARC for Proofpoint Essentials in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757968-proofpoint-essentials#h_0dc25df6a8)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757968-proofpoint-essentials#h_8a440e5d7c)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757968-proofpoint-essentials#h_45d84b6e32)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Proofpoint Essentials** and how are they managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

**Configuring DKIM authentication for your Proofpoint Essentials emails**
-------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each domain that sends email can be individually configured to sign outbound messages with DKIM. To enable the feature, you will need to create a new signing key, add the public key in Valimail Enforce, and verify that its been added correctly.

**1.** Navigate to **Administration** -> **Account Management** -> **Domains**.

**2.** Select the domain you want to configure and click the **vertical ellipsis** on the right-hand side of the Domains table.

**3.** Click the option labeled **Configure DKIM**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055386/5e20ece723322961d5ab4e87/2RkknyTg20OCOS5V7PSFEoNtq70HOA9wpQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ab47934672e0b6544b57549421a8eb07ed1052cd8ee197d1f0240708cdb062fa&req=fSImFsx7nolZFb4f3HP0gGwUkw05OJGBDt%2BHWGgBg807HADDYQJnqYP6kanv%0ArQgNZVyki2jpY%2BoUzg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055386/5e20ece723322961d5ab4e87/2RkknyTg20OCOS5V7PSFEoNtq70HOA9wpQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ab47934672e0b6544b57549421a8eb07ed1052cd8ee197d1f0240708cdb062fa&req=fSImFsx7nolZFb4f3HP0gGwUkw05OJGBDt%2BHWGgBg807HADDYQJnqYP6kanv%0ArQgNZVyki2jpY%2BoUzg%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** A drawer will appear on the right side of the screen, listing all the currently configured DKIM keys. If this is your first time configuring DKIM, no keys will be listed. Click **Create New DKIM Signing Key**.

**5.** The form will appear asking you to specify a selector. A selector is used to locate the public key in the DNS (_or in Valimail Enforce, if you publish the DKIM key there_) and is not visible to end users. A value is pre-populated, but you can change it if you'd like. Click **Create**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055394/49104ad7940a9c6df0e65db5/mXdBKQBHaLpRy339Fsfl78OmtuCOa7PMww.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dbe7c34ed15c28f9aa671cb1eff75f45018b9453f401fa53d68720dd02f233a7&req=fSImFsx7nohbFb4f3HP0gF7y1XFXORNNlcm0DjjnTjzyzTPYSTxUF8uK6okF%0APXrOiXxgdcdHzYAuQw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055394/49104ad7940a9c6df0e65db5/mXdBKQBHaLpRy339Fsfl78OmtuCOa7PMww.png?expires=1755792000&signature=dbe7c34ed15c28f9aa671cb1eff75f45018b9453f401fa53d68720dd02f233a7&req=fSImFsx7nohbFb4f3HP0gF7y1XFXORNNlcm0DjjnTjzyzTPYSTxUF8uK6okF%0APXrOiXxgdcdHzYAuQw%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** The resulting screen will give you the DKIM record that you need to publish in **Valimail Enforce**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055399/af99ea9089b22ddbf2428e67/JBEA5odsE7BqdM55IuvC28xnaboXtl1t7w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2a9a034b62be9e113c660a24583bb4928b8c18393305afd3e354a2e9494bc8d1&req=fSImFsx7nohWFb4f3HP0gGk9NhDxmNcHmWXeKKWT79vzWVRwB7b1p5r8kdKk%0A0ENFQFqm%2BLIsfpKJ9g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055399/af99ea9089b22ddbf2428e67/JBEA5odsE7BqdM55IuvC28xnaboXtl1t7w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=2a9a034b62be9e113c660a24583bb4928b8c18393305afd3e354a2e9494bc8d1&req=fSImFsx7nohWFb4f3HP0gGk9NhDxmNcHmWXeKKWT79vzWVRwB7b1p5r8kdKk%0A0ENFQFqm%2BLIsfpKJ9g%3D%3D%0A)

**IMPORTANT**: In Valimail Enforce, you will only add the selector and the public value of the DKIM key.

The key selector is in the above case **selector-1607357288** and the value is the whole string of numbers and letters that starts after the **p=** tag (omitting the tag).

**7.** Once you've published the DKIM key in Valimail Enforce, Proofpoint will need to validate that the record was added correctly. To do so, click the **Verify Key** button in the key's context menu.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055402/54ccdac9ce3e5771d0de9d58/gxVTsmjAkf-m4tDFY0EdOy_p7Rzfa-5ajQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9835cd65a32f22a74b3fece818758870468a584d9c280e04d42e4a837eaf46e7&req=fSImFsx7mYFdFb4f3HP0gIR9CUZF9xPO2A9SEtomkNxAl48suvkFY1pozm%2F3%0A9BC%2BqWFG8vdvpDRwPA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055402/54ccdac9ce3e5771d0de9d58/gxVTsmjAkf-m4tDFY0EdOy_p7Rzfa-5ajQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=9835cd65a32f22a74b3fece818758870468a584d9c280e04d42e4a837eaf46e7&req=fSImFsx7mYFdFb4f3HP0gIR9CUZF9xPO2A9SEtomkNxAl48suvkFY1pozm%2F3%0A9BC%2BqWFG8vdvpDRwPA%3D%3D%0A)

**8.** Once the key is successfully verified, outbound DKIM signing is automatically enabled for this domain.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055409/da16f79c243f835c155fb60d/jxG-IxHoEQiFnBJSN2U0x5nmTRZrXz7WwQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3847e1b53cc54d6ff329827f8ba9edcafbe6698e728dd1c4ec016bb88bf908f6&req=fSImFsx7mYFWFb4f3HP0gABlmdyF1tm83fx9l6nMEYmca7JB7hfdMCNHXH6J%0ApDOVpaoe9TJuWyQxLA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055409/da16f79c243f835c155fb60d/jxG-IxHoEQiFnBJSN2U0x5nmTRZrXz7WwQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=3847e1b53cc54d6ff329827f8ba9edcafbe6698e728dd1c4ec016bb88bf908f6&req=fSImFsx7mYFWFb4f3HP0gABlmdyF1tm83fx9l6nMEYmca7JB7hfdMCNHXH6J%0ApDOVpaoe9TJuWyQxLA%3D%3D%0A)

  
​

You can find the instructions on how to setup DKIM in Proofpoint **[here](https://help.proofpoint.com/Proofpoint_Essentials/Email_Security/Administrator_Topics/030_domains/Configuring_Outbound_DKIM_Signing)
**.

**Adding a Proofpoint Essentials DKIM key in Valimail**
-------------------------------------------------------

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

**Configuring SPF authentication for your Proofpoint Essentials emails**
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once you establish that Proofpoint is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# ProtonMail | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757969-protonmail#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757969-protonmail#h_41d2f10334)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757969-protonmail#h_8c086e3ab7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757969-protonmail#h_59dd457530)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

ProtonMail

ProtonMail
==========

How to set up DMARC for ProtonMail in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757969-protonmail#h_41d2f10334)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757969-protonmail#h_8c086e3ab7)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757969-protonmail#h_59dd457530)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **ProtonMail** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your ProtonMail emails
==========================================================

**1.** In your browser, log in to your Proton Mail account and go to **Settings** → **Go to settings** → **Organization** → **Domain names** → **Actions column** → **Review** button next to the domain you want to add a DKIM record for.

**2.** Select the **DKIM** tab. Here you will see the three host names and values that you will need to add to your domain’s DNS settings.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add all the 3 CNAME DKIM keys on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055820/96c570fef4743613c6f202ac/IK54yioqUW5CgPm50BOl4YKteGGcZFwbfQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=af566f6fb90af9b0d4c0077b6d7c82e800bb61a9c9351681e76d09485251ff1d&req=fSImFsx7lYNfFb4f3HP0gBRfB%2Bs8SecDTYxYKwW7MATbHuNZFNii85k5%2F6VA%0AetkNbvDK8ydw0JX%2BYQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921055820/96c570fef4743613c6f202ac/IK54yioqUW5CgPm50BOl4YKteGGcZFwbfQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=af566f6fb90af9b0d4c0077b6d7c82e800bb61a9c9351681e76d09485251ff1d&req=fSImFsx7lYNfFb4f3HP0gBRfB%2Bs8SecDTYxYKwW7MATbHuNZFNii85k5%2F6VA%0AetkNbvDK8ydw0JX%2BYQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3\.** Once you have added these records, Proton Mail will handle the rest for you. Following current security best practices, we will generate a new 2048-bit key every six months and use it to sign your emails.

The CNAME records you add in Valimail Enforce must be an exact match with the ones shown in your setup wizard. Once we detect these records in your DNS, the DKIM tab will show a green tick icon. We will then notify you and start signing outgoing emails from your custom domain with DKIM, just like we do for other Proton Mail addresses.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for ProtonMail, **[here](https://proton.me/support/anti-spoofing-custom-domain)
**.

Add a ProtonMail DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your ProtonMail emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that ProtonMail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Rejoiner

Rejoiner
========

How to set up DMARC for Rejoiner in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758043-rejoiner#h_1b6eeff4ec)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758043-rejoiner#h_b9d0e0eb7f)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758043-rejoiner#h_26462ec6a8)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Rejoiner** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Rejoiner emails
========================================================

Rejoiner typically uses a dedicated subdomain setup for DKIM authentication, a subdomain which you will need to create in your DNS.

You will then need to contact Rejoiner support, so they can help you set up DKIM for your domain.

You can find out more on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Rejoiner, **[here](https://www.rejoiner.com/resources/what-is-a-dkim-record#configuring-dkim-records)
**.

Add a Rejoiner DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Rejoiner emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Rejoiner is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

# NICE InContact | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757559-nice-incontact#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757559-nice-incontact#h_741d00d54b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757559-nice-incontact#h_bc49d6051a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757559-nice-incontact#h_563c99f8a8)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

NICE InContact

NICE InContact
==============

How to set up DMARC for NICE InContact in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757559-nice-incontact#h_741d00d54b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757559-nice-incontact#h_bc49d6051a)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757559-nice-incontact#h_563c99f8a8)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(not supported)

This article covers the SPF authentication process for **NICE inContact** and how is that managed in Valimail Enforce.

At present time, we have yet to see any evidence that NICE inContact supports actual DKIM implementation.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your NICE inContact emails
==============================================================

Although NICE inContact's current documentation states that they have a process of setting up DKIM for your domain, we have yet to see any DKIM implementation for any domain which is using this sending service.

If you are on the verge of setting up DKIM in NICE inContact for your domain and you are managing DKIM in Valimail, please contact us at [\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#9be8eeebebf4e9efdbedfaf7f2f6faf2f7b5f8f4f6)
 and let us know about your DKIM implementation with NICE inContact.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up SPF alignment on NICE inContact, **[here](https://help.nice-incontact.com/content/acd/channels/email/setupemail.htm)
**.

Configuring SPF authentication for your NICE inContact emails
=============================================================

Once you establish that Hushmail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in NICE InContact for your domain
======================================================

You may notice that **NICE InContact** emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added NICE InContact to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by NICE InContact not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by making sure the return path of the NICE InContact emails is changed to match your domain.

To that end, please contact your NICE InContact service administrator and make sure they are turning on SPF alignment in NICE InContact, for your domain.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757966-prezly#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757966-prezly#h_9e4a61f678)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Prezly

Prezly
======

How to set up DMARC for Prezly in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757966-prezly#h_4e3c1c0ae2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757966-prezly#h_86c1c035ba)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757966-prezly#h_9e4a61f678)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Prezly** and how they are managed in Valimail Enforce. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Prezly emails
======================================================

**1.** Go to **Organisation Settings** -> **Domain Verification**.

Access the Domain Verification page in your Prezly account [here](https://rock.prezly.com/settings/domain-verification)
.

**2.** Find the domain name you want to verify.

As you may have added several emails to your Prezly account, you'll see the list of emails in the **Domain Verification** page. Click on the one you want to verify. Please remember that if you don't verify your email with Prezly, you will only be able to send campaigns from the "@prezlymail.com" email.

**3.** Copy the CNAME records you see on the screen and add them to your DNS.

You'll see three to four records on the screen. All of them will have the type CNAME to it, with a Name and a Value. You'll need to copy both the name and the value you see for each record. Then, copy and paste them onto your domain provider settings page.

In case you're not sure about how to handle those records, you can easily share them with your IT team or domain provider support, so they can either add those for you or further advise you on how to add them. Click on the **Share Instructions** link and you send instructions directly via email or share the link to the instructions page.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the 2 CNAME DKIM keys (**prezly** and **prezly2**) on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce and add the other 2 CNAME records in your DNS.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921053309/4fde5466fbcf2f37e24eded5/qWicgcqFv3XrauhxkapWLAP0ZvpJSoynvg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=408aa1894d775b83617edad69413a0c236892d811630965117a3e205a9e67966&req=fSImFsx9noFWFb4f3HP0gCbMWoEGKzGo85FnN2Xg8iWM8G2WWvar2mjlAkIm%0AAXKmp6qLvBobLYRYpg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921053309/4fde5466fbcf2f37e24eded5/qWicgcqFv3XrauhxkapWLAP0ZvpJSoynvg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=408aa1894d775b83617edad69413a0c236892d811630965117a3e205a9e67966&req=fSImFsx9noFWFb4f3HP0gCbMWoEGKzGo85FnN2Xg8iWM8G2WWvar2mjlAkIm%0AAXKmp6qLvBobLYRYpg%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** After the records are added, run the **Status Check** in Prezly.

Once you have added the records with your domain provider, it will take between 30 minutes to 48 hours for Prezly to recognize that everything is set correctly. After that period of time, feel free to run a **Status Check**, and when everything is linked up properly, you'll see a green sign next to the domain you verified.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921053313/31057bf688b4d6a44978dbec/2rofs-ptTpgAia75-TzojxDHjCfaPJIgig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=bc5d1243832bf6d3c6f5435786d8320c43e2054b0f54e42c599f4f596385b154&req=fSImFsx9noBcFb4f3HP0gDx5yLWJakQZm3ajTSMDjn9IdkFD7jwusoxXykVC%0AB4rXmV74%2FN8c8vhsBw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921053313/31057bf688b4d6a44978dbec/2rofs-ptTpgAia75-TzojxDHjCfaPJIgig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=bc5d1243832bf6d3c6f5435786d8320c43e2054b0f54e42c599f4f596385b154&req=fSImFsx9noBcFb4f3HP0gDx5yLWJakQZm3ajTSMDjn9IdkFD7jwusoxXykVC%0AB4rXmV74%2FN8c8vhsBw%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Prezly, **[here](https://www.prezly.com/help/verify-your-sender-address-domain)
**.

Add a Prezly DKIM key in Valimail
=================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Prezly emails
=====================================================

Once you establish that Prezly is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Rackspace Hosted Exchange

Rackspace Hosted Exchange
=========================

How to set up DMARC for Rackspace Hosted Exchange in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758039-rackspace-hosted-exchange#h_1fb49453d5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758039-rackspace-hosted-exchange#h_d8c300b774)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758039-rackspace-hosted-exchange#h_6fa8fb8111)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Rackspace Hosted Exchange** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Rackspace Hosted Exchange emails
=========================================================================

**1.** Log in to the **[Cloud Office Control Panel](https://cp.rackspace.com/)
**.

**2.** In the **Domains** section of the home page, click the **Sender Authentication (DKIM)** link.

**3.** On the **Sender Authentication (DKIM)** page, click the domain for which you want to enable DKIM.

**4.** Click **Enable DKIM for**.

**5.** Add the TXT DKIM key in **Valimail Enforce**.

After adding the DKIM TXT record in Valimail Enforce, click **Verify TXT Record** to ensure that the new DKIM record is publicly available and is correct.

If validation fails, then an appropriate error message displays to help you correct the problem.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921062979/54e1993df37577bf6bbae748/iNZERWn0WXmho6wx1nLK6jAHjwbWkSXYcg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1179aa9e7c76823528ee52564dd02c2e4d75ebfc8ee976570d8c7445d8907e64&req=fSImFs98lIZWFb4f3HP0gI%2FJrjbh9aiwOQsY1JETwm8OMmGsNBCWJxfAJPcO%0A1fLYj0jMmC9AjauuZw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921062979/54e1993df37577bf6bbae748/iNZERWn0WXmho6wx1nLK6jAHjwbWkSXYcg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1179aa9e7c76823528ee52564dd02c2e4d75ebfc8ee976570d8c7445d8907e64&req=fSImFs98lIZWFb4f3HP0gI%2FJrjbh9aiwOQsY1JETwm8OMmGsNBCWJxfAJPcO%0A1fLYj0jMmC9AjauuZw%3D%3D%0A)

After DKIM is enabled, all new outgoing email messages are signed using your new DKIM key. You can repeat the DKIM-enabling process on other supported domains.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DMARC for Rackspace Hosted Exchange **[here](https://docs.rackspace.com/support/how-to/enable-dkim-in-the-cloud-office-control-panel/)
**.

Add a Rackspace Hosted Exchange DKIM key in Valimail
====================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Rackspace Hosted Exchange emails
========================================================================

Once you establish that Rackspace Hosted Exchange is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Rave Alert

Rave Alert
==========

How to set up DMARC for Rave Alert in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758041-rave-alert#h_7fe3ef3fcd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758041-rave-alert#h_fc97edb4a6)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758041-rave-alert#h_a20e825690)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758041-rave-alert#h_7dabfc4f17)

SPF(not supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the DKIM authentication process for **Rave Alert** and how it is being managed in Valimail.

**Rave Alert does not support SPF alignment.**

**NOTE**: _We have recently received signals that this sender might be changing their DKIM set-up method to one using a single DKIM TXT key that leverages Mailgun's infrastructure. If that is the case, the below information might be subject to change._

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Rave Alert emails
==========================================================

For Rave Alert to send authenticated emails on your behalf, you must update your domain’s DNS records to point to the public keys we use to sign messages.

Once you update your DNS records, send a list of your updated email domains to

**[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#f88c9d9b908b8d8888978a8cb88a998e9d95979a91949d8b999e9d8c81d69b9795)
**. Our services team will turn on DKIM for your Rave Alert instance.

Once enabled for DKIM, Rave Alert signs emails using different domains depending on the

email’s destination. Rave Alert will sign emails going to internal addresses you control with whichever domain you specify in the from: field.

Rave Alert will sign emails going to external addresses with your domain’s default getrave.com email address.

Steps to Setup DKIM in Rave Alert:
==================================

**1.** Publish the below CNAME records on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail by pointing to the public keys Rave uses to sign messages.

Here is an example of the CNAME records you must add:

**rave1.\_domainkey.customer.com -> rave1.\_domainkey.getrave.com**

**rave2.\_domainkey.customer.com -> rave2.\_domainkey.getrave.com**

**rave3.\_domainkey.customer.com -> rave3.\_domainkey.getrave.com**

**Note**: In Valimail you will only need to add the DKIM selector like presented in the **Add a Rave Alert DKIM key in Valimail** chapter.

**2.** Contact **[\[email protected\]](https://support.valimail.com/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#97e3f2f4ffe4e2e7e7f8e5e3d7e5f6e1f2faf8f5fefbf2e4f6f1f2e3eeb9f4f8fa)
** with a list of email domains you’ve added our CNAME records to. Rave Alert Support will turn on DKIM for your Rave Alert domain.

Once enabled, Rave Alert will sign all emails.

Rave Alert publishes 3 selectors for DKIM authentication – rave1, rave2, and rave3. They use one selector at a time to sign their DKIM messages and rotate them periodically.

**Please make sure you add all 3 DKIM keys to Valimail.**

Maintaining multiple selectors lets Rave Alert rotate keys, increase key length, and manage other security best practices seamlessly, without requiring any action from you. Once you set up the CNAME records, they will take care of the DKIM records validation.

Add a Rave Alert DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Rave Alert emails
=========================================================

Once you establish that Rave Alert is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Postmark

Postmark
========

How to set up DMARC for Postmark in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757814-postmark#h_0f30b30ebe)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757814-postmark#h_9c340b90d5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757814-postmark#h_094c783790)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8757814-postmark#h_3f095b362e)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Postmark** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Postmark emails
========================================================

**1.** Navigate to your **Sender Signatures** tab in your Postmark account.

**2.** Under the domain you are adding DKIM authentication for, click **DNS Settings** or **Add a DKIM DNS record**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921047191/8ba85f2775221c5c974fd70e/nhB4DP6PkA--y31xJduEbX1afD9n9vomKg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=af9f5703b08507962d6d94bfb8228d17739dac9d0635838c1adf36a7c7e31677&req=fSImFs15nIheFb4f3HP0gH%2B%2Br8V7lOhH2XvsL5dZTTwRio3HdWD9WR%2Fv1eWo%0AjRg%2BV7QF8yvkUMdn%2FQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921047191/8ba85f2775221c5c974fd70e/nhB4DP6PkA--y31xJduEbX1afD9n9vomKg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=af9f5703b08507962d6d94bfb8228d17739dac9d0635838c1adf36a7c7e31677&req=fSImFs15nIheFb4f3HP0gH%2B%2Br8V7lOhH2XvsL5dZTTwRio3HdWD9WR%2Fv1eWo%0AjRg%2BV7QF8yvkUMdn%2FQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** A DKIM record will be shown that needs to be added to your DNS as a TXT record.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921047198/2ef9ca58a05e3ddc33745e56/oFXkX4YkH-LhEQ4h61qpagF0JEwqqvGhHA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e22a9f882ea12c88c5cbdbbb2bbf04ad92349f6bcd7dbc15cd28ac3450a13596&req=fSImFs15nIhXFb4f3HP0gH4hCTawLkFBaF8OG%2Bdd1RUAlbh7Qooouw3ckZDm%0AsO3t%2BSSNNhF46Qm4iA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921047198/2ef9ca58a05e3ddc33745e56/oFXkX4YkH-LhEQ4h61qpagF0JEwqqvGhHA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=e22a9f882ea12c88c5cbdbbb2bbf04ad92349f6bcd7dbc15cd28ac3450a13596&req=fSImFs15nIhXFb4f3HP0gH4hCTawLkFBaF8OG%2Bdd1RUAlbh7Qooouw3ckZDm%0AsO3t%2BSSNNhF46Qm4iA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** The Postmark TXT DKIM key record needs to be published in **Valimail Enforce**.

**5.** Once the record is added Valimail Enforce, DKIM will show as verified in your Postmark account. You can use the **Verify** button to initiate a manual verification check if you do not see the record automatically become verified.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921047204/d047cd975143c8bee821a9f2/Xk08OTqgTk0FpjsXrvk0HkzbbmvcXxtdCQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b5846c5bdd0edf50e801367d87cb56854f65ff98e7ebe73c53bb42cd612a1468&req=fSImFs15n4FbFb4f3HP0gLG4hFsnEJZFmrMqI1wLyAjbv%2F5bgFW7jm5XnL01%0ApGROD96SLDbzdmU9zw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921047204/d047cd975143c8bee821a9f2/Xk08OTqgTk0FpjsXrvk0HkzbbmvcXxtdCQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b5846c5bdd0edf50e801367d87cb56854f65ff98e7ebe73c53bb42cd612a1468&req=fSImFs15n4FbFb4f3HP0gLG4hFsnEJZFmrMqI1wLyAjbv%2F5bgFW7jm5XnL01%0ApGROD96SLDbzdmU9zw%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** After the DKIM record is verified, we will begin signing emails sent through Postmark using this domain with DKIM.

[![a white background with black text](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921047213/9364acaf15f3b9c1bbbd51a0/hCZ4GmOlxSFCYmim5jdLgG-DRyR8H-RcQQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a1e7859b927ca09f7866fbb631173a5739371104648d135a77bad474016b9504&req=fSImFs15n4BcFb4f3HP0gNUmcQ%2F2oLG17EMsml%2BebSxg5P6fcrsjL1WLJgnM%0AxwDve2c8sVP7c5D%2Ftw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921047213/9364acaf15f3b9c1bbbd51a0/hCZ4GmOlxSFCYmim5jdLgG-DRyR8H-RcQQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a1e7859b927ca09f7866fbb631173a5739371104648d135a77bad474016b9504&req=fSImFs15n4BcFb4f3HP0gNUmcQ%2F2oLG17EMsml%2BebSxg5P6fcrsjL1WLJgnM%0AxwDve2c8sVP7c5D%2Ftw%3D%3D%0A)

You can find the instruction on how to set up DKIM in Postmark **[here](https://postmarkapp.com/support/article/1091-how-do-i-set-up-dkim-for-postmark)
**.

Add a Postmark DKIM key in Valimail
===================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Postmark emails
=======================================================

Once you establish that Postmark is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in Postmark for your domain
================================================

You may notice that Postmark emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added Postmark to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by Postmark not sending SPF aligned-mail and can be corrected by configuring a CNAME record.

More details on how to achieve that are found in the article **[here](https://postmarkapp.com/support/article/910-how-do-i-add-a-custom-return-path)
**.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758045-robly#h_9d4cf06cb7)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Robly

Robly
=====

How to set up DMARC for Robly in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758045-robly#h_64049108bd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758045-robly#h_38e0b54b57)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758045-robly#h_9d4cf06cb7)

SPF(supported) (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Robly** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Robly emails
=====================================================

**1.** To set up DKIM, go to **My Account** -> **Settings** -> **Branding** -> **Add New Brand**.

[![a screenshot of a website](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921068658/c0906396999dad26c623b2b1/entE0lEUrJSTL_FdqSXSb2qgNiNWMaFg_g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=30a3d2ef916d8794dfb17929a0e828cee460dc14ca92dd6296c36f6ec59da09a&req=fSImFs92m4RXFb4f3HP0gAI%2FsVUPb3qmijaOLMJGsKkoq7wW5G5XDUYFtJyp%0A1xmyowv1wMJAo2eCEw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921068658/c0906396999dad26c623b2b1/entE0lEUrJSTL_FdqSXSb2qgNiNWMaFg_g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=30a3d2ef916d8794dfb17929a0e828cee460dc14ca92dd6296c36f6ec59da09a&req=fSImFs92m4RXFb4f3HP0gAI%2FsVUPb3qmijaOLMJGsKkoq7wW5G5XDUYFtJyp%0A1xmyowv1wMJAo2eCEw%3D%3D%0A)

**2.** Fill in your brand name, from domain, and link subdomain, then click **Save**:

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921068660/3432e0c59eeb09519238add5/j5JmkQ_Tdz_9fZPTPV7F4vM0POwt6gJyJQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0e7eb177d7d185aaa1c061e81ffbb40a2d9707ea0091ef3aeb3803029e244fc3&req=fSImFs92m4dfFb4f3HP0gDq0qzPi2CJtXaY%2F7vtHAUet61czWhQ%2FVS3MXQ8Z%0Ak6NAsNFMnm0D07Wj3g%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921068660/3432e0c59eeb09519238add5/j5JmkQ_Tdz_9fZPTPV7F4vM0POwt6gJyJQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0e7eb177d7d185aaa1c061e81ffbb40a2d9707ea0091ef3aeb3803029e244fc3&req=fSImFs92m4dfFb4f3HP0gDq0qzPi2CJtXaY%2F7vtHAUet61czWhQ%2FVS3MXQ8Z%0Ak6NAsNFMnm0D07Wj3g%3D%3D%0A)

**Brand Name**: the name of your brand. For example: “Robly”.

**From domain**: this should be your domain and the gTLD. For example: “robly.com”.

**Link Subdomain**: The subdomain is what appears before your domain. For example using “links” would result in the subdomain “links.robly.com.” This is the encoded domain that will be used for all links in an email campaign. We auto-suggest “links” but really this could be any word you wanted to use, because if you own your domain, then you have control over what subdomains you create within that domain. It follows, then, that if you’re setting up Branding for a domain, you should make sure you have access to the

**3.** After you enter your brand information and click Save, you’ll see a chart generate with four DNS records (3 CNAME and 1 TXT).

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921068664/503f2bf675f6050d925ae887/qr69gJMtBwbk_gsWaeT5djQ0sxr0nRY14Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=aa3af0f0d590efc7e8f1fa04b279d7e3879df431b90ad863aec3e7d07a06cfd1&req=fSImFs92m4dbFb4f3HP0gOrIxEEXENdmx7EY7Z0V5P9N4%2F8ff%2B35o0px%2FK7N%0A%2FjSLE1ebVyztxeWTOA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921068664/503f2bf675f6050d925ae887/qr69gJMtBwbk_gsWaeT5djQ0sxr0nRY14Q.png?expires=1755792000&signature=aa3af0f0d590efc7e8f1fa04b279d7e3879df431b90ad863aec3e7d07a06cfd1&req=fSImFs92m4dbFb4f3HP0gOrIxEEXENdmx7EY7Z0V5P9N4%2F8ff%2B35o0px%2FK7N%0A%2FjSLE1ebVyztxeWTOA%3D%3D%0A)

**Important Note**: If you are managing DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the DKIM TXT record issued in Robly on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail. The other 3 CNAME records will need to be added in your DNS host.

**4.** After records are entered into Valimail Enforce and your DNS correctly, your domain should authenticate within a few moments. In some cases, it may take a bit longer.

After you’ve added these records to your DNS provider, you can check the **I’ve added these records** box and click **Verify** to check the **Status** of each record.

Each record will need a status of **Valid** before the process can be successful.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921068670/f089ecbe590eed6e09dc3b37/Lk3q-g5RFbQuXxa6D7acQuK6VtZcnVJQoA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8902b443abd1a86ee5a1a3f7b73bdf6eac320850a165261f3a028f18ca224916&req=fSImFs92m4ZfFb4f3HP0gDD9i9ny7i31eJP0UYu9v%2BubsbDkqADXzZ74qG01%0AakQNzt3%2BqUdtaBfRHg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921068670/f089ecbe590eed6e09dc3b37/Lk3q-g5RFbQuXxa6D7acQuK6VtZcnVJQoA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=8902b443abd1a86ee5a1a3f7b73bdf6eac320850a165261f3a028f18ca224916&req=fSImFs92m4ZfFb4f3HP0gDD9i9ny7i31eJP0UYu9v%2BubsbDkqADXzZ74qG01%0AakQNzt3%2BqUdtaBfRHg%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Robly **[here](https://support.robly.com/custom-dkim-and-spf/)
**.

Add a Robly DKIM key in Valimail
================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Robly emails
====================================================

Once you establish that Robly is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758135-turbosmtp#h_fec6c4b058)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758135-turbosmtp#h_015ac73ae2)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

TurboSMTP

TurboSMTP
=========

How to set up DMARC for TurboSMTP in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758135-turbosmtp#h_1eb3911ad1)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758135-turbosmtp#h_fec6c4b058)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758135-turbosmtp#h_015ac73ae2)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **TurboSMTP** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your TurboSMTP emails
=========================================================

The DKIM configuration for turboSMTP involves the creation of a TXT-type records for the subdomain “turbo-smtp.\_domainkey” as shown below:

Domain

_turbo-smtp_.\_domainkey

Value

k=rsa; p=_MIGfMA0GCSqGSIb3DQEBAQUAA4GNADCBiQKBgQDT3MWLni6so1q9eQggRYBCLHFjohZkCnYHH8gZNDBm6zRrodRVpWpJQW7x3cWWiuBhS1X0IfBB80l5tqFa+yc+mVgnk8tkUzOHFbPQPp4fi7egTpMtsQW/ZMrxw73SItNvPr72qvJTYZNPxarMx+ULjEWybcfEdXHPY8jslGcpCwIDAQAB_

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail, yo uwill need to add the key selector and value from above on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

To guarantee an optimum delivery rate and prevent your emails from ending up in spam, after adding the TXT DKIM key in Valimail Enforce as shown below, you have to activate the **Domain Authentication** mode in your turboSMTP account. Here are the simple steps to follow:

**1.** Log in to your **turboMSTP** account.

**2.** From the main menu select **Settings**.

[![a screenshot of a mail activity](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072353/3d54af0fe0b10a913a4192ba/-1u5kPqa_pv7iKCsb6UZxNUpCtG0AaEueQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0a5a3b3c578e5f12e4167412544d7cd877c0bc57c132b8f9ade34437234f2cf1&req=fSImFs58noRcFb4f3HP0gGqIxn8CWh%2BVv8waq%2BlptQ4RwjHcSQaBe5%2Fsian8%0AUCG1ngKXUXcCYAI05Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072353/3d54af0fe0b10a913a4192ba/-1u5kPqa_pv7iKCsb6UZxNUpCtG0AaEueQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0a5a3b3c578e5f12e4167412544d7cd877c0bc57c132b8f9ade34437234f2cf1&req=fSImFs58noRcFb4f3HP0gGqIxn8CWh%2BVv8waq%2BlptQ4RwjHcSQaBe5%2Fsian8%0AUCG1ngKXUXcCYAI05Q%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** In the **DNS Records** box, click the **Edit** button.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072355/20b3cbc0dd83ecf8ecdc8e41/Z5vuy6MV6Hi3LshwtNvKpH338RQM_y6psw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c838420f8fdec95111437a57fa0f83d92499f74760c29ed62dbb923635383d31&req=fSImFs58noRaFb4f3HP0gLGCsoN6%2FUDQTrUmz%2FkJY5XtWcIqRtQYW9ax5eHJ%0AjrWTkrvrqZzOh%2FALmw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072355/20b3cbc0dd83ecf8ecdc8e41/Z5vuy6MV6Hi3LshwtNvKpH338RQM_y6psw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=c838420f8fdec95111437a57fa0f83d92499f74760c29ed62dbb923635383d31&req=fSImFs58noRaFb4f3HP0gLGCsoN6%2FUDQTrUmz%2FkJY5XtWcIqRtQYW9ax5eHJ%0AjrWTkrvrqZzOh%2FALmw%3D%3D%0A)

**4\.** Then click on the gray switch labeled **Disabled**.

[![a screen shot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072359/2b138cd8e4fda419e29131e2/yQpnvxPyx1VY8DXkIsEbD_ZXx-ftnX0yoQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=219eb1e0ebe1ff25fd18a0b3665e22b7f300ec6e9d0546b1afd8c48fdfa59f92&req=fSImFs58noRWFb4f3HP0gK%2FLzS%2FfktSW9ht3FxnEGTHOyArAzo5m4gwgcBhn%0AR%2FLl4jsSbMiCRmWlMQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072359/2b138cd8e4fda419e29131e2/yQpnvxPyx1VY8DXkIsEbD_ZXx-ftnX0yoQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=219eb1e0ebe1ff25fd18a0b3665e22b7f300ec6e9d0546b1afd8c48fdfa59f92&req=fSImFs58noRWFb4f3HP0gK%2FLzS%2FfktSW9ht3FxnEGTHOyArAzo5m4gwgcBhn%0AR%2FLl4jsSbMiCRmWlMQ%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** Wait for the confirmation message, the switch will turn green and its label will change to **Enabled**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072364/c307db77c8ca99287bf0b714/uYNn-jyOLfQkgk9ElODxxLh2sRdZCF5iBw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0943a9c659ad9fe6c0f4cd35eb4adaaa35137758323f2a5ef3d3fc0edfe08501&req=fSImFs58nodbFb4f3HP0gCyhFtzzsjU2lR98nz0NRE7GCvMDSNwWYQ4GLGcD%0AQpMDbBhA5kPdfs5H%2Bw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072364/c307db77c8ca99287bf0b714/uYNn-jyOLfQkgk9ElODxxLh2sRdZCF5iBw.png?expires=1755792000&signature=0943a9c659ad9fe6c0f4cd35eb4adaaa35137758323f2a5ef3d3fc0edfe08501&req=fSImFs58nodbFb4f3HP0gCyhFtzzsjU2lR98nz0NRE7GCvMDSNwWYQ4GLGcD%0AQpMDbBhA5kPdfs5H%2Bw%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for TurboSMTP, **[here](https://serversmtp.com/how-to-set-up-email-authentication/)
**.

Add a TurboSMTP DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your TurboSMTP emails
========================================================

Once you establish that TurboSMTP is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758042-reachmail#main-content)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758042-reachmail#h_1b8fe75ccd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758042-reachmail#h_d40e36bcd2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758042-reachmail#h_41c73c57d8)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

ReachMail

ReachMail
=========

How to set up DMARC for ReachMail in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758042-reachmail#h_1b8fe75ccd)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758042-reachmail#h_d40e36bcd2)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758042-reachmail#h_41c73c57d8)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **ReachMail** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your ReachMail emails
=========================================================

**1.** Navigate to your **Account** tab, select **Campaign Settings**, and choose **Domain Authentication**.

**2.** Enter your sub-domain (if applicable) and your domain on this page. Once you've entered your information, you will click on the + **Add DKIM Key** button.

[![domain authentication add dkim key button](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921065736/698e312120da1c733cf51dbb/4gWYDahE-NScB-xYlhtP8xCGDDYCkYgVZg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ff20588c88883b071f30c76da95e610104cc8814118500ac7a512523159d8548&req=fSImFs97moJZFb4f3HP0gNPFDkAkyAW5yTiwiyOVMFCbk2YHRnaCjnPf70RX%0AVdV4qKXpwZEtU1YuuQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921065736/698e312120da1c733cf51dbb/4gWYDahE-NScB-xYlhtP8xCGDDYCkYgVZg.png?expires=1755792000&signature=ff20588c88883b071f30c76da95e610104cc8814118500ac7a512523159d8548&req=fSImFs97moJZFb4f3HP0gNPFDkAkyAW5yTiwiyOVMFCbk2YHRnaCjnPf70RX%0AVdV4qKXpwZEtU1YuuQ%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** Once you've added a DKIM domain, our system will offer you instructions on setting up SPF and DKIM. On the left, you will see the SPF record and on the right, your DKIM instructions.

[![domain authentication invalid records](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921065748/51e25bc71019bb05c9f2e609/PQibC6stqB4O5excLyzkJJLQcMrXUeETig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=898ed8b29d253da6963e8081cff1346d1c9c10aec491399f352a9f9db680b024&req=fSImFs97moVXFb4f3HP0gBCxBXDR0O5zmXzZjRcunHg19qRaPALt5PW2RXLJ%0A0aLlbbo5y8GtrhEQMA%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921065748/51e25bc71019bb05c9f2e609/PQibC6stqB4O5excLyzkJJLQcMrXUeETig.png?expires=1755792000&signature=898ed8b29d253da6963e8081cff1346d1c9c10aec491399f352a9f9db680b024&req=fSImFs97moVXFb4f3HP0gBCxBXDR0O5zmXzZjRcunHg19qRaPALt5PW2RXLJ%0A0aLlbbo5y8GtrhEQMA%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** You will need to make these changes on your DNS. Once you've made the necessary changes, you should wait about 10 minutes and use the **Check SPF Record** and **Check DKIM Record** buttons. You will see green success messages if the installation was successful.

[![domain configuration valid records](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921065753/63390d17616c097c032acf6f/2Q7eT40OlImmURm1TATK8w-SdWSdYpIA_A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7885466b43841c192aeca2abe0681cca1e7e96caad8eab7b8c0ccac6b83fb044&req=fSImFs97moRcFb4f3HP0gPqQEjPz5L1UG060%2Fl6XtIRdIBEuAM7U0rkAWUgA%0AB52mtvRUp8j4rWwu0Q%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921065753/63390d17616c097c032acf6f/2Q7eT40OlImmURm1TATK8w-SdWSdYpIA_A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7885466b43841c192aeca2abe0681cca1e7e96caad8eab7b8c0ccac6b83fb044&req=fSImFs97moRcFb4f3HP0gPqQEjPz5L1UG060%2Fl6XtIRdIBEuAM7U0rkAWUgA%0AB52mtvRUp8j4rWwu0Q%3D%3D%0A)

**Important**: If you manage DKIM for your domain in Valimail, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for ReachMail, **[here](https://reachmail.com/support/article/dkim-record-installation)
**.

Add a ReachMail DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your ReachMail emails
========================================================

Once you establish that ReachMail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

Trustwave MailMarshal

Trustwave MailMarshal
=====================

How to set up DMARC for Trustwave MailMarshal in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758134-trustwave-mailmarshal#h_b2e97e06d3)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758134-trustwave-mailmarshal#h_6e9e10cf48)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758134-trustwave-mailmarshal#h_228b576091)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758134-trustwave-mailmarshal#h_a94c41efc9)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758134-trustwave-mailmarshal#h_f8211a7db4)

SPF(supported) / (dedicated subdomain)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Trustwave MailMarshal** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Trustwave MailMarshal emails
=====================================================================

**To configure DKIM validation:**
---------------------------------

**1.** In the left pane of the Management Console click **System Configuration**.

**2.** From the right pane menu expand Receiver Properties and select **DKIM**.

**3.** To enable DKIM validation, check the box **Enable DKIM Detection**. To disable the feature, clear the box.

**4.** To apply the changes, commit the configuration.

**To configure DKIM keys for local domains:**
---------------------------------------------

**1.** In the left pane of the Management Console click **System Configuration**.

**2.** In the right pane, click **Local Domains**. Select a domain, and then click **Edit**.

**3.** Click the **DKIM tab**.

**4.** Click **Add** to generate or import a key, and select appropriate settings. For details, see Help.

**Note**: Keep a copy of the key file in a secure location. DKIM signing keys are not included in the MailMarshal configuration backup.

**5.** Ensure that you have published a DNS TXT record that includes the related public key. The DKIM Key window provides the appropriate record text for the key.

**Important**: If you manage DKIM in Valimail for your domain, you will need to add the TXT DKIM key on your domain's Configuration page in Valimail Enforce.

[![](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072722/4f8e3a0caac67fb5911b567e/BdFb5L54IfQ4Rw-i-fXo0H_wJuWS3IVd9A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1e9bf7a02687ec43295b74f3a6fc0f06fdc6f5cf07a89ec5c5a848d23fd25d49&req=fSImFs58moNdFb4f3HP0gIiu5ZWWa1EKVjgXuD0JInq6Yu0Iv0o41Az2MN1U%0ACFgEpPN6JnQ5wmgx0w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921072722/4f8e3a0caac67fb5911b567e/BdFb5L54IfQ4Rw-i-fXo0H_wJuWS3IVd9A.png?expires=1755792000&signature=1e9bf7a02687ec43295b74f3a6fc0f06fdc6f5cf07a89ec5c5a848d23fd25d49&req=fSImFs58moNdFb4f3HP0gIiu5ZWWa1EKVjgXuD0JInq6Yu0Iv0o41Az2MN1U%0ACFgEpPN6JnQ5wmgx0w%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** To sign messages, once the DKIM key is created and published, use a Content Analysis Rule to apply the DKIM signature. See [Apply DKIM signature](https://support.trustwave.com/search/mailmarshal_smtp/files/MMAS-UnderstandingRuleActions.htm#XREF_54915_6_6_1_19_Apply)
.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Trustwave MailMarshal, **[here](https://support.trustwave.com/search/mailmarshal_smtp/files/mmas-configuringdkim.htm)
**.

Add a Trustwave MailMarshal DKIM key in Valimail
================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Trustwave MailMarshal emails
====================================================================

Once you establish that Trustwave MailMarshal is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

* * *

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

rapidmail

rapidmail
=========

How to set up DMARC for rapidmail in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758040-rapidmail#h_35b092b240)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758040-rapidmail#h_e68c8a8654)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758040-rapidmail#h_3509d273f5)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758040-rapidmail#h_10976e134b)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **rapidmail** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your rapidmail emails
=========================================================

A DKIM record is only required once per domain. If you only have one domain, you only have to do the DKIM entry once.

**1.** To generate your DKIM key and then store it in the DNS with your web host, first go to your rapidmail account.

**2.** Then, in your account, click on your **Abbreviation** -> **Username**.

[![a screenshot of a phone](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063814/20921bc1629a2be84ac346d1/RaTgsKtqtRsz9xyKPmuwlzy4FCabVngDSQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=67a3bdacb1323349c27d2f45eabe1b6f51c5275595f51a88f871a649ad44d9e8&req=fSImFs99lYBbFb4f3HP0gM9BCOQ3phGI1BwehtpH%2B3xyauHSMtlH2OjplYNa%0A8yr8K385MZq6JB%2BiXg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063814/20921bc1629a2be84ac346d1/RaTgsKtqtRsz9xyKPmuwlzy4FCabVngDSQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=67a3bdacb1323349c27d2f45eabe1b6f51c5275595f51a88f871a649ad44d9e8&req=fSImFs99lYBbFb4f3HP0gM9BCOQ3phGI1BwehtpH%2B3xyauHSMtlH2OjplYNa%0A8yr8K385MZq6JB%2BiXg%3D%3D%0A)

**3.** From there, select the **Sender Settings** category in the left pane. At this point you can see all your sender addresses and you can also add new ones there.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063818/b95b2164f980a0fe24c78c38/m_vghSEeSNNttHmikrt-qfzSG1fu7eOM_g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=49d2ab9e23e4627fe2d9ed7ef0976a180ef9482af59ab40bb5f0803e14c16d3a&req=fSImFs99lYBXFb4f3HP0gHCTx30VNtKAQ7JuMSRThyyyppgSWx7n90MtDjVu%0AuYny34%2FUGD99EDbiAg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063818/b95b2164f980a0fe24c78c38/m_vghSEeSNNttHmikrt-qfzSG1fu7eOM_g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=49d2ab9e23e4627fe2d9ed7ef0976a180ef9482af59ab40bb5f0803e14c16d3a&req=fSImFs99lYBXFb4f3HP0gHCTx30VNtKAQ7JuMSRThyyyppgSWx7n90MtDjVu%0AuYny34%2FUGD99EDbiAg%3D%3D%0A)

**4.** Then click on the **Sender Authentication** tab. Here you can see your sender domain. If you have created several sender domains, they are also listed here. You must store a new DKIM key for each sender domain. If an entry has already been made correctly, a green tick will appear in front of DKIM.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063821/850572307673cb1c20d12ab4/s7x5UmRqMIIQWgxrhRHVPE40Jn07YV1TUA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f573ee87306a1bd2132640137647449e9b1c690709f9cb5a55564297a8f3c13e&req=fSImFs99lYNeFb4f3HP0gOp%2FL0YtT9csshyWJoFU4VlIIn%2FZFau5e5sn%2Fonn%0AM18gwT19mmO%2Bqy4%2B5w%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063821/850572307673cb1c20d12ab4/s7x5UmRqMIIQWgxrhRHVPE40Jn07YV1TUA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=f573ee87306a1bd2132640137647449e9b1c690709f9cb5a55564297a8f3c13e&req=fSImFs99lYNeFb4f3HP0gOp%2FL0YtT9csshyWJoFU4VlIIn%2FZFau5e5sn%2Fonn%0AM18gwT19mmO%2Bqy4%2B5w%3D%3D%0A)

If the entry was made incorrectly, you will see a warning signal:

[![a red and white sign with a exclamation mark](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063825/c4e85e88ffba5e875a5093ce/fMFJzcgFvK1IzFw5fJMv54dWLIUro0HipA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b2bd2f83e0b598a14aede9678c7c7c8e0bcee0a0f61b0ea5561e39798411390d&req=fSImFs99lYNaFb4f3HP0gDdgwbAA2joTFwGP0XpUNqGuSlK%2B3v%2Bj%2FLTUpmKA%0A5c4Vtnx6rDUtmYEKMg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063825/c4e85e88ffba5e875a5093ce/fMFJzcgFvK1IzFw5fJMv54dWLIUro0HipA.png?expires=1755792000&signature=b2bd2f83e0b598a14aede9678c7c7c8e0bcee0a0f61b0ea5561e39798411390d&req=fSImFs99lYNaFb4f3HP0gDdgwbAA2joTFwGP0XpUNqGuSlK%2B3v%2Bj%2FLTUpmKA%0A5c4Vtnx6rDUtmYEKMg%3D%3D%0A)

If it has not yet been carried out, a gray cross is displayed. In this case, please click on **Show** (eye symbol).

[![a black eye in a red frame](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063830/adec8c01c69c271a07359603/IYjkdaYyn72Cmrysarip4q8jSUMge4pQZQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=69848e028bd5229086c4d6e148da44763e8348c347b34877d2a19d91f326cfd1&req=fSImFs99lYJfFb4f3HP0gOjLm4BnRnGoA7EUb6gOJIbOBYqDgnYdVLvdsw8S%0A693j7PwRCDXrGSfAnw%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063830/adec8c01c69c271a07359603/IYjkdaYyn72Cmrysarip4q8jSUMge4pQZQ.png?expires=1755792000&signature=69848e028bd5229086c4d6e148da44763e8348c347b34877d2a19d91f326cfd1&req=fSImFs99lYJfFb4f3HP0gOjLm4BnRnGoA7EUb6gOJIbOBYqDgnYdVLvdsw8S%0A693j7PwRCDXrGSfAnw%3D%3D%0A)

**5.** The DKIM key is displayed in a separate window. You can now add the displayed information to your DNS. Keep the browser window open with the DKIM data and copy the text under **Target and Value**.

[![a screenshot of a computer](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063838/0b5e376e94f8784aec0d9ff9/vZikEuE3xoeywY62ho1nIRiVrVQ3nbJs0w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7b661477dceb6e0ead3e0086577d40a44dd19667888817d62ede4a78b8e5c9c2&req=fSImFs99lYJXFb4f3HP0gFUAbEoiflQtH3%2FFD6nze7%2FfE7rjfkaEKLINrdTP%0ApDNxC7uRn%2FGzxUXVzg%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063838/0b5e376e94f8784aec0d9ff9/vZikEuE3xoeywY62ho1nIRiVrVQ3nbJs0w.png?expires=1755792000&signature=7b661477dceb6e0ead3e0086577d40a44dd19667888817d62ede4a78b8e5c9c2&req=fSImFs99lYJXFb4f3HP0gFUAbEoiflQtH3%2FFD6nze7%2FfE7rjfkaEKLINrdTP%0ApDNxC7uRn%2FGzxUXVzg%3D%3D%0A)

**6.** Please add the DKIM key in **Valimail Enforce**.

**7.** In the rapidmail account, please click on the **refresh** button on the far right to check whether the key is stored correctly.

If so, a green check mark appears in the DKIM column.

If the settings are incorrect, a warning signal is displayed.

[![a white background with blue text](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063844/19a565f6450769734999e688/Rn6M2zC-1ehtpuCDoKzm6RHFrU2Lvsgi2g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a1e53aae45f9c1b43dfe34ddd60fe4b4fb08a3fea3a67ce72651aafbd4d9af2d&req=fSImFs99lYVbFb4f3HP0gDtPjOYv0QheyS4jjLZ6nramGz0cwE1HQuRLO36l%0ASsRhqh9WoQZw9Zp7cQ%3D%3D%0A)](https://valimail-2bbeb801593d.intercom-attachments-1.com/i/o/921063844/19a565f6450769734999e688/Rn6M2zC-1ehtpuCDoKzm6RHFrU2Lvsgi2g.png?expires=1755792000&signature=a1e53aae45f9c1b43dfe34ddd60fe4b4fb08a3fea3a67ce72651aafbd4d9af2d&req=fSImFs99lYVbFb4f3HP0gDtPjOYv0QheyS4jjLZ6nramGz0cwE1HQuRLO36l%0ASsRhqh9WoQZw9Zp7cQ%3D%3D%0A)

You can also find the instructions to set up DKIM for rapidmail **[here](https://www.rapidmail.de/hilfe/dkim-key-mit-rapidmail-erstellen)
**.

Add a rapidmail DKIM key in Valimail
====================================

#### You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your rapidmail emails
========================================================

Once you establish that rapidmail is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.

Enable SPF alignment in rapidmail for your domain
=================================================

You may notice that rapidmail emails are not authenticated via SPF, even after you have added rapidmail to your domain's configuration. This is likely caused by rapidmail not sending SPF aligned-mail.

More details on how to achieve that are found in the article, **[here](https://www.rapidmail.de/hilfe/spf-eintrag-mit-rapidmail-erstellen)
**.

* * *

[SALESmanago](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758315-salesmanago)

# Trend Micro Email Security | Valimail Help Center

[Skip to main content](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758133-trend-micro-email-security#main-content)

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758133-trend-micro-email-security#h_1ca5aaf228)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758133-trend-micro-email-security#h_8ca761d58b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758133-trend-micro-email-security#h_102f34cc1f)

[All Collections](https://support.valimail.com/en/)

[Email Service Providers](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/7633239-email-service-providers)

[Configuration Guides](https://support.valimail.com/en/collections/6449834-configuration-guides)

Trend Micro Email Security

Trend Micro Email Security
==========================

How to set up DMARC for Trend Micro Email Security in Valimail

Updated over a year ago

Table of contents

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758133-trend-micro-email-security#h_1ca5aaf228)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758133-trend-micro-email-security#h_8ca761d58b)

[](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8758133-trend-micro-email-security#h_102f34cc1f)

SPF(supported)  
DKIM(recommended)

This article covers the SPF and DKIM authentication processes for **Trend Micro Email Security** and how they are managed in Valimail. While only one of the two authentication methods is required for an email to pass DMARC, our recommendation is to configure both whenever possible.

Configuring DKIM authentication for your Trend Micro Email Security emails
==========================================================================

The DKIM signing settings apply only to the selected sender domain.

**1.** Log into your Trend Micro Email Security console.

**2.** Go to **Outbound Protection** -> **DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) Signing**.

**3.** Click **Add**.

The Add DKIM Signing Settings screen appears.

**4.** Select a specific sender domain from the **Managed domain** drop-down list.

**5.** Select **Enable DKIM signing**.

**6.** Optionally select **Sign email messages with no envelope sender addresses**.

For email messages with no envelope sender addresses (such as auto-reply messages or bounced messages), Trend Micro Email Security attempts to find the sender domain from the email header From and applies DKIM signing settings of the sender domain.

**7.** Configure general settings for DKIM signing.

**SDID**: select a signing domain identifier from the drop-down list.

**Selector**: selector to subdivide key namespace. Retain the default value.

**Headers to sign**: select one or multiple headers to sign and customize more headers if necessary.

**Wait time**: specify how long it takes for a key pair to take effect. Trend Micro Email Security starts to count the wait time once if finds the public key in the DNS.

**Key pair**: select a key length and click Generate to generate a key pair.

**Note**: If you are managing DKIM in Valimail for your domain, the DKIM key/s will need to be added in the Configuration page for your domain in Valimail Enforce.

You can also find the instructions on how to set up DKIM and SPF for Trend Micro Email Security, **[here](https://docs.trendmicro.com/en-us/enterprise/trend-micro-email-security-online-help/inbound-and-outbound/domain-based-authent/domainkeys-identifie/adding-dkim-signing-.aspx#:~:text=Trend%20Micro%20Email%20Security%20supports,not%20been%20modified%20during%20transport.)
**.

Add a Trend Micro Email Security DKIM key in Valimail
=====================================================

You can find more detailed information on how to add a DKIM key in Valimail, here:

[Add a DKIM key in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8466384-adding-a-dkim-key-in-enforce-align)

Configuring SPF authentication for your Trend Micro Email Security emails
=========================================================================

Once you establish that Trend Micro Email Security is an authorized sender for your domain, you will need to add the service in your Enabled Senders.

You will find more detailed information on how to add a service for your domain in Valimail, here:

[Enabling a sender for SPF in Valimail](https://support.valimail.com/en/articles/8733052-how-to-enable-a-sender-for-spf-in-enforce-align)

**_Note_**: We encourage you to use the comment section for any useful information about your sending service, such as the name of the service owner, change request ticket numbers, etc.
